events.title: REA Group - Simplicity Loans & Advisory - Phase 1 determination
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
REA Financial Services Holding Co. Pty Ltd, as a subsidiary of REA
Group Ltd (REA Group) (ASX:REA), proposes to acquire 70% of the
issued share capital in Simplicity Loans & Advisory Pty Ltd
(Simplicity) (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

The acquirer, REA Group, provides digital advertising and mortgage
brokerage services through the below brands and platforms. News
Corp owns a ~61% controlling stake in REA Group.
REA Group owns and operates:
• Mortgage Choice Pty Ltd and Smartline Operations Pty
Ltd (Mortgage Choice), a mortgage broker which
specialises in residential mortgage broking services
(homes and personal loans) and also provides financial
planning services. Mortgage Choice also acts as an
aggregator – it connects brokers seeking residential
loans to a panel of lenders.
• Simpology (32.97% interest held by REA Group), an e-
lodgement platform used by brokers to submit loans to
a range of lenders.
• Athena Home Loans (19.9% interest held by REA Group),
a non-bank lender which has focuses on residential
home loans.
• Online property advertising platforms: realestate.com.au
(residential property), realcommercial.com.au
(commercial property), flatmates.com.au (share
accommodation), Spacely (short-term commercial and
co-working property), PropTrack (property data services
and market insights) and 1Form (centralised platform
for rental property applications).
The target, Simplicity, is a boutique mortgage broker operating in
Sydney, Melbourne and Brisbane specialising in commercial and
developer mortgages.
Simplicity also operates Marketplace Finance platform (MKP), a
bilateral mortgage referral tool connecting third-party referrers
(typically residential mortgage brokers with no commercial
experience) with Simplicity’s commercial mortage brokers.
Fulfilment of the loans occurs by Simplicity’s employed brokers via
aggregator panels or direct lender channels.
The reasons for the ACCC’s determination are set out in section 2.

determination_statement_of_reasons.text: When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the interests of consumers.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines and interim merger process guidelines.
For the reasons given below, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into effect.
Industry background
Mortgage broking services form part of the broader mortgage distribution supply chains shown in Figure 1 below: Figure 1: Mortgage distribution supply chain1

items: Lenders provide finance to borrowers seeking to buy property. Lenders in Australia include the ‘big 4’ banks, other banks with an Australian presence2 and non-banks (e.g. La Trobe Financial, Pepper Money). Lenders can distribute their loans either directly or indirectly through mortgage brokers.

Mortgage aggregators such as Mortgage Choice, LMG, and AFG act as an intermediary between brokers and lenders, providing brokers with access to a panel of lenders and a range of mortgage products, which they in turn recommend to customers. Aggregators have contractual agreements with the lenders on their panel, which allows their affiliated brokers to arrange loans from those lenders for their customers.

Mortgage brokers, including Simplicity and the Mortgage Choice broker network act as an intermediary by connecting borrowers to lenders, generally via 1 ACCC, ‘AFG Connective - Statement Of Issues - 12 February 2020’, ACCC, 12 Feb 2020, accessed 29 April 2026. 2 For example, ING Bank, Bendigo and Adelaide Bank, Suncorp Bank and Macquarie Bank. aggregators. Mortgage brokers help customers select an appropriate loan, prepare the loan application, negotiate with lenders and settle the loan. Mortgage brokers are paid by lenders for the services they provide to consumers.

determination_statement_of_reasons.text: Overlap and relationship between the merger parties
The merger parties overlap in the supply of mortgage broking services in Australia:

items: Mortgage Choice’s core business is residential mortgage broking with a small number of commercial settlements via its national network of franchisees and credit representatives.
Simplicity is a boutique mortgage broker operating in Sydney, Melbourne and Brisbane specialising in commercial and developer mortgages.

determination_statement_of_reasons.text: The merger parties may also have a non-horizontal relationship where REA Group through Mortgage Choice brokers or via its online real estate advertising platforms may refer commercial borrower leads to Simplicity’s commercial mortgage brokers.
Competition assessment

The ACCC has considered the effects of the Acquisition by comparing the likely future state of competition if the Acquisition proceeds against the continuation of the current state of competition (being the likely future state of competition if the Acquisition does not proceed).
Relevant areas of competition

The ACCC considers that the relevant areas of competitive overlap for the purposes of its assessment of the competitive effects of the Acquisition are the supply of residential and commercial mortgage broking services in Australia.

The ACCC considers it likely that there are separate markets for residential, and commercial mortgage broking. Individual home buyers for residential mortgage broking and commercial borrowers (corporate clients, property developers, commercial real estate investors etc.) are likely to have different needs. Commercial lending arrangements are also likely to be more complex than residential home loans and require specialist mortgage broking skill sets.
Competition effects in the supply of residential and commercial mortgage broking services

The ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the effect of substantially lessening competition in relation to the supply of residential and/or commercial mortgage broking services in Australia for the reasons set out below:

items: REA Group’s Mortgage Choice and Simplicity are unlikely to be close substitutes as they serve different customers with different needs. Based on information from the merger parties, Mortgage Choice’s core customer base comprises of individual home buyers for residential mortgage broking with a small number of commercial settlements while Simplicity’s core business is in commercial mortgages with minimal residential mortgages;

Based on information provided by the merger parties and publicly available information, the market share aggregation arising from the Acquisition is estimated to be low on any market definition, with an estimated post-acquisition market share of below 5 per cent;

Based on information from the merger parties and publicly available information, the merged firm would likely continue to be constrained by other residential mortgage brokers such as Aussie Home Loans, LMG and Lendi; and commercial mortgage brokers such as Stamford Capital Australia, Green Finance Group and Rethink Financing.

determination_statement_of_reasons.text: Non-horizontal effects in commercial mortgage broking services

The ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the effect of substantially lessening competition because the merged entity is unlikely to have the ability and/or incentive to harm the competitiveness of rival commercial mortgage brokers, for example by limiting referrals of commercial borrowing leads from Mortgage Choice or its online real estate advertising platforms to Simplicity for the reasons set out below:

items: Based on information from the merger parties and market feedback, Mortgage Choice is not a significant source of leads for commercial borrowers and post- acquisition, individual Mortgage Choice brokers will continue to retain discretion over the commercial brokers to which they choose to refer commercial leads;

Commercial brokers are not reliant on referrals from residential brokers. Market feedback suggests the most important referral channels for commercial brokers are existing client relationships, professional referral networks, real estate agents and direct marketing;

While REA Group will generate some leads from its online real estate advertising platforms, these leads are unlikely to have a significant impact on the ability of alternative mortgage brokers to remain competitive;

Post-acquisition, the merged firm would continue to face competition from alternative suppliers of commercial borrower referral programs or platforms, which enables advisors and residential mortgage brokers to refer commercial borrower leads.

mergers.merger_description:
CVC Capital Partners plc (CVC Capital Partners) proposes to indirectly acquire 100% of the shares in Marathon Asset Management, LP, Marathon GP Holdings I, LLC and Marathon GP Holdings II, LLC (together, Marathon Asset Management) (the Acquisition).

CVC Capital Partners is listed on the Euronext Amsterdam Stock Exchange and is part of the CVC Network, a global private equity and investment advisory firm that provides investment advice to and/or manages investments on behalf of certain funds and investment vehicles with assets under management (AuM) globally. The CVC Network manages AuM across private equity, credit, secondaries and infrastructure. It holds interests in a range of industries, including energy, renewables, transportation, chemicals, utilities, manufacturing, retail and distribution. CVC Capital Partners has interests in Etraveli Group AB, a flight online travel agency (OTA), and in companies which provide pharmaceutical packaging, excipients and specialty chemicals, being Syntegon Technology GmbH, DFE Pharma GmbH & Co. KG, and Cohizon Life Sciences Limited.

Marathon Asset Management is a New York-based global asset manager focused on liquid (public) and private credit. In addition to its credit asset management and advisory activities, Marathon Asset Management-advised funds and accounts hold equity stakes in a small number of companies and own and lease out numerous assets. Marathon Asset Management has an interest in Auto Europe Group, a car rental OTA business and in Kaléo Inc,a pharmaceutical manufacturer of auto-injector drug delivery systems for epinephrine and naloxone, which are used to treat anaphylaxis, or severe allergic reactions, and opioid overdoses respectively.

Both CVC Capital Partners and Marathon Asset Management supply asset management services, specifically in credit asset management, globally.

determination_table_content.details:
CVC Capital Partners plc’s (CVC Capital Partners) proposed
acquisition of 100% of the shares in Marathon Asset Management,
LP, Marathon GP Holdings I, LLC and Marathon GP Holdings II, LLC
(together, Marathon Asset Management) (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

CVC Capital Partners is listed on the Euronext Amsterdam Stock
Exchange and is part of the CVC Network, a global private equity and
investment advisory firm that provides investment advice to and/or
manages investments on behalf of funds and investment vehicles
with assets under management (AuM) globally. The CVC Network
manages AuM across private equity, credit, secondaries and
infrastructure. It holds interests in a range of industries, including
energy, renewables, transportation, chemicals, utilities,
manufacturing, retail and distribution.
CVC Capital Partners has interests in:
• Etraveli Group AB, a flight online travel agency (OTA).
• Companies which provide pharmaceutical packaging,
excipients and specialty chemicals, being Syntegon
Technology GmbH, DFE Pharma GmbH & Co. KG, and
Cohizon Life Sciences Limited.
Marathon Asset Management is a New York-based global asset
manager focused on liquid (public) and private credit. In addition to
its credit asset management and advisory activities, Marathon Asset
Management-advised funds and accounts hold equity stakes in a
small number of companies and own and lease out numerous
assets.
Marathon Asset Management has an interest in:
• Auto Europe Group, a car rental OTA business.
• Kaléo Inc, a pharmaceutical manufacturer of auto-injector
drug delivery systems for epinephrine and naloxone, which
are used to treat anaphylaxis, or severe allergic reactions,
and opioid overdoses respectively.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition
and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition
assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an
acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in
Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have
regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the
interests of consumers.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines
and interim merger process guidelines.
In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered
the information and documents that were submitted with the
notification form and publicly available information.
The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into
effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the
effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In
reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC
makes the following findings:
• The Parties’ market share aggregation in the supply of credit
asset management and any potential sub-segment is estimated
to be minimal both globally and in the APAC region, and their
market shares remain low.
• In relation to the Parties’ interests in travel related companies,
non-horizontal competition concerns would be unlikely to arise
as CVC Capital Partners would not have the ability and incentive
to foreclose rivals due to the existence of competitors, including
Flight Centre Travel, Webjet, Expedia, Trip.com, Edreams,
Skyscanner, Rental Cars, Economy Bookings and Discover Cars.
• In relation to the Parties’ interests in pharmaceutical related
companies, non-horizontal competition concerns would be
unlikely to arise as there are no existing supply relationships and
there appears to be a low risk of potential foreclosure concerns
post-acquisition.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by

days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s
Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any

Tribunal.

AtkinsRéalis Group Inc. through its wholly owned subsidiary AtkinsRéalis Australia Holdings Pty Ltd (AtkinsRéalis) proposes to acquire 100% of the shares in WGA Group Holdings Pty Ltd (WGA) through a scheme of arrangement.

AtkinsRéalis Group Inc. is a publicly traded, Canadian engineering and construction company with a global footprint. In Australia, AtkinsRéalis operates as a professional engineering and technical consultancy firm that provides engineering, design, advisory and delivery services to public and private customers across the transport, buildings and places, defence, energy and water sectors.

WGA is a multi-disciplinary engineering and project management consultancy firm, which operates across Australia and New Zealand. It provides engineering, technical, advisory, delivery and project management services as stand-alone commissions or multi-disciplinary packages to both public and private clients across the following sectors: buildings urban development, defence and space, energy, ports and marine, resources, sports and recreation, transport and water.

The ACCC notes that until 20 May 2026, this page identified the acquiring entity as AtkinsRéalis Engineering Pty Ltd, another wholly-owned subsidiary of AtkinsRéalis Group Inc. The new acquiring entity, AtkinsRéalis Australia Holdings Pty Ltd, was incorporated on 13 May 2026.

mergers.merger_description:
Freudenberg Home and Cleaning Solutions GmbH (Freudenberg) proposes to acquire 100% of share capital in Nilfisk Holding A/S (Nilfisk).

Freudenberg supplies manual cleaning tools and other cleaning products in Australia. Freudenberg’s brands include Vileda and Oates and Ansell (which is used under licence). Its products include mops and floorcare, brooms, brushware, cloths and wipes, dusters, cleaning carts and trolleys, waste management, window cleaning equipment, spray bottles, durable gloves, and various cleaning chemicals.

Freudenberg is a wholly-owned subsidiary of Freudenberg & Co. KG, which is headquartered in Germany.

Nilfisk supplies professional cleaning machines in Australia including floorcare machines, vacuum cleaners, pressure washers, related accessories, consumables, machine repair and maintenance services. Nilfisk also has businesses in IVS (industrial vacuum systems) and the food industry (cleaning equipment for the food industry).

Nilfisk is the holding company of the Nilfisk group of companies (the Nilfisk Group). The Nilfisk Group is a global original equipment manufacturer for these cleaning machines.

events.title: Freudenberg - Nilfisk - Phase 1 Determination
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Freudenberg Home and Cleaning Solutions GmbH’s (Freudenberg)
proposes to acquire 100% of share capital in Nilfisk Holding A/S
(Nilfisk) (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

The acquirer, Freudenberg, supplies manual cleaning tools and other
cleaning products in Australia. Freudenberg’s brands include Vileda
and Oates. Its products include mops and floorcare, brooms,
brushware, cloths and wipes, dusters, cleaning carts and trolleys,
waste management, window cleaning equipment, spray bottles,
durable gloves, and various cleaning chemicals. It supplies these
products primarily for retailer customer use.
Freudenberg is a wholly-owned subsidiary of Freudenberg & Co. KG,
which is headquartered in Germany. Freudenberg manufactures its
manual cleaning products overseas and imports them into Australia
through third party distributors and large retailers which on-sell to
end customers.
The target, Nilfisk, supplies professional cleaning machines for
commercial use in Australia including floorcare machines, vacuum
cleaners, pressure washers and related accessories and
consumables. It also provides machine repair and maintenance
services.
Nilfisk is the holding company of the Nilfisk group of companies (the
Nilfisk Group). The Nilfisk Group is a global original equipment
manufacturer for these cleaning machines. Nilfisk manufactures its
professional cleaning machines overseas and imports them into
Australia through third party distributors which then on-sell to end
customers.

While the Parties both supply cleaning products, there is minimal
overlap between the Parties. Freudenberg supplies manual cleaning
products, primarily for retailer customer use, and some professional
use. Nilfisk supplies cleaning machines, primarily for professional
use.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition
and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition
assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an
acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in
Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have
regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the
interests of consumers.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines
and interim merger process guidelines.
In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered
the information and documents that were submitted with the
notification form, information from third partes, and publicly
available information.
The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into
effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the
effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In
reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC
makes the following findings.
• The Parties are not close competitors, and its products are not
readily substitutable for each other. Freudenberg supplies
manual cleaning products (such as mops, brooms, cloths and
wipes) for both consumer and professional use, whilst Nilfisk
supplies professional cleaning machines (such as pressure
washers, and commercial and industrial vacuum cleaners, for
professional cleaning customers) largely for commercial use.
• Post acquisition, the Parties would continue to face competition
from alternative suppliers of manual cleaning products and
professional cleaning machines in Australia, including several
competitors which are of a similar size, or larger than the
Parties.
• While some market participants suggested that the acquisition
could enable the Parties to tie or bundle manual cleaning
products and professional cleaning machines, or foreclose
access to inputs used in the manufacture of cleaning machines,
the ACCC considers that the merged entity would be unlikely to
have the ability and incentive to do so due to the presence of
competitors in both the manual cleaning product and
professional cleaning machine markets in Australia.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by
the Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are
dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the
Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to
the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar
days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s
Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any
application for review, please contact the Australian Competition
Tribunal.

Quanta Services Australia Pty Ltd (**Quanta**) proposes to acquire 100% of the shares in PSD Global Holdings Pty Ltd, including ownership of its wholly owned subsidiaries, PSD Energy Pty Ltd and PSD Plant & Equipment Pty Ltd, and all assets constituting the business trading as PSD Energy (together, **PSD**).

Quanta, through its subsidiaries, is involved in engineering, procurement and construction of infrastructure projects in the energy sector, including high voltage (**HV**) electricity projects. Quanta’s operations in HV electricity projects include transmission lines, substations, grid-scale Battery Energy Storage Systems (**BESS**), and the supply of protection panels and switchrooms.

PSD is an electrical engineering, design and construction company specialising in HV electrical infrastructure and secondary systems. PSD’s projects include substations, solar farms, BESS and the supply of protection panels, switchrooms and related facilities.

events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Philip Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Ventura Motors NewCo Pty Ltd, a wholly owned subsidiary of
Ventura Motors Pty Ltd (together with its subsidiaries, Ventura
Motors) proposes to acquire the business assets (including the
vehicles, plant and equipment, IP and IP licences, goodwill, and
business records) of Crown Coaches Pty Ltd and Crown Hybrid Pty
Ltd that constitute the Crown Coaches business (Crown Coaches)
(the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

The acquirer, Ventura Motors, is an Australian bus operator primarily
active in metropolitan Melbourne. It supplies metropolitan bus
services, public school bus services, and private school and other
private charter bus services, as well as regional intercity coach
transport.
The target, Crown Coaches, is a family-owned bus operator that
supplies public school bus services, and private school and other
private charter bus services in metropolitan Melbourne.

Ventura Motors and Crown Coaches both supply:
• bus services to public specialised schools in metropolitan
Melbourne
• private charter bus services in metropolitan Melbourne.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition
and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition
assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an
acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in
Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have
regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the
interests of consumers.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines
and interim merger process guidelines.
In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered
the information and documents that were submitted with the
notification form, information from third partes, and publicly
available information.
The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into
effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the
effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In
reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC
makes the following findings.
• Notwithstanding a concern expressed by a third party that the
Acquisition may reduce options available to customers, post-
acquisition, the merged firm would likely continue to face
competition from alternative suppliers of public specialised
school bus services and private charter bus services in
metropolitan Melbourne.
• The aggregation arising from the Acquisition is low in relation to
public specialised school bus services and private charter bus
services in metropolitan Melbourne.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by
the Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are
dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the
Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to
the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar
days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s
Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any
application for review, please contact the Australian Competition
Tribunal.

mergers.merger_description:
GIP V Snow Bidco Limited (the **Acquirer**) proposes to acquire 100% of the issued share capital of Envol Holdings Limited and its subsidiaries including TCR Group (the **Target**) (the **Acquisition**).

The Acquirer is a special purpose vehicle controlled by funds managed and/or advised by Global Infrastructure Management, LLC (together with is affiliates, **Global Infrastructure Management**).

Global Infrastructure Management is the global independent infrastructure fund manager of Global Infrastructure Partners (**GIP**), headquartered in New York, United States (**US**), and primarily focused on investing in transportation, energy, waste, water, and digital infrastructure sectors. Global Infrastructure Management also holds a 37% interest in Sydney Aviation Alliance Holdings Pty Ltd, which fully owns Sydney Airport Corporation Ltd. Global Infrastructure Management is ultimately controlled by BlackRock, Inc. (**BlackRock**).

BlackRock is a US company listed on the New York Stock Exchange. BlackRock is active in the provision of global investment management, risk management and advisory services to institutional and retail clients around the world.

The Target is the holding entity of TCR Group (**TCR**), which is primarily active in full-service leasing of ground support equipment (**GSE**) to independent ground handlers, airlines, and airports globally. TCR also provides repair and maintenance services for GSE (on both leased and third-party fleet) and fleet management services. TCR is headquartered in Steenokkerzeel, Belgium.

In Australia, TCR is involved in the leasing out and maintenance of GSE at airports around Australia through various subsidiaries.

TCR is currently indirectly solely controlled by 3i Group plc. 3i Group plc is an international investment company listed on the London Stock Exchange, with offices in Europe and North America.

events.title: Global Infrastructure Management - TCR – Phase 1 determination

determination_table_content.details:
GIP V Snow Bidco Limited proposes to acquire 100% of the issued
share capital of Envol Holdings Limited and its subsidiaries including
TCR Group.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

The acquirer, GIP V Snow Bidco Limited (the Acquirer) proposes to
acquire 100% of the issued share capital of Envol Holdings Limited
and its subsidiaries including TCR Group (the Target)
(the Acquisition).
The Acquirer is a special purpose vehicle controlled by funds
managed and/or advised by Global Infrastructure Management, LLC
(together with is affiliates, Global Infrastructure Management).
Global Infrastructure Management is the global independent
infrastructure fund manager of Global Infrastructure Partners (GIP),
headquartered in New York, United States (US), and primarily
focused on investing in transportation, energy, waste, water, and
digital infrastructure sectors. Relevantly to the Acquisition, Global
Infrastructure Management also holds a 37% interest in Sydney
Aviation Alliance Holdings Pty Ltd, which fully owns Sydney Airport
Corporation Ltd. (Sydney Airport). Sydney Airport owns small
amounts of GSE with which it provides services to airlines. Global
Infrastructure Management is ultimately controlled by BlackRock,
Inc. (BlackRock).
BlackRock is a US company listed on the New York Stock Exchange.
BlackRock is active in the provision of global investment
management, risk management and advisory services to
institutional and retail clients around the world.
The target is the holding entity of TCR Group (TCR), which is
primarily active in full-service leasing of ground support equipment
(GSE) to independent ground handlers, airlines, and airports globally.
TCR also provides repair and maintenance services for GSE (on both
leased and third-party fleet) and fleet management services. TCR is
headquartered in Steenokkerzeel, Belgium.
In Australia, TCR is involved in the leasing out and maintenance of
GSE at airports around Australia through various subsidiaries.
TCR is currently indirectly solely controlled by 3i Group plc. 3i Group
plc is an international investment company listed on the London
Stock Exchange, with offices in Europe and North America.

determination_table_content.item:
Overlap and relationship
between the parties
determination_table_content.details:
There are vertical relationships between Global Infrastructure
Management and TCR relating to services at Sydney Airport. The
parties also overlap in the supply of GSE services to airlines at
Sydney Airport.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition
and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition
assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an
acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in
Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have
regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the
interests of consumers.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines
and interim merger process guidelines.
In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered
the information and documents that were submitted with the
notification form, information submitted by third parties and publicly
available information.
The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into
effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the
effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In
reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC
makes the following findings.
• Noting a concern was raised by a third party that the Acquisition
may reduce competitors’ access to space at Sydney Airport, the
merged firm would be unlikely to have the ability to foreclose
rivals’ access to inputs at Sydney Airport as it does not have
sufficient control over the entities that ultimately control Sydney
Airport so as to foreclose rivals’ access and Sydney Airport
would not have the incentive to foreclose rivals’ access.
• The merged firm would likely continue to be constrained by
alternative suppliers of GSE services.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by
the Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are
dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the
Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to
the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar
days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s
Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any
application for review, please contact the Australian Competition
Tribunal.

mergers.merger_description:
Restaurant Brands New Zealand Limited (**Restaurant Brands**) and Yum! Brands, Inc. (**Yum**) propose to acquire, via a joint venture (Taco Ventures Pty Ltd (**JV Co**)):

* 9 Taco Bell restaurants from Restaurant Brands’ subsidiary QSR Pty Ltd, and
* 20 Taco Bell restaurants from Collins Foods Subsidiary Pty Ltd, a subsidiary of Collins Foods Limited (**Collins**)

Restaurant Brands will control JV Co and hold a 60% interest, while Gardiner Lane Capital, LLC, a wholly owned subsidiary of Yum, will acquire a 40% interest.

Yum is a NYSE-listed, US-based restaurant company that owns, operates and franchises various fast-food brands and associated restaurants, including KFC, Pizza Hut and Taco Bell. Yum holds equity in and operates around 70 KFC restaurants in Australia but does not operate or hold equity in any Taco Bell or Pizza Hut restaurants in Australia.

Restaurant Brands is a Yum corporate franchisee. In Australia, it operates 9 Taco Bell restaurants, all of which are in NSW, and 74 KFC restaurants.

Collins is an ASX-listed Australian fast-food corporation that owns and operates 294 KFC restaurants and 20 Taco Bell restaurants in Australia, under franchise arrangements with Yum.

mergers.merger_name: CVS Group – Paws at Prospect Vet and Eyes at Prospect Vet
mergers.merger_description:
CVS Vets (Australia) Proprietary Limited, which is part of CVS Group Plc (together, **CVS Group**), proposes to acquire 60% of the shares in Newman Vet Pty Ltd (trading as Paws at Prospect Vet and Eyes at Prospect Vet) (**Paws**), a small animal veterinary practice in Adelaide (the **Acquisition**). The existing owners of Paws will retain the remaining 40% of shares in Paws.

CVS Group is an integrated veterinary services provider based in the United Kingdom and listed on the London Stock Exchange. In Australia, CVS Group owns a number of small animal veterinary practices in Queensland, New South Wales, the Australian Capital Territory, Victoria, South Australia and Western Australia. CVS Group currently owns three veterinary practices in Adelaide: Walkerville Vet, Pet Universe Manningham and Pet Universe Northgate.

CVS Group and Paws both supply small animal veterinary services in Adelaide. Small animal veterinary practices attend to small animals (such as cats, dogs, guinea pigs, birds, rabbits and other pets), rather than larger animals (such as cattle, sheep and horses), and offer services such as consultation, general and preventative health care/advice, surgery, microchipping, dentistry, radiology, pathology, pharmacy and related services such as puppy school.

In addition to first opinion small animal vet services, Paws also provides specialist ophthalmic vet services on referral from first opinion vets. CVS does not own any specialist vet services clinics in South Australia.

mergers.merger_description:
DPG Services Pty Ltd (**DPG**) and OHCA Property Holdings Pty Limited (being related bodies corporate, and together referred to as **Opal Healthcare**) propose to acquire the land, assets and business comprising the Sunnymeade Park Aged Care Community (**Sunnymeade Park**) (**Acquisition**).

DPG is a registered provider of approved residential care homes, and owns and operates 144 residential aged care homes across New South Wales, Victoria, Queensland, Western Australia and South Australia.

Sunnymeade Park is a privately-owned, single approved residential care home with 172 beds, located at 362-376 King Street, Caboolture in Queensland. Sunnymeade Park provides the services required under the *Aged Care Act 2024 (Cth)*, including 24/7 nursing care, ageing in place, secure dementia areas and palliative care. The sellers are Jomal Pty Ltd and Margaret Jean Walker, the latter also as Executor of the estate of Leslie Kenneth Walker and Margaret Jean Walker, being partners of the Leslie K & Margaret J Walker Partnership.

The Acquisition gives rise to a competitive overlap between the parties’ activities in South-East Queensland.

events.title: Opal Healthcare - Sunnymeade Park Aged Care Community - Phase 1 determination
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
DPG Services Pty Ltd and OHCA Property Holdings Pty Limited
(being related bodies corporate, and together referred to as Opal
Healthcare) propose to acquire the land, assets and business
comprising the Sunnymeade Park Aged Care Community
(Sunnymeade Park) (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

The acquirer, Opal Healthcare, owns and operates 144 residential
aged care facilities across New South Wales, Victoria, Queensland,
Western Australia and South Australia. It provides accommodation
and services for residents, including physiotherapy and wellness
programs, dementia care, respite care and palliative care. Relevant
to the Acquisition, Opal Healthcare owns and operates 21 residential
care facilities in Queensland, including 7 in the Brisbane
metropolitan area, 3 on the Sunshine Coast, 7 on the Gold Coast and
4 located between the north of Brisbane and the Sunshine Coast.
Sunnymeade Park is a privately-owned, single-approved residential
aged care facility with 172 beds, located in Caboolture, Queensland.
Sunnymeade Park provides the services required under the Aged
Care Act 2024 (Cth) as an approved residential care home, including
24/7 nursing care, ageing in place, secure dementia areas and
palliative care. The sellers are Jomal Pty Ltd and Margaret Jean
Walker, the latter also as Executor of the estate of Leslie Kenneth
Walker and Margaret Jean Walker, being partners of the Leslie K &
Margaret J Walker Partnership.

Opal Healthcare and Sunnymeade Park overlap in the supply of
residential aged care facilities in South-East Queensland.
However, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission
(ACCC) has not reached a concluded view on market definition given
that, based on the information available and for the reasons below, it
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the effect of
substantially lessening competition in any market whether a narrow
or broad geographic market dimension is adopted.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the ACCC undertakes a
competition assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for
an acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in
Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have
regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the
interests of consumers.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines
and interim merger process guidelines.
In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered
the information and documents that were submitted with the
notification form and publicly available information.
The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into
effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the
effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In
reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC
makes the following findings.
• The aggregation arising from the Acquisition is estimated to be
low at a local and regional level (as well as at the national level)
given the number of beds at Sunnymeade Park and the total
number of beds available in the relevant areas.
• Opal Healthcare would likely continue to face competition from
alternative suppliers of residential aged care facilities including
from large national providers such as Bolton Clarke and Regis
which have facilities in the local area.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by
the Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are
dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the
Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to
the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar
days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s
Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any
application for review, please contact the Australian Competition
Tribunal.

mergers.merger_name: Ramsay Health Care – National Capital Private Hospital

acquirers.name: RAMSAY HEALTH CARE AUSTRALIA PTY LIMITED
RAMSAY HEALTH CARE INVESTMENTS PTY LIMITED

mergers.merger_description:
Ramsay Health Care Investments (Ramsay) or one or more of its wholly owned subsidiaries proposes to acquire the businesses, assets and certain assumed liabilities of National Capital Private Hospital (NCPH) from Healthscope pursuant to a Business and Asset Sale Agreement.

Ramsay is a publicly listed private hospital operator with over 75 hospitals, clinics and surgical centres across Australia. Ramsay does not currently have any facilities in the Australian Capital Territory (ACT) or its surrounding regions. The closest Ramsay operated private hospitals to NCPH are Southern Highlands Private Hospital in Bowral, New South Wales (NSW) and Nowra Private Hospital in Nowra, NSW.

NCPH is a private hospital in Garran, ACT which is currently operated by Healthscope, a private hospital operator with 36 hospitals Australia-wide. In May 2025, Healthscope’s parent entities went into voluntary administration and receivers and administrators were appointed by its lenders and the Healthscope Board, respectively, to conduct an orderly sale of the hospitals in its network.

Private hospitals supply services to patients, private health funds in respect of their members and to government for Medicare patients. Private hospital operators also supply facilities to medical and allied health professionals providing services within the hospitals that they operate.

events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
The proposed acquisition by Ramsay Health Care Investments Pty
Limited (Ramsay) or one or more of its wholly owned subsidiaries of
the businesses, assets and certain assumed liabilities of National
Capital Private Hospital (National Capital) from Healthscope
pursuant to a Business and Asset Sale Agreement (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

The acquirer, Ramsay, is a publicly listed private hospital operator
with over 75 hospitals, clinics and surgical centres across Australia.
The target, National Capital, is a private hospital in Garran, ACT
which is currently operated by Healthscope, a private hospital
operator with 36 hospitals Australia-wide.
In May 2025, Healthscope’s parent entities went into voluntary
administration, and receivers and administrators were appointed by
its lenders and the Healthscope Board, respectively, to conduct an
orderly sale of the hospitals in its network.

Ramsay is a private hospital operator and National Capital is a
private hospital located in Garran, ACT.
Ramsay does not currently have any facilities in the Australian
Capital Territory (ACT) or its surrounding regions. The closest
Ramsay operated private hospitals to National Capital are Southern
Highlands Private Hospital in Bowral, New South Wales (NSW) and
Nowra Private Hospital in Nowra, NSW, both of which are more than
150km drive from National Capital.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition
and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition
assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an
acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in
Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have
regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the
interests of consumers.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines
and interim merger process guidelines.
In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered
the information and documents that were submitted with the
notification form and information from third parties.
The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into
effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the
effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In
reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC
makes the following findings:
• there is limited horizontal overlap between National Capital and
Ramsay-operated private hospitals, as these hospitals have
largely different geographical catchment zones for patients, and
limited overlap of Visiting Medical Officers (VMOs). Accordingly,
the Acquisition is not likely to enable Ramsay to profitably
worsen either non-price terms to patients, or price or non-price
terms to VMOs serving the Canberra region; and
• the Acquisition is not likely to substantially alter the competitive
dynamics between Ramsay and private health insurers, such
that there would be a substantial lessening of competition in the
supply of Hospital Purchaser Provider Agreements (HPPAs) to
private health insurers, enabling Ramsay to profitably increase
the prices or worsen the non-price terms of its HPPAs with
private health insurers, either in relation to Ramsay’s existing
hospitals or National Capital itself.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by
the Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are
dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the
Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to
the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar
days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s
Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any
application for review, please contact the Australian Competition
Tribunal.

mergers.merger_description: Parker-Hannifin Corporation (**Parker-Hannifin**) proposes to acquire 100% of the share capital in Filtration Group Corporation (**Filtration Group**). Parker-Hannifin is a global engineering company headquartered in Cleveland, Ohio, USA. It designs, manufactures and supplies motion and control technologies and systems to a variety of industries, including aerospace and defence, transportation, energy, and HVAC (heating, ventilation and air conditioning) and refrigeration. Filtration Group is a global filtration and separation technology company headquartered in Illinois, USA. Filtration Group’s core business is the design, manufacture and supply of filtration and separation products for life sciences, indoor air quality and industrial applications. Parker-Hannifin and Filtration Group both supply filtration products for use in industrial applications in Australia.

events.title: Parker-Hannifin - Filtration Group - Phase 1 determination
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Parker-Hannifin Corporation (Parker-Hannifin)’s proposed
acquisition of 100% of the share capital in Filtration Group
Corporation (Filtration Group) (together, the Parties) (the
Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

The acquirer, Parker-Hannifin, is a global engineering company
headquartered in Cleveland, Ohio, USA. It designs, manufactures and
supplies motion and control technologies and systems to a variety
of industries, including aerospace and defence, transportation,
energy, and HVAC (heating, ventilation and air conditioning) and
refrigeration.
The target, Filtration Group, is a global filtration and separation
technology company headquartered in Illinois, USA. Filtration
Group’s core business is the design, manufacture and supply of
filtration and separation products for life sciences, indoor air quality
and industrial applications.

In Australia, Parker-Hannifin and Filtration Group overlap in the
supply of filtration products for use in industrial applications in the
following categories:
(a) Filter cartridges used in the processing of oil and gas
(b) Filters for hydraulic fluid systems
(c) Process water filtration products
(d) Condensate drains used for compressed air systems.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition
and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition
assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an
acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in
Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have
regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the
interests of consumers.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines
and interim merger process guidelines.
In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered
the information and documents that were submitted with the
notification form and information from third parties.
The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into
effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the
effect of substantially lessening competition in any market in
Australia. In reaching its decision, and based on the material before
it, the ACCC makes the following findings.
• The Parties would continue to face competition from alternative
large, established suppliers of filtration products in Australia, as
well as many smaller competitors.
• Filtration products are readily interchangeable and built to
standardised specifications making it easy for customers to
switch brands.
• Filtration products are not generally purchased under long-term
supply agreements, meaning customers are able to switch
suppliers with relative ease.
• Filtration Group is a relatively small competitor in the supply of
filtration products in Australia, so there will be a small increase
in the market share of the combined entity.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by
the Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are
dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the
Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to
the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar
days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s
Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any
application for review, please contact the Australian Competition
Tribunal.

mergers.merger_description:
Charter Hall Holdings Pty Limited (**Charter Hall Holdings**) is wholly owned by Charter Hall Limited (ASX: CHC) and proposes to acquire 100% of JGS Private Capital Pty Ltd (**JGS Private Capital**), which is wholly owned by J.G. Service Pty Limited (the **Proposed Acquisition**).

JGS Private Capital operates an investment and property management business and serves as trustee of 8 unit trusts, with 7 of the units holding interests in commercial real estate and one being a finance trust (**Trusts**). CareSuper Pty Ltd (**CareSuper**) is the sole unitholder of each Trust.

Charter Hall Holdings operates a property investment and management business and serves as trustee of land-holding trusts.

Both Charter Hall Holdings and JGS Private Capital are involved in leasing and/or licensing commercial real estate to existing or prospective tenants to generate rental returns.

CareSuper is JGS Private Capital’s only client, and is also an existing client of Charter Hall Holdings, but JGS Private Capital and Charter Hall Holdings are otherwise unrelated.

There are no vertical relationships between Charter Hall Holdings and JGS Private Capital.

Through the Proposed Acquisition Charter Hall Holdings will acquire the right to manage and control the Trusts and their underlying assets. JGS Private Capital owns no beneficial ownership interest in any Trust or any of the real property assets held by the Trusts, which will continue following completion. Beneficial ownership of the Trusts and their underlying assets will remain with CareSuper following completion of the transaction.

events.title: Charter Hall Holdings - JGS Private Capital - Phase 1 Determination
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Charter Hall Holdings Pty Limited (Charter Hall Holdings) is wholly
owned by Charter Hall Limited (ASX:CHC) and proposes to acquire
100% of JGS Private Capital Pty Ltd (JGS Private Capital), which is
wholly owned by J.G. Service Pty Limited (the Proposed
Acquisition).
JGS Private Capital operates an investment and property
management business and serves as trustee of 8 unit trusts, with 7
of the units holding interests in commercial real estate and 1 being a
finance trust (Trusts). CareSuper Pty Ltd (CareSuper) is the sole
unitholder of each Trust.
Through the Proposed Acquisition, Charter Hall Holdings will acquire
the right to manage and control the Trusts and their underlying
assets. JGS Private Capital has no beneficial ownership interest in
any Trust or any of the real property assets held by the Trusts, which
will continue following completion. Beneficial ownership of the
Trusts and their underlying assets will remain with CareSuper
following completion of the transaction.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

The acquirer, Charter Hall Holdings, is an Australian property
investment and funds management group. Charter Hall Holdings is
involved in leasing and/or licensing commercial real estate to
existing or prospective tenants to generate investment returns. This
may include incidental services such as property maintenance.
The target, JGS Private Capital, operates an investment and property
management business and serves as trustee of the Trusts.
CareSuper is the sole unitholder of each Trust.

Charter Hall Holdings and JGS Private Capital (together, the Parties)
overlap in the supply of commercial real estate investment services
and the supply of commercial real estate leasing services.
Commercial real estate investment services include investment
management services (asset acquisition, divestments, restructuring
and management for the purpose of generating a financial return to
investors) and property management services (leasing,
maintenance, development and associated activities in connection
with the management and operation of Australian commercial real
estate assets). These services facilitate passive income generation
and capital growth for investors located across Australia, and
function via commercial real estate assets held and managed by the
JGS Private Capital and Charter Hall Holdings.
In respect of investment services, CareSuper is JGS Private Capital’s
only client, and is also an existing client of Charter Hall Holdings.
JGS Private Capital and Charter Hall Holdings are otherwise
unrelated.
Commercial real estate leasing services may include incidental
services such as maintaining properties and dealing with lease
expiries and extensions, and are for the purpose of generating
investment returns for investors in commercial real estate assets.
In respect of leasing services, the Parties overlap in Canberra,
Melbourne and Sydney.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition
and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition
assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an
acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in
Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have
regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the
interests of consumers.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines
and interim merger process guidelines.
In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered
the information and documents that were submitted with the
notification form and publicly available information.
The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into
effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the
effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In
reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC
makes the following findings.
• JGS Private Capital’s portfolio of assets under management is
relatively small in comparison to Charter Hall Holdings and other
suppliers of commercial real estate investment and leasing
services.
• Charter Hall Holdings’ market share aggregation in respect of
the supply of commercial real estate investment and leasing
services is estimated to be low.
• After the Proposed Acquisition, Charter Hall Holdings would
likely continue to face competition from alternative suppliers of
commercial real estate investment and leasing services, such as
Goodman Group, Lendlease, Dexus, GPT Group and Mirvac.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by
the Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are
dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the
Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to
the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar
days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s
Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any
application for review, please contact the Australian Competition
Tribunal.

mergers.merger_description:
DPG Services Pty Ltd (**DPG**) and OHCA Property Holdings Pty Ltd (**OHCA**) (being related bodies corporate, and together referred to as Opal Healthcare) propose to acquire the land, assets and business comprising the Greenwich Place residential aged care home located in Greenwich, Sydney (**Greenwich Place**) (**Acquisitio**n).

DPG is a registered provider of approved residential care homes and owns and operates 145 residential aged care homes across New South Wales, Victoria, Queensland, Western Australia and South Australia.

Greenwich Place is a privately-owned, single approved residential care home with 90 beds, located at 33 Greenwich Rd, Greenwich in New South Wales. Greenwich Place provides the services required under the Aged Care Act 2024 (Cth), including permanent care, respite care and palliative care.

The Acquisition will involve:

* DPG acquiring the assets comprising the Greenwich Place business from Greenwich Place Pty Ltd, Greenwich Care Pty Limited and Stamen Greenwich Property Ltd; and
* OHCA acquiring the land on which Greenwich Place is operated from Stamen Greenwich Property Pty Ltd.

There is a competitive overlap between Opal Healthcare’s activities and the activities of the target, Greenwich Place, in Sydney.

mergers.merger_description:
Seismic Software, Inc (**Seismic**) proposes to acquire 100% of the share capital of Highspot, Inc. (**Highspot**), via merger.

Both Seismic and Highspot are US-headquartered businesses which offer sales enablement software platforms and products to Australian customers as well as globally. Sales enablement software helps improve enterprise sales teams’ performance by providing content management and collaboration capabilities, data analytics capabilities and learning and coaching modules to build sales skills. Both parties’ sales enablement platforms are enhanced by artificial intelligence. Both parties supply their products to customers across a range of industry sectors.

Seismic is majority owned by Permira Advisers LLP (**Permira**). Permira would remain the controlling shareholder of Seismic following the proposed acquisition.

events.title: Seismic - Highspot - Phase 1 Determination
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Seismic Software, Inc (Seismic) proposes to acquire 100% of the
share capital of Highspot, Inc. (Highspot) by way of merger (the
Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

Both the acquirer, Seismic, and the target, Highspot, are US-
headquartered businesses which offer sales enablement software
platforms and products to Australian customers as well as globally.
Sales enablement software helps improve enterprise sales teams’
performance by providing content management and collaboration
capabilities, data analytics capabilities and learning and coaching
modules to build sales skills. Both parties’ sales enablement
platforms are enhanced by artificial intelligence. Both parties supply
their products to customers across a range of industry sectors.
Seismic is majority owned by Permira Advisers LLP (Permira).
Permira would remain the controlling shareholder of Seismic
following the Acquisition.

The parties overlap in the supply of sales enablement software
globally. The parties supply integrated sales enablement platforms
that offer a full range of sales enablement applications and also
offer each sales enablement application separately.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition
and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition
assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an
acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in
Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have
regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the
interests of consumers.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines
and interim merger process guidelines.
In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered
the information and documents that were submitted with the
notification form, information from third parties and publicly
available information.
The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into
effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the
effect of substantially lessening competition in any market in
Australia. In reaching its decision, and based on the material before
it, the ACCC makes the following findings.
• The parties both have a limited presence in Australia, with few
customers and relatively low revenue.
• Most Australian customers of sales enablement software
acquire one or more single applications from the overall range of
sales enablement applications, rather than a full platform
solution. Information before the ACCC indicates that there are
multiple alternative suppliers of the range of single applications
offered by the parties. While some Australian customers use a
full platform solution for which there are fewer alternatives, they
did not express concerns about the impact of the transaction on
competition.
• The ACCC closely considered information, including third party
feedback, regarding Seismic’s strong position in the supply of
sales enablement software platforms and the potential for the
Acquisition to further strengthen that position. However, given
that alternatives, such as Showpad, will remain available, and
taking into account other information including third party
feedback, the ACCC found that customers would continue to
have a range of competitive options for sales enablement
software post-acquisition.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by
the Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are
dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the
Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to
the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar
days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s
Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any
application for review, please contact the Australian Competition
Tribunal.

mergers.merger_description:
Fulton Hogan Industries Pty Ltd (**Fulton Hogan**) proposes to acquire 100% of the shares in Avionics Holdings Pty Ltd representing the Australian operations of the Avionics Group business (**Avionics**) (the **Acquisition**).

Fulton Hogan is a civil construction and infrastructure provider with primary operations in Australia and New Zealand. It delivers projects and long‑term maintenance across airports, bridges, communications, energy, rail, roads and water sectors. Relevantly, Fulton Hogan’s airfield works operations primarily involve airfield pavement construction services such as runway resurfacing, taxiway upgrades and apron rehabilitation. Fulton Hogan typically operates as head contractor, whereby it also coordinates the delivery of projects and subcontracts third parties to complete constituent components of the project, including aeronautical ground lighting (**AGL**). Occasionally, Fulton Hogan will be appointed to deliver airfield pavement and construction services on projects where it is not the head contractor.

AGL is a specialised system of in-ground and above-ground lights and other visual aids used to guide pilots during take-off, landing and taxiing. Avionics supplies and installs AGL as part of airside works projects in Australia. Avionics also provides related services such as design, repair and maintenance of AGL. Avionics and other AGL providers either supply these services directly to airfield operators or to head contractors which coordinate delivery of major airfield works.

Bain Capital Investors, LLC (**Bain Capital)** proposes to indirectly acquire 100% of the shares in MCJ Co., Ltd. (**MCJ**) through a newly created special purpose vehicle, BCPE Meta Cayman, L.P.

Bain Capital is a limited liability company organised in Delaware under United States law and is the ultimate general partner of numerous private equity funds. determination_table_content.details:
Bain Capital Investors, LLC (Bain Capital) proposes to indirectly
acquire 100% of the shares in MCJ Co., Ltd. (MCJ) through a newly
created special purpose vehicle, BCPE Meta Cayman, L.P. (the
Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

The acquirer, Bain Capital, is the entity responsible for managing
numerous private equity funds. These funds invest in companies
with activities in various industries, including technology, media and
telecommunications, healthcare, retail and consumer products,
financial and business services, and industrial/manufacturing.
The companies that generate revenue in Australia and are active in
technology, media and telecommunications are:
• CentralSquare Technologies – a provider of public sector
software
• Citius Tech – a provider of consulting and digital technology
to healthcare and life sciences companies
• EnterpriseDB – a provider of relational database
management systems
• Envestnet – a provider of integrated wealth technology
software and solutions to financial advisors and institutions.
The goods and services supplied by these companies do not overlap
with the goods and services supplied by the target, MCJ.
MCJ, is an integrated Information and Communications Technology
business that operates primarily through its group companies that
are predominantly active in Japan.
In Australia, the only services that MCJ supplies are IT product repair
and support services through its brand R-Logic which supplies one
Australian customer.

determination_table_content.item:
Overlap and relationship
between the parties
determination_table_content.details:
There is no horizontal overlap or vertical relationship between Bain
Capital and MCJ.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition
and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition
assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an
acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in
Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have
regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the
interests of consumers.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines
and interim merger process guidelines.
In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered
the information and documents that were submitted with the
notification form.
The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into
effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the
effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In
reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC
makes the following findings:
• there is no horizontal overlap or vertical relationship between
Bain Capital and MCJ in Australia.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by
the Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are
dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the
Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to
the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar
days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s
Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any
application for review, please contact the Australian Competition
Tribunal.

mergers.merger_description:
Audiotonix Limited (Audiotonix) intends to acquire 100% of the shares and sole control of Soundtech Group S.r.l. (Soundtech Group) through 2 special purpose vehicles, Sound Bidco S.r.l and Sound Bidco (Denmark) ApS. Both special purpose vehicles will be 100% directly owned by A6 Audio Bidco Limited (A6 Audio), which, in turn, is 100% indirectly owned by Audiotonix. A6 Audio and Audiotonix are part of Audiotonix Group, a UK-headquartered group active in the design, engineering and manufacturing of professional audio mixing consoles, as well as associated equipment such as networking (allowing consoles to connect with other consoles), interface products (used to physically control music editing and mixing remotely from a console), software (e.g., audio processing, control software and operating systems) and spare parts.

Audiotonix is under the sole control of PAI Partners, a private equity firm with a focus on investing in business services, food and consumer, general industrials and healthcare.

In Australia, Audiotonix’s subsidiary Sound Devices is a wholesale supplier of wireless microphone systems.

Soundtech Group is a supplier in the professional audio industry which is active in the design, manufacturing and application of micro-electronic mechanisms.

Soundtech Group’s subsidiaries include DPA Microphones A/S (DPA Microphones), Austrian Audio GmbH (Austrian Audio) and Wisycom S.r.l. (Wisycom).

In Australia, DPA Microphones and Austrian Audio are wholesale suppliers of high-end microphones, and Wisycom is a wholesale supplier of wireless microphone systems and wireless in-ear monitoring systems.
