determination_table_content.details:
Abbott Laboratories (Abbott) proposes to acquire 100% of the
shares of Exact Sciences Corporation (Exact Sciences) (together,
the Parties) through an agreement and plan of merger (the
Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

The acquirer, Abbott, is a global healthcare company headquartered
in the US, active in the provision of diagnostics, medical devices,
nutrition, and branded generic pharmaceuticals.
The target, Exact Sciences, is a US headquartered company active in
the provision of advanced molecular diagnostics, specialising in
cancer screening and testing. It has no corporate presence in
Australia. It supplies one product in Australia, Oncotype DX Breast
Recurrence Score, via an Australian distributor, Specialised
Therapeutics H Pty Ltd.

The Parties overlap in the supply of oncology molecular diagnostics.
Oncology diagnostics are highly specific. Diagnostic products are
specific to the disease type (e.g. breast cancer) and then focused to
sub-segmentations within the disease type (e.g. HER-2 positive
breast cancer).
Abbott supplies two oncology molecular diagnostics tests in
Australia: (1) Vysis CLL FISH, which tests for disease prognosis and
treatment selection for chronic lymphocytic leukemia; and (2) Alinity
m High Risk (HR) HPV, which screens for variants of the common
HPV (human papillomavirus) virus. Although not sold in Australia,
Abbott also supplies PathVysion which tests whether a patient is
HER2 positive which guides breast cancer treatment selection.
Exact Sciences supplies Oncotype DX, which is designed to predict
the recurrence of cancer in patients diagnosed with breast cancer
and help guide treatment decisions.
The Parties do not supply any products in Australia that are
substitutable for one another given they target different disease
types.
Even if Abbott’s PathVysion was sold in Australia, it is functionally
different to Oncotype DX and these tests would not be substitutable.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition
and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition
assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an
acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in
Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have
regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the
interests of consumers.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines
and interim merger process guidelines.
In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered
the information and documents that were submitted with the
notification form and information from third parties.
The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into
effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the
effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In
reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC
makes the following findings.
• There is limited horizontal overlap between the Parties. While
the Parties operate in the oncology diagnostic sector, the
Parties’ products are functionally different and are not
substitutes.
• If a broader relevant market were applied that includes all cancer
screening tests, the Parties' market share aggregation as a
result of the Acquisition is estimated to be very low.
• The Parties will continue to face competition from a number of
other well-resourced companies in their respective test types
both in Australia and globally.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by
the Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are
dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the
Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to
the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar
days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s
Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any
application for review, please contact the Australian Competition
Tribunal.

mergers.merger_description:
CVS Vets (Australia) Pty Ltd, a wholly owned subsidiary of CVS (Australia) Holdings Pty Ltd which is part of CVS Group Plc (together, **CVS Group**) proposes to acquire 100% of the shares of Pittwater Animal Hospital, a small animal veterinary practice in Warriewood in Sydney's Northern Beaches region (the **Acquisition**).

CVS Group is an integrated veterinary services provider based in the United Kingdom, and listed on the London Stock Exchange. In Australia, CVS Group owns a number of veterinary practices in Queensland, New South Wales, the Australian Capital Territory, Victoria, South Australia and Western Australia. CVS Group owns a number of veterinary practices in metropolitan Sydney, including holding a majority interest in Sydney Animal Hospitals (**SAH**). SAH operates two clinics in the Northern Beaches region, SAH Newport and SAH Avalon.

CVS Group and Pittwater Animal Hospital both supply small animal veterinary services in the Northern Beaches region on the fringes of metropolitan Sydney. Small animal veterinary practices attend to small animals (such as cats, dogs, guinea pigs, birds, rabbits and other pets), rather than larger animals such as cattle, sheep and horses, and offer services such as consultation, general and preventative health care/advice, surgery, microchipping, dentistry, radiology, pathology, pharmacy and related services such as puppy school.

There are no vertical overlaps between the parties.

determination_table_content.details:
CVS Vets (Australia) Pty Ltd, a wholly owned subsidiary of CVS
(Australia) Holdings Pty Ltd which is part of CVS Group Plc
(together, CVS Group) proposes to acquire 100% of the shares of
Pittwater Animal Hospital (Pittwater), a small animal veterinary
practice in Warriewood in Sydney's Northern Beaches region
(the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

The acquirer, CVS Group, is an integrated veterinary services
provider based in the United Kingdom and listed on the London
Stock Exchange. In Australia, CVS Group owns a number of
veterinary practices in Queensland, New South Wales, the Australian
Capital Territory, Victoria, South Australia and Western Australia.
CVS Group owns a number of veterinary practices in metropolitan
Sydney, including holding a majority interest in Sydney Animal
Hospitals (SAH). SAH operates two clinics in the Northern Beaches
region, SAH Newport and SAH Avalon.
The target, Pittwater, is a veterinary practice located in Warriewood
in Sydney’s Northern Beaches region.
The reasons for the ACCC’s determination are set out in section 2.

determination_statement_of_reasons.text: When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the interests of consumers.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines and interim merger process guidelines.

In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered the information and documents that were submitted with the notification form, additional information provided by the merger parties, information provided by third parties and publicly available information.
For the reasons given below, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into effect.
Overlap between the parties

CVS and Pittwater (together, the Parties) overlap in the supply of small animal, general practice veterinary services supplied in standard hours (General Veterinary Services) in the Northern Beaches region of Sydney. None of Pittwater, or SAH’s Avalon and Newport veterinary practices supply after-hours emergency veterinary services themselves. Instead, each of these veterinary practices refers patients to the Northside Emergency Veterinary Service (NEVS) located in Terrey Hills for after-hours emergency care.

The ACCC has considered whether the Acquisition is likely to substantially lessen competition in the supply of General Veterinary Services in the Northern Beaches region of Sydney by removing competition between Pittwater and CVS, through the latter’s SAH clinics located in Newport and Avalon.
Industry background

Small animal veterinary practices (sometimes referred to as companion animal veterinary practices) typically provide a range of general practice veterinary services, including first opinion consultations, general and preventative healthcare and advice, surgery, microchipping, dentistry, radiology, laboratory testing and pharmacy services to household pets such as dogs, cats, birds, rabbits and guinea pigs. Small animal veterinary services may be distinguished from large animal veterinary services, which are provided to animals such as cattle, sheep and horses.

There are a number of such small animal veterinary practices in the Northern Beaches region of Sydney. Figure 1 below depicts the location of some key small animal veterinary practices servicing the Northern Beaches region, including the target Pittwater site and CVS’ existing SAH practices located in Avalon and Newport. Key alternative suppliers of small animal general practice veterinary services in this region include Bilgola Veterinary Clinic, Mona Vale Veterinary Hospital, Elanora Veterinary Clinic, Collaroy Veterinary Hospital, Aubreen Vet Hospital and Dee Why Veterinary Hospital. Figure 1: Veterinary Practices in the Northern Beaches region Map prepared by ACCC

Practices in the Northern Beaches commonly refer after-hours emergency cases to the Northside Emergency Veterinary Service (NEVS) located in Terrey Hills. NEVS provides after-hours and overnight care for pets from other practices, which are then generally referred back to general veterinary practices during standard operating hours.
Competition assessment

The ACCC has considered the effects of the Acquisition by comparing the likely future state of competition if the Acquisition proceeds against the continuation of the current state of competition (being the likely future state of competition if the Acquisition does not proceed).
Relevant areas of competition
The ACCC has considered whether the Acquisition is likely to substantially lessen competition in the supply of General Veterinary Services in the Northern Beaches region of Sydney.

The ACCC has had regard to customer location data provided by the Parties, drive-time analysis and the geographic features of Sydney’s Northern Beaches region (including the peninsula to the north, and South Creek and Narrabeen Lagoon to the south) as part of its assessment. However, the ACCC did not find it necessary to reach a concluded view on the precise geographic dimension of this market.
Horizontal unilateral effects in the supply of General Veterinary Services
The ACCC considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the effect of substantially lessening competition in the supply of General Veterinary Services in the Northern Beaches region of Sydney.

The ACCC has considered how closely each of the small animal, general veterinary practices in Sydney’s Northern Beaches region compete, having regard to their respective range of services offered, location, opening hours, number of veterinarians, and annual revenue and the price of a standard consultation where available. In reaching its decision, and based on the materials before it, the ACCC makes the following findings:

items: Pittwater does not compete closely with SAH Avalon;

while Pittwater and SAH Newport have a greater overlap of customer catchment areas than Pittwater and SAH Avalon, the material before the ACCC does not indicate that Pittwater and SAH Newport are each other’s closest competitor;

post-acquisition, the merged entity would continue to face competition from various alternative veterinary practices that supply substantially similar small animal, general practice veterinary services with similar opening hours to Pittwater, SAH Avalon and SAH Newport. The strength of competition from each of these alternative veterinary practices is likely to vary for different customer catchment areas. These alternative veterinary practices include:
Bilgola Veterinary Clinic, located on the peninsula and north of Pittwater and SAH Newport;
Collaroy Veterinary Hospital, Aubreen Vet Hospital and Dee Why Veterinary Hospital, which are located in the southern area of the Northern Beaches region; and
Mona Vale Veterinary Hospital and Elanora Veterinary Clinic, which are more proximate to Pittwater; and

while Sydney’s Northern Beaches region is relatively well serviced by small animal veterinary practices currently, material before the ACCC does not indicate that the merged entity would be insulated from the threat of new entry or expansion. There is existing capacity for at least some alternative small animal veterinary practices in the local area to take on new patients.

determination_statement_of_reasons.text: The ACCC has also had regard to third party feedback regarding the buying power of the CVS Group for veterinary equipment and medications, and barriers to new veterinarians entering the Northern Beaches given the existing density of veterinary practices in the region. However, these factors were not sufficient to indicate a likely substantial lessening of competition given the other factors identified above.

mergers.merger_description:
Dubai Aerospace Enterprise Ltd (**DAE**) proposes to acquire 100% of the issued share capital in Macquarie AirFinance Limited (**Macquarie AirFinance**) (the **Acquisition**).

DAE is a globally active aircraft lessor, headquartered in Dubai. DAE is active in leasing regional, narrow-body and wide-body aircraft to airlines. DAE consists of two divisions: DAE Capital and DAE Engineering. DAE Capital carries out aircraft leasing, aircraft trading and provides aircraft investor services. DAE Engineering, through Joramco, provides regional maintenance repair & overhaul services.

Macquarie AirFinance is a globally active aircraft lessor of civil aviation aircraft, headquartered in Dublin. Macquarie AirFinance is primarily active in leasing narrow-body aircraft, and, to a lesser extent, wide-body aircraft to airlines.

Both DAE and Macquarie AirFinance are globally active suppliers of aircraft dry leases.

events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Dubai Aerospace Enterprise (DAE) Ltd (DAE) proposes to acquire
100% of the issued share capital in Macquarie AirFinance Limited
(Macquarie AirFinance) (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

DAE is a globally active aircraft lessor, headquartered in Dubai. DAE
is active in leasing regional, narrow-body and wide-body aircraft to
airlines. DAE consists of two divisions: DAE Capital and DAE
Engineering. DAE Capital carries out aircraft leasing, aircraft trading
and provides aircraft investor services. DAE Engineering, through its
subsidiary Joramco, provides regional maintenance repair and
overhaul services.
Macquarie AirFinance is a globally active aircraft lessor of civil
aviation aircraft, headquartered in Dublin. Macquarie AirFinance is
primarily active in leasing narrow-body aircraft, and, to a lesser
extent, wide-body aircraft to airlines.

determination_table_content.item:
Overlap and relationship
between the parties
determination_table_content.details:
DAE and Macquarie AirFinance overlap in the supply of aircraft dry
leases to airlines.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition
and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition
assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an
acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in
Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have
regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the
interests of consumers.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines
and interim merger process guidelines.
In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered
the information and documents that were submitted with the
notification form, publicly available information, and feedback from
market participants.
The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into
effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the
effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In
reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC
makes the following findings.
• Aggregation in the supply of aircraft dry leases resulting from
the Acquisition appears low globally, including into Australia,
and the merged entity would likely continue to be constrained by
alternative suppliers of aircraft dry leases post-acquisition.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by
the Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are
dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the
Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to
the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar
days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s
Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any
application for review, please contact the Australian Competition
Tribunal.

mergers.merger_description:
Eli Lilly and Company (**Eli Lilly**) proposes to acquire 100% of the issued shares in Kelonia Therapeutics, Inc (**Kelonia Therapeutics**) (the **Acquisition**).

Eli Lilly is a US-based pharmaceutical company, which discovers, develops, manufactures and markets human pharmaceutical products for various therapeutic areas, including diabetes, oncology, immunology and neuroscience. Eli Lilly is headquartered in Indianapolis, US and is publicly listed on the New York Stock Exchange. Eli Lilly has an Australian subsidiary that registers and markets medicine in Australia.

Kelonia Therapeutics is a US-based clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on the development of genetic medicines using its in vivo gene placement system (iGPS). Kelonia Therapeutics is developing a pipeline of lentiviral-based in vivo CAR-T therapies primarily for oncology indications. Its lead asset (KLN-1010) is a clinical-stage (Phase 1) that uses iGPS to deliver an anti-BCMA CAR to T cells for treatment of multiple myeloma.

mergers.merger_description:
Accenture International B.V. (**Accenture**) proposes to acquire the issued share capital of Faculty Science Limited (**Faculty Science**) pursuant to a share purchase agreement.

Accenture is a global professional services firm with operations in over 120 countries. It provides a comprehensive suite of professional services designed to assist public and private sectors to navigate digital transformation, optimise operations, and build advanced technological capabilities.

Relevantly, in Australia, Accenture provides IT services, including application implementation tools and managed services, IT infrastructure implementation tools and managed services, and technology consulting services including to Government, utilities and energy customers.

Faculty Science is an artificial intelligence (**AI**) services firm, headquartered in the United Kingdom. Founded in 2014, its focus is on supplying (i) AI strategy and solution development services (including, AI strategy, design, infrastructure, development, operations and change); and (ii) a decision intelligence platform called ‘Frontier’, which analyses data infrastructure and provides insights to enhance decision-making.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Accenture International B.V. (Accenture) applied for a notification
waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of the issued share
capital in Faculty Science Limited (Faculty Science), as described in
the transaction document provided as part of the application (the
Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
20 January 2026

The acquirer, Accenture, is a global professional services company
headquartered in Dublin, Ireland. Accenture provides a broad range of
services across strategy, consulting, digital, technology, and
operations designed to assist public and private sector clients with
navigating digital transformation, optimising operations, and building
advanced technological capabilities. Relevantly, in Australia,
Accenture’s IT services include application implementation tools and
managed services, IT infrastructure implementation tools and
managed services, and technology consulting services including to
Government, utilities and energy customers.
The target, Faculty Science, is an artificial intelligence (AI)
consultancy and technology services company headquartered in the
United Kingdom (UK). Faculty Science’s focus is on supplying: (i) AI
strategy and solution development services (including, AI strategy,
design, infrastructure, development, operations and change); and (ii)
a decision intelligence platform called ‘Frontier’, which analyses data
infrastructure and provides insights to enhance decision-making.
The parties both supply IT services, including more narrowly
application implementation services and technology consulting
services, which include some applied AI services. Faculty Science
has limited operations and a de minimis presence in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the
information provided with the notification waiver application and had
regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act), being:
(i) the object of the Act,
(ii) the interests of consumers,
(iii) the likelihood that, if the acquisition were put into effect,
the notification thresholds in section 51ABP(1) of the Act
would apply, and
(iv) the likelihood that the acquisition, if put into effect, would
in all the circumstances have the effect, or be likely to
have the effect, of substantially lessening competition in
any market.
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. Faculty Science has limited operations and presence in
Australia.
b. There is a small horizontal overlap between Accenture and
Faculty Science in the supply of application implementation
services and technology consulting services in Australia.
c. The parties’ combined market share in the broader supply of
application implementation and technology consulting
services in Australia is low with alternative suppliers
expected to constrain the merged firm.
d. There is a low risk of concerning conglomerate effects
resulting from bundling or tying of products post-merger.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to
be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects
more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification
waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Eli Lilly and Company (**Eli Lilly**) proposes to acquire 100% of the issued shares in Orna Therapeutics Holdings, LLC (**Orna Therapeutics**) pursuant to an Agreement and Plan of Merger.

Eli Lilly is a US-based pharmaceutical company, which discovers, develops, manufactures and markets human pharmaceutical products for various therapeutic areas, including diabetes, oncology, immunology and neuroscience. Eli Lilly is headquartered in Indianapolis, US and is publicly listed on the New York Stock Exchange.

Orna Therapeutics is a US-based pre-clinical biotechnology company developing a new class of engineered circular RNA (**oRNA**) therapeutics and immune tropic lipid nanoparticles (**LNP**) delivery technologies for the treatment of autoimmune disorders, and cancer conditions. Orna Therapeutics has a pipeline of pre-clinical in vivo Chimeric Antigen Receptor T-cell (**CAR-T**) therapies designed to treat B cell-driven autoimmune diseases.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Eli Lilly and Company (Eli Lilly) applied for a notification waiver in
respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the issued shares in
Orna Therapeutics Holdings, LLC (Orna Therapeutics), via Eli Lilly’s
subsidiary BLJX Acquisition, LLC, as described in the transaction
documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
After the Acquisition, Orna Therapeutics would be a wholly owned
subsidiary of Eli Lilly.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(b) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) not to make the notification waiver
determination applied for.
12 March 2026

The acquirer, Eli Lilly, is a US-based pharmaceutical company, which
discovers, develops, manufactures and markets human
pharmaceutical products for various therapeutic areas, including
diabetes, oncology, immunology and neuroscience. Eli Lilly is
headquartered in Indianapolis, US and is publicly listed on the New
York Stock Exchange.
The target, Orna Therapeutics, is a US-based pre-clinical
biotechnology company developing a new class of engineered
circular RNA (oRNA) therapeutics and immune tropic lipid
nanoparticles (LNP) delivery technologies for the treatment of
autoimmune disorders, and cancer conditions. Orna Therapeutics
has a pipeline of pre-clinical in vivo Chimeric Antigen Receptor T-cell
(CAR-T) therapies designed to treat B cell-driven autoimmune
diseases.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
certain publicly available information and had regard to the factors in
section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010
(Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, including the
parties’ revenue in Australia and the transaction value, the ACCC
considers that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification
thresholds would be met.
Further, based on the information currently before it, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition may raise competition issues. It
considers that the Acquisition warrants consideration through the
notification process, including via further information gathering and
consultation with third parties. This may include a focus on:
a. the extent to which the Acquisition would remove potential
competition from Orna Therapeutics or Eli Lilly
b. the extent to which the parties are likely to face competition
from alternative suppliers of circular RNA therapeutics, LNP
delivery technologies and CAR-T therapies to treat
autoimmune diseases, cancer and other diseases
c. the nature and extent of any vertical or conglomerate
relationship between the parties and their ability and/or
incentive to engage in exclusionary conduct, bundling or
tying of products post-Acquisition.
Given the above, the ACCC has determined not to make the
notification waiver determination applied for.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

Pursuant to an Equity Purchase Agreement between SW Soccer Corp Holdings LLC (**SW Soccer Corp**, the target), SW Soccer HoldCo LLC (the seller), BSN Sports, LLC (**BSN Sports**, the acquirer), and Varsity Brands, Inc (**Varsity Brands**, the acquirer’s parent company), BSN Sports intends to acquire 100% of the equity interests in SW Soccer Corp, subject to the rollover by certain direct or indirect holders of equity interests of the seller.

SW Soccer Corp’s operating business is Sports Endeavors (collectively, **Sports Endeavors**). Sports Endeavors is based in the United States. Sports Endeavors primarily serves youth club teams in soccer and soccer retail customers in the United States.

BSN Sports is a subsidiary and operating division of Varsity Brands, and is based in the United States. Varsity Brands is a portfolio company indirectly majority owned by investment funds, vehicles and/or accounts advised and managed by KKR & Co. Inc. (together with its affiliated entities, **KKR**).

Sports Endeavors and BSN Sports are active in the supply of sporting apparel and equipment.

determination_table_content.details:
BSN Sports, LLC (BSN Sports) applied for a notification waiver in
respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the equity interests in
SW Soccer Corp Holdings LLC (SW Soccer Corp), subject to the
rollover by certain direct or indirect holders of equity interests of the
SW Soccer Corp, as described in the transaction documents
provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
6 March 2026

The acquirer, BSN Sports, is based in the United States. BSN Sports
is active in the supply of sporting apparel and equipment primarily in
the United States. BSN Sports primarily serves the high school and
university channel, with a focus on football, basketball, baseball and
softball.
BSN Sports is a subsidiary and operating division of Varsity Brands,
Inc (Varsity Brands). Varsity Brands is a portfolio company indirectly
majority owned by investment funds, vehicles and/or accounts
advised and managed by KKR & Co. Inc. (together with its affiliated
entities, KKR).
The target, Sports Endeavors, is SW Soccer Corp’s operating
business. Sports Endeavors is based in the United States. Sports
Endeavors primarily serves youth club teams in soccer and soccer
retail customers in the United States.
BSN Sports and Sports Endeavors are active in the supply of sporting
apparel and equipment.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. BSN Sports and Sports Endeavors have a very small
presence in Australia
b. the presence of alternative suppliers of sports apparel and
equipment in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
Given the above considerations that material competition concerns
are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is
not required to be notified.
In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to
reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification
thresholds being met.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Winning Appliances is a retailer of kitchen, laundry and other home appliances which it supplies to consumers and businesses across Australia.

Winning Appliances proposes to establish a new holding company Winning Holdings and undertake the following steps:

1. the sale of all shares in Winning Appliances by the existing Winning Appliances shareholders to Winning Holdings, and
2. the issuing of shares in Winning Holdings to the previous shareholders of Winning Appliances, in equal proportions to their shareholdings in Winning Appliances (together,the **Acquisition**).

The share classes and rights of Winning Holdings following the Acquisition will reflect the share classes and rights of the existing shares in Winning Appliances.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Woodward pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Winning Group Holdings Pty Ltd (Winning Holdings) applied for a
notification waiver in respect of:
• Winning Holding’s acquisition of all shares in Winning Appliances
Pty Ltd (Winning Appliances) from the existing Winning
Appliances shareholders (the Shareholders), and
• the Shareholders’ acquisition, in equal proportions to their former
shareholdings in Winning Appliances, of all shares in Winning
Holdings,
as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
23 April 2026

Winning Appliances supplies and installs kitchen, laundry and other
home appliances including household whitegoods, homewares and
audio-visual products, to consumers and businesses. Winning
Appliances also provides delivery, installation, warehousing and
commercial supply services to builders and developers through its
subsidiaries.
Winning Holdings is a newly established holding company that was
incorporated for the purposes of the Acquisition. It does not
currently provide, nor does it own or have an interest in any business
that provides, goods or services. As such, there is no competitive
overlap between the parties.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular, there is no horizontal
consolidation, removal of a competitor, or change to Winning
Appliances’ competitive position as a result of the Acquisition.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
The ACCC also considered the potential application of section
51ABD of the Act. The parties submit that there are strong grounds
that section 51ABD applies to the Acquisition, however, on the facts
of this matter, including the staged structure of the Acquisition, there
is some uncertainty about whether the Acquisition falls within the
scope of that provision. Having regard to the particular
circumstances, including the consequences of non-notification in
reliance on the exemption if it was found not to apply, the ACCC
considered it appropriate to make a determination on the basis that
the acquisition provisions in the Act apply to the Acquisition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Energy Bay Kappa Fund Pty Ltd as trustee for the Energy Bay Kappa Fund (**Energy Bay**) proposes to acquire from various subsidiaries of the Centuria Capital Group (the **CCG Members**):

* energy infrastructure assets and the exclusive right to operate existing embedded networks at 49 sites,
* energy infrastructure assets and the exclusive right to install and operate embedded networks at a further 211 sites, and
* first and last rights of refusal to acquire analogous rights in respect of any further sites of the kind described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application.

Energy Bay is a part of Energy Bay Group, which is a distributed energy infrastructure company operating embedded networks at large commercial and industrial sites in New South Wales (**NSW**), Australian Capital Territory (**ACT**), Western Australia (**WA**), Victoria (**VIC**) and Queensland (**QLD**). Energy Bay Group also owns Diamond Energy Pty Ltd (**Diamond Energy**), an electricity retailer that operates in all National Electricity Market (**NEM**) jurisdictions.

The CCG Members are subsidiaries of the Centuria Capital Group, which is comprised of Centuria Capital Limited (**CNI**) and Centuria Funds Management Limited (**CFML**). CNI is an ASX-listed funds manager that creates, manages and invests in listed and unlisted real estate funds, each of which holds properties for third party investors. CFML is the responsible entity for the Centuria Capital Fund. The CCG Members invest in and manage properties for investment funds. As part of that primary business activity, in each of NSW, ACT, VIC, QLD, South Australia (**SA**), and WA, they also:

* operate embedded network infrastructure at some of their sites,
* engage in behind the meter generation or storage at their sites, and
* supply and sell electricity to their commercial tenants connected to their embedded networks (**embedded network services**).

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Energy Bay Kappa Fund Pty Ltd, as trustee for the Energy Bay Kappa
Fund (Energy Bay), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its
proposed acquisition of:
a. energy infrastructure assets and the exclusive right to
operate existing embedded networks at 49 sites,
b. energy infrastructure assets and the exclusive right to install
and operate embedded networks at a further 211 sites, and
c. first and last rights of refusal to acquire analogous rights in
respect of any further sites of the kind described in the
transaction documents,
from various subsidiaries of the Centuria Capital Group (the CCG
Members), which is comprised of Centuria Capital Limited (CNI) and
Centuria Funds Management Limited (CFML), as described in the
transaction documents provided as part of the application (the
Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(b) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) not to make the notification waiver
determination applied for.

The acquirer, Energy Bay, is a part of Energy Bay Group, which is a
distributed energy infrastructure company operating embedded
networks at large commercial and industrial sites in New South
Wales (NSW), Australian Capital Territory (ACT), Western Australia
(WA), Victoria (VIC) and Queensland (QLD). Energy Bay Group also
owns Diamond Energy Pty Ltd, an electricity retailer that operates in
all National Electricity Market (NEM) jurisdictions.
The vendors, the CCG Members, are subsidiaries of the Centuria
Capital Group, which is comprised of CNI and CFML. CNI is an ASX-
listed funds manager that creates, manages and invests in listed and
unlisted real estate funds, each of which holds properties for third
party investors. CFML is the responsible entity for the Centuria
Capital Fund.
In each of NSW, ACT, VIC, QLD, South Australia, and WA, the CCG
Members:
a. operate embedded network infrastructure,
b. engage in behind the meter generation and storage at their
embedded network sites, and
c. supply and sell electricity to commercial tenants connected
to their embedded networks.
(together, Embedded Network Services). Through the Acquisition,
Energy Bay Group will increase its share of supply of Embedded
Network Services in WA and the NEM.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the
information provided with the notification waiver application and
certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors
in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Act.
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers
that the Acquisition may raise competition issues. In particular:
a. there is meaningful horizontal overlap between Energy Bay
Group and the CCG Members in the supply of Embedded
Network Services in WA and the NEM, and Energy Bay
appears to be one of the larger suppliers of Embedded
Network Services in the NEM
b. the extent to which Energy Bay Group is likely to be
constrained by alternative suppliers of Embedded Network
Services post-acquisition is uncertain
c. there is a vertical relationship between the Embedded
Network Services supplied by the parties and the electricity
retailing operations of Energy Bay Group’s subsidiary,
Diamond Energy, and the impact of the Acquisition on
Energy Bay Group’s ability and/or incentive to foreclose
rivals warrants further consideration.
The ACCC considers that these potential competition issues warrant
consideration through the notification process, including via
consultation with third parties.
Given the above, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to reach a
concluded view on the likelihood of the Acquisition meeting the
notification thresholds. However, the ACCC notes that based on the
Australian revenue and transaction value figures provided, the
Acquisition may meet the monetary notification thresholds in ss 2-1
and 2-3 of the Competition and Consumer (Notification of
Acquisitions) Determination 2025.
For these reasons, the ACCC has determined not to make the
notification waiver determination applied for.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

anzsic_codes.name: Technical and Vocational Education and Training

mergers.merger_description:
Swan Topco Pty Ltd proposes to acquire 100% of the issued share capital in Stanley International College Pty Ltd (**Stanley International College**) by an acquisition vehicle, Swan Bidco Pty Ltd, that is indirectly and solely controlled by funds managed by Quadrant Private Equity (the **Acquisition**).

Quadrant Private Equity is a private equity firm headquartered in Sydney, Australia, and supplying financial and investment management services across Australia and New Zealand. It typically invests in mid-market companies across consumer and retail, healthcare, business, professional and industrial services, as well as technology and tech-enabled service industries. Quadrant Private Equity’s portfolio companies do not, and have not previously, held any interests in any higher education, vocational education or training service providers.

Stanley International College is an independent institute of higher education and vocational education and training services. It employs over 200 staff and is a multi-campus institution with a presence in Perth and Adelaide. Stanley International College provides a range of vocational training, bachelor’s and post-graduate degrees and professional year qualifications.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Woodward pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Swan Topco Pty Ltd (Swan Topco), through its indirect wholly-owned
subsidiary Swan Bidco Pty Ltd (Swan Bidco), applied for a
notification waiver in respect of Swan Bidco’s proposed acquisition
of 100% of the issued share capital in Stanley International College
Pty Ltd (Stanley International College) from Chintalia Pty Ltd and
Kaishwer Pty Ltd (together, the Vendors), as described in the
transaction documents provided as part of the application (the
Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, Swan Bidco, is indirectly controlled by funds managed
by Quadrant Private Equity (QPE). QPE is a private equity firm
headquartered in Sydney, Australia, and supplying financial and
investment management services across Australia and New
Zealand. It typically invests in mid-market companies across
consumer and retail, healthcare, business, professional and
industrial services, as well as technology and tech-enabled service
industries.
The target, Stanley International College, is an independent institute
of higher education and vocational education and training services. It
employs over 200 staff and is a multi-campus institution with a
presence in Perth and Adelaide.
Stanley International College provides a range of vocational training,
bachelor and post-graduate degrees and professional year
qualifications.
Quadrant Private Equity’s portfolio companies do not, and have not
previously, held any interests in any higher education, vocational
education or training service providers.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular, there is no horizontal overlap between
QPE’s portfolio companies and Stanley International College in the
supply of higher education and/or vocational education training
services.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Adyen N.V. (**Adyen**) is a financial technology company headquartered in Amsterdam, The Netherlands. Adyen is active in the supply of a global payments platform that enables businesses to accept and process payments across online, mobile, and instore channels, and offers related products and services.

Talon.One GmbH (**Talon.One)** is a software company headquartered in Berlin, Germany Talon.One is active in the supply of a promotion, loyalty and incentive management platform through a 'software as a service' (SaaS) model.

Adyen proposes to acquire 100% of the issued shares in Talon.One from its existing shareholders. Upon completion, Adyen will acquire sole control over Talon.One and its three wholly-owned subsidiaries: Talon.One Inc. (United States), Talon.One UK Ltd. (United Kingdom) and Talon.One SG Pte Ltd. (Singapore).

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Adyen N.V. (Ayden) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its
proposed acquisition of 100% of the share capital in Talon.One
GmbH (Talon. One), as described in the transaction documents
provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, Adyen is a financial technology company
headquartered in Amsterdam, The Netherlands. Adyen is active in
the supply of a global payments platform that enables businesses to
accept and process payments across online, mobile, and instore
channels, and offers related products and services.
The target, Talon.One is a software company headquartered in
Berlin, Germany. Talon.One is active in the supply of a promotion,
loyalty and incentive management platform through a 'software as a
service' (SaaS) model.
Following completion, Adyen and Talon.One intend to develop
functionality that would allow loyalty features to be integrated more
closely into the payment processing flow.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. there is no horizontal overlap between Adyen and Talon.One
in the supply of promotion, loyalty and/or incentive
management platform services
b. there is a low risk of foreclosure or other concerning vertical
or conglomerate effects resulting from exclusionary
conduct, bundling or tying of products and services post-
acquisition
c. there are alternative suppliers of payments platforms and
promotion, loyalty and incentive management platforms in
Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_name: Ochre Health - Airlie Women's Clinic, VIC

acquirers.name: Ochre Group Commercial Property Pty Ltd

mergers.merger_description:
Ochre Health Pty Limited proposes to acquire all of the shares in the Airlie Women’s Clinic Pty Ltd (**AWC**) through a share sale agreement, and Ochre Group Commercial Property Pty Ltd as trustee for Ochre Group Commercial Property Unit Trust proposes to acquire the land on which AWC’s general practice medical clinics on Malvern Road, Malvern VIC and Airlie Avenue, Prahran VIC are located. The parties are seeking a notification waiver in respect of both the share and land acquisitions (together, the **Acquisition**).

The acquirers (together with their connected entities, **Ochre Health)** support independent doctors to provide general practice medical services in regional, rural and urban communities around Australia.

Ochre Health currently operates 69 medical centres and urgent care clinics located in the ACT, NSW, Queensland, Tasmania and Victoria, but currently does not operate any medical centres or urgent care clinics in Melbourne, Victoria. Ochre Health Group’s medical recruitment business, Ochre Recruitment, also works with hospitals and medical practices across Australia and NZ to source and place locum and permanent doctors across a range of specialties.

Ochre Health is majority owned and controlled by Genesis Capital, an Australian-based private equity firm that invests in businesses operating in the healthcare sector.

The target, AWC, is a family medical practice with an all-female team of doctors providing comprehensive general practice medical care to men, women and children from its 3 clinics in the southeastern Melbourne suburbs of Prahran and Malvern, Victoria. As noted above, as part of the Acquisition, AWC is selling the land on which 2 of its 3 clinics is located.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Ochre Health Pty Limited applied for a notification waiver in respect
of:
• its proposed acquisition of all of the shares in Airlie
Women’s Clinic Pty Ltd (AWC), and
• Ochre Group Commercial Property Pty Ltd as trustee for
Ochre Group Commercial Property Unit Trust’s proposed
acquisition of the land on which 2 of AWC’s 3 general
practice medical clinics are operated,
as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
3 June 2026

The acquirers, Ochre Health Pty Limited and Ochre Group
Commercial Property Pty Ltd as trustee for Ochre Group Commercial
Property Unit Trust (together with their connected entities, Ochre
Health), support independent doctors to provide general practice
medical services in regional, rural and urban communities around
Australia.
Ochre Health currently operates 69 medical centres and urgent care
clinics located in the ACT, NSW, Queensland, Tasmania and Victoria,
but currently does not operate any medical centres or urgent care
clinics in Melbourne, Victoria. Ochre Health Group’s medical
recruitment business, Ochre Recruitment, also works with hospitals
and medical practices across Australia and NZ to source and place
locum and permanent doctors across a range of specialities.
Ochre Health is majority owned and controlled by Genesis Capital, an
Australian-based private equity firm that invests in businesses
operating in the healthcare sector.
AWC is a family medical practice with an all-female team of doctors
providing comprehensive general practice medical care to men,
women and children from its 3 clinics in the southeastern Melbourne
suburbs of Prahran and Malvern, Victoria.
Ochre Health and AWC supply general practice medical services in
Victoria, however Ochre Health does not operate any medical
centres or urgent care clinics within 80km of the AWC clinics.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is no overlap in the supply of general practice medical
services in the local Prahran and Malvern areas or in the
greater Melbourne area
b. there are other clinics supplying general practice medical
services near Prahran and Malvern, Victoria and in the
greater Melbourne area.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_name: INPEX – minority interests in Beetaloo development projects

targets.name: Daly Waters FSDA North Pty Ltd
Daly Waters FSDA South Pty Ltd

mergers.merger_description:
INPEX Corporation, through its wholly owned Australian subsidiaries - INPEX FSDA N&S Pty Ltd and INPEX BCD Pty Ltd (collectively, **INPEX**) entered into a farm-in agreement with Daly Waters Energy LP (**DWE**) to acquire:

1. a 20% interest in the Beetaloo Central Development project;
2. an 11.25% interest in the First Strategic Development Area (**FSDA**) South project; and
3. an 11.25% interest in the FSDA North project, (together, the **Acquisition**).

The projects will be operated as an unincorporated joint venture between DWE (via its subsidiaries), Tamboran Resources Corporation (via its subsidiaries) and INPEX (acting via its subsidiaries) (together, the **Joint Venture Parties**), and will be governed by separate joint operating agreements.

Pursuant to the terms and conditions of the Retention Licences that will be obtained for each project and a joint operating agreement, the Joint Venture Parties will explore for, and undertake appraisal activities to confirm, a commercial resource of conventional and unconventional oil and gas resources within each project area.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
INPEX Corporation (INPEX), via wholly owned subsidiaries INPEX
FSDA N&S Pty Ltd and INPEX BCD Pty Ltd, applied for a notification
waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of:
• a 20% interest in the Beetaloo Central Development project
• an 11.25% interest in the First Strategic Development Area
South project, and
• an 11.25% interest in the FSDA North project
(together, the Projects)
via a farm-in agreement with Daly Waters Energy LP (DWE), as
described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
3 June 2026

The acquirer, INPEX, is an oil and gas exploration and production
company headquartered in Tokyo, Japan. Its main business includes
research, exploration, development, production and sales of oil,
natural gas and other mineral resources, as well as investment and
lending to the companies engaged in these activities.
In Australia, INPEX:
• owns a majority stake and operates the Icthys LNG project in
offshore Western Australia
• has a minority participating interest in Prelude FLNG Project
in offshore Western Australia
• has a minority participating interest in Darwin LNG Project,
located in the Northern Territory
• has a minority participating interest in the Ravensworth Oil
Field in offshore Western Australia
• has a 50% interest in Potentia Energy.
Furthermore, INPEX is engaged in oil and gas exploration through
interests in leases in and around Western Australia and the Northern
Territory, principally in the area surrounding the Ichthys LNG Project.
INPEX also supplies LNG in the global seaborn LNG industry and
natural gas in the Northern Territory.
The Projects, which are the target of the Acquisition, are located
within the Beetaloo Sub-basin, an undeveloped but prospective shale
gas region in the Northern Territory.
The Projects would be operated as an unincorporated joint venture
between DWE (via its subsidiaries), Tamboran Resources
Corporation (via its subsidiaries) and INPEX (acting via its
subsidiaries) (together, the Joint Venture Parties), and would be
governed by separate joint operating agreements. The acquirer,
INPEX, would acquire a minority interest in each of the Projects and
would not be a project operator for any of the Projects.
Pursuant to the terms and conditions of the Retention Licences that
will be obtained for each Project and a joint operating agreement, the
Joint Venture Parties will explore for, and undertake appraisal
activities to confirm a commercial resource of conventional and
unconventional oil and gas resources within each Project area.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular
a. the Acquisition would lead to a very small increase in
INPEX’s share of gas or LNG production in Australia (to the
extent gas or LNG is produced from the Projects)
b. there are alternative producers and suppliers of natural gas
or LNG in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
KKR Spire Aggregator L.P., a special purpose vehicle indirectly wholly owned by investment funds, vehicles and/or accounts advised and managed by Kohlberg Kravis Roberts & Co. L.P. (together with its affiliates, **KKR**) alongside Carrick Capital and other members of the consortium propose acquiring control over Saviynt, Inc.  

KKR is a global investment firm that offers alternative asset management as well as capital markets and insurance solutions. KKR sponsors investment funds that invest in private equity, credit and real assets and has strategic partners that manage hedge funds.

Saviynt is a cyber security provider focused on identity solutions. Saviynt’s solutions allow companies (and other organisations) to manage log-in details for their employees when accessing company resources. Saviynt provides an Identity Governance & Administration software that centralizes lifecycle management, access requests, compliance, and audit across hybrid IT environments, including human and non-human identities, along with a number of adjacent identity products.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
KKR Spire Aggregator L.P. applied for a notification waiver in respect
of the Kohlberg Kravis Roberts & Co. L.P. (KKR) led consortium’s
proposed acquisition of share capital in Saviynt, Inc. (Saviynt), as
described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
27 January 2026

KKR is a global investment firm that offers alternative asset
management as well as capital markets and insurance solutions.
KKR sponsors investment funds that invest in private equity, credit
and real assets and has strategic partners that manage hedge funds
KKR Spire Aggregator L.P. is a special purpose vehicle indirectly
wholly owned by investment funds, vehicles and/or accounts
advised and managed by KKR.
Alongside KKR, vehicles controlled by asset management company
Carrick Management Partners II (collectively, Carrick Capital), LLC,
Verify Investments, LLC and Harjeet Kaur Shariff, LLC are proposing
to acquire an interest in Saviynt. Carrick Capital is an investment firm
focussed on enterprise software, software-as-a-service and
technology-enabled services, with experience in founding, scaling
and optimising businesses.
The target, Saviynt, is a cyber security provider focused on identity
solutions. Saviynt’s solutions allow companies (and other
organisations) to manage log-in details for their employees when
accessing company resources. Saviynt provides interoperable
Identity Governance & Administration software that centralises
lifecycle management, access requests, compliance, and audit
across hybrid IT environments, including human and non-human
identities, along with a number of adjacent identity products.
The KKR led consortium does not supply goods or services that
horizontally overlap with Saviynt. The KKR led consortium, with its
investments in software-related services, may be considered to
provide products that are vertical and/or complementary in nature to
Saviynt’s cybersecurity products.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the
information provided by the applicant, including in the notification
waiver application, and had regard to the factors in section
51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act).
Based on the information provided by the applicant, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. There is no horizontal overlap between the KKR led
consortium’s portfolio companies’ and Saviynt in the supply
of identify focussed cybersecurity software.
b. Whilst there is a potential vertical and/or conglomerate
relationship between cybersecurity products supplied by the
KKR led consortium and Saviynt, the parties are likely to
continue to be competitively constrained by a wide range of
alternative suppliers of cybersecurity and identity focussed
cybersecurity software in Australia and globally.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
IBWM Pty Ltd (**IBWM**), a subsidiary of Ironbark Investment Partners Pty Ltd (**Ironbark**), will acquire 40% of the shares on issue in Viola Group Holdings Pty Ltd (**Viola Group**). Viola Group is the parent company of Viola Finance Pty Ltd, Viola Private Holdings Pty Ltd, Viola Services Pty Ltd and Viola Private Wealth Pty Ltd (**Viola**).

Ironbark has a business division which provides financial advice to retail and wholesale clients. Viola Group focuses on private wealth and high net worth clients.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
IBWM Pty Ltd, a subsidiary of Ironbark Investment Partners Pty Ltd
(Ironbark), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed
acquisition of 40% of the shares on issue in Viola Group Holdings Pty
Ltd (Viola Group), as described in the transaction documents
provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
9 April 2026

The acquirer, IBWM Pty Ltd, is a subsidiary of Ironbark. Ironbark has
a business division which provides financial advice to retail and
wholesale clients.
The target, Viola Group, is the parent company of Viola Finance Pty
Ltd, Viola Private Holdings Pty Ltd, Viola Services Pty Ltd and Viola
Private Wealth Pty Ltd (Viola). Viola Group focuses on private wealth
and high net worth clients.
Both parties provide financial advice and wealth management
services in Sydney. The services provided by Ironbark and Viola
include financial planning advice, superannuation advice, retirement
planning, cash flow planning, listed and unlisted securities advice,
transaction execution and management, tax advice and life
insurance advice.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors
in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010
(Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is a small horizontal overlap between Ironbark and
Viola in the supply of financial advice and wealth
management services in Sydney and across Australia
b. the parties’ combined share in the supply of financial advice
and wealth management services in Sydney and across
Australia is estimated to be low and the increase in share of
supply arising from the Acquisition would be small
c. there are alternative suppliers of financial advice and wealth
management services in Sydney and across Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on

mergers.merger_description:
The acquisition will involve a deleveraging through a debt-to-equity swap resulting in Segula’s creditors, Eiffel Investment Group SAS (**Eiffel**) and Eurazeo SE (**Eurazeo**), acquiring joint control of Segula Holding SAS (**Segula**).

The acquisition is part of a financial restructuring aimed at saving Segula from judicial liquidation. It will be carried out under the supervision of the Nanterre Commercial Court (accelerated safeguard procedure) in France and result in Eiffel and Eurazeo each holding 50% of the voting rights in Segula.

Eiffel is an asset management company ultimately and exclusively controlled by Impala SAS (**Impala**). Impala is a French private diversified investment company, active in France and internationally in asset management and corporate finance, as well as in the energy and finance sectors.

Eurazeo is a French listed global investment group active in private equity, private debt and real assets.

Segula is a global engineering and technology consulting group providing technical services and innovation solutions primarily for the automotive, aerospace, energy, rail and marine sectors. Segula’s activities in Australia are currently limited to the provision of professional engineering services to primarily the automotive and rail sectors.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Segula Holdings SAS (Segula) has agreed with the primary holders
of bond debt issued by Segula to convert their existing liabilities to
equity. As part of the restructure, Eiffel Investment Group SAS
(Eiffel) and Eurazeo SE (Eurazeo) will acquire joint control of Segula
by way of a debt-to-equity swap (the Acquisition).
Eiffel and Eurazeo jointly applied for a notification waiver in respect
of the Acquisition.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
26 February 2026

Eiffel is an asset management company ultimately and exclusively
controlled by Impala SAS (Impala). Impala is a French private
diversified investment company, active in France and internationally
in the asset management, finance, and energy sectors.
Eurazeo is a French listed global investment group active in private
equity, private debt and real assets.
The target, Segula, is a global engineering and technology consulting
group providing technical services and innovation solutions primarily
for the automotive, aerospace, energy, rail and marine sectors.
Segula’s activities in Australia are currently limited to the provision of
professional engineering services to primarily the automotive and
rail sectors.
Eiffel and Eurazeo do not provide engineering and technology
consulting services in Australia. Eiffel and Eurazeo’s connected
entities may have commercial relationships with Segula as potential
customers for engineering consulting services or as potential
suppliers of solutions or equipment useful to Segula.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors
in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010
(Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is no horizontal overlap between Eiffel, Eurazeo and
Segula in the supply of engineering and technology
consulting services in Australia, and there is a limited
commercial relationship between Eiffel’s and Eurazeo’s
connected entities and Sequla
b. Segula’s share of engineering and technology consulting
services in Australia is low
c. there are alternative suppliers of engineering and technology
consulting services in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
