mergers.merger_description:
The Carlyle Group Inc. (**Carlyle**) is a global investment firm headquartered in Washington DC, USA, with assets under management across three business segments: (i) Global Private Equity (including corporate private equity and real estate funds); (ii) Global Credit (including liquid credit, illiquid credit and real assets credit); and (iii) Carlyle AlpInvest (including primary, secondary, and co-investments, commingled funds, and separately managed accounts).

Peloton Computer Enterprises Ltd. (**Peloton**) is a global supplier of software predominantly used by exploration and production (**E&P**) operators in the upstream oil and gas industry. E&P is an early phase in the oil and gas industry concerned with locating and extracting raw materials.

Carlyle, via its controlled investment vehicle (CG Panther Parentco, L.P., **Panther**), proposes to acquire non-voting and non-convertible preferred equity in Peloton, as well as warrants that (if exercised) would provide Carlyle with up to c. 14% of the fully-diluted equity in Peloton (pro forma for the transaction). Carlyle will ultimately (via Panther) hold only (i) preferred equity and (ii) warrants amounting up to c. 14% holding of common equity interests in Peloton on a lookthrough basis.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Woodward pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
CG Panther Parentco, L.P. (Panther), which is a controlled
investment vehicle of The Carlyle Group Inc. (Carlyle), applied for a
notification waiver in respect of Carlyle’s proposed acquisition of:
a. non-voting and non-convertible preferred equity in Peloton
Computer Enterprises Ltd. (Peloton), and
b. warrants that (if exercised) would provide Carlyle with up to c.
14% of the fully-diluted equity in Peloton (pro forma for the
transaction),
as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
30 January 2026

The acquirer is Carlyle, a global investment firm headquartered in
Washington DC, USA. Carlyle has assets under management across
three business segments: Global Private Equity (including corporate
private equity and real estate funds); Global Credit (including liquid
credit, illiquid credit and real assets credit); and Carlyle AlpInvest
(including primary, secondary, and co-investments, commingled
funds, and separately managed accounts).
The target, Peloton, is a global supplier of software predominantly
used by exploration and production operators in the upstream oil and
gas industry.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the
information provided by the applicant, including in the notification
waiver application, and had regard to the factors in section
51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act).
Based on the information provided by the applicant, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. The Acquisition does not appear likely to result in any
horizontal overlaps between Carlyle (including its connected
entities) and Peloton in Australia.
b. There appears to be a low risk of foreclosure or other
concerning vertical or conglomerate effects resulting from
exclusionary conduct, bundling or tying of products post-
acquisition.
c. Peloton appears to have a relatively small presence in
Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
H-E Parts International Mining Solutions Pty Ltd (**H-E Mining**) intends to enter into an agreement for lease with ATB Morton Pty Ltd (**ATB Morton**) in respect of land located at 88 Michelmore Street, Paget, Mackay, Queensland (the **Property**) and improvements to be constructed by ATB Morton.

ATB Morton will acquire and construct a purpose-built industrial warehouse and workshop facility on the Property. ATB Morton will then lease the Property and improvements constructed by ATB Morton to H-E Mining.

The Property will be used by H-E Mining and H-E Parts International Mining Solutions Cooling Division Pty Ltd (**H-E Cooling**).

HEP Australia Holdings Pty Ltd is the parent entity of H-E Mining and H-E Cooling. These companies are providers of parts, engineered components, and service support for heavy-duty equipment used in mining and related industries, supporting both surface and underground operations as well as crushing and materials processing applications.

ATB Morton is a commercial and industrial property developer.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
H-E Parts International Mining Solutions Pty Ltd (H-E Mining) applied
for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed agreement for
lease with ATB Morton Pty Ltd (ATB Morton) in respect of land
located at 88 Michelmore Street, Paget, Mackay, Queensland (the
Property) and improvements to be constructed by ATB Morton, as
described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
12 February 2026

The acquirer, H-E Mining, supplies parts, engineered components,
and service support for heavy-duty equipment used in mining and
related industries, supporting both surface and underground
operations as well as crushing and materials processing
applications. H-E Parts International Mining Solutions Cooling
Division Pty Ltd (H-E Cooling) provides cooling and heat-transfer
solutions for mining and heavy industrial equipment. HEP Australia
Holdings Pty Ltd is the parent entity of H-E Mining and H-E Cooling.
ATB Morton is a commercial and industrial property developer, and
will be the landlord of the Property. ATB Morton will construct a
purpose-built industrial warehouse and workshop facility on the
Property. ATB Morton will then lease the Property and improvements
constructed by ATB Morton to H-E Mining.
The Property will be used by H-E Mining and H-E Cooling, and would
be an input to their operations.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the
information provided with the notification waiver application and had
regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act).
Based on the information provided in the application and publicly
available information, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is
unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In
particular:
a. There is no horizontal competitive overlap.
b. There appear to be alternative sites available for H-E
Mining’s and H-E Cooling’s competitors.
c. The Acquisition does not appear likely to otherwise limit or
prevent competition from rivals.
In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to
reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification
thresholds being met.
Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the
ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Telstra Limited (**Telstra**) is a leading Australian telecommunications and technology company, offering a range of communications services. Telstra provides consumer and business connectivity across mobile and fixed broadband, and delivers enterprise solutions including network, cloud, security, data and analytics, software development, and managed/professional services.

Infosys Technologies Limited (**Infosys**) is a global technology consultancy and outsourcing provider. It provides end-to-end solutions spanning consulting, technology, engineering and outsourcing services, and also offers software products and platforms. The company has a large global footprint, with operations across 59 countries.

Under an agreement, Telstra will outsource certain business functions to Infosys and Infosys will transform Telstra’s enterprise IT stack in respect of underlying carriage services supplied to certain Telstra Enterprise and Mid-market customers. As part of the agreement, Telstra and Infosys will acquire rights in relation to certain intellectual property.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Telstra Limited (Telstra) applied for a notification waiver in respect
of a proposed agreement with Infosys Technologies Limited
(Infosys), to the extent it involves an acquisition of assets and/or
legal or equitable rights that are not property (the Acquisition). The
agreement provides for Telstra to outsource certain business
functions to Infosys and for Infosys to transform its Enterprise IT
stack in respect of underlying carriage services provided to certain
Telstra Enterprise and Mid-market customers, as described in the
transaction documents provided as part of the application. The
Acquisition includes Telstra and Infosys acquiring rights in relation
to certain intellectual property.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
19 March 2026

Telstra is an Australian telecommunications and technology
company, offering a range of communications services. Telstra
provides consumer and business connectivity across mobile and
fixed broadband, and delivers enterprise solutions including network,
cloud, security, data and analytics, software development, and
managed/professional services.
Infosys is a global technology consultancy and outsourcing provider.
It provides end-to-end solutions spanning consulting, technology,
engineering and outsourcing services, and also offers software
products and platforms. The company has a large global footprint,
with operations across 59 countries.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided, the ACCC considers that the
Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. the Acquisition is not likely to result in a material increase in
concentration in the supply of the services offered by Telstra
and Infosys in Australia
b. there is a low risk of foreclosure resulting from exclusionary
conduct post-acquisition.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply. While
the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be
met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise,
the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
The ACCC notes that it also considered whether the Acquisition fell
within the scope of sections 51ABU and 51ABV of the Act. Based on
the information provided by the parties and the facts of this matter,
in particular the assets and/or legal or equitable rights involved,
including intellectual property rights, the ACCC considered it
appropriate to make a determination in relation to the notification
waiver application.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

other_parties.name: Ellerslie Free Range Farms Pty Ltd

anzsic_codes.name: Other Agriculture and Fishing Support Services

mergers.merger_description:
Hall & McLean Pty Ltd (**Hall & McLean**) proposes to acquire a 100% freehold interest in commercial land comprising Lot 8 on SP296133, known as 2 Foundation Street, Wellcamp, QLD 4350 (the **Property**), from Vision Street Property Pty Ltd (the **Vendor**) (the **Acquisition**).

Hall & McLean is a 50:50 joint venture between Ellerslie Free Range Farms Pty Ltd (**Ellerslie**) and the McLean family (through Alpair Pty Ltd).

Alpair Pty Ltd, is an egg producer based on the Darling Downs in Queensland

Ellerslie is an agribusiness entity operating principally in the chicken egg production sector.

Ellerslie is 60% owned by PSP Investments, with the remaining 40% held by DHP Consolidated Pty Ltd on behalf of the Hall family.

Ellerslie and Alpair Pty Ltd each have a 50% interest in Sunny Queen, an egg distributer.

The Property is located ~13km outside of Toowoomba in Queensland and currently used by Ellerslie as an egg grading and cold storage site.

Ellerslie has been renting the Property from the Vendor under a 5-year lease for the purposes of egg grading and cold storage for its egg production business. Prior to Ellerslie entering into that lease, the Property was vacant for a period of approximately 5 years.

Following completion of the Acquisition, Ellerslie will continue to use the Property for egg grading and cold storage, albeit as owner rather than lessee.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Hall & McLean Pty Ltd (Hall & McLean) applied for a notification
waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of a 100% freehold
interest in commercial land comprising Lot 8 on SP296133, known
as 2 Foundation Street, Wellcamp QLD 4350 (the Property), from
Vision Street Property Pty Ltd (the Vendor), as described in the
transaction documents provided as part of the application (the
Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
23 March 2026

The acquirer, Hall & McLean, is a 50:50 joint venture between
Ellerslie Free Range Farms Pty Ltd (Ellerslie) and the McLean family
(through Alpair Pty Ltd).
Alpair Pty Ltd, is an egg producer based in the Darling Downs region
in Queensland.
Ellerslie is an agribusiness entity operating principally in the chicken
egg production sector. Ellerslie layer farms produce caged, cage
free, free range, pasture raised and organic shell eggs.
Ellerslie is 60% owned by PSP Investments, with the remaining 40%
held by DHP Consolidated Pty Ltd on behalf of the Hall family.
Ellerslie and Alpair Pty Ltd each have a 50% interest in Sunny Queen,
an egg distributor.
Ellerslie has been renting the Property from the Vendor under a 5-
year lease for the purposes of egg grading and cold storage for its
egg production business.
The Vendor does not operate any egg farming or production
business in Australia.
The Property would be an input into the operations of Ellerslie.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors
in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010
(Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. the Acquisition is an acquisition of land by an egg producer
from a party who is not an egg producer
b. Ellerslie has been using the land as a lessee for 5 years and
intends to continue using the land for the same purposes,
but as an owner
c. there appear to be alternative sites available for Ellerslie’s
and the McLean family’s competitors
In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to
reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification
thresholds being met.
Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the
ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_name: PT Asian Bulk Logistics – Engage Marine

other_parties.name: AUSTRALIAN GLOBAL MARINE AND LOGISTICS PTY LTD

mergers.merger_description:
PT Asian Bulk Logistics, through its Australian subsidiary, Global Engage Marine Pty Ltd, proposes to acquire 100% of the issued share capital in Engage Marine TopCo Pty Ltd (**Engage Marine**).

Engage Marine provides terminal and harbour towage including the berthing and sailing of Cape-size, Panamax, and Handy-size vessels. Engage Marine also provides ad-hoc towage emergency response solutions in salvage situations. The main industry Engage Marine operates in is marine services (towage and port services).

PT Asian Bulk Logistics is an integrated logistics and infrastructure group supplying bulk logistics services, port services and marine services. It primarily operates in Indonesia.

Through one of its subsidiaries, Transhipment Services Australia, PT Asian Bulk Logistics operates a port-specific towage fleet supporting transhipment and logistics operations.

PT Asian Bulk Logistics operates two additional subsidiary companies in Australia – One Rail Australia and SCF Containers.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Global Engage Marine Pty Ltd, a subsidiary of PT Asian Bulk
Logistics, applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed
acquisition of 100% of the issued share capital in Engage Marine
TopCo Pty Ltd (Engage Marine), as described in the transaction
documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(b) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) not to make the notification waiver
determination applied for.
19 March 2026

PT Asian Bulk Logistics is an integrated logistics and infrastructure
group supplying bulk logistics services, port services and marine
services. It primarily operates in Indonesia.
Through one of its subsidiaries, Transhipment Services Australia, PT
Asian Bulk Logistics operates a port-specific towage fleet supporting
transhipment and logistics operations. Transhipment Services
Australia primarily services Australian mining companies in northern
Queensland.
PT Asian Bulk Logistics also operates two additional subsidiary
companies in Australia, One Rail Australia and SCF Containers. One
Rail Australia is an Australian rail-based freight and logistics
company specializing in coal, bulk, and intermodal transport,
primarily operating in New South Wales and Queensland.
The target, Engage Marine, provides terminal and harbour towage
including the berthing and sailing of Cape-size, Panamax, and Handy-
size vessels. Engage Marine also provides ad-hoc towage
emergency response solutions in salvage situations. The main
industry Engage Marine operates in is marine services (towage and
port services). Engage Marine services mining companies, port
authorities, terminal operators, and vessels directly, at major ports
around Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, including the
parties’ revenue in Australia and the transaction value, the ACCC
considers that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification
thresholds would be met.
The ACCC is unable to reach a view that the Acquisition would not be
likely to raise competition issues in any market based on the
information provided. It considers that the Acquisition warrants
consideration through the notification process, including via further
information gathering and consultation with third parties. This may
include a focus on:
a. the extent of any potential horizontal overlap and/or
complementary relationship between PT Asian Bulk
Logistics (including its subsidiaries) and Engage Marine in
the supply of marine services, transhipment services, port
logistics, and freight and logistics, in Australia
b. the relevant geographic dimension of the supply of PT Asian
Bulk Logistics’ and Engage Marine’s services in Australia
c. the nature and extent of PT Asian Bulk Logistics’ and Engage
Marine’s supply of services to mining companies in Australia
d. the extent to which the parties are likely to face competition
from alternative suppliers of marine services, transhipment
services, and port logistics in Australia.
Given the above, the ACCC has determined not to make the
notification waiver determination applied for.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_name: Herron Todd White – CJA Lee Property Valuers
mergers.merger_description:
Herron Todd White (Bidco) Pty Ltd (**HTW Bidco**) proposes to acquire 100% of the shares in CJA Lee Property Pty Ltd (**CJA Lee Property**) from C McMahon Enterprises Pty Ltd as trustee for the C&N McMahon Family Trust (**C McMahon Enterprises**) (50%) and T.J. Shaw Pty Ltd as trustee for the T.J. Shaw Family Trust (**T.J. Shaw**) (50%) (the **Acquisition**). As part of the Acquisition, C McMahon Enterprises and T.J. Shaw will be issued shares in the ultimate parent company of HTW Bidco – Herron Todd White (Holdings) Pty Ltd (**HTW Holdings**). Post-completion, C McMahon Enterprises and T.J. Shaw will own 0.15% of the shares in HTW Holdings.

HTW Holdings and its fully owned subsidiaries (**HTW Group**) provide property valuation and property advisory services in Australia. HTW Group primarily provides residential property valuation services to Australian authorised deposit-taking institutions.

HTW Group is majority-owned by funds managed by QPE No. 7 Management Pty Ltd, an entity within the Quadrant Private Equity group (**QPE**). Apart from HTW Group, QPE is not involved in the supply of property valuation and property advisory services in Australia.

CJA Lee Property is an independent advisory firm that provides property valuations, property advisory, and energy advisory services in the Gippsland / south-east Victoria area. Historically, CJA Lee Property has predominantly supplied residential property valuation services to Australian authorised deposit-taking institutions.

C McMahon Enterprises and T.J. Shaw are holding companies of CJA Lee Property and, apart from CJA Lee Property, do not operate any other businesses.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Herron Todd White (Bidco) Pty Ltd (HTW Bidco) applied for a
notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of
the shares in CJA Lee Property Pty Ltd (CJA Lee Property) from C
McMahon Enterprises Pty Ltd as trustee for the C&N McMahon
Family Trust (C McMahon Enterprises) and T.J. Shaw Pty Ltd as
trustee for the T.J. Shaw Family Trust (T.J. Shaw), as described in
the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the
Acquisition).
As part of the Acquisition, C McMahon Enterprises and T.J. Shaw
will be issued shares in Herron Todd White (Holdings) Pty Ltd. Post-
completion, they will own 0.15% of the shares in that entity.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(b) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) not to make the notification waiver
determination applied for.

The acquirer, HTW Bidco, is a subsidiary of Herron Todd White
(Holdings) Pty Ltd (together with its fully owned subsidiaries, HTW
Group). The HTW Group provides property valuation and property
advisory services in Australia. HTW Group primarily provides
residential property valuation services to Australian authorised
deposit-taking institutions.
HTW Group is majority-owned by funds managed by QPE No. 7
Management Pty Ltd, an entity within the Quadrant Private Equity
group (QPE). Apart from HTW Group, QPE is not involved in the
supply of property valuation and property advisory services in
Australia.
CJA Lee Property is an independent advisory firm that provides
property valuations, property advisory, and energy advisory services
in the Gippsland/south-east Victoria area. Historically, CJA Lee
Property has predominantly supplied residential property valuation
services to Australian authorised deposit-taking institutions.
C McMahon Enterprises and T.J. Shaw are holding companies of
CJA Lee Property and, apart from CJA Lee Property, do not operate
any other businesses.
HTW Group and CJA Lee Property both supply physical and
electronic property valuation and advisory services.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the
information provided with the notification waiver application and had
regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Act.
Based on the information provided in the application, including HTW
Group’s revenue in Australia and the cumulative Australian revenue
of CJA Lee Property and from acquisitions in the past three years,
the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be
met.
Based on the information provided in the application the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition may raise competition issues. In
particular:
a. there is a material horizontal competitive overlap between
HTW Group and CJA Lee Property in the supply of physical
property valuation and advisory services in the Gippsland
region of Victoria
b. the merger parties’ combined share in the supply of physical
property valuation and advisory services to customers in the
Gippsland region appears to be substantial
c. the extent to which the parties are likely to be constrained by
alternative suppliers of physical property valuation and
advisory services in the Gippsland region is uncertain.
The ACCC considers that these potential competition issues warrant
consideration through the notification process, including via
consultation with third parties.
Given the above, the ACCC has determined not to make the
notification waiver determination applied for.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

anzsic_codes.name: Engineering Design and Engineering Consulting Services

mergers.merger_description:
Goldcup 39483 AB (under name change to Prometheus BidCo AB), a company ultimately controlled by IK Partners (**IK Partners**), intends to acquire all shares in SELATEK Holding AB and its subsidiaries (together, **Selatek**) through a share sale.

IK Partners is a European private equity firm focused on investing primarily across Northern Continental Europe. It has eleven current funds under management, and its portfolio companies operate across a range of different business sectors.

Selatek is a Swedish company that operates across three core business areas: Electrical, Automation and Security. Selatek’s subsidiaries are active primarily as consultants, installers and suppliers within the electrical engineering and building services sector, as well as in the construction, servicing and installation of automation and security solutions. Selatek derives Australian revenue through one subsidiary, Deltatec AB (**Deltatec**), which is a company incorporated in Sweden. In Australia, Deltatec supplies limited programming services of control systems involving water and sanitation engineering services.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Goldcup 39483 AB (under name change to Prometheus BidCo AB), a
company controlled by IK Partners (IK Partners), applied for a
notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of all of the
shares in SELATEK Holding AB and its subsidiaries (Selatek), as
described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, IK Partners, is a European private equity firm which
invests primarily across Northern Continental Europe. Its portfolio
companies operate across a range of different business sectors. The
Wendel Group, a French investment firm, has a majority shareholding
in IK Partners.
The target, Selatek, is a Swedish company that operates across three
core business areas: Electrical, Automation and Security. Selatek’s
subsidiaries are active primarily as consultants, installers and
suppliers within the electrical engineering and building services
sector, as well as in the construction, servicing and installation of
automation and security solutions. Selatek’s only subsidiary with
sales to Australia is Deltatec AB (Deltatec). Deltatec is a Swedish
company which supplies limited programming services of control
systems involving water and sanitation engineering services in
Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is no or limited horizontal overlap and no vertical
relationship between IK Partners and Selatek (including their
connected entities) in Australia
b. Selatek has a small presence in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
The transaction concerns the proposed acquisition of sole control of Protego Holdco B.V. (**Protego**) by Ivera Bidco B.V., an entity indirectly owned by funds managed and/or advised by the Triton Group (**Triton**) (the **Acquisition**).

Protego and its subsidiary group hold the Integris Composites business (**Integris**). Integris is a global supplier of composite armour systems and protection solutions for armed forces and defence contractors.

Prior to the Acquisition, Protego is indirectly majority owned by Agilitas Protego 2026 Subsidiary Fund LP, a limited partnership established under Jersey law. Agilitas Protego 2026 Subsidiary Fund LP is a partnership within the group of funds advised by Agilitas Private Equity.

Triton is an investment firm based in Luxembourg managing and advising companies, primarily medium-sized businesses headquartered in Central and Northern Europe, in particular in Austria, Germany, Switzerland, the Benelux, and Nordic countries. Triton focuses on businesses in three core sectors: Business Services, Industrials and Health.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Ivera Bidco B.V. (Ivera), an entity indirectly owned by funds managed
and/or advised by the Triton Group (Triton), applied for a notification
waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the share
capital in Protego Holdco B.V. (Protego), as described in the
transaction documents provided as part of the application (the
Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

Triton is an investment firm based in Luxembourg managing and
advising companies, primarily medium-sized businesses
headquartered in Central and Northern Europe, in particular in
Austria, Germany, Switzerland, the Benelux, and Nordic countries.
Triton focuses on businesses in three core sectors: Business
Services, Industrials and Health.
The target, Protego, and its subsidiary group, hold the Integris
Composites business (Integris). Integris is a global supplier of
composite armour systems and protection solutions for armed
forces and defence contractors.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. there is no horizontal overlap or vertical relationships
between Triton’s connected entities and Integris in the
supply of composite armour systems and protection
solutions for armed forces and defence contractors
b. there are several alternative suppliers of composite armour
systems and protection solutions to customers in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_name: PRIME Asia – Stockland Sienna Wood and Providence

acquirers.name: Prime Property Fund Asia Limited Partnership

targets.name: Stockland Sienna Wood Retail No. 2 Trust

mergers.merger_description:
Prime Property Fund Asia Limited Partnership (**PRIME Asia**) proposes to acquire units in the Stockland Sienna Wood Retail No. 2 Trust and the Stockland Providence Retail Trust (the **Acquisition**).

The Acquisition is taking place as part of arrangements pursuant to which PRIME Asia as an institutional investor will provide capital for the development, operation and management of retail town centre assets (including relevantly those located in Sienna Wood in Western Australia and Providence in Queensland) by Stockland Corporation Ltd and Stockland Trust (**Stockland**).

PRIME Asia is a fund advised by Morgan Stanley Real Estate Investing. It is an open-ended commingled real estate investment fund with an investment mandate to acquire institutional-quality real estate investments across the Asia region, including in Australia.

As part of its activities, Stockland owns and operates a portfolio of retail town centres.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Prime Property Fund Asia Limited Partnership (PRIME Asia) applied
for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of
49.9% of the units in the Stockland Sienna Wood Retail No. 2 Trust
(Sienna Wood Trust) and 70.1% of the units in the Stockland
Providence Retail Trust (Providence Trust), as described in the
transaction documents provided as part of the application (the
Acquisition).
The Acquisition is taking place as part of arrangements pursuant to
which PRIME Asia as an institutional investor will provide capital for
the development, operation and management of retail town centre
assets (including relevantly those located in Sienna Wood in Western
Australia and Providence in Queensland) by Stockland Corporation
Ltd and Stockland Trust (Stockland).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
2 June 2026

The acquirer, PRIME Asia, is a fund advised by Morgan Stanley Real
Estate Investing. It is an open-ended commingled real estate
investment fund with an investment mandate to acquire institutional-
quality real estate investments across the Asia region, including in
Australia.
The Sienna Wood and Providence sites are retail town centres within
master planned communities located in South East Perth, Western
Australia, and South West Brisbane, Queensland, respectively, that
are currently indirectly wholly owned by Stockland.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors
in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010
(Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular, there is no horizontal overlap or vertical
relationship between any existing interests of PRIME Asia (and its
connected entities) in the local regions and the Sienna Wood and
Providence retail town centres.
In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to
reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification
thresholds being met.
Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the
ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
KK Group Invest A/S (**KK Group Invest**) plans to acquire 100% of the share capital of PowerCon Holding ApS and its wholly-owned subsidiaries PowerCon A/S and PowerCon USA Inc (collectively, **PowerCon**), by way of share transfer (the **Acquisition**).

PowerCon is a group of companies headquartered in Denmark that supply infrastructure solutions for shore power supply for use by ships at berth in ports globally, including in Denmark, Germany, the Netherlands, Spain, Australia and the USA. In Australia, PowerCon supplies a shore power supply system for the Port Authority of New South Wales.

KK Group Invest is a Danish-registered global technology company whose core business is the development, production, sale and servicing of power electronics system solutions and technology for onshore and offshore wind turbines. In Australia, KK Group Invest supplies mostly monitoring and service products to customers based in Australia.

KK Group Invest is part of the A.P. Moller Group. A.P. Møller Holding A/S is the parent company of this group and is a wholly-owned subsidiary of the Danish commercial foundation A.P. Møller og Hustru Chastine Mc-Kinney Møllers Fond til almene Formaal.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
KK Group Invest A/S (KK Group Invest) applied for a notification
waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the share
capital of PowerCon Holding ApS, and its subsidiaries PowerCon A/S
and PowerCon USA Inc (together, PowerCon), as described in the
transaction documents provided as part of the application (the
Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
1 June 2026

The acquirer, KK Group Invest, is a Danish-registered global
technology company whose core business is the development,
production, sale and servicing of power electronics system solutions
and technology for onshore and offshore wind turbines. In Australia,
KK Group Invest supplies mostly monitoring and service products to
customers based in Australia.
KK Group Invest is part of the A.P. Moller Group. Other entities within
the A.P Moller Group include Svitzer, a provider of global towage
services, and A.P. Moller-Maersk A/S (APMM), a container liner
shipping, container terminal, port operations, and port services
provider.
The target, PowerCon, is a group of companies headquartered in
Denmark that supply infrastructure solutions for shore power supply
for use by ships at berth in ports globally, including in Denmark,
Germany, the Netherlands, Spain, Australia and the USA. In Australia,
PowerCon supplies a shore power supply system for the Port
Authority of New South Wales for use in the White Bay Cruise
Terminal in Sydney.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. there is no horizontal overlap between KK Group Invest (or
Svitzer and APMM) and PowerCon in the supply of shore
power supply systems in Australia
b. there is a low risk of foreclosure or other concerning vertical
or conglomerate effects resulting from exclusionary
conduct, bundling or tying of maritime services post-
acquisition.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_name: Regent Motors Group – Esperance Motor Group (Esperance Toyota & Ford)

targets.name: BUVERIE PTY LTD as trustee for SCHWARZBACK FAMILY TRUST

mergers.merger_description:
Albany Motors Pty Ltd (**Albany Motors**), a member of Regent Motors Pty Ltd (**Regent Motors Group**), proposes to acquire 100% of the issued shares in Esperance Motor Group Pty Ltd (**Esperance Motor Group**) from Buverie Pty Ltd as trustee for the Schwarzback Family Trust.

Regent Motors Group owns and operates franchised motor vehicle dealerships in Western Australia and Northern Territory, and supplies new (by brand) and used motor vehicles, plus aftersales services including servicing and repairs, parts and accessories, fleet sales/service, and finance and insurance facilitation.

Esperance Motor Group operates a Toyota and Ford dealership in Esperance, Western Australia, supplying new and demonstrator Toyota and Ford vehicles, used vehicles, and aftersales services including servicing and repairs, parts and accessories, fleet activities, and finance and insurance facilitation.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Luke Woodward pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Albany Motors Pty Ltd (Albany Motors) applied for a notification
waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the share
capital of Esperance Motor Group Pty Ltd (Esperance Motor Group),
as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
5 February 2026

The acquirer, Albany Motors, is a member of Regent Motors Pty Ltd
(Regent Motors Group). Regent Motors Group owns and operates
franchised motor vehicle dealerships across Western Australia and
Northern Territory, and supplies new and used motor vehicles
(including Toyota, Ford, Hyundai, Volkswagen, BMW, MG, Mini, Isuzu,
Renault, GMSV, Suzuki and Subaru vehicles), plus after sale services
including servicing and repairs, parts and accessories, fleet
sales/service, and finance and insurance facilitation.
The target, Esperance Motor Group, operates a Toyota and Ford
dealership in Esperance, Western Australia, supplying new and
demonstrator Toyota and Ford vehicles, used vehicles, and after sale
services including servicing and repairs, parts and accessories, fleet
activities, and finance and insurance facilitation.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the
information provided by the applicant, including in the notification
waiver application, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)
of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act).
Based on the information provided by the applicant, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. There is no horizontal overlap between Regent Motors Group
and Esperance Motor Group in the supply of new and used
motor vehicles and after sale services in Esperance and the
surrounding areas.
b. There is a potential horizontal overlap between Regent
Motors Group and Esperance Motor Group in the broader
area of southern WA. If Perth is excluded from this area,
there are independent alternatives in Albany, Kalgoorlie,
Katanning and Narrogin. If Perth is included in this area, the
merged parties would likely face competition from
numerous additional alternative dealerships in Perth.
c. Any increment to Regent Motors Group share of the supply
of Toyota and Ford vehicles in Western Australia is small.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Delinea Inc. (**Delinea**), a company ultimately controlled by funds managed and/or advised by affiliates of TPG Inc. (**TPG**), proposes to acquire 100% of the issued share capital in StrongDM, Inc (**StrongDM**).

TPG is a global alternative asset manager firm founded in San Francisco.

Delinea is a software business specialising in solutions for identity security through its identity platform that enables enterprises to securely vault credentials, efficiently manage access for human and machine identities, assign appropriate access levels, detect irregularities, manage identity lifecycles, and respond to threats in real-time.

StrongDM is a provider of just-in-time based Privileged Access Management (**PAM**) software primarily deployed and operated in cloud environments. StrongDM's products allow temporary access to IT resources which is granted at a specific time to a specific identify for a specific purpose.

Delinea and StrongDM supply software focused on validating the identity of users to enforce policies regarding the authorisation of users to specific IT resources and distributed business systems, known as Identity and Access Management (**IAM**) software. IAM products verify and manage digital identities and govern, administer, and enforce user and machine access rights to systems, applications, and data across on-premises and cloud environments. Delinea and StrongDM supply PAM solutions, which are a subset of IAM, which provide an elevated level of technical access.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Delinea Inc. (Delinea) applied for a notification waiver in respect of
its proposed acquisition of 100% of the issued share capital in
StrongDM, Inc. (StrongDM), through a merger with, Spartan Merger
Sub, Inc., a wholly owned subsidiary of Delinea, as described in the
transaction documents provided as part of the application (the
Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
3 February 2026

The acquirer, Delinea, is a software business specialising in
solutions for identity security through its identity platform that
enables enterprises to securely vault credentials, efficiently manage
access for human and machine identities, assign appropriate access
levels, detect irregularities, manage identity lifecycles, and respond
to threats in real-time. Delinea is a company ultimately controlled by
funds managed and/or advised by affiliates of TPG Inc. (TPG). TPG
is a global alternative asset manager firm founded in San Francisco.
The target, StrongDM, delivers just-in-time based privileged access
management (PAM) software primarily deployed and operated in
cloud environments. StrongDM's products allow temporary access
to IT resources which is granted at a specific time to a specific
identify for a specific purpose.
Delinea and StrongDM overlap in the supply of software focused on
validating the identity of users to enforce policies regarding the
authorisation of users to specific IT resources and distributed
business systems, known as Identity and Access Management (IAM)
software. IAM products verify and manage digital identities and
govern, administer, and enforce user and machine access rights to
systems, applications, and data across on-premises and cloud
environments. Delinea and StrongDM supply PAM solutions, which
are a subset of IAM, which provide an elevated level of technical
access.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the
information provided with the notification waiver application, public
information and other industry information held by the ACCC, and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act).
Based on the information outlined above, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. StrongDM has a very small presence in Australia.
b. There are numerous alternative suppliers of IAM or PAM
software in Australia and globally.
c. While there is horizontal overlap between the software
supplied by Delinea and StrongDM, the increase in share of
supply in Australia arising from the Acquisition would be
very small.
d. Apart from Delinea, no horizontal overlap has been identified
between any of TPG’s other connected entities and
StrongDM.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_name: Australian Venue Co. – leasehold and certain other assets of Quay Restaurant, Sydney
mergers.merger_description:
AVC Operations Pty Limited (**AVC**) proposes to acquire a leasehold (via lease assignment), the benefit of a liquor licence and certain plant and equipment (with certain exclusions) of Quay Restaurant (**Quay**) from Q.R.A. Pty Limited (the **Acquisition**). The Vendor will not transfer the existing business of Quay to AVC, only the specific assets mentioned above.   

AVC is a subsidiary of Australian Venue Co. Holdings Limited which operates licensed venues throughout Australia and New Zealand, primarily pubs, as well as bars, clubs and licensed restaurants. AVC's venues are individually branded and mostly located in inner-city and metro areas, offering food, alcoholic and non-alcoholic beverages, function rooms, live entertainment and gaming facilities.   

Quay is situated on the Sydney harbourfront at Tenancy 6, Upper Level, Overseas Passenger Terminal, The Rocks. Quay Restaurant offers fine dining with views of the Sydney Opera House and Harbour Bridge.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Philip Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Australian Venue Co. Holdings Limited (Australian Venue Co), via
AVC Operations Pty Limited (AVC), applied for a notification waiver
in respect of its proposed acquisition of a leasehold, benefit of a
liquor licence and certain assets of Quay Restaurant (Quay), as
described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
9 February 2026

The acquirer, Australian Venue Co, operates licensed venues
throughout Australia and New Zealand, primarily pubs, as well as
bars, clubs and licensed restaurants. Their venues are individually
branded and mostly located in inner-city and metro areas, offering
food, alcoholic and non-alcoholic beverages, function rooms, live
entertainment and gaming facilities.
The target, Quay, is situated on the Sydney harbourfront at Tenancy
6, Upper Level, Overseas Passenger Terminal, The Rocks. Quay
Restaurant is a contemporary restaurant that offers fine dining with
views of the Sydney Opera House and Harbour Bridge and plans to
cease trading in mid-February 2026.
Australian Venue Co, through its venue The Squire’s Landing, and
Quay both supply retail hospitality restaurants in the same building in
the Overseas Passenger Terminal.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the
information provided with the notification waiver application and had
regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
• While there is horizontal overlap between Quay and
Australian Venue Co, the combined share of the parties in
the supply of retail hospitality restaurants in the Circular
Quay area of Sydney is low.
• The presence of alternative suppliers of retail hospitality
restaurants in proximity to the Circular Quay area of Sydney.
In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to
reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification
thresholds being met. Given that material competition concerns are
unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not
required to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Five V Capital Pty Ltd (**Five V Capital**) is an Australian-incorporated private equity and venture capital firm with headquarters in Australia and New Zealand. Five V Capital manages a number of Five V funds, including Horizons Fund and Frontier Fund. Horizons Fund and Frontier Fund are investment funds that pursue a strategy of investing in private mid-market businesses.

Five V Capital and an incorporated company that is wholly owned by Horizons Fund and Frontier Fund, Overwatch BidCo Pty Ltd, propose to acquire the majority of the issued shares in FTP Group Holdings Pty Ltd and its subsidiaries (the **FTP Group**) from the existing shareholders of FTP Group Holdings Pty Ltd (the **Acquisition**).

FTP Group is based in Australia, Canada and New Zealand and operates under the business names ‘FTP Solutions’, ‘Faarm’ and ‘Yarn Mesh’.

FTP Solutions is a provider of integrated network and operational monitoring software to the global mining industry. Its flagship product, ‘IMS’, is a hardware agnostic software tool designed to monitor and visualise the network and operations of mine sites in real-time. IMS allows its users to quickly identify and resolve network issues that could result in operational downtime.

Faarm and Yarn Mesh operate in the agricultural sector. Faarm is a software platform that aggregates disparate farm data (e.g. water usage, fertiliser, soil quality, weather and livestock metrics) into a single interface. Yarn Mesh designs and manufactures long-range mesh networking hardware that delivers connectivity across remote environments.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Overwatch Bidco Pty Ltd, Five V Capital Pty Ltd and Five V Horizons
Pty Ltd applied for a notification waiver in respect of Overwatch
Bidco Pty Ltd’s proposed acquisition of the majority of the issued
shares in FTP Group Holdings Pty Ltd and its subsidiaries (the FTP
Group), as described in the transaction documents provided as part
of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
24 March 2026

The acquirer, Overwatch Bidco Pty Ltd, is indirectly wholly owned by
Horizons Fund and Frontier Fund, investment funds managed by Five
V Capital Pty Ltd (Five V Capital). Horizons Fund and Frontier Fund
pursue strategies of investing in private mid-market businesses. Five
V Capital is an Australian-incorporated private equity and venture
capital firm with headquarters in Australia and New Zealand. It
manages a number of Five V funds which have investments in a wide
range of businesses and industries.
The target, FTP Group, is based in Australia, Canada and New
Zealand and operates under the business names ‘FTP Solutions’,
‘Faarm’ and ‘Yarn Mesh’. FTP Solutions provides integrated network
and operational monitoring software to the global mining industry.
Faarm is a software platform that aggregates disparate farm data
into a single interface to enable farmers to monitor farms. Yarn
Mesh designs and manufactures long-range mesh networking
hardware that delivers connectivity across remote environments.
A connected entity of Five V Capital, Beacon TopCo Pty Ltd trading
as Digital Matter (Digital Matter), supplies IoT hardware solutions
and device management software that enable long-term asset
tracking, sensor monitoring and telematics applications. Digital
Matter’s solutions can be used in several settings, including in the
agricultural setting, for example for remote sensor monitoring,
environmental monitoring, and water monitoring.
Broadly, Digital Matter and FTP Group’s Faarm business (Faarm)
both supply solutions that facilitate remote monitoring for use in
agricultural settings. Digital Matter supplies hardware devices (with
some associated software such as device management software),
whereas Faarm offers a platform that integrates with third party
hardware.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. There is no horizontal overlap or vertical relationship
between FTP Group and Five V Capital and its connected
entities.
b. There are several other suppliers of agricultural monitoring
solutions and of IoT hardware solutions and device
management software in Australia.
c. Faarm has a relatively small presence in Australia.
d. While there is a potential complementary relationship
between Digital Matter and Faarm in the supply of
monitoring solutions for use in agricultural settings, there is
a low risk of concerning conglomerate effects resulting from
exclusionary conduct and/or bundling or tying of products
post-Acquisition.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

targets.name: GROWTHPOINT PROPERTIES AUSTRALIA LIMITED AS TRUSTEE FOR THE WHOLESALE INDUSTRIAL PROPERTY FUND

mergers.merger_description:
Brisbane Airport Corporation Pty Limited (**Brisbane Airport Corporation**) operates, manages, and develops Brisbane Airport. Brisbane Airport Corporation facilitates international and domestic travel, manages over 2,700 hectares of land, provides infrastructure (terminals, runways, roads), and facilitates commercial/retail/industrial activities through the leasing of the airport land.

Growthpoint Properties Australia Limited (**GOZ**) is an ASX-listed real estate investment trust, which carries on a business primarily engaged in leasing, buying and selling land and managing a portfolio of office, industrial and retail assets for third party wholesale syndicates and institutional investors. GOZ invests in Australian real estate and has a directly owned portfolio of office and industrial properties which it leases to commercial tenants. Growthpoint Properties Australia Limited as trustee of the Wholesale Industrial Property Fund (**Growthpoint**) holds a leasehold interest (as lessee) of the properties located at 3, 5 and 7A Viola Place, Brisbane Airport under the Head Leases from Brisbane Airport Corporation (as lessor).

Brisbane Airport Corporation has entered into a Contract for the Sale and Purchase of Commercial Real Estate to acquire from Growthpoint its interest as lessee under the Head Leases and as sublessor under the Subleases for lots located at 3, 5 and 7A Viola Place, Brisbane Airport, Queensland.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Woodward pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Brisbane Airport Corporation Pty Limited (Brisbane Airport
Corporation) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its
proposed acquisition from Growthpoint Properties Australia Limited
as trustee of the Wholesale Industrial Property Fund (Growthpoint)
of Growthpoint’s interest as lessee under the Head Leases and as
sublessor under the Subleases for lots located at 3, 5 and 7A Viola
Place, Brisbane Airport, Queensland, as described in the transaction
documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
8 April 2026

The acquirer, Brisbane Airport Corporation, operates, manages, and
develops Brisbane Airport. Brisbane Airport Corporation facilitates
international and domestic travel, manages over 2,700 hectares of
land, provides infrastructure (terminals, runways, roads), and
facilitates commercial, retail and industrial activity through the
leasing of the airport land.
Growthpoint Properties Australia Limited (GOZ) is an ASX-listed real
estate investment trust, which carries on a business primarily
engaged in leasing, buying and selling land and managing a portfolio
of office, industrial and retail assets for third party wholesale
syndicates and institutional investors. GOZ invests in Australian real
estate and has a directly owned portfolio of office and industrial
properties which it leases to commercial tenants.
Growthpoint holds a leasehold interest (as lessee) of the properties
located at 3, 5 and 7A Viola Place, Brisbane Airport (the Property)
under the Head Leases from Brisbane Airport Corporation (as
lessor).
The Property would be an input into the operations of Brisbane
Airport Corporation.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors
in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010
(Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. Brisbane Airport Corporation is the head lessor in respect of
the Property and will be acquiring interests in existing leases
b. there are alternative sites available to develop or lease at
Brisbane Airport, and in the wider Brisbane area, that are
held by third parties.
In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to
reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification
thresholds being met.
Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the
ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
The INNIO Group operates in the industrial equipment and energy infrastructure sector, primarily as a global manufacturer of gas power units under the Jenbacher and Waukesha brands. Its products are supplied to industries such as power generation, oil and gas (particularly for gas compression), utilities and large-scale industrial users. INNIO’s power units are used for distributed power generation, combined heat and power applications, and gas compression, serving a broad range of commercial, industrial and utility customers.

Enerflex Australia operates in the energy infrastructure services sector with a strong focus on supporting the gas industry as well as industrial energy users. The company provides aftermarket services such as maintenance, repair, spare parts, field services, retrofits, and operations and maintenance for a wide variety of gas compression and engine-power equipment.

Two wholly owned subsidiaries of INNIO Holding (INNIO Jenbacher International and INNIO Jenbacher Netherlands) propose to acquire from Enerflex Ltd (together with all its subsidiaries, **Enerflex Group**) the issued share capital in certain Enerflex Group operating companies in Australia, Indonesia and Thailand (**Target Companies**), which will transfer Enerflex Group’s aftermarket service businesses in those countries to the INNIO Group (**APAC Transaction**).

The Australian element of the APAC Transaction (the **Acquisition**) comprises the acquisition by INNIO Jenbacher International of all of the issued share capital in Enerflex Service Pty Limited (**Enerflex Australia**) from Enerflex Australasia Holdings Pty Ltd., an indirect, wholly-owned subsidiary of Enerflex Ltd. On completion of the Acquisition, Enerflex Australia will become an indirect, wholly owned subsidiary of INNIO Holding and part of the INNIO Group’s global Jenbacher/Waukesha gas compression power units and services business.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Two wholly owned subsidiaries of INNIO Holding GmbH (INNIO
Jenbacher International B.V. and INNIO Jenbacher Netherlands B.V.),
propose to acquire from Enerflex Ltd (together with all its
subsidiaries, Enerflex Group) the issued share capital in certain
Enerflex Group operating companies in Australia, Indonesia and
Thailand, which will transfer Enerflex Group’s aftermarket service
businesses in those countries to the INNIO Group (APAC
Transaction).
The Australian element of the APAC Transaction comprises the
acquisition by INNIO Jenbacher International B.V. of all of the issued
share capital in Enerflex Service Pty Limited (Enerflex Australia)
from Enerflex Australasia Holdings Pty Ltd., an indirect, wholly-
owned subsidiary of Enerflex Ltd, as described in the transaction
documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
On completion of the Acquisition, Enerflex Australia will become an
indirect, wholly owned subsidiary of INNIO Holding GmbH and part of
the INNIO Group’s global Jenbacher/Waukesha gas compression
power units and services business.
INNIO Jenbacher International B.V., INNIO Jenbacher Netherlands
B.V., INNIO Holding GmbH and INNIO Holding subsidiaries (together,
the INNIO Group) applied for a notification waiver in respect of the
Acquisition.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(b) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) not to make the notification waiver
determination applied for.
16 April 2026

The INNIO Group operates in the industrial equipment and energy
infrastructure sector, primarily as a global manufacturer of gas
power units under the Jenbacher and Waukesha brands. Its products
are supplied to industries such as power generation, oil and gas,
utilities and large-scale industrial users. INNIO’s power units are
used for distributed power generation, combined heat and power
applications, and gas compression, serving a broad range of
commercial, industrial and utility customers.
INNIO Holding GmbH, and thereby the INNIO Group, is indirectly held,
managed and/or advised by Advent International, L.P. (Advent)
and/or its affiliates. Advent is a globally active private equity investor
based in Boston, USA.
The target, Enerflex Australia, operates in the energy infrastructure
services sector with a focus on supporting the gas industry as well
as industrial energy users. The company provides aftermarket
services such as maintenance, repair, spare parts, field services,
retrofits, and operations and maintenance for a wide variety of gas
compression and engine-power equipment.
The INNIO Group is an upstream supplier of new compression and
power generation power units. Enerflex Australia is a downstream
multi-brand aftermarket services provider for energy infrastructure
operators. The INNIO Group and Enerflex Australia also both supply
certain spare parts for gas compression power units to customers.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the
information provided with the notification waiver application and had
regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Act.
Based on the information provided in the application, including the
parties’ revenue in Australia, the ACCC considers that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds would be
met.
The ACCC is unable to reach a view that the Acquisition would not be
likely to raise competition issues in any market based on the
information provided. It considers that the Acquisition warrants
consideration through the notification process, including via further
information gathering and consultation with third parties. This may
include a focus on:
a. the vertical relationship between the products and services
supplied by the INNIO Group and Enerflex Australia, their
relative market position, and the likely impact of the
Acquisition on the parties’ ability and/or incentive to
foreclose rivals
b. the extent to which the parties are likely to be constrained by
alternative suppliers of gas compression power units and
aftermarket services (including the provision of spare parts)
to customers in Australia.
Given the above, the ACCC has determined not to make the
notification waiver determination applied for.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
