mergers.merger_description:
BGIS Pty Ltd (**BGIS**) proposes to acquire 100% of the issued shares in the Ascot Air Facilities Pty Ltd (**Ascot Air**) from its shareholders (the **Acquisition**).

BGIS is a national provider of integrated facilities management services and supplies heating, ventilation and air conditioning (**HVAC**) services largely as part of broader facilities management arrangements for portfolio customers, either through internal service capability or specialist subcontractors.

Ascot Air is a provider of commercial HVAC and mechanical services, supplying commercial HVAC design and construction, chilled water systems, refrigeration, ventilation ductwork, pipework and mechanical services, computer room air conditioning systems, building management systems and controls, drafting and design support and commissioning services.

Ascot Air provides services across South East Queensland, including Brisbane, Ipswich, Logan, Redland, Moreton Bay and the Gold Coast.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
BGIS Pty Ltd (BGIS) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its
proposed acquisition of 100% of the share capital in Ascot Air
Facilities Pty Ltd (Ascot Air), as described in the transaction
documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
7 April 2026

The acquirer, BGIS, is a national provider of integrated facilities
management services and supplies heating, ventilation and air
conditioning (HVAC) services largely as part of broader facilities
management arrangements for portfolio customers, either through
internal service capability or specialist subcontractors.
The target, Ascot Air, is a provider of commercial HVAC and
mechanical services, supplying commercial HVAC design and
construction, chilled water systems, refrigeration, ventilation
ductwork, pipework and mechanical services, computer room air
conditioning systems, building management systems and controls,
drafting and design support and commissioning services. Ascot Air
provides services across South East Queensland, including Brisbane,
Ipswich, Logan, Redland, Moreton Bay and the Gold Coast.
Both parties supply commercial HVAC and related mechanical
services within South East Queensland.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors
in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010
(Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is a small horizontal overlap between BGIS and Ascot
Air in the supply of commercial HVAC and related
mechanical services to areas within South East Queensland
b. while there is a potential vertical supply relationship between
the parties, there appears to be a low risk of foreclosure or
other concerning vertical effects resulting from exclusionary
conduct post-acquisition
c. there are alternative suppliers of commercial HVAC and
related mechanical services in areas within South East
Queensland.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on

mergers.merger_description:
Rosewood AG, LP, a Guernsey limited partnership (indirectly owned by the Eighth Cinven Fund) proposes to acquire voting rights in Collagen Holdings, LLC (trading as **Regenity**) (the **Acquisition**).

The Eighth Cinven Fund is indirectly managed and controlled by Cinven Limited. Cinven Limited has exclusive authority to make investment and management decisions for Eighth Cinven Fund.

Eighth Cinven Fund and Cinven Limited are part of Cinven. Cinven is a private equity firm engaged in the provision of investment management and investment advisory services to a number of investment funds. The controlled companies in Cinven’s portfolio of investments are active in a variety of sectors including business services, consumer, financial services, healthcare, industrials and technology, media and telecommunications.

The Acquisition will result in Cinven Limited and the existing investor, Linden Capital Partners, having joint control of Regenity.

Linden Capital Partners is a Chicago-based private equity firm focused exclusively on the healthcare industry.

Regenity is a global developer and manufacturer of bioresorbable technologies. The company is headquartered in Paramus, New Jersey.

mergers.merger_name: CVC Capital Partners – The animal nutrition and health division of the DSM-Firmenich Group

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
CVC Capital Partners plc. (CVC Capital Partners) proposes to
acquire the animal nutrition and health division of the DSM-
Firmenich Group as described in the transaction documents
provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Acquisition consists of:
• CVC Capital Partners to indirectly acquire, through Specialty
Bidco B.V., Speciality (U.S.) Bidco, Inc., Essential Bidco B.V.,
and Essential (U.S.) Bidco, Inc., an 80% shareholding and
sole control over the SpecialtyCo Business and EssentialCo
Business (which together comprise the animal nutrition and
health division), and
• the DSM-Firmenich Group to (indirectly through to be
incorporated entities) reinvest in each of the SpecialityCo
Business and the EssentialCo Business to retain a ~20%
non-controlling, minority stake in each.
Specialty Bidco B.V., Speciality (U.S.) Bidco, Inc., Essential Bidco
B.V., and Essential (U.S.) Bidco, Inc applied for a notification waiver
in respect of the Acquisition.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
7 April 2026

The acquirer, CVC Capital Partners, is a global alternative investment
manager focused on private equity, credit, infrastructure and
secondaries. CVC Capital Partners hold interests in companies
active in a variety of industries around the world, including in
financial services, chemicals, utilities, manufacturing, retailing and
distribution, primarily in Europe, the US and the Asia-Pacific region.
The target, the animal nutrition and health division of the DSM-
Firmenich Group, is a global provider of science-based animal
nutrition and health solutions. Its core business is the manufacture
and supply of animal nutrition ingredients, particularly vitamins and
carotenoids, for livestock and aquaculture feed. It is also involved in
the premix industry, which serve as a link from feed additives to final
feed composition in the animal nutrition value chain.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver and had regard
to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular, there is no horizontal overlap
between the products and services supplied by the animal nutrition
and health division of the DSM-Firmenich Group and CVC Capital
Partners controlled portfolio companies in Australia, or any vertical
relationships.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Host-Plus Pty. Limited (**Hostplus**) proposes to acquire 50% of CH Investment Holding Trust (**CH Holding Trust**), a Charter Hall managed vehicle (the **Acquisition**).

Through the Acquisition, Hostplus will gain indirect economic exposure to 50% of the commercial property assets indirectly owned by CH Holding Trust, including an office building in the Sydney CBD.

Hostplus is the trustee for both the Hostplus Pooled Superannuation Trust and the Hostplus Superannuation Fund. The Hostplus Pooled Superannuation Trust is the unit trust vehicle that the Hostplus Superannuation Fund utilises to access investments for its members.

Charter Hall is an Australian property investment and funds management group, acting as a fiduciary for wholesale, institutional, and retail investors.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Host-Plus Pty. Limited as trustee for the Hostplus Pooled
Superannuation Trust (Hostplus) applied for a notification waiver in
respect of its proposed acquisition of 50% of CH Investment Holding
Trust (CH Holding Trust), as described in the transaction documents
provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, Hostplus, is the trustee for each of the Hostplus Pooled
Superannuation Trust and the Hostplus Superannuation Fund. The
Hostplus Pooled Superannuation Trust is the unit trust vehicle that
the Hostplus Superannuation Fund utilises to access investments for
its members. The Hostplus Superannuation Fund has 1.87 million
members (as at 30 June 2025), with current funds under
management of $134 billion (as at 30 June 2025). Hostplus invests
across a diversified portfolio of asset classes, including equities,
fixed income, infrastructure, property, private equity and alternative
investments, for the benefit of its members. Hostplus has passive
minority investments in funds that have exposure to commercial
office space in the Sydney CBD.
The target, CH Holding Trust, indirectly holds commercial property
assets in the Sydney CBD. CH Holding Trust is managed by Charter
Hall which is an Australian property investment and funds
management group, that acts as a fiduciary for wholesale,
institutional, and retail investors.
Through the Acquisition, Hostplus will gain a 50% indirect interest in
the commercial property assets indirectly owned by CH Holding
Trust in the Sydney CBD.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application, and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. the increment to Hostplus’ interests in commercial office
property in Sydney CBD will be small
b. there are alternative commercial office offerings in the
Sydney CBD.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Ryowa II GPS Pty Ltd (**Ryowa**) proposes to acquire:

* all or substantially all of the assets of the Phosphate Hill fertiliser mine and manufacturing plant (**Phosphate Hill**), and
* 100% of the issued share capital in PH Queensland Gas Pty Ltd (**PHQG**),

from Dyno Nobel Limited (**DNL**) and Southern Cross Operations Pty Ltd (**SCO**) (collectively, the **Sellers**) through an Asset Purchase Agreement.

Ryowa's ultimate parent company, Mayfair Australia Corporation Pty Ltd (**Mayfair Corporation**), is an Australian energy and resources company.  Ryowa seeks to enter the agriculture industry through the acquisition of Phosphate Hill. Ryowa currently owns a minority interest in the Gladstone Power Station.

Phosphate Hill is a fertiliser manufacturing plant located in Queensland. PHQG owns a minority interest in an undeveloped gas tenement in Queensland’s Surat Basin.

DNL is an international explosive manufacturing business. In Australia, DNL manufactures and supplies commercial explosives and provides commercial blasting services. SCO is a wholly owned subsidiary of DNL which operates in the fertiliser manufacturing business.

The Sellers seek to exit from Phosphate Hill to complete DNL's separation from its fertiliser endeavours.

determination_table_content.details:
Ryowa II GPS Pty Ltd (Ryowa), whose ultimate parent company is
Mayfair Australia Corporation Pty Ltd (Mayfair Corporation), applied
for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of:
a. all or substantially all of the assets of the Phosphate Hill
fertiliser mine and manufacturing plant (Phosphate Hill), and
b. 100% of the issued share capital in PH Queensland Gas Pty
Ltd (PHQG)
from Dyno Nobel Limited (DNL) and Southern Cross Operations Pty
Ltd (SCO), as described in the transaction documents provided as
part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, Ryowa, currently owns a minority interest in the
Gladstone Power Station. Ryowa’s ultimate parent company, Mayfair
Corporation, is an Australia energy and resources company.
The target, Phosphate Hill, is currently owned by DNL, an
international explosive manufacturing business. In Australia, DNL
manufactures and supplies commercial explosives and provides
commercial blasting services. SCO is a wholly owned subsidiary of
DNL, which currently operates in the fertiliser manufacturing
business through Phosphate Hill.
PHQG owns a minority interest in the Range Gas Project, a
development project in Queensland’s Surat Basin.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors
in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010
(Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular, there is no horizontal overlap or vertical
relationship between the merger parties in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Zijin Gold (Group) Co., Ltd., (**Zijin**), a wholly-owned subsidiary of Zijin Mining Group Co., Ltd. (**Zijin Mining**), proposes to acquire an interest in Chifeng Jilong Gold Mining Co., Ltd. (**Chifeng**), so that post-acquisition Zijin Mining as a whole will hold approximately 25.85% of the total number of shares in Chifeng, and acquire controlling power in Chifeng.

Zijin Mining is a global mining company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange and the Shanghai Stock Exchange. It is primarily engaged in exploration, mining and processing of mineral resources. Its principal products include gold, copper, zinc, lithium, silver, molybdenum and other metals such as iron ore concentrate, cobalt and sulfuric acid.

Zijin, a wholly-owned subsidiary of Zijin Mining invests and manages the exploration and mining of mineral resources.

Norton Gold Fields Pty Ltd (**Norton**), a wholly-owned subsidiary of Zijin Gold International Company Limited (a company under control of Zijin Mining), is a gold producer with mining and processing operations in Western Australia. Norton has a number of owned and controlled Australian entities, including Paddington Gold Pty Limited. The Norton Group is one of Australia's largest domestic gold producers with mining and processing complexes located in Western Australia's Kalgoorlie gold region.

Chifeng is a gold mining company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange and the Shanghai Stock Exchange. Chifeng owns 7 operating mines and a resources recycling company in China, Southeast Asia and West Africa. Chifeng’s main product is gold, and it also produces copper, lead, zinc, molybdenum and rare earths.

Chifeng exports gold doré to customers in Australia but does not have any operating mines, assets or subsidiaries in Australia.

determination_table_content.details:
Zijin Gold (Group) Co., Ltd., a wholly-owned subsidiary of Zijin Mining
Group Co., Ltd. (Zijin Mining), applied for a notification waiver in
respect of its proposed acquisition of an interest Chifeng Jilong Gold
Mining Co., Ltd. (Chifeng), as described in the transaction
documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
Post-Acquisition, Zijin Mining will hold approximately 25.85% of the
total number of shares in Chifeng, and acquire controlling power in
Chifeng.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

Zijin Mining is a global mining company listed on the Hong Kong
Stock Exchange and the Shanghai Stock Exchange. It is primarily
engaged in exploration, mining and processing of mineral resources.
Its principal products include gold, copper, zinc, lithium, silver,
molybdenum and other metals such as iron ore concentrate, cobalt
and sulfuric acid.
Norton Gold Fields Pty Ltd (Norton), a wholly-owned subsidiary of
Zijin Gold International Company Limited (a company under control
of Zijin Mining), is a gold producer with mining and processing
operations in Western Australia. The Norton Group is one of
Australia's largest domestic gold producers with mining and
processing complexes located in Western Australia's Kalgoorlie gold
region.
Chifeng is a gold mining company listed on the Hong Kong Stock
Exchange and the Shanghai Stock Exchange. Chifeng owns 7
operating mines and a resources recycling company in China,
Southeast Asia and West Africa. Chifeng’s main product is gold, and
it also produces copper, lead, zinc, molybdenum and rare earths.
Chifeng does not have any operating mines, assets or subsidiaries in
Australia.
Zijin Mining and Chifeng both supply gold doré to customers in
Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. the parties’ combined share in the supply of gold doré to
customers in Australia is low
b. there are several alternative suppliers of gold doré to
customers in Australia
c. there is a low risk of foreclosure or other concerning vertical
or conglomerate effects resulting from exclusionary
conduct, bundling or tying of gold concentrate, gold doré and
gold bullion post-acquisition.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
The proposed acquisition involves the acquisition of the entire issued and to be issued share capital of Schroders plc (**Schroders**) by Pantheon, LLC (**Pantheon**), a wholly owned special purpose vehicle of Nuveen, LLC (**Nuveen**) (the **Acquisition**). Following completion, Nuveen, wholly owned by the Teachers Insurance and Annuity Association of America (**TIAA**), will hold 100% of the issued share capital of Schroders and therefore will have sole control of Schroders.

On 12 February 2026, the Boards of Nuveen and Schroders announced that they had agreed the terms of the Acquisition which is governed by the UK Takeover Code and is intended to be implemented by way of a court‑sanctioned scheme of arrangement.

Nuveen is the asset manager for TIAA. It is US-headquartered and offers a comprehensive range of investment solutions designed to secure the long-term financial goals of institutional and individual investors. Nuveen has ~AUD 2.17 trillion (~USD 1.4 trillion) in assets under management (**AUM**) as of 31 December 2025 – with approximately 93.5% in the Americas, 4% in Europe, the Middle East, and Africa (**EMEA**) and 2.5% in Asia-Pacific (**APAC**).

TIAA is a New York stock life insurance company that provides secure retirements and outcome-focused investment solutions primarily to US-based individuals and institutions. TIAA has ~AUD 2.33 trillion (~USD 1.5 trillion) in AUM, and administration and total statutory capital of ~AUD 74.4 billion (~USD 48 billion) as of 31 December 2025.

Pantheon is a direct, wholly owned subsidiary of Nuveen that has been newly incorporated for the purpose of facilitating the Acquisition.

Schroders is a UK-based global investment manager which provides active asset management, wealth management and investment solutions. Schroders had ~AUD 1.68 trillion (~GBP 824 billion) of AUM as of 31 December 2025, with 12% in the Americas, 64% in EMEA and 25% in APAC. Schroders operates three core business divisions: Public Markets, Wealth Management, and Schroders Capital, which focuses on private markets, including private equity and real estate.

events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Woodward pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Pantheon LLC (Pantheon), a wholly owned special purpose vehicle of
Nuveen, LLC (Nuveen), applied for a notification waiver in respect of
its proposed acquisition of the entire issued and to be issued share
capital of Schroders plc (Schroders), as described in the transaction
documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
Following completion, Nuveen will hold 100% of the issued share
capital of Schroders and will have sole control of Schroders.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, Pantheon, is a wholly owned special purpose vehicle of
Nuveen.
Nuveen is the asset manager for, and wholly owned by, the Teachers
Insurance and Annuity Association of America (TIAA). Nuveen is US-
headquartered and offers a comprehensive range of investment
solutions designed to secure the long-term financial goals of
institutional and individual investors. Nuveen has ~AUD 2.17 trillion
(~USD 1.4 trillion) in AUM as of 31 December 2025 – with
approximately 93.5% in the Americas, 4% in Europe, the Middle East,
and Africa (EMEA) and 2.5% in Asia-Pacific (APAC).
TIAA is a New York stock life insurance company that provides
secure retirements and outcome-focused investment solutions
primarily to US-based individuals and institutions. TIAA has ~AUD
2.33 trillion (~USD 1.5 trillion) in assets under management (AUM),
and administration and total statutory capital of ~AUD 74.4 billion
(~USD 48 billion) as of 31 December 2025.
The target, Schroders is a UK-based global investment manager
which provides active asset management, wealth management and
investment solutions. Schroders had ~AUD 1.68 trillion (~GBP 824
billion) of AUM as of 31 December 2025, with 12% in the Americas,
64% in EMEA and 25% in APAC. Schroders operates three core
business divisions: Public Markets, Wealth Management, and
Schroders Capital, which focuses on private markets, including
private equity and real estate.
Both Nuveen and Schroders supply asset management services to
customers in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. the parties’ combined share in the supply of asset
management services to customers in Australia is low
b. there are alternative suppliers of asset management
services to customers in Australia
c. there is a low risk of foreclosure or other concerning vertical
or conglomerate effects resulting from exclusionary
conduct, bundling or tying of asset management and wealth
management services post-acquisition.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
The Acquisition involves the merger of Indovida India Private Limited (**Indovida India**) with and into EPL Limited (**EPL**), by way of absorption of Indovida India as a going concern. In consideration of the merger, EPL (as the post-merger resultant entity) will issue and allot shares on a proportionate basis to the shareholders of Indovida India. As a result of the Acquisition, Indorama Netherland B.V. (**INBV**) (an indirect wholly owned subsidiary of Indorama Ventures Public Company Limited (**IVL**)) and Blackstone Inc. (**Blackstone**, which exercises sole control over Epsilon Bidco Pte. Ltd. (**Epsilon**), the existing controlling shareholder of EPL) will acquire indirect joint control over EPL. As a consequence of the Acquisition, INBV's direct interest in EPL will increase from 24.51% to approximately 52%.

EPL is a publicly listed Indian company engaged in the manufacturing and sale of packaging products, including laminated and extruded plastic tubes, caps and closures, and laminates. Its products are supplied to customers in the fast-moving consumer goods and pharmaceutical industries.

Prior to completion of the Acquisition, various subsidiaries of Indovida Netherlands B.V. will be consolidated under Indovida India. Globally, the subsidiaries which will form part of the Indovida Group are engaged in the manufacture and sale of pre-forms, bottles, and high-density polyethylene (**HDPE**) and polypropylene (**PP**) closures for use in the food and beverage and consumer products sectors. The Indovida Group's activities in Australia are limited to the supply of imported packaging products, specifically pre-forms made from polyethylene terephthalate (**PET**), and closures made from HDPE and PP.

The Indovida Group forms part of the wider IVL Group, a global conglomerate headed by IVL. It has diversified business interests, including in plastic packaging.

Epsilon is a Singaporean-registered investment holding entity owned by funds advised and/or managed by affiliates of Blackstone, a global alternative asset manager.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Woodward pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
EPL Limited (EPL) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its
proposed acquisition of 100% of the issued share capital of Indovida
India Private Limited (Indovida India) and its proposed subsidiaries,
through a share-for-share exchange, as described in the transaction
documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, EPL, is a publicly listed Indian company engaged in the
manufacturing and sale of packaging products, including laminated
and extruded plastic tubes, caps and closures, and laminates. Its
products are supplied to customers in fast-moving consumer goods
and pharmaceutical industries, including in Australia.
The target, Indovida India, and its proposed subsidiaries (described
below) (together, the Indovida Group) form part of the wider IVL
Group, a global conglomerate headed by Indorama Ventures Public
Company Limited. It has diversified business interests, including in
plastic packaging.
Prior to completion of the Acquisition, various subsidiaries of
Indovida Netherlands B.V. will be consolidated under Indovida India.
Subsequently, the Indovida Group will comprise Indovida India and
its proposed subsidiaries, which are engaged in the manufacture and
sale of pre-forms, bottles, and high-density polyethylene (HDPE) and
polypropylene (PP) closures for use in the food and beverage and
consumer products sectors globally. The Indovida Group’s activities
in Australia are limited to the supply of imported packaging products,
specifically pre-forms made from polyethylene terephthalate, and
closures made from HDPE and PP.
The parties are therefore both active in the supply of plastic
packaging products in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors
in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010
(Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is limited overlap between the parties in the supply of
plastic packaging products in Australia, as their respective
offerings serve different use cases
b. the parties each have a small Australian presence
c. there are alternative suppliers of the types of plastic
packaging products offered by the parties in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Laundy Woy Freehold Pty Ltd and Laundy Woy Trading Pty Ltd (together, **Laundy)** propose to acquire the freehold land and operating business known as the Woy Woy Hotel, located at 33–39 The Boulevarde, Woy Woy, NSW from Amal Trustees Pty Limited as trustee for Harvest Pub Fund Trust 2 and Harvest Pub Operations 2 Limited, pursuant to a contract dated 20 April 2026.

Laundy operates a portfolio of licensed hospitality venues across New South Wales, including pubs and hotels located in metropolitan and regional areas.

The Woy Woy Hotel is an existing licensed hotel business comprising gaming, food and beverage, bottle shop and hospitality services.

The acquisition involves the transfer of ownership of both the freehold land and the operating hotel business to Laundy. Following completion, Laundy will operate the Woy Woy Hotel as part of its existing hospitality portfolio.

determination_table_content.details:
Laundy Woy Freehold Pty Ltd and Laundy Woy Trading Pty Ltd
(together, Laundy) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its
proposed acquisition of the freehold land and operating business
known as the Woy Woy Hotel, located at 33–39 The Boulevarde, Woy
Woy NSW, from Amal Trustees Pty Limited as trustee for Harvest
Pub Fund Trust 2 and Harvest Pub Operations 2 Limited, as
described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(b) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) not to make the notification waiver
determination applied for.
4 June 2026

The acquirer, Laundy, operates a portfolio of licensed hospitality
venues across New South Wales, including pubs and hotels located
in metropolitan and regional areas. Laundy currently owns and
operates the Bayview Hotel located at 2/16, The Boulevard, Woy
Woy, NSW.
The Woy Woy Hotel is an existing licensed hotel business
comprising gaming, food and beverage, bottle shop and hospitality
services. It is located at 33-39 The Boulevard, Woy Woy, NSW.
The Acquisition involves the transfer of ownership of both the
freehold land and the operating hotel business to Laundy.
Woy Woy is a coastal town in the Central Coast region of the New
South Wales state of Australia. Woy Woy has two pubs, the Woy Woy
Hotel and the Bayview Hotel, and the nearest pub outside of Woy
Woy is in Umina.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the
information provided with the notification waiver application and had
regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Act.
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers
that the Acquisition may raise competition issues. In particular:
a. there is significant horizontal overlap between Laundy
and the Woy Woy Hotel in the supply of on-premises
hospitality products and services (including liquor for
on-site consumption, dining and gaming) in the Woy Woy
area and the acquisition would result in Laundy owning
both pubs in Woy Woy.
b. there is uncertainty regarding the level of competitive
constraint likely to be exerted by alternative suppliers of
on-premises hospitality products and services (including
liquor for on-site consumption, dining and gaming) in
Woy Woy and the surrounding area including from the
Country Club and RSL Club in Woy Woy and the Ocean
Beach Hotel in Umina.
The ACCC considers that these potential competition issues warrant
consideration through the notification process, including further
information gathering and consultation with third parties.
Given the above, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to reach a
concluded view on the likelihood of the Acquisition meeting the
notification thresholds. The ACCC notes that Laundy has
acknowledged that the proposed acquisition may meet the
notification thresholds.
For these reasons, the ACCC has determined not to make the
notification waiver determination applied for.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
McCarroll’s of Moss Vale Pty Ltd (**MMV**) proposes to acquire substantially all of the assets of 2 separate and independently owned motor vehicle dealership businesses, Country Motor Company Pty Ltd (**Country Motor Company**) and Kinghorn Motors Pty Ltd (**Kinghorn Motors**), via a special purpose vehicle, MAG South Coast Pty Ltd (**MAG South Coast**). As part of the transaction, MMV, via McCarroll’s Property Holdings Pty Ltd, also proposes to acquire the land on which Country Motor Company and Kinghorn Motors respectively operate.

MMV is a new and used vehicle retail business operating 15 franchised dealerships across locations in the Sydney, North Shore, Hornsby, and Newcastle areas of New South Wales (**NSW**). It is the authorised dealer of 15 original equipment manufacturer brands, including Alfa Romeo, BYD, GWM, Ferrari, Maserati, and Volkswagen, and also supplies parts, servicing, and finance.

Country Motor Company operates three franchised dealerships in the Nowra region on the NSW South Coast, representing Hyundai, Isuzu, and GWM. Kinghorn Motors operates two franchised dealerships in the Nowra region, representing Ford and Volkswagen. Both businesses also supply parts, servicing, and finance.

The parties all supply new and used cars, parts, servicing, and financing solutions. However, MMV does not presently operate any franchised dealerships in the Nowra area and is not an authorised dealer for Ford, Hyundai, or Isuzu.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Woodward pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
McCarroll’s of Moss Vale Pty Ltd (MMV) applied for a notification
waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of:
• 2 separate and independently owned motor vehicle dealership
businesses, Country Motor Company Pty Ltd (Country Motor
Company) and Kinghorn Motors Pty Ltd (Kinghorn Motors)
(together, the Targets), via a special purpose vehicle, MAG South
Coast Pty Ltd (MAG South Coast), and
• the land on which the Country Motor Company and Kinghorn
Motors businesses are operated, via McCarroll Property
Holdings Pty Ltd,
as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(b) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) not to make the notification waiver
determination applied for.

The acquirer, MMV, is a new and used vehicle retail business
operating 15 franchised dealerships across locations in the Sydney,
North Shore, Hornsby, and Newcastle areas of New South Wales
(NSW). It is the authorised dealer of 15 original equipment
manufacturer (OEM) brands. It also supplies ancillary services
including parts, servicing, and finance.
The Targets, Country Motor Company and Kinghorn Motors, are
independently owned and operated, franchised dealerships located
in the Nowra region on the South Coast of NSW which supply new
and used cars, aftermarket parts, car servicing, and finance. Country
Motor Company operates three dealerships (representing Hyundai,
Isuzu, and GWM), and Kinghorn Motors operates two dealerships
(representing Ford and Volkswagen).

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application, and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, including the
parties’ revenues in Australia, the ACCC considers that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds would be
met.
The ACCC is unable to reach a view that the Acquisition, which
involves multiple independently operated dealerships, would not be
likely to raise competition issues in any market based on the
information provided. In particular:
a. the Acquisition would combine the separately owned
second- and third- largest independent dealerships and
reduce the number dealerships of new and used cars in
the Nowra region of NSW
b. the merged entity’s combined share of supply of new
and used cars in the Nowra region of NSW may be
significant
c. there is uncertainty regarding the extent to which the
merged entity would be likely to face intra-brand and
inter-brand competition from alternative dealerships in
the Nowra region, the surrounding areas, or in NSW
more broadly.
The ACCC considers that the Acquisition warrants consideration
through the notification process, including via consultation with third
parties.
Given the above, the ACCC has determined not to make the
notification waiver determination applied for.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_name: AXDI - minority interest in La Trobe Financial
mergers.merger_description:
AXDI SPV 5 Ltd (**AXDI**) proposes to acquire a minority interest in AMC AusCo 1 Ltd (the **Target**) from BCP AMC Aus LP (by its general partner Brookfield Capital Partners VI GP LLC, by its sole member Brookfield Capital Partners VI Officer GP LLC) (**Brookfield**), by way of a share sale agreement.

AXDI is a newly incorporated holding entity managed by Axight Capital Limited (**Axight**), that was established for the sole purpose of acquiring an interest in the issued share capital of the Target pursuant to the acquisition.

The Target is the holding company of La Trobe Financial, an Australian alternative asset manager with over A$22 billion in assets under management, specialising in finance and credit investment solutions. La Trobe Financial operates across the following aspects of the wealth value chain:

* investment management and product manufacturing (managers investing client capital and manufacturers creating products to be acquired),
* platforms (technology platforms that allow advisers to manage clients' portfolios), and
* distribution (professional financial advisers who provide tailored advice to individuals and execute investment decisions).

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
AXDI SPV 5 Ltd (AXDI) applied for a notification waiver in respect of
its proposed acquisition of a minority interest in AMC AusCo 1 Ltd
(the holding company of La Trobe Financial) (the Target) from BCP
AMC Aus LP (by its general partner Brookfield Capital Partners VI GP
LLC, by its sole member Brookfield Capital Partners VI Officer GP
LLC) (Brookfield), as described in the transaction documents
provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, AXDI, is a newly incorporated holding entity managed
by Axight Capital Limited (Axight), established for the sole purpose
of acquiring a minority interest in the issued share capital of the
Target. Axight is an Abu Dhabi-based private equity-focused
investment manager with over US$5 billion in assets under
management and a focus on investing in developed and developing
markets across Asia Pacific.
The Target is the holding company of La Trobe Financial, an
Australian alternative asset manager with over A$22 billion in assets
under management, specialising in finance and credit investment
solutions. La Trobe Financial’s core offering is Australian Real Estate
Private Credit, being the provision of residential and commercial
loans originated through independent mortgage broker channels in
Australia. It also offers:
a. an ASX-listed Private Credit Fund, providing investors with
monthly income and daily liquidity from diversified
Australian real estate private credit and US mid-market
corporate credit, and
b. a US Private Credit Fund, providing both wholesale and retail
Australian investors with access to a curated and unique set
of investment products.
The vendor, Brookfield, operates in the private equity industry,
focusing on acquiring high-quality, cash-generative businesses in
various sectors.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver, and had regard
to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular, there is no horizontal overlap or vertical
relationship between the target or La Trobe Financial and AXDI (or
their portfolio companies) in the supply of residential and
commercial loan or alternative asset management services in
Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

determination_table_content.details:
MedHealth Pty Limited (MedHealth) applied for a notification waiver
in respect of its proposed acquisition of the business and assets of
Connect Allied Health Pty Ltd (Connect), as described in the
transaction documents provided as part of the application (the
Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, MedHealth is an Australian subsidiary of the
ExamWorks Group (ExamWorks). ExamWorks is a United States-
based independent provider of services including independent
medical examinations, peer reviews, bill reviews, Medicare
compliance, record retrieval, document management and related
services.
ExamWorks operates in Australia through MedHealth, which
provides a range of services to a variety of businesses and
consumers across the private and public sectors.
MedHealth’s Allied Health Services Division provides allied health
services to a range of patients including NDIS participants, private
health insurance clients and customers from other funding sources
(for example, Department of Veterans’ Affairs, Medicare, My Aged
Care and workers compensation) across Australia through a number
of brands. Its brands include Ability Action Australia and Living to the
Max which operate in South Australia.
In South Australia, MedHealth provides occupational therapy, speech
pathology, exercise physiology, and physiotherapy allied health
services.
The target, Connect, was founded in 2016 and is a multi-disciplinary
AHS provider operating in South Australia. Connect provides clinic
and community-based speech therapy, occupational therapy,
physiotherapy, positive behavioural support, psychology and podiatry
services across Adelaide and the Riverland.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. the parties’ combined share in the supply of allied health
services in South Australia is low and the increment arising
from the Acquisition would be small
b. there are numerous alternative suppliers of various allied
health services to customers in Adelaide and regions in
South Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

anzsic_codes.name: Other Agriculture and Fishing Support Services

mergers.merger_description:
Alta Genetics Australia Pty Ltd (**Alta Genetics**) is an Australian subsidiary of the URUS Group. The URUS Group is a global company active in the agricultural sector, specifically providing products and services in bovine genetics and reproductive technology.

Valley Agricultural Software, Inc. (**VAS**) is a subsidiary of the URUS Group. VAS supplies software and information solutions targeted specifically to dairy herd management for intensive dairy operations. The URUS Group, and by extension its subsidiaries, are indirectly controlled by funds advised or managed by affiliates (the **CVC Funds**) of CVC Capital Partners plc (**CVC Capital Partners**), a public limited company incorporated in Jersey and listed in Amsterdam. The CVC Network is a global alternative investment manager focused on private equity, credit, infrastructure and secondaries, and includes CVC Capital Partners, its subsidiaries, and the CVC Funds.

AgriWebb Pty Ltd (**AgriWebb**) is an Australian provider of livestock and farming management software. AgriWebb's software management solutions are designed to assist farmers in managing their day-to-day operational business with farm record keeping, farm mapping, livestock management, pasture and grazing management, task and workflow management, and farm auditing and compliance.

The parties are both active in the supply of livestock management software. VAS's software is designed to support dairy herd management for intensive dairy operations, whereas AgriWebb's software is directed towards grazing livestock (i.e. beef cattle and sheep) and farming management.

The Acquisition concerns the acquisition of 100% of the shares in AgriWebb by the URUS Group (via Alta Genetics).

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Alta Genetics Australia Pty Ltd (Alta Genetics), an Australian
subsidiary of the URUS Group which is indirectly controlled by funds
advised or managed by affiliates of CVC Capital Partners plc (CVC
Capital Partners), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its
proposed acquisition of 100% of the shares in AgriWebb Pty Ltd
(AgriWebb), as described in the transaction documents provided as
part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, Alta Genetics, is an Australian subsidiary of the URUS
Group. The URUS Group is a global company active in the agricultural
sector, specifically providing products and services in bovine
genetics and reproductive technology. Another subsidiary of the
URUS Group, Valley Agricultural Software, Inc. (VAS), supplies
software and information solutions targeted to dairy herd
management for intensive dairy operations.
The URUS Group, and by extension its subsidiaries, are indirectly
controlled by funds advised or managed by affiliates of CVC Capital
Partners (together, the CVC Funds), a public limited company
incorporated in Jersey and listed in Amsterdam. The CVC Network is
a global alternative investment manager focused on private equity,
credit, infrastructure, and secondaries, and includes CVC Capital
Partners, each of its subsidiaries, and the CVC Funds.
The target, AgriWebb, is an Australian provider of livestock and
farming management software. AgriWebb’s software management
solutions are designed to assist farmers in managing their day-to-
day operational business with farm record keeping, farm mapping,
livestock management, pasture and grazing management, task and
workflow management, and farm auditing and compliance.
The parties are both active in the supply of livestock management
software in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors
in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010
(Cth) (the Act).
• Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is limited overlap between the product offerings of the
parties, as their respective offerings primarily serve different
use cases
b. there are alternative providers of the types of livestock
management software offered by the parties, both globally
and in Australia
c. VAS has a small Australian presence, and any increment in
share of supply arising from the Acquisition is estimated to
be small.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_name: Tinicum and Blackstone led consortium – Senior
mergers.merger_description:
BMA IX LLC is a wholly owned Delaware subsidiary of Blackstone, Inc. (together with its subsidiaries, **Blackstone**). Zeus UK Bidco Limited (Bidco),a newly formed company which will be indirectly jointly controlled by funds managed and/or advised by Tinicum Incorporated (**Tinicum**) and by funds advised and/or managed by affiliates of Blackstone (together, the **Consortium**), propose to acquire 100% of the shares in Senior plc (together with its subsidiaries, **Senior**), by way of a Court approved scheme of arrangement (Scheme) under Part 26 of the UK Companies Act 2006 (the **Acquisition**). Following the Acquisition, Tinicum and Blackstone will have a controlling interest in Senior.

Tinicum is a US-based private investment firm, focused on investing in both private and public companies across various sectors, including engineered products, industrial distribution and services, industrial software and technology, and specialty infrastructure. Funds managed and/or advised by Tinicum control two portfolio companies active in the aerospace fluid conveyance sector, PennAero and AeroFlow.

Blackstone is a US-headquartered global alternative asset manager. Blackstone has a broad portfolio of investments across multiple sectors. As part of the structure of the Acquisition, Blackstone will acquire joint control of Tinicum subsidiary, AeroFlow.

Senior is a UK publicly listed company that designs and manufactures high-technology components and systems for principal original equipment manufacturers in the worldwide aerospace and defence, land vehicle, power and energy, and industrials markets.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
BMA IX LLC is a wholly owned subsidiary of Blackstone, Inc.
(together with its subsidiaries, Blackstone). Zeus UK Bidco Limited
(Zeus) proposes to acquire 100% of the shares in Senior plc (Senior),
by way of a Court approved scheme of arrangement under Part 26 of
the UK Companies Act 2006, as described in the transaction
documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
Zeus is indirectly jointly controlled by funds managed and/or advised
by Tinicum Incorporated (Tinicum) and by funds advised and/or
managed by affiliates of Blackstone.
Following the Acquisition, Tinicum and Blackstone will have a
controlling interest in Senior. The Acquisition will also provide
Blackstone joint control together with Tinicum over AeroFlow
Technologies, L.L.C. (AeroFlow).
The application for a notification waiver in respect of the Acquisition
was jointly made by:
• Zeus
• Tinicum
• BMA IX LLC.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
1 June 2026

Tinicum is a US-based private investment firm, focused on investing
in both private and public companies across sectors including
engineered products, industrial distribution and services, industrial
software and technology, and specialty infrastructure. Tinicum has
an existing ~2.36% interest in Senior. Funds managed and/or
advised by Tinicum also control PennAero and AeroFlow, which are
active in the aerospace fluid conveyance sector. As a preparatory
step to the Acquisition, AeroFlow will be transferred to Zeus as a
capital contribution for the Acquisition through an intra-group
reorganisation.
Blackstone is a US-based investment management firm.
The Acquisition also includes a non-controlling minority co-
investment from Cliffwater. Cliffwater is an independent alternative
investment advisor and fund manager. Its investment in Zeus is a
passive investment with no control rights.
The target, Senior, is a UK publicly listed company that designs and
manufactures high-technology components and systems for
principal original equipment manufacturers in the worldwide
aerospace and defence, land vehicle, power and energy, and
industrials industries.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is
unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In
particular:
a. there is no horizontal overlap and limited vertical
relationships between the parties in Australia
b. there is a low risk of foreclosure or other concerning vertical
or conglomerate effects resulting from exclusionary
conduct, bundling or tying of aerospace components post-
acquisition
c. AeroFlow and Senior have a small presence in Australia.
In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to
reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification
thresholds being met.
Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the
ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

anzsic_codes.name: Engineering Design and Engineering Consulting Services

mergers.merger_description:
WSP Global Inc. (**WSP**) is a global engineering consulting services firm, which is listed on the Toronto Stock Exchange. In Australia, WSP operates through its wholly owned subsidiary, WSP Australia Pty Limited, and provides engineering services to customers across various industries (except where otherwise indicated, WSP and WSP Australia Pty Ltd are collectively referred to as WSP).

TRC Companies, Inc. (**TRC**) (referred to as the Target, together with its related subsidiaries forming the TRC group) is a US-based privately held engineering consulting and construction services firm which predominantly services clients in the US who own or operate large-scale infrastructure and land assets. TRC has no subsidiary, employees or other physical presence in Australia and has immaterial supplies to Australian customers from its offshore operations.

WSP has entered into an agreement to indirectly acquire 100% of the shares of TRC.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Chair Cass-Gottlieb pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
WSP Global Inc. (WSP) applied for a notification waiver in respect of
its proposed acquisition of 100% of the share capital in TRC
Companies, Inc. (TRC), as described in the transaction documents
provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
12 January 2026

The acquirer, WSP, is a global engineering consulting services firm
which provides services related to planning, engineering, quality
assurance and implementation of large engineering projects in the
construction, energy, transport, infrastructure, building, and natural
resource sectors in Australia.
The target, TRC, is an engineering consulting and construction
management firm that predominantly works with clients in the US
who own or operate large-scale infrastructure and land assets,
including energy providers, utility providers, transportation agencies,
government bodies, and industrial and commercial enterprises. TRC
provides limited services to customers outside of the US.
The parties both supply engineering consulting services, including for
the energy, transport and infrastructure sectors.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the
information provided with the notification waiver application and had
regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act), being:
(i) the object of the Act,
(ii) the interests of consumers,
(iii) the likelihood that, if the acquisition were put into effect,
the notification thresholds in section 51ABP(1) of the Act
would apply, and
(iv) the likelihood that the acquisition, if put into effect, would
in all the circumstances have the effect, or be likely to
have the effect, of substantially lessening competition in
any market.
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. There is very limited horizontal overlap between WSP and
TRC in the supply of goods or services in Australia. The
information provided by WSP in its application indicates that
TRC currently has no presence in Australia and generates
negligible revenue from offshore sales to customers in
Australia.
b. The parties’ combined market share in the broader supply of
engineering consulting services in Australia is very low and
any aggregation in more specialised service offerings would
be minimal.
c. The parties are likely to continue to be constrained by
alternative global or national suppliers of engineering
consulting services in Australia, including design and project
management services in the energy, environment, transport
and infrastructure sectors.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to
be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects
more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification
waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

events.url_gh: /mergers/WA-35001/WSP-TRC (WA-35001) - Notification waiver determination - Acquisition not required to be notified - 12 January 2026.pdf

comments.commentary:
WSP's global acquisition of TRC Companies was the one of the first two acquisitions to be granted a notification waiver by the ACCC. Both parties supply engineering consulting services, including for energy, transport and infrastructure sectors. The ACCC determined the acquisition did not require notification because:

- there is very limited horizontal overlap in Australia, as TRC has no Australian presence and generates negligible revenue from Australian customers;
- the parties' combined market share in engineering consulting services in Australia is very low; and
- alternative global and national suppliers remain.

While notification thresholds were likely met, the ACCC waived the requirement given its view that there were minimal competition concerns.

mergers.merger_description:
IFS North America Inc. (**IFS North America**), via its wholly owned subsidiary incorporated for the purposes of the transaction, Fjord Merger Sub, Inc. (**Merger Sub**), proposes to acquire 100% of the issued and outstanding shares of Softeon, Inc. (**Softeon**) through a reverse triangular merger where:

(a) Merger Sub will merge with and into Softeon; and

(b) Merger Sub will then cease, and Softeon will be the surviving company.

As a result, Softeon will become a wholly owned subsidiary of IFS North America.

IFS North America is owned by Industrial and Financial Systems Aktiebolag, which is headquartered in Linköping, Sweden (together, IFS). IFS develops and commercialises business software for customers that manufacture and distribute goods, maintain assets and manage services-focused operations. Its technology enables manufacturing businesses to maintain complex assets and manage service-focused operations to enhance productivity, efficiency and sustainability.

Softeon is headquartered in Reston, VA. and supplies a supply chain fulfilment software platform for warehouses. Softeon operates in the warehouse management systems segment and provides a suite of supply chain software solutions on a single, integrated services-based technology platform that offers the following cloud-based software capabilities: warehouse management system, warehouse execution system and distributed order management solutions.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
IFS North America Inc. (IFS North America) applied for a notification
waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition, via its subsidiary Fjord
Merger Sub Inc (Merger Sub), of 100% of the share capital in Softeon
Inc. (Softeon), as described in the transaction documents provided
as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
27 January 2026

The acquirer, IFS North America, develops and commercialises
business software for customers that manufacture and distribute
goods, maintain assets and manage services-focused operations.
The software includes functionalities that use artificial intelligence to
support productivity, efficiency, and sustainable objectives.
IFS North America is owned by Industrial and Financial Systems
Aktiebolag, which is headquartered in Linköping, Sweden (together,
IFS). IFS is jointly controlled by EQT IX, an investment fund managed
by EQT Fund Management S.à r.l. (a global investment organisation),
and by Hg Pooled Management Limited, an investor in software and
services businesses, with a focus on Europe and the United States.
The target, Softeon, is a global software provider of warehouse
management systems and innovative supply chain solutions. Softeon
is headquartered in Reston, Virginia, USA and has a very limited
presence in Australia.
Both parties supply enterprise application software, however IFS’s
Australian business mainly supplies enterprise resource planning
services, whereas Softeon specialises in warehouse management
and supply chain management solutions. These services are largely
complementary.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the
information provided with the notification waiver application and had
regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. Softeon currently has a very small presence in Australia.
b. There is a small horizontal overlap between IFS and Softeon
in the supply of enterprise application software in Australia,
and no overlap in the supply of enterprise resource planning
and supply chain management services.
c. The parties’ combined shares in the supply of enterprise
application software in Australia are small, and they are likely
to continue to be constrained by several competitors.
d. There is a low risk of concerning conglomerate effects
resulting from bundling or tying of products post-merger.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to
be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects
more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification
waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
