mergers.merger_description:
Clifford Hallam Healthcare Pty Limited, a wholly-owned subsidiary of Paragon Care Limited (**Paragon Care**), proposes to acquire the Presidental business (**Presidental**) through an Asset Purchase Agreement from Presidental Pty Limited and Rockpac South Pacific Pty Ltd.

Paragon Care is a publicly traded company listed on the Australian Stock Exchange. It supplies pharmaceuticals, medical equipment and devices as well as medical and specialist dental consumables to the healthcare market in Australia, New Zealand and Asia. Paragon Care established its dedicated dental consumables business in May 2025.

Presidental is an Australian-owned business focusing on the supply of dental equipment and maintenance and repair services for dental equipment to dental clinics and surgeries in New South Wales, Brisbane and parts of regional Queensland, Victoria and Tasmania. Presidental also supplies a small volume of dental consumables associated with the dental equipment it supplies, as well as some steriliser machines and associated consumables to medical practices.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Clifford Hallam Healthcare Pty Limited, a wholly owned subsidiary of
Paragon Care Limited (Paragon Care), applied for a notification
waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of the assets of
Presidental Pty Limited and Rockpac South Pacific Pty Ltd
(Presidental), as described in the transaction documents provided
as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
16 February 2026

Paragon Care is a publicly traded company listed on the Australian
Stock Exchange. It supplies pharmaceuticals, medical equipment
and devices as well as medical and specialist dental consumables to
the healthcare market in Australia, New Zealand and Asia. The
acquirer, Clifford Hallam Healthcare Pty Limited, is a wholly owned
subsidiary of Paragon Care.
The target, Presidental, is an Australian-owned business focusing on
the supply of dental equipment and maintenance and repair services
for dental equipment to dental clinics and surgeries in New South
Wales, Queensland, Victoria and Tasmania. Presidental also supplies
some dental consumables associated with the dental equipment it
supplies, as well as some steriliser machines and associated
consumables to medical practices.
The parties both supply dental consumables to dental surgeries and
clinics. The application states that dental consumables cover a
broad range of products that are typically used on a single use or
limited use basis in the course of delivering dental treatment and
maintaining clinical operations, such as face masks, fillings and
dental instruments.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the
information provided with the notification waiver application and had
regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act).
Based on the information provided by the applicant, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. The horizontal overlap between the parties in the supply of
dental consumables is small.
• b. The parties’ combined share in the supply of dental
consumables is low.
c. There are a number of alternative suppliers of dental
consumables in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_name: Aluminium Specialties Group – Manufacturing facility site in Orchard Hills, NSW
mergers.merger_description:
Aluminium Specialties Group Pty Ltd (**Aluminium Specialities Group**) has entered into agreements for lease of manufacturing premises owned by Atilol Holdings Pty Ltd (**Atilol**), located on two lots in the same parcel of land in the Alspec Industrial Business Park in 221–227 Luddenham Road, Orchard Hills NSW.

Aluminium Specialities Group manufactures and extrudes aluminium systems supplies such as door and window frames through a related entity known as Alspec.

Atilol owns and leases non-residential property. Atilol is constructing the manufacturing premises in consultation with Aluminium Specialities Group.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Aluminium Specialties Group Pty Ltd (Aluminium Specialities Group)
applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed
agreements for lease with Atilol Holdings Pty Ltd (Atilol) for Lots 1
and 2 of Alspec Industrial Business Park located at 221–227
Luddenham Road, Orchard Hills, NSW (the Property), as described in
the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the
Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
11 March 2026

The acquirer, Aluminium Specialities Group, manufactures and
extrudes aluminium systems supplies such as door and window
frames through a related entity known as Alspec.
Atilol owns and leases non-residential property. Atilol is constructing
the manufacturing premises on the Property in consultation with
Aluminium Specialities Group and will then lease the Property to
Aluminium Specialities Group.
The Property will be used by Aluminium Specialities Group and
would be an input to their operations in manufacturing and
distributing aluminium systems.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided by the applicant and certain publicly
available information, and had regard to the factors in section
51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the
Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is no horizontal competitive overlap
b. there appear to be alternative sites available for Aluminium
Specialities Group’s competitors
c. the Acquisition does not appear likely to otherwise limit or
prevent competition from rivals.
In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to
reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification
thresholds being met.
Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the
ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Henkel AG & Co. KGaA, through its subsidiary Henkel Global Supply Chain B.V. (**Henkel**), proposes to acquire all the shares in and sole control of Stahl Parent B.V. (together with subsidiaries, **Stahl**) from Stahl Group S.A., which is indirectly controlled by affiliates of Wendel S.E. (**Wendel**).

Wendel is headquartered in Paris, France, and is a private assets-focused investment firm listed at the Euronext Paris stock exchange.

Henkel is a German public company listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange, headquartered in Düsseldorf. It is active worldwide through its Adhesive Technologies business and Consumer Brands business.

Henkel’s Adhesive Technologies business is a producer of adhesives, sealants and functional coatings for industrial customers, consumers and craftsmen. Its Adhesive Technologies brands available in Australia include Loctite, Teroson, Bonderite, Clingtape, Technomelt, Aquence and Pritt. Henkel’s Consumer Brands business offers a diverse portfolio of consumer brands with a focus on Laundry & Home Care and Hair Care. Its consumer brands include Persil, Schwarzkopf, Perwoll, Syoss, Somat and Bref.

Stahl is a manufacturer of specialty coatings for flexible materials headquartered in the Netherlands. With subsidiaries in Europe, Asia, and the Americas, the company is active worldwide. Its portfolio comprises leather finishing coatings, performance coatings, and packaging coatings, serving customers mainly in mobility (automotive and aviation interiors), packaging, fashion & lifestyle, and home furnishing industries.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Henkel Global Supply Chain B.V. (Henkel), which is a subsidiary of
Henkel AG & Co. KGaA, applied for a notification waiver in respect of
its proposed acquisition of all of the share capital in Stahl Parent
B.V. (together with its subsidiaries, Stahl), as described in the
transaction documents provided as part of the application (the
Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
7 April 2026

The acquirer, Henkel, is a German public company listed on the
Frankfurt Stock Exchange, headquartered in Düsseldorf. It is active
worldwide through its Adhesive Technologies business and
Consumer Brands business. Henkel’s Adhesive Technologies
business is a producer of adhesives, sealants and functional
coatings for industrial customers, consumers and craftsmen. Its
Adhesive Technologies brands available in Australia include Loctite,
Teroson, Bonderite, Clingtape, Technomelt, Aquence and Pritt.
Henkel’s Consumer Brands business offers a portfolio of consumer
brands with a focus on Laundry & Home Care and Hair Care. Its
consumer brands include Persil, Schwarzkopf, Perwoll, Syoss, Somat
and Bref.
The target, Stahl, is a manufacturer of specialty coatings for flexible
materials headquartered in the Netherlands. With subsidiaries in
Europe, Asia, and the Americas, the company is active worldwide. Its
portfolio comprises leather finishing coatings, performance coatings,
and packaging coatings, serving customers mainly in mobility
(automotive and aviation interiors), packaging, fashion & lifestyle,
and home furnishing.
In Australia, the parties both supply barrier (interior) coatings for
paper packaging and laminating adhesives for flexible packaging to
industrial customers, which are subsets of packaging coatings and
packaging adhesives respectively.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
• there is a small horizontal overlap between the parties in the
supply of packaging coatings and packaging adhesives
• the parties combined market shares in the supply of packaging
coatings and packaging adhesives are estimated to be low
• there are alternative providers of packaging coatings and
packaging adhesives in Australia, including in the supply of the
subsets of these products in which the parties overlap
• there appears to be a low risk of foreclosure or other concerning
vertical or conglomerate effects resulting from exclusionary
conduct, bundling or tying of products post-acquisition.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
On 5 March 2026, Advanced Navigation Pty. Ltd (**AdNav**), several existing shareholders of AdNav, and several new investors, entered into a sale and subscription deed (**SSD**). Under the SSD, Airtree Ventures Opportunity Fund V Partnership and its affiliates, National Reconstruction Fund Corporation, Australian Retirement Trust, Franklin Park Venture Capital Opportunity Fund II, L.P., LGPE SEC S.C.Sp, Crown Growth Opportunities IV SCSp-RAIF, Crown Asia-Pacific Private Equity V Master SCSp, Crown Asia-Pacific Private Equity VI Alt S.C.Sp., and Harvard Private Capital Realty Inc (collectively, the **New Investors**) along with a variety of other investors will directly or indirectly (through a separate nominee vehicle or trust) acquire shares of AdNav and enter into a shareholders agreement (the **Acquisition**).

AdNav develops, manufacturers and offers AI powered navigation solutions, primarily for defence primes.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Advanced Navigation Pty. Ltd (AdNav), certain shareholders of
AdNav, and certain new investors, entered into a sale and
subscription deed (SSD). Under the SSD, Airtree Ventures
Opportunity Fund V Partnership and its affiliates (Airtree), National
Reconstruction Fund Corporation (NRF), a number of co-investors,
along with a variety of other investors propose to directly or
indirectly (through a separate nominee vehicle or trust) acquire
shares of AdNav and enter into a shareholders agreement (the
Acquisition).
Airtree applied for a notification waiver on behalf of the co-investors
and in respect of the co-investors’ acquisition of shares of AdNav.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

Airtree is a limited partnership that is managed by Airtree Ventures
Fund V Manager Pty Ltd, an entity which forms part of Airtree
Ventures, a venture capital firm based in Sydney, Australia. Airtree
Ventures primarily focuses on early-stage investments in technology
startups across Australia and New Zealand.
The NRF is an Australian Government specialist investment vehicle,
established in 2023 under the National Reconstruction Fund
Corporation Act 2023. The NRF’s investment function is to provide
finance in the form of debt, equity and guarantees for projects in 7
priority areas of the Australian economy.
The following parties are acquiring shares in AdNav as part of the
Acquisition alongside Airtree and NRF (referred to as co-investors).
• Australian Retirement Trust is one of Australia’s largest
superannuation funds, managing in the order of $350 billion
of retirement savings for more than 2.4 million members and
has a diverse global portfolio.
• Franklin Park Venture Capital Opportunity Fund II, L.P is a
venture capital fund managed by Franklin Park, which is a US
based investment manager and adviser that specialises in
private equity, private debt and venture capital, primarily
through fund‑of‑funds, co‑investment vehicles and bespoke
advisory mandates.
• LGPE SEC S.C.Sp, Crown Growth Opportunities IV SCSp-
RAIF, Crown Asia-Pacific Private Equity V Master SCSp and
Crown Asia-Pacific Private Equity VI Alt S.C.Sp are global
investment firms and are part of the LGT Capital Partners
Group. LGT Capital Partners are involved in managing capital
on behalf of institutional investors, sovereign entities,
insurers, endowments and foundations.
• Harvard Private Capital Realty, Inc is an investment entity
associated with Harvard Management Company, a nonprofit,
wholly owned subsidiary of Harvard University. Its activities
are limited to managing and investing endowment assets
across a diversified portfolio of asset classes.
The target, AdNav, develops, manufacturers and offers AI powered
navigation solutions, primarily for defence primes.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. Airtree, NRF and the co-investors will have a relatively small
interest in AdNav post-Acquisition
b. there is no horizontal overlap, and limited potential vertical
relationships, between the co-investors and their connected
entities and AdNav in the supply of AI powered navigation
software in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Signant Health Holding Corp (**Signant**) proposes to acquire 100% of the shares in ActiGraph Holdings, LLC (together with its subsidiaries, **ActiGraph**).

Signant is a US-based evidence generation company that provides services used in clinical trials. Signant does not have a physical presence in Australia but provides electronic clinical outcome assessments (**eCOA**), clinical trial randomisation and supply management (**RTSM**) services, and supplementary clinical trial professional services. Signant supplies these services to both contract research organisations (**CROs**) and directly to pharmaceutical and biotechnology companies.

Ametris (formerly ActiGraph) is a US-based provider of digital health technology (**DHT**) for clinical trials, specialising in wearables – in particular, wrist-worn wearables and other sensors – which track biovitals and other digital endpoints for patients participating in clinical trials. Ametris does not have a physical presence in Australia but supplies its DHT products in Australia for non-commercial academic research.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Signant Health Holding Corp (together with its subsidiaries, Signant)
proposes to acquire 100% of the shares in ActiGraph Holdings, LLC
(together with its subsidiaries, Ametris (formerly ActiGraph)), as
described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
16 April 2026

The acquirer, Signant, is a US-based evidence generation company
that provides services used in clinical trials. Signant does not have a
physical presence in Australia but provides electronic clinical
outcome assessments (eCOAs), a data collection service for clinical
trials, clinical trial randomisation and supply management (RTSM)
services, and supplementary clinical trial professional services.
Signant supplies these services to both contract research
organisations (CROs) and directly to pharmaceutical and
biotechnology companies (together referred to as Sponsors).
The target, Ametris, is a US-based provider of digital health
technology (DHT) for clinical trials. Ametris specialises in wearables
– in particular, wrist worn wearables and other sensors – which track
biovitals and other digital endpoints for patients participating in
clinical trials. Ametris does not have a physical presence in Australia
but supplies its DHT products in Australia for non-commercial
academic research.
Both Signant and Ametris supply services used to collect digital
endpoints in clinical trials.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors
in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010
(Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
• there is limited overlap between the parties in the supply of
digital endpoint collection services in Australia, as their
respective offerings primarily serve different use cases for
clinical trials
• the parties each have a small Australian presence
• there are alternative suppliers of the types of digital endpoint
collection services offered by the parties in Australia
• there appears to be a low risk of foreclosure or other
conglomerate effects resulting from exclusionary conduct,
or bundling or tying of services, post-acquisition.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
The Acquisition concerns the indirect acquisition of sole control of Nippon Sheet Glass Company, Limited (**Nippon**) by funds managed or advised by affiliates of Apollo Capital Management, L.P., which is a wholly owned indirect subsidiary of Apollo Global Management, Inc. (together with its indirect subsidiaries, **Apollo**). Apollo and the investment funds managed by affiliates of Apollo are together referred to as the Apollo Funds.

Apollo is a high-growth global alternative asset manager headquartered in New York City and incorporated in the US. Through its fully integrated investment platform spanning the full risk-reward spectrum from investment grade to private equity, Apollo offers investment management and financial planning services to individuals and institutional investors on a global basis. Examples of Apollo’s current investments include, inter alia, companies in education, insurance, financial services, and leisure businesses.

Nippon Sheet Glass is a global manufacturer and supplier of a wide range of glass products, headquartered in Tokyo. It operates across three global business units: architectural glass, automotive glass, and creative technology glass (or technical glass).

mergers.merger_name: Australian Venue Co – assets of White Cockatoo Hotel, Petersham
mergers.merger_description:
AVC Operations Pty Ltd (**AVC**) proposes to acquire substantially all of the assets of the licensed hotel trading as the White Cockatoo Hotel (including the exclusive right to carry on the business in succession of the vendor and the benefit of the lease) from Petersham Pub Pty Ltd (**Vendor**).

AVC is a subsidiary of Australian Venue Co. Holdings Limited which operates over 200 licensed venues throughout Australia and New Zealand, primarily pubs, as well as some bars, clubs and licensed restaurants. AVC's venues are individually branded and are mostly located in inner-city and metro areas, offering a range of products and services including food, alcoholic and non-alcoholic beverages, function rooms, live entertainment and gaming facilities.

The White Cockatoo Hotel is a contemporary licensed hotel located at 30 Terminus Street, Petersham NSW 2049. The venue is a typical Australian pub with a public bar, bistro and outdoor courtyard. The business assets of White Cockatoo Hotel are currently owned and operated by the Vendor as part of Reilly Group.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Australian Venue Co. Holdings Limited (Australian Venue Co), via
AVC Operations Pty Limited (AVC), applied for a notification waiver
in respect of its proposed acquisition of substantially all of the
assets of the licensed hotel trading as the White Cockatoo Hotel
(WCH) (including the exclusive right to carry on the business in
succession of the vendor and the benefit of the lease) from
Petersham Pub Pty Ltd, as described in the transaction documents
provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, AVC, is a wholly owned subsidiary of Australian Venue
Co. Australian Venue Co operates over 200 licensed venues
throughout Australia and New Zealand, primarily pubs, as well as
bars, clubs and licensed restaurants. Their venues are individually
branded and mostly located in inner-city and metro areas, offering
food, alcoholic and non-alcoholic beverages, function rooms, live
entertainment and gaming facilities.
The target, WCH, is a licensed hotel with no gaming facilities, located
at 30 Terminus Street, Petersham NSW 2049. WCH facilities include
a public bar, bistro and outdoor courtyard.
Australian Venue Co, through its venues (including Public House
Petersham), and WCH both supply retail hospitality services in the
local area surrounding WCH.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver and had regard
to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
• while there is horizontal overlap between Australian Venue
Co and WCH, the combined share of the parties in the supply
of retail hospitality services is low
• there are alternative suppliers of retail hospitality venues in
the local area surrounding WCH.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Francisco Partners Management, L.P. (**Francisco Partners**) is a global investment firm specialising in technology and technology-enabled businesses, headquartered in San Francisco, United States with offices in New York and London. Francisco Partners has several portfolio companies that operate across multiple different sectors.

Sermo Limited (**Sermo**) is a global medical social platform founded in the United States in 2005. Sermo enables healthcare professionals to share knowledge, obtain medical insights from peers on patient cases, access medical information and education, review and rate medicines, participate in anonymous peer consultations and collaborative crowdsourcing, and complete paid surveys to earn money. Sermo has no subsidiary, employees or other physical presence in Australia, but supplies to some Australian customers from its offshore operations.

A bidding vehicle, Sigrun Bidco Limited, controlled by funds advised by Francisco Partners, is proposing to acquire 83% of the shares in Sermo through a share purchase agreement, with the remainder rolling over to Sermo’s founder / CEO.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Sigrun Bidco Limited (Sigrun), a bidding vehicle controlled by funds
advised by Francisco Partners Management, L.P (Francisco
Partners), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed
acquisition of 83% of the shares in Sermo Limited (Sermo), with the
remainder rolling over to Sermo’s founder / CEO, as described in the
transaction document provided as part of the application (the
Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
20 February 2026

Francisco Partners is a global investment firm specialising in
technology and technology-enabled businesses, headquartered in
San Francisco, United States with offices in New York and London.
Sigrun is a bidding vehicle controlled by funds advised by Francisco
Partners.
Sermo is a global medical social platform founded in the United
States in 2005. Designed by doctors, Sermo enables healthcare
professionals to share knowledge, obtain medical insights from
peers on patient cases, access medical information and education,
review and rate medicines, participate in anonymous peer
consultations and collaborative crowdsourcing, and complete paid
surveys to earn money. Sermo has no subsidiary, employees or other
physical presence in Australia and has immaterial supplies to
Australian customers from its offshore operations.
One of Francisco Partners’ connected entities, Merative, operates in
the healthcare sector in Australia, including in data and analytics.
However, the applicant states that neither it nor its connected
entities offer social platform services to healthcare professionals in
Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the
information provided with the notification waiver application and
certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors
in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010
(Cth) (Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is no horizontal overlap between Francisco Partners
and Sermo in the supply of social networking or platform
services for healthcare professionals in Australia, and there
are no vertical relationships in this respect
b. while there may be a small horizontal or complementary
relationship between the healthcare market research, data
and analytics services provided by Sermo and Merative,
there are numerous alternative suppliers of these services in
Australia
c. Sermo has a small presence in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given the above consideration that material competition
concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the
Acquisition is not required to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_name: Allied Beef - Lease of Yarranbrook Feedlot
mergers.merger_description:
Allied Beef Group Pty Ltd (**Allied Beef**) proposes to lease the Yarranbrook Feedlot land (**Yarranbrook**) from Yarranbrook Property Pty Ltd (**Lessor**) pursuant to a lease agreement.

Yarranbrook is a beef cattle feedlot with associated grazing and cropping activities located at 188 Cremascos Road in Inglewood, Queensland.

Allied Beef is an Australian-based company that primarily owns and leases land for beef cattle farming and feedlot operations in Queensland and New South Wales. Allied Beef also supplies associated services including private cattle marketing, backgrounding and contract cattle breeding. Allied Beef is ultimately controlled by Laguna Bay Agricultural Fund No 1.

Laguna Bay Agricultural Fund No 1 is an Australian-based investment fund which specialises in investing in agricultural businesses in Australia.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Allied Beef Group Pty Ltd (Allied Beef) applied for a notification
waiver in respect of its proposed agreement to lease the
Yarranbrook Feedlot land (Yarranbrook) located at 188 Cremascos
Road, Inglewood, Queensland, as described in the transaction
documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
6 March 2026

The acquirer, Allied Beef, is an Australian-based company that
primarily owns and leases land for beef cattle farming and feedlot
operations in Queensland and New South Wales. Allied Beef also
supplies associated services including private cattle marketing,
backgrounding and contract cattle breeding. Allied Beef is ultimately
controlled by Laguna Bay Agricultural Fund No 1. Laguna Bay
Agricultural Fund No 1 is an Australian-based investment fund which
specialises in investing in agricultural businesses in Australia.
The target, Yarranbrook, is a beef cattle feedlot with associated
grazing and cropping activities located at 188 Cremascos Road in
Inglewood, Queensland.
Yarranbrook Property Pty Ltd will be the landlord of Yarranbrook and
will lease the feedlot to Allied Beef.
Both Allied Beef and Yarranbrook operate beef cattle feedlots with
associated grazing activities in Queensland. Allied Beef operates a
beef cattle feedlot located in Central Queensland while Yarranbrook
is located in Southern Queensland.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors
in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010
(Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. each of the feedlots operated by Allied Beef post-Acquisition
are likely to serve farmers over a wide area
b. there are several other operators of beef cattle feedlots in
Queensland and Northern NSW.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met based on the information in the application, given that
material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has
determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

Airtree Fund is a venture capital fund managed by Airtree. Airtree is a venture capital firm based in Australia which predominantly invests in early-stage and high-growth Australia and New Zealand-based technology companies. Funds managed by Airtree (including the Airtree Fund) hold minority investments in over a hundred companies in Australia, New Zealand and around the world.

mergers.merger_description:
Blackbird FOF25 Pty Ltd as trustee for Blackbird Ventures 2025 Follow-On Fund Trust (**Blackbird FOF25**) and Blackbird FSA Pty Ltd as trustee for Blackbird FSA Coinvestment Trust (**Blackbird FSA**) each propose to purchase shares in Halter USA Inc's (**Halter**) capital raise, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application.

Blackbird FOF25 and Blackbird FSA are venture capital funds managed by Blackbird Ventures. Blackbird Ventures is a venture capital firm based in Australia and New Zealand that invests in early-stage and high-growth technology companies. Funds managed by Blackbird Ventures hold minority investments in hundreds of companies in Australia, the United States, New Zealand and around the world.

Halter is an agricultural technology company which was founded in New Zealand and is now domiciled in the United States. Halter specialises in developing advanced livestock management and 'virtual fencing' systems for pasture-based farming.

Halter supplies collars to be worn by cattle, which enable remote monitoring, real time tracking and guidance of cattle, and the creation and management of virtual pasture boundaries. Halter's collar system is designed to improve productivity, enhance grazing efficiency, reduce labour and fencing costs, and support sustainable land use practices by providing data-driven insights into herd behaviour and pasture utilisation.

Blackbird FOF25 Pty Ltd as trustee for Blackbird Ventures 2025
Follow-On Fund Trust (Blackbird FOF25) and Blackbird FSA Pty Ltd
as trustee for Blackbird FSA Coinvestment Trust (Blackbird FSA)
applied for a notification waiver in respect of their proposed
acquisition of shares in Halter USA Inc.’s (Halter) capital raise, as
described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
1 April 2026

Blackbird Ventures is a venture capital firm based in Australia and
New Zealand that invests in early-stage and high-growth technology
companies. Blackbird Ventures' portfolio spans a wide range of
industries within the technology space.
Blackbird FOF25 and Blackbird FSA are venture capital funds
managed by Blackbird Ventures. Venture capital funds raise and
invest capital to produce financial returns for their investors. Funds
managed by Blackbird Ventures hold minority investments in
hundreds of companies in Australia, the United States, New Zealand
and around the world.
The target, Halter, is an agricultural technology company which was
founded in New Zealand and is now domiciled in the United States.
Halter specialises in developing advanced livestock management
and 'virtual fencing' systems for pasture-based farming. Halter
supplies collars to be worn by cattle, which enable remote
monitoring, real time tracking and guidance of cattle, and the
creation and management of virtual pasture boundaries.
There is no competitive overlap between Blackbird FoF25 or
Blackbird FSA and Halter, or between any Blackbird Ventures
portfolio companies and Halter, in the supply of livestock
management and 'virtual fencing' systems.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided by the applicant, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. There are no horizontal overlaps or vertical relationships
between Blackbird Ventures’ portfolio companies, Blackbird
FOF25 or Blackbird FSA and Halter.
b. There is no removal of a competitor as a result of the
Acquisition.
Given the above considerations that material competition concerns
are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is
not required to be notified.
In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to
reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification
thresholds being met.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Star Hotels Group is an independently owned and operated pub and liquor retail group, primarily operating pubs along the eastern seaboard of Queensland and four venues in South Australia. It currently also operates several pubs in western Queensland, including in Stanthorpe, Westbrook, and Dalby, as well as a retail liquor store in Toowoomba under the Star Liquor brand.    

  

Under the acquisition, Star Hotels Group will acquire both the freehold land and business assets of Fiveways Hotel Toowoomba, as well as two adjacent freehold residential lots.   

  

Fiveways Hotel Toowoomba is currently closed to the public but previously operated as a licensed bar with food and gaming, as well as a drive-through liquor store. The two adjoining freehold lots are currently rented to private individuals. Post-acquisition, Fiveways Hotel Toowoomba will be integrated into the Star Hotels Group portfolio under its existing name.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
SGL Hotels Pty Ltd and SCHF Pty Ltd (together, Star Hotels Group)
applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed
acquisition of the business assets and freehold land of Fiveways
Hotel Toowoomba, as well as two adjacent freehold residential lots,
from MPG Fiveways Prop Pty Ltd and MPG Fiveways Op Pty Ltd, as
described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
23 April 2026

The acquirer, Star Hotels Group, is an independently owned and
operated hospitality group, primarily operating pubs along the
eastern seaboard of Queensland and four venues in South Australia.
It also operates liquor retail outlets under brands including Star
Liquor, Sense of Taste, and Bob’s Bulk Booze, as well as
accommodation at certain venues. In the Toowoomba area, Star
Hotels Group operates Westbrook Tavern (a licensed pub with a
bistro and gaming facilities) and Star Liquor Ruthven Street (a retail
liquor outlet).
The target business, Fiveways Hotel Toowoomba, is currently closed
but previously operated a bar with food and gaming, as well as a
drive-through liquor store. Under the Acquisition, Star Hotels Group
will acquire the freehold land of the business, two adjacent freehold
residential lots, and the business assets (including fixtures, fittings,
and licences) of Fiveways Hotel Toowoomba.
Star Hotels Group and Fiveways Hotel Toowoomba overlap in the
supply of dining, gaming, and liquor services in the Toowoomba
area.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver, and had regard
to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is a small horizontal overlap between Star Hotels
Group and Fiveways Hotel Toowoomba in the supply of
dining, gaming and liquor for onsite and offsite consumption
in the Toowoomba area
b. the combined share of the parties in the supply of dining,
gaming, and liquor services in the Toowoomba area is low
c. there are alternative suppliers of dining, gaming, and liquor
services at the local and regional level.
In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to
reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification
thresholds being met.
Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the
ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_name: Searchlight Capital Partners - Additional interest in KORE Group Holdings Inc.

acquirers.name: ABRY Partners VII Co-Investment Fund, LP

mergers.merger_description:
On 27 February 2026, KONA Parent, L.P. (**KONA Parent**), Kona Merger Sub Co. (**KONA Merger Sub**), a wholly owned subsidiary of KONA Parent, and Kore Group Holdings, Inc. (**KORE**) entered into an Agreement and Plan of Merger pursuant to which KONA Merger Sub will merge with and into KORE with KORE surviving the merger as a wholly owned subsidiary of KONA Parent (the **Acquisition**). Each share of KORE common stock outstanding at the time of closing will convert to the right to receive $9.25 per share. The Acquisition is valued at an enterprise value of approximately USD$726 million, including debt. As a result of the Acquisition, funds managed by Searchlight Capital Partners (**Searchlight**) will hold an interest of approximately 68.7% in and indirectly acquire sole control of KORE , while funds managed by Abry Partners (**Abry**) will continue to hold a non-controlling minority interest of approximately 21.2%.

Searchlight, which manages Searchlight Capital IV LP, is an investment firm dedicated to identifying global investment opportunities in both private equity and debt, in sectors such as communications, media and financial services, operating in North America and Europe.

Abry, which manages ABRY Partners VII LP and ABRY Partners VII Co-Investment Fund LP, is an investment fund focused on middle market companies in the communications, media, information and business services industries across North America and Europe. Abry was founded in 1989 and is headquartered in Boston, Massachusetts (USA) with offices in Charlotte, North Carolina (USA) and London.

KORE is a provider of Internet-of-Things connectivity to the internet and other IoT applications and solutions.

Collectively these entities constitute the Parties, who expect to close the Acquisition in the 2nd or 3rd quarter of 2026.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
KONA Parent, L.P. (KONA Parent), KONA Merger Sub Co. (KONA
Merger Sub), Searchlight Capital IV LP, ABRY Partners VII, LP, and
ABRY Partners VII Co-Investment Fund, LP applied for a notification
waiver in respect of the proposed merger of KONA Merger Sub with
and into KORE Group Holdings, Inc. (KORE), with KORE surviving the
merger as a wholly owned subsidiary of KONA Parent, as described
in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the
Acquisition).
The Acquisition would result in:
• funds managed by Searchlight Capital Partners (Searchlight)
holding an interest of approximately 68.7% in and indirectly
acquiring sole control of KORE, and
• funds managed by Abry Partners (Abry) continuing to hold a
non-controlling minority interest of approximately 21.2% in
KORE.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
22 April 2026

The acquirer, KONA Parent, was formed solely for the purpose of
entering into the Acquisition. Following the Acquisition, KONA Parent
would be indirectly owned by:
• Searchlight (with an approximate interest of 68.7%), which
manages Searchlight Capital IV LP, and is an investment
firm in both private equity and debt, in sectors such as
communications, media and financial services, operating in
North America and Europe, and
• Abry (with an approximate interest of 21.2%), which
manages ABRY Partners VII, LP and ABRY Partners VII Co-
Investment Fund LP, and is an investment fund focused on
middle market companies in the communications, media,
information and business services industries.
The target, KORE, would be a wholly owned subsidiary of KONA
Parent following the Acquisition. It is a supplier of Internet-of-Things
(IoT) connectivity to the internet and other IoT applications and
solutions based in the US. In Australia, KORE supplies these services
to industries including connected health, industrial IoT, EV charging,
fleet management, smart buildings and sustainability.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the Acquisition is
unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In
particular:
a. there is no horizontal overlap between the products and
services supplied by KORE, and Searchlight and Abry
(including their controlled portfolio companies) in Australia,
and there are no material vertical relationships
b. while there may be a small complementary relationship
between the products and services supplied by KORE, and
Searchlight and Abry (including their controlled portfolio
companies), there are alternative suppliers of these products
and services in Australia, and
c. KORE has a small presence in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_name: Oracle – Leasehold interest in AirTrunk data centre
mergers.merger_description:
Oracle Corporation Australia Pty Ltd (**Oracle**) has entered into a Data Centre Services Order (**DCSO**) with AirTrunk SYD3 Pty Ltd, as trustee for AirTrunk SYD3 Land Trust (**AirTrunk**).

The DCSO includes provisions for, upon the satisfaction of certain conditions precedent, AirTrunk to provide Oracle with a lease to use certain areas of a data centre in the Sydney metropolitan area owned by AirTrunk, including colocation space, office and storage space.

Oracle is an indirect Australian subsidiary of American multinational technology company, Oracle Corporation. In Australia, Oracle is a provider of enterprise software, cloud and hardware solutions. Oracle neither develops nor owns any data centre buildings/facilities, or supplies any data centre colocation space, office and storage space, power and associated services, to any third parties in Australia. Instead, it licenses or leases the space from third parties such as AirTrunk.

AirTrunk is a company that develops and operates data centre campuses. It is a hyperscale data centre specialist creating a platform for cloud, content and large enterprise customers across Asia Pacific and the Middle East region.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Woodward pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Oracle Corporation Australia Pty Ltd (Oracle) applied for a
notification waiver in respect of a proposed Data Centre Services
Order (DCSO) with AirTrunk SYD3 Pty Ltd, as trustee for AirTrunk
SYD3 Land Trust (AirTrunk), to the extent it involves an acquisition
of assets, any kind of property and/or legal or equitable rights that
are not property and as described in the transaction documents
provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The DCSO provides for an exclusive lease to, and control over,
specific areas within a data centre operated by AirTrunk in the
Sydney metropolitan area, including colocation space, office and
storage space.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
1 June 2026

The acquirer, Oracle, is an indirect Australian subsidiary of American
multinational technology company, Oracle Corporation. In Australia,
Oracle is a provider of enterprise software, cloud and hardware
solutions. Oracle neither develops nor owns any data centre
buildings/facilities, or supplies any data centre colocation space,
office and storage space, power and associated services, to any
third parties in Australia. Instead, it licenses or leases the space from
third parties such as AirTrunk.
The lessor, AirTrunk, is a company that develops and operates data
centre campuses. It is a hyperscale data centre specialist creating a
platform for cloud, content and large enterprise customers across
Asia Pacific and the Middle East region.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors
in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010
(Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is no horizontal overlap between the parties
b. there appears to be alternative colocation data centre space
and capacity available for Oracle’s competitors
c. the Acquisition does not appear likely to otherwise limit or
prevent competition from rivals.
In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to
reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification
thresholds being met.
Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the
ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
AVC Operations Pty Ltd (**AVC**) proposes to acquire substantially all the assets of the licensed hotel business trading as the Courthouse Hotel (including the exclusive right to carry on the business in succession of the vendors and the benefit of the lease) from Flotto Holdings Pty. Ltd. as trustee for Milton Trust and Austlen Pty Limited as trustee for The Courthouse Trust (the **Acquisition**).

AVC is a subsidiary of Australian Venue Co. Holdings Limited which operates over 200 licensed venues throughout Australia and New Zealand, primarily pubs, as well as some bars, clubs and licensed restaurants. AVC's venues are individually branded and are mostly located in inner-city and metro areas, offering a range of products and services including food, alcoholic and non-alcoholic beverages, function rooms, live entertainment and gaming facilities.

Courthouse Hotel is a licensed hotel with an outdoor courtyard, located at 202 Australia Street, Newtown, NSW.

determination_table_content.details:
AVC Operations Pty Ltd (AVC), a subsidiary of Australian Venue Co.
Holdings Limited, applied for a notification waiver in respect of its
proposed acquisition of substantially all the assets of the licensed
hotel business trading as the Courthouse Hotel, as described in the
transaction documents provided as part of the application (the
Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, AVC, operates over 200 licensed venues throughout
Australia and New Zealand, primarily pubs, as well as some bars,
clubs and licensed restaurants. AVC's venues are individually
branded and are mostly located in inner-city and metro areas,
offering a range of products and services including food, alcoholic
and non-alcoholic beverages, function rooms, live entertainment and
gaming facilities.
The target, Courthouse Hotel, is a licensed hotel with an outdoor
courtyard, located at 202 Australia Street, Newtown, NSW.
AVC and the Courthouse Hotel both supply retail hospitality services
in metropolitan Sydney.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. the parties’ combined share in the number of venues
supplying retail hospitality services in the local area
surrounding the Courthouse Hotel is low
b. there are alternative suppliers of retail hospitality services in
the local area surrounding the Courthouse Hotel and in the
broader Sydney area.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering

mergers.merger_description:
Blackbird FOF25 Pty Ltd as trustee for Blackbird Ventures 2025 Follow-On Fund Trust (**Blackbird FOF25**) proposes to purchase shares of Series E Preferred Stock in a capital raise by Baseten Labs, Inc (**Baseten**).

Blackbird FOF25 is a venture capital fund managed by Blackbird Ventures. Blackbird Ventures is a venture capital firm based in Australia and New Zealand that invests in early-stage and high-growth technology companies. Funds managed by Blackbird Ventures (including Blackbird FOF25) hold minority investments in companies in Australia, New Zealand and around the world.

Baseten is a provider of an artificial intelligence (**AI**) inference platform and is based in San Francisco. Baseten's platform enables customers to deploy AI models across multiple clouds, regions, and geographies through its multi-tenant, single-tenant, and self-hosted deployment options. Baseten also offers on demand AI training capabilities for companies deploying custom or fine-tuned models. The Baseten platform encompasses models across various modality and use cases, including large language models, speech-to-text, text-to-speech, image generation, video generation, embeddings, and multi-model workflows.

determination_table_content.details:
Blackbird FOF25 Pty Ltd as trustee for Blackbird Ventures 2025
Follow-On Fund Trust (Blackbird FOF25) applied for a notification
waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of shares of Series E
Preferred Stock in Baseten Labs, Inc (Baseten), as described in the
transaction documents provided as part of the application (the
Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
10 February 2026

Blackbird Ventures is a venture capital firm based in Australia and
New Zealand that invests in early-stage and high-growth technology
companies. Blackbird FOF25 is a venture capital fund managed by
Blackbird Ventures. Funds managed by Blackbird Ventures (including
Blackbird FOF25) hold minority investments in hundreds of
companies in Australia, New Zealand and around the world.
The target, Baseten, is a provider of an artificial intelligence (AI)
inference platform and is based in San Francisco. Baseten's platform
enables customers to deploy AI models across multiple clouds,
regions, and geographies through its multi-tenant, single-tenant, and
self-hosted deployment options. Baseten also offers on demand AI
training capabilities for companies deploying custom or fine-tuned
models. The Baseten platform encompasses models across various
modality and use cases, including large language models, speech-to-
text, text-to-speech, image generation, video generation,
embeddings, and multi-model workflows.
The application states that there is no overlap between Blackbird
Ventures’ existing portfolio companies and Baseten.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the
information provided with the notification waiver application and had
regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act).
Based on the information provided by the applicant, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. The size of the interest in Baseten being acquired by
Blackbird Ventures is small.
b. Baseten has a single customer in Australia.
c. There does not appear to be horizontal overlap between
Blackbird Ventures’ portfolio companies and Baseten in the
supply of AI inference products in Australia.
d. There appears to be a low risk of foreclosure or other
concerning vertical or conglomerate effects resulting from
exclusionary conduct, bundling or tying of products post-
acquisition.
Given the above considerations that material competition concerns
are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is
not required to be notified.
In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to
reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification
thresholds being met.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_name: General Atlantic and CPP Investments led consortium - Boats Group
mergers.merger_description:
Funds managed by each of General Atlantic (**GA**) and Canada Pension Plan Investment Board (**CPP Investments**) propose to indirectly acquire interests in Boats Group Holdings, Inc (**Boats Group**) through acquiring interests in Bimini Holdings, LP, which indirectly owns 100% of Boats Group (the **Acquisition**).

Boats Group operates online marketplaces for recreational boats and yachts (including Boat Trader, YachtWorld, boats.com and European portals such as iNautia, Botentekoop, Boats and Outboards, and Boatshop24). Boats Group has subsidiaries in the US, UK, Canada, Spain, France, the Netherlands and Germany. Boats Group is currently controlled and majority owned by Permira Holdings Limited (**Permira**).

Permira is a Guernsey-registered private equity business engaged, through its subsidiaries and affiliates, in the provision of investment management services to a number of investment funds. Post completion, Permira will retain an interest in Boats Group, alongside GA and CPP Investments.

GA is a global private equity firm headquartered in the United States.

CPP Investments is a professional investment management organisation that invests the funds transferred to it by the Canada Pension Plan (**CPP Fund**) that are not needed by the CPP Fund to pay current benefits in the best interests of more than 22 million contributors and beneficiaries.

Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act).

mergers.merger_description:
Resonetics, LLC (**Resonetics**) proposes to acquire:

(i) 100% of the issued and outstanding voting securities of Arcline Resolution Blocker, Inc. (**Blocker**), which owns minority non-corporate interests of Resolution Medical MidCo, LLC (**Resolution Medical**) and

(ii) the remaining non-corporate interests of Resolution Medical.

Resolution Medical is a provider of integrated design and manufacturing solutions for the full lifecycle of complex medical devices. Resolution Medical is headquartered in Fridley, Minnesota, USA with additional operations in the Netherlands, and is currently owned by Arcline Investment Management, LP.

Resonetics is a high technology manufacturer, supplying product and manufacturing services primarily to the medical device industry for medical device components, beginning at the prototyping phase through to contract manufacturing. Resonetics also designs and manufactures customized laser-based manufacturing and scientific equipment, and provides design and development services to life science companies.

Resonetics is jointly controlled by CP Granite Holdings, L.P., an affiliate of The Carlyle Group Inc. (**Carlyle**), and GTCR (Regatta Investors) LP and Regatta Medical Holdings, LLC, affiliates of GTCR LLC (**GTCR**).

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Woodward pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Resonetics, LLC (Resonetics) applied for a notification waiver in
respect of its proposed acquisition of:
(i) 100% of the issued and outstanding voting securities of Arcline
Resolution Blocker, Inc. (Blocker), which owns minority non-
corporate interests of Resolution Medical MidCo, LLC
(Resolution Medical), and
(ii) the remaining non-corporate interests of Resolution Medical,
as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
11 March 2026

Resonetics is a high technology manufacturer, supplying product
and manufacturing services primarily to the medical device industry
for medical device components, beginning at the prototyping phase
through to contract manufacturing. Resonetics also designs and
manufactures customised laser-based manufacturing and scientific
equipment, and provides design and development services to life
science companies. Resonetics is jointly controlled by CP Granite
Holdings, L.P., an affiliate of The Carlyle Group Inc., and GTCR LP
and Regatta Medical Holdings, LLC, affiliates of GTCR LLC, and is
based in the US.
Resolution Medical is a provider of integrated design and
manufacturing solutions for the full lifecycle of complex medical
devices. Resolution Medical is currently owned by Arcline Investment
Management, LP and is based in the US.
Resonetics and Resolution Medical supply input products and
services to the medical device manufacturing industry. In Australia,
Resonetics supplies component manufacturing services from
overseas locations to Australian customers, while Resolution
Medical supplies contract development, design and engineering
services from overseas locations to Australian customers.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. Resonetics and Resolution Medical have a small presence in
Australia, with Resonetics primarily focused on
manufacturing and Resolution Medical on contract
development, design and engineering
b. limited horizontal overlap or vertical relationship in Australia
c. there are alternative global suppliers of input products and
services to the medical device manufacturing industry.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
OEP IX Master Coöperatief U.A. (**OEP IX**) will acquire from certain wholly owned subsidiaries of CRH PLC shares representing 100% of the total voting rights of the Leviat Group (**Leviat**), pursuant to a share purchase agreement.

OEP IX is a financial holding company forming part of One Equity Partners Fund Complex IX that is managed and advised by OEP Group. The OEP Group focuses on investments in industrial, healthcare and technology sector businesses across North America and Europe.

Leviat is a global design and manufacturing business specialising in high‑performance, engineered construction accessories used to lift, connect, anchor, reinforce, and insulate building and infrastructure projects. In Australia, the Leviat Group through its Australian subsidiary supplies anchoring and connecting products, reinforcement and formwork products, masonry support products and lifting products. Leviat is ultimately owned and currently controlled by CRH PLC.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
OEP IX Master Coöperafief U.A. (OEP IX), through its wholly owned
subsidiaries, proposes to acquire shares representing 100% of the
total voting rights of the Leviat Group (Leviat) from certain wholly
owned subsidiaries of CRH PLC, as described in the transaction
documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition). OEP
IX applied for a notification in respect of the Acquisition.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
16 March 2026

The acquirer, OEP IX, is a financial holding company forming part of
One Equity Partners Fund Complex IX that is managed and advised
by OEP Group. The OEP Group focuses on investments in industrial,
healthcare and technology sector businesses across North America
and Europe.
The target, Leviat, is a global design and manufacturing business
specialising in high‑performance, engineered construction
accessories used to lift, connect, anchor, reinforce, and insulate
building and infrastructure projects. In Australia, the Leviat Group,
through its Australian subsidiary supplies anchoring and connecting
products, reinforcement and formwork products, masonry support
products and lifting products. Leviat is ultimately owned and
currently controlled by CRH PLC.
The application states that none of OEP’s portfolio companies
supply products that are functional substitutes for, or complements
to, or which are otherwise closely related to Leviat’s construction
accessories and engineered connection systems.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular, there is no competitive or
complementary relationship between OEP IX and its portfolio
companies and Leviat in the supply of engineered construction
accessories in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
