mergers.merger_name: BHP – expansion of Special Mining Lease at Olympic Dam, SA

acquirers.name: BHP OLYMPIC DAM CORPORATION PTY LTD

anzsic_codes.name: Copper, Silver, Lead and Zinc Smelting and Refining

mergers.merger_description:
The parties to this acquisition are BHP Olympic Dam Corporation Pty Ltd, which is ultimately 100% owned by BHP Group Limited (**BHP**), and the State of South Australia.

BHP is an Australian headquartered natural resources company. BHP is a global leading producer of iron ore, copper and metallurgical coal. Relevantly, BHP operates the Olympic Dam Mine in South Australia, which is one of most significant global mineral deposits.

The State of South Australia, through the Department of Energy and Mining, is responsible for regulating mining rights in South Australia and owns/manages the underlying legal rights to the minerals. It does not compete with BHP in relation to the extraction or supply of any of the minerals extracted from the Olympic Dam precinct.

BHP proposes to acquire mining rights over an additional area of land located in Olympic Dam, South Australia from the State of South Australia. The additional area (SML1 Additional Area) is adjacent to BHP’s existing ‘Special Mining Lease 1’ (**SML1**) at Olympic Dam.

The acquisition, by way of expansion of the area of existing SML1, is for the purpose of a proposal by BHP to increase mining activity and potentially expand the existing mineral processing facilities at Olympic Dam. The acquisition also provides BHP with additional non-proprietary rights in relation to its operations on SML1.

The main mineral mined at Olympic Dam is copper, with gold, silver and uranium as by-products.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Philip Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
BHP Olympic Dam Corporation Pty Ltd, a subsidiary of BHP Group
Limited (BHP), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its
proposed acquisition of mining rights over an additional area of land
located in Olympic Dam, South Australia from the State of South
Australia, as described in the transaction documents provided as
part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

BHP is an Australian headquartered natural resources company.
BHP is a global leading producer of iron ore, copper and
metallurgical coal. Relevantly, BHP operates the Olympic Dam Mine
in South Australia, which is one of most significant global mineral
deposits.
The State of South Australia through the Department of Energy and
Mining, is responsible for regulating mining rights in South Australia
and owns/manages the underlying legal rights to the minerals.
BHP proposes to acquire mining rights over an additional area of
land located at Olympic Dam, as well as additional non-proprietary
rights in relation to its existing operations on the Olympic Dam mine.
The additional area is adjacent to BHP’s existing special mining
lease at Olympic Dam.
The main mineral mined at Olympic Dam is copper, with gold, silver
and uranium as by-products.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. there is no removal of a competitor as a result of the
Acquisition
b. the Acquisition is unlikely to affect BHP’s ability to influence
the domestic price of copper, as prices are typically set by
reference to global conditions
c. there are alternative producers and suppliers of copper, gold,
silver and uranium in Australia and globally.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
The parties have executed an agreement that provides for funds (**the 26North Funds**) managed by 26North Partners LP (**26North Partners**) to acquire Intermedia Cloud Communications, Inc. and its subsidiaries (**Intermedia**), via Unite Holdco LLC (**Unite Holdco**) – an acquisition vehicle owned and controlled by the 26North Funds.

Under the acquisition, Unite Holdco’s wholly owned subsidiary, Unite Merger Sub Inc., will merge with and into Intermedia, with Intermedia continuing as the surviving entity. As a result, Unite Holdco (and indirectly the 26North Funds) will acquire 100% of the issued and outstanding shares of Intermedia.

Intermedia is a corporation incorporated in Delaware, United States. It operates a global business supplying cloud‑based communications and collaboration software services to business customers. These services primarily relate to cloud telephony voice services, as well as video conferencing, messaging, email, file sharing, archiving, security and related cloud‑based tools. Intermedia’s offerings are primarily targeted at small and medium‑sized businesses. Intermedia has several subsidiaries in different jurisdictions which will be indirectly acquired as part of the acquisition, including an Australian subsidiary - Intermedia.net Australia Pty Ltd.

26North Partners is an integrated, multi-asset-class investment platform headquartered in New York that provides investment advice and opportunities to its clients across private equity, credit, insurance and reinsurance strategies.

The 26North Funds were established in 2024 as 26North Partners' flagship fund. The 26North Funds have interests in businesses which supply goods and services in Australia, including kitchen appliances (Composition Brands), digital workplace services (AVI-SPL) and broadcast production services (NEP Group).

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Unite Holdco LLC (Unite Holdco), an entity owned and controlled by
funds managed by 26North Partners LP (the 26North Funds), applied
for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition,
indirectly via a subsidiary, of 100% of the issued and outstanding
shares of Intermedia Cloud Communications, Inc. (Intermedia), as
described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, the 26North Funds, was established in 2024 as
26North Partners LP’s flagship fund. 26North Partners LP is an
integrated, multi-asset-class investment platform that provides
investment advice and opportunities to its clients across private
equity, credit, insurance and reinsurance strategies.
The 26North Funds has interests in various businesses which supply
goods and services in Australia, including a majority, controlling
interest in AVI-SPL Global LLC (AVI-SPL). AVI-SPL supplies digital
workplace services including multi-office collaboration and audio
video technologies and managed audio video services.
The target, Intermedia, operates a global business supplying
cloud-based communications and collaboration software services to
business customers. These services primarily relate to cloud
telephony voice services, as well as video conferencing, messaging,
email, file sharing, archiving, security and related cloud-based tools.
In Australia, Intermedia’s products and services are supplied via
resellers.
Intermedia and AVI-SPL both supply products and services related to
communications in Australia. Intermediary’s business largely
focusses on the supply of unified communications software as a
service. AVI-SPL focusses on installation and integration services
primarily for enterprise conference room hardware, with some
unified communications software solutions ancillary to this.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is a small horizontal overlap between the 26North
Funds (via its interest in AVI-SPL) and Intermedia in the
supply of unified communications software solutions
b. Intermedia has a small presence in Australia
c. there are alternative suppliers of unified communications
software solutions in Australia.
In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to
reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification
thresholds being met.
Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the
ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Hillhouse Investment Management, Ltd (**Hillhouse**) is a global alternative investment management firm headquartered in Singapore which focuses on long-term investments in high quality businesses. It has offices in London, Sydney, Tokyo, Hong Kong, Beijing, Mumbai and New York.

The target, Barwon Investment Partners Pty Ltd (**Barwon**), is an Australian alternatives fund manager focusing on healthcare property, real estate credit and private equity. Barwon has one office in Sydney.

The parties supply investment management services, which involves the professional and customised management of various financial assets on behalf of institutional and wholesale investor clients.

Hillhouse proposes to acquire a majority interest in Barwon.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Barwon Bidco Pty Ltd (Bidco), which is ultimately owned by
Hillhouse Investment Management, Ltd (Hillhouse), applied for a
notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of a
majority interest in Barwon Investment Partners Pty Ltd (Barwon), as
described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, Hillhouse, is a global alternative investment manager
that deploys capital across multiple strategies, including public
equities, private equity, private credit, and real assets. Through its
private equity arm, Hillhouse invests in long-term global
opportunities across the business services, healthcare, consumer,
and industrials sectors.
The target, Barwon, is a specialist fund manager investing in
alternative assets across healthcare property, real estate credit and
global investments, as well as in private equity for institutional and
wholesale investors.
Both parties supply investment management services in Australia,
although their focus is on different asset classes and in different
sectors.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is no or limited horizontal overlap between Hillhouse
and Barwon’s respective services and investment portfolios
(including between their connected entities)
b. there are alternative suppliers of investment management
services in Australia
c. there appears to be a low risk of foreclosure or other
concerning vertical or conglomerate effects resulting from
exclusionary conduct post-acquisition.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_name: Black Rhino Group – Granville Tavern and Motel and Cambridge Street Bottle Shop & Cellars in Granville, QLD

acquirers.name: The Trustee for It Is What It Is (Granville) Trust

mergers.merger_description:
The Black Rhino Group is a national hospitality group operating 48 venues across Queensland, Victoria and South Australia. The Black Rhino Group operates hospitality venues, including hotels, bottleshops, accommodation, and food and beverage offerings, with a particular focus on gaming, wagering, family bistros, sports bars, retail liquor, entertainment and accommodation. Black Rhino (Granville) Pty Ltd and the trustee for the It Is What It Is (Granville) Trust are part of the Black Rhino Group. The Black Rhino Group has 5 licensed venues and locations in Queensland.

Granville Hotel Qld Pty Ltd (**Vendor**) owns and operates the freehold land, buildings, licences and business assets (both tangible and intangible) of:

* Granville Tavern and Motel; and
* Cambridge Street Bottle Shop & Cellars.

Granville Tavern and Motel is a commercial hotel motel business which offers dining, accommodation and gaming machines. Cambridge Street Bottle Shop & Cellars is a bottle shop that offers packaged liquor for off-premises consumption. Both businesses are based in Granville, Queensland.

Black Rhino (Granville) Pty Ltd and the trustee for the It Is What It Is (Granville) Trust propose to acquire 100% of the Vendor’s interest in the aforementioned businesses, together with the relevant freehold from which the businesses operate.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Black Rhino (Granville) Pty Ltd and the trustee for the It Is What It Is
(Granville) Trust, as part of the Black Rhino Group, applied for a
notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of
Granville Hotel Qld Pty Ltd’s interest in the Granville Tavern and
Motel and the Cambridge Street Bottle Shop & Cellars businesses,
together with the relevant freehold land from which the businesses
operate, as described in the transaction documents provided as part
of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The Black Rhino Group is a national hospitality group operating 48
venues across Queensland, Victoria and South Australia. The Black
Rhino Group operate licensed venues, including hotels, bottleshops,
accommodation, and food and beverage offerings, with a particular
focus on gaming, wagering, family bistros, sports bars, retail liquor,
entertainment and accommodation. Black Rhino (Granville) Pty Ltd
and the trustee for the It Is What It Is (Granville) Trust are part of the
Black Rhino Group. The Black Rhino Group has 5 licensed venues
and locations in Queensland.
The Granville Tavern and Motel is a commercial hotel motel
business which offers dining, accommodation and gaming
machines. The Cambridge Street Bottle Shop & Cellars is a bottle
shop that offers packaged liquor for off-premises consumption. Both
businesses are based in Granville, a suburb of Maryborough in
Queensland’s Fraser Coast Region.
The Black Rhino Group’s closest venue to the Granville Tavern and
Motel and Cambridge Street Bottle Shop & Cellars is approximately
390km away by drive.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the ACCC considers that the
Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular, there is no local overlap between the Black
Rhino Group and the Granville Tavern and Model and the Cambridge
Street Bottle Shop & Cellars in the supply of on-premises hospitality
products and services (including liquor for on-site consumption,
dining and gaming), accommodation, or packaged liquor for offsite
consumption, in Granville and surrounding areas.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
The Acquisition is a capital structuring transaction which will involve funds and/or vehicles advised and/or managed by affiliates of Blackstone Alternative Credit Advisors LP (**Blackstone Credit**) converting a portion of their existing debt interest in Medallia Parent, LP (together with its direct and indirect subsidiaries, **Medallia**) into a majority equity interest (the **Acquisition**) to recapitalise Medallia and support its continued operation. This is in light of Medallia’s pending financial inability to support its ongoing debt service obligations under the existing capital structure.

Blackstone Credit is part of the Credit & Insurance segment within Blackstone Inc. (together with any of its affiliates or affiliated investment funds, **Blackstone**).

Blackstone is a US-based corporation, incorporated under the laws of Delaware, United States. Blackstone operates as an investment management firm, not as a conglomerate or holding company. Accordingly, each company in its investment portfolio is independently managed and financed and each has different investors (although the investors in different investment funds may overlap). Each Blackstone portfolio company has its own board of directors, which generally also include Blackstone representatives.

Medallia is a US-based software company that specialises in providing customer and employee experience management solutions. Using its experience management platform and AI-driven software, Medallia collects a broad range of feedback about end-customer and employee experiences through surveys, digital behaviour analysis, and social media to provide actionable insights to help its enterprise customers improve their employee and customer experience.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Blackstone Alternative Credit Advisors LP (Blackstone Credit)
applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed
conversion of its existing debt interest in Medallia Parent, LP
(Medallia) into a majority 56% equity interest, as described in the
transaction documents provided as part of the application (the
Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, Blackstone Credit, is part of the Credit & Insurance
segment within Blackstone Inc. (together with any of its affiliates or
affiliated investment funds, Blackstone). Blackstone is a US-based
investment management firm. It has interests in Prodege, a
consumer marketing and insights platform with a limited presence in
Australia, and Material+, which provides marketing strategy and
insight services including to customers in Australia.
The target, Medallia, is a US-based software company that
specialises in providing customer and employee experience
management solutions. Using its experience management platform
and AI-driven software, Medallia collects feedback about end-
customer and employee experiences through surveys, digital
behaviour analysis, and social media to provide actionable insights
to help its enterprise customers improve their employee and
customer experience.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver and had regard
to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. there is no horizontal overlap between the products and
services supplied by Blackstone Credit, Blackstone (or its
controlled portfolio companies) and Medallia in Australia,
and no material vertical relationships, and
b. while there may be a complementary relationship between
certain products and services supplied by Blackstone’s
connected entities and Medallia, there are alternative
suppliers of these products and services in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_name: Ethos (TPG) – EMIS and certain related assets
mergers.merger_description:
Ethos Bidco, Ltd (**Ethos**) proposes to indirectly acquire the following from affiliates of UnitedHealth Group Incorporated (**UHG**) pursuant to a Share and Asset Purchase Agreement (**EMIS SAPA**):

* 100% of the shares of EMIS Group Limited (**EMIS**); and
* Certain healthcare technology assets used in the UK by UHG's wholly owned subsidiary, Optum Health Solutions (UK) Limited (**Transferred Assets**).

Ethos also proposes to acquire 100% of EMIS Health India Private Limited (**EMIS India**) (together with EMIS and the Transferred Assets, **the Target Group**) from UHG through a separate Share Purchase Agreement (**EMIS India** **SPA**, together with EMIS SAPA, **the Acquisition**).

Ethos is a special purpose vehicle established by funds managed by affiliates of TPG Inc. (**TPG**) for the purpose of the Acquisition. TPG is a leading global alternative asset manager firm founded in San Francisco in 1992.

UHG is an NYSE-listed multinational healthcare and well-being company. EMIS is a UK-based healthcare software business ultimately owned by UHG. Its core product is a primary care electronic patient record (**EPR**) system.

In Australia, the Target Group's activities are limited to the provision of ancillary services to a single customer in relation to the Target Group's legacy EPR system.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Chair Cass-Gottlieb pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Ethos Bidco, Ltd (Ethos) applied for a notification waiver in respect
of its proposed acquisition of the following from affiliates of
UnitedHealth Group Incorporated (UHG), as described in the
transaction documents provided as part of the application:
a. 100% of the share capital in EMIS Group Limited (EMIS),
b. certain healthcare technology assets used in the UK by UHG’s
wholly owned subsidiary, Optum Health Solutions (UK) Limited
(Transferred Assets), and
c. 100% of the share capital of EMIS Health India Private Limited
(EMIS India),
(the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
12 January 2026

TPG Inc. (TPG) is a global alternative asset manager firm founded in
San Francisco in 1992. Certain funds managed by affiliates of TPG
have established Ethos, a special purpose vehicle with no prior
business activities, for the purpose of the Acquisition. In Australia,
TPG's portfolio covers transportation, healthcare, real estate,
technology, telecommunications, and real estate industries.
The Acquisition target consists of EMIS, the Transferred Assets and
EMIS India. EMIS is a healthcare software business that is based in
the UK. Its core product is a primary care electronic patient record
(EPR) system. EMIS also supplies other software or services,
including in relation to pharmacy patient medical records, data
analytics, hospital care software, life sciences, medicine
optimisation software and population health management. EMIS
India and the Transferred Assets have no presence in Australia. UHG
is an NYSE-listed multinational healthcare and well-being company.
Both EMIS and two portfolio companies (Nextech and Covetrus)
controlled by funds managed by affiliates of TPG supply healthcare
software services in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the
information provided with the notification waiver application and had
regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act), being:
(i) the object of the Act,
(ii) the interests of consumers,
(iii) the likelihood that, if the acquisition were put into effect,
the notification thresholds in section 51ABP(1) of the
Act would apply, and
(iv) the likelihood that the acquisition, if put into effect,
would in all the circumstances have the effect, or be
likely to have the effect, of substantially lessening
competition in any market.
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. There is very limited horizontal overlap between entities
associated with Ethos (i.e. TPG or TPG’s portfolio
companies) and EMIS in the supply of healthcare software in
Australia.
i. Nextech supplies speciality-specific electronic
medical record and practice management software,
to customers such as dermatology and plastic
surgery clinics in Australia. Covetrus supplies
electronic medical record and practice management
software to veterinary practices.
ii. In Australia, EMIS’ activities are limited and currently
only involve the supply of certain maintenance
services to a single customer in connection with a
legacy EPR system.
b. The parties are likely to continue to be constrained by
alternative suppliers of EPR systems and other healthcare
software in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

comments.commentary:
TPG's acquisition of EMIS Group and related assets was the one of the first two acquisitions to be granted a notification waiver by the ACCC. EMIS's core product is a primary care electronic patient record (EPR) system. The ACCC determined the acquisition did not require notification because:

- there is very limited horizontal overlap in Australia between TPG's portfolio companies (Nextech supplies specialty-specific software to dermatology/plastic surgery clinics, while Covetrus supplies software to veterinary practices) and EMIS;
- EMIS' Australian activities are limited to maintenance services for a single customer with a legacy EPR system; and
- alternative suppliers of EPR systems and healthcare software remain in Australia.

While notification thresholds were likely met, the ACCC waived the requirement given its view that there were minimal competition concerns.

mergers.merger_description:
This application relates to the Acquisition by Intrepid Travel Pty Ltd (**Intrepid**) of 100% shares in Wild Bush Luxury Experience Pty Ltd (**Wild Bush Luxury**) held by Experience Co Limited (**ExCo**).

Intrepid Group Pty Ltd (**IG**) is a global, purpose-led experiential travel company, specialising in small group tours and travel experiences. IG has 31 offices around the world and operates more than 1,000 small group adventures in 118 countries. Intrepid is a subsidiary of IG.

Wild Bush Luxury is a boutique Australian tourism operator that manages and operates the 'The Maria Island Walk' (in Tasmania), 'Arkaba Walk (in South Australia), 'Arkaba Homestead' (in South Australia), and 'Bamurru Plains' (in the Northern Territory).

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Intrepid Travel Pty Ltd (Intrepid Travel) applied for a notification
waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the share
capital in Wild Bush Luxury Experience Pty Ltd (Wild Bush Luxury),
as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(b) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) not to make the notification waiver
determination applied for.
5 February 2026

The acquirer, Intrepid Travel, is a subsidiary of Intrepid Group Pty Ltd
which is a global, experiential travel company specialising in small
group tours and travel experiences. Intrepid Group Pty Ltd has 31
offices around the world and operates more than 1,000 small group
adventures in 118 countries.
The target, Wild Bush Luxury, is a boutique Australian tourism
operator that manages and operates the 'The Maria Island Walk' (in
Tasmania), 'Arkaba Walk (in South Australia), 'Arkaba Homestead' (in
South Australia), and 'Bamurru Plains' (in the Northern Territory).
Wild Bush Luxury is currently held by Experience Co Limited, a
tourism company and offers adventure experiences of tandem
skydiving, aerial high ropes and ziplining activities and tours to the
Great Barrier Reef and Daintree regions.
Intrepid Travel and Wild Bush Luxury both supply tourism services in
Australia, and particularly in Top End (Northern Territory), Flinders
Ranges (South Australia) and Maria Island (Tasmania).

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the
information provided with the notification waiver application and had
regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Act.
Based on the information provided in the application, including the
revenue of the parties in Australia and relevant cumulative
acquisitions the ACCC considers that, if the Acquisition were put into
effect, the notification thresholds would be met.
The ACCC is unable to reach a view that the Acquisition would not be
likely to raise competition issues in any market based on the
information provided. It considers that the Acquisition warrants
consideration through the notification process, including via further
information gathering and consultation with third parties. This may
include a focus on:
a. The extent of any horizontal overlap between Intrepid
Travel and Wild Bush Luxury in the supply of tourism
services in Australia, and particularly in Maria Island, the
Top End and Flinders Ranges,
b. The extent to which the parties are likely to face
competition from alternative suppliers at these
locations, particularly in relation to Maria Island where
alternatives appear limited.
Given the above, the ACCC has determined not to make the
notification waiver determination applied for.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

acquirers.name: GENERAL DENTAL HOLDINGS PTY LTD trading as Impression Dental Group

mergers.merger_description:
General Dental Holdings Pty Ltd, trading as Impression Dental Group (**IDG**), proposes to acquire 100% of the assets of the dental practice known as Happy Rock Dental (**Happy Rock Dental Practice**), through a business sale agreement.

Happy Rock Dental Practice is owned and operated by Dr Wei-Jie Jiang and Wei-Jie Jiang Pty Ltd and supplies general dental services to the public from its premises in Cronulla, NSW.

IDG is an Australian-based dental support organisation that provides centralised support in areas including recruitment, payroll, marketing, IT, finance, and operations, to enable dental practitioners to focus on providing dental services. IDG currently owns and supports 22 independently branded dental practices in Australia which provide dental services to the public. IDG does not have a premises in Cronulla, NSW.

IDG is controlled by Genesis Capital which is an Australian-based private equity firm that invests in businesses operating in the healthcare sector.

Genesis Capital’s investments also include Pacific Smiles Group which sets up new dental centres from which dentists run their practice and provides operational support. Pacific Smiles Group has over 120 dental centres across Australia, including one in the Sutherland Shire which is not in Cronulla, NSW. Pacific Smiles Group’s dental centres are mainly located in shopping centres and operate under the Pacific Smiles Dental and NIB Dental Care brands on the East Coast and under the HBF brand in Western Australia.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
General Dental Holdings Pty Ltd, trading as Impression Dental Group
(IDG), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed
acquisition of 100% of the assets of Happy Rock Dental practice
(Happy Rock), as described in the transaction documents provided
as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
26 February 2026

The acquirer, IDG, is an Australian-based dental support organisation
that provides centralised support in areas including recruitment,
payroll, marketing, IT, finance, and operations, to enable dental
practitioners to focus on providing dental services. IDG currently
owns and supports 22 independently branded dental practices in
Australia (including 10 dental practices in NSW).
IDG is controlled by Genesis Capital (Genesis), an Australian-based
private equity firm that invests in businesses operating in the
healthcare sector. Genesis’ investments also include Pacific Smiles
Group, which sets up new dental centres from which dentists run
their practice and provides operational support. Pacific Smiles Group
has over 120 dental centres across Australia, including one in the
Sutherland Shire. Neither IDG or Pacific Smiles Group have dental
practices in Cronulla, NSW.
The target, Happy Rock, is owned and operated by Dr Wei-Jie Jiang
and Wei-Jie Jiang Pty Ltd, and supplies general dental services to the
public from its premises in Cronulla, NSW.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the
information provided with the notification waiver application and
certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors
in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010
(Cth) (Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is no horizontal overlap between Genesis and Happy
Rock in Cronulla, NSW and a small overlap in the broader
Sutherland Shire Council area
b. there are numerous alternative suppliers of dental services
in the area
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Scopely, Inc. (**Scopely**) proposes to acquire:

* an initial 51% of the shares of Loom Games Oyun Yazılım ve Pazarlama Anonim Şirketi (**Loom Games**), and
* 4 related 12.25% stakes, up to 100% of the shares of Loom Games.

Scopely is a wholly-owned subsidiary of Saudi Electronic Games Holding Company (doing business as Savvy Games Group (**Savvy**)), which itself is a wholly-owned subsidiary of the Public Investment Fund (**PIF**), the sovereign wealth fund of Saudi Arabia.

Scopely is an interactive entertainment company specialising in the development and publishing of mobile-first video games. Scopely develops games in-house, through Scopely’s development studios, as well as through external partner studios.

Scopely’s published mobile games currently available include MONOPOLY GO!, Star Trek Fleet Command, Marvel Strike Force, Pokémon GO, Monster Hunter Now, Campfire, Wayfarer, Pikmin Bloom, Bingo Bash: Live Bingo Games, Tiki Solitaire TriPeaks, Yahtzee with Buddies, WWE Champions, Scrabble GO, GSN Casino: Slot Machine Games, Stumble Guys, Looney Tunes World of Mayhem, Dice With Buddies: Social Game, Wheel of Fortune: Show Puzzles, Temple Run: Puzzle Adventure, and Grand Casino: Slots Games. Games produced by Scopely are built on its Playgami platform, which includes both publishing tools and a learning system to help support game development during all aspects of its development cycle and after launch.

Savvy is the ultimately controlling company within PIF that is active in gaming. The only Savvy-owned companies active in mobile gaming are Scopely and Scopely’s wholly owned subsidiaries, Niantic, and Tag Games. Savvy is otherwise active in organising and commercialising eSports events, developing and investing in experimental gaming venues, and developing video games.

Aside from Savvy, PIF’s portfolio consists of companies active in various industries, including aerospace and defence, construction, financial services, healthcare, mining, real estate and others.

Loom Games is a mobile game developer engaged in the development, distribution and operation of puzzle games, based in Istanbul, Turkey, and established in 2025. Loom Games’ games are (i) Pixel Flow! and (ii) Ropeland (which is currently dormant). It distributes its games mainly through global app stores such as Google Play and Apple App Store, with its core revenue streams derived from in-app advertising and in-app purchasing/subscriptions.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Scopely, Inc (Scopely) applied for a notification waiver in respect of
its proposed acquisition of:
• an initial 51% of the shares of Loom Games Oyun Yazılım ve
Pazarlama Anonim Şirketi (Loom Games), and
• 4 related 12.25% stakes, up to 100% of the shares of Loom
Games,
as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
6 March 2026

The acquirer, Scopely, is an interactive entertainment company
specialising in the development and publishing of mobile-first video
games. Scopely develops games in-house, through Scopely’s
development studios, as well as through external partner studios.
Games produced by Scopely are built on its Playgami platform,
which includes both publishing tools and a learning system to help
support game development during all aspects of its development
cycle and after launch.
Scopely is wholly owned by Savvy Games Group (Savvy). Savvy is
active in organising and commercialising eSports events, developing
and investing in experimental gaming venues, and developing video
games.
Savvy is a wholly-owned subsidiary of the Public Investment Fund
(PIF), the sovereign wealth fund of Saudi Arabia. PIF’s portfolio
consists of companies active in various industries, including
aerospace and defence, construction, financial services, healthcare,
mining, real estate and others.
The target, Loom Games, is a mobile game developer engaging in
the development, distribution and operation of puzzle games. Loom
Games’ games are (i) Pixel Flow! and (ii) Ropeland (which is currently
dormant). It distributes its games mainly through global app stores
such as Google Play and Apple App Store.
Scopely and Loom Games both develop and publish video games for
mobile devices in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. the parties’ combined share in the supply of mobile video
games in Australia is low and the increase in share of supply
arising from the Acquisition would be small
b. the presence of alternative developers and publishers of
mobile video games in Australia.
Given the above considerations that material competition concerns
are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is
not required to be notified.
In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to
reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification
thresholds being met.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Advance Holdings II, LLC (a vehicle ultimately controlled by Platinum Equity, LLC (**Platinum Equity**)) proposes to acquire BCI Acquisitions, Inc. (together with its direct and indirect subsidiaries, **Burke**).

Burke designs, manufactures and supplies outdoor playground and recreational equipment, as well as ancillary products for recreational spaces.

Platinum Equity is a global private equity and investment strategies firm, which is specialised in the merger, acquisition and operation of companies providing services and solutions to customers in a broad range of sectors, including business services, information technology, telecommunications, logistics, metal services, manufacturing and distribution. Among such companies is Play Holdings, Inc. (together with its direct and indirect subsidiaries, **PlayPower**). PlayPower is a designer, manufacturer and supplier of outdoor playground equipment, sport and fitness equipment, and park and urban furniture.

Burke and PlayPower are headquartered in the US with no corporate or physical footprint in Australia.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Advance Holdings II, LLC (Advance Holdings) applied for a
notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of BCI
Acquisitions, Inc (together with its direct and indirect subsidiaries,
Burke), effected by way of a merger of Activate Play Merger
Corporation (a subsidiary of Advance Holdings) and Burke, as
described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition). This will involve Burke continuing as
the surviving corporation and becoming a wholly owned subsidiary
of Advance Holdings.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
6 March 2026

The acquirer, Advance Holdings, is a vehicle established for the
purpose of the Acquisition and is ultimately controlled by Platinum
Equity, LLC (Platinum Equity). Platinum Equity is a global private
equity and investment strategies firm specialising in the merger,
acquisition and operation of companies providing services and
solutions to customers in a broad range of sectors, including
business services, information technology, telecommunications,
logistics, metal services, manufacturing and distribution. Among
such companies is Play Holdings, Inc. (together with its direct and
indirect subsidiaries, PlayPower). PlayPower is a designer,
manufacturer and supplier of outdoor playground equipment, sport
and fitness equipment, and park and urban furniture. PlayPower is
headquartered in the US.
The target, Burke, designs, manufactures and supplies outdoor
playground and recreational equipment, as well as ancillary products
for recreational spaces. Burke is headquartered in the US.
PlayPower and Burke both supply commercial playground and
outdoor recreation equipment globally and in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. PlayPower and Burke have a small presence in Australia
b. the parties’ combined share in the supply of playground and
outdoor recreation equipment is likely to be low
c. the presence of alternative global and national suppliers of
playground and outdoor recreation equipment, including
commercial.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Tetra Tech Australia Group Holdings Pty Ltd (**Tetra Tech**) proposes to acquire 100% of the shares in Providence Consulting Group Pty Ltd (**Providence Consulting**) through a Share Purchase Agreement.

Tetra Tech is a wholly owned subsidiary of Tetra Tech, Inc., a publicly listed corporation (NASDAQ:TTEK), and is the holding company for various subsidiaries.

Tetra Tech's subsidiaries are involved in a variety of industries, in particular water, energy, infrastructure, mining, defence and development, and provide high-end consulting, technical and advisory services in Australia to a variety of customers, both public and private. Tetra Tech also has operations and interests across the Asia-Pacific region.

Providence Consulting is an advisory and project management consultancy based in Australia. Providence Consulting supplies program delivery, systems engineering, and protective security consultancy services for defence, government, and industry clients.

Tetra Tech Australia Group Holdings Pty Ltd (Tetra Tech) applied for
a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100%
of the share capital in Providence Consulting Group Pty Ltd
(Providence Consulting), as described in the transaction documents
provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has

31 March 2026

The acquirer, Tetra Tech, is a wholly owned subsidiary of Tetra Tech,

infrastructure, mining, defence and development, and provide high-
end consulting, technical and advisory services in Australia to a

The target, Providence Consulting, is an advisory and project

supplies program delivery, systems engineering, and protective
security consultancy services for defence, government, and industry
clients.
Providence Consulting appears primarily to be a specialist defence
consulting firm whereas Tetra Tech provides consulting services
more generally, in a variety of fields.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian

mergers.merger_description:
Infotrust Ltd (ASX:ITS) (**Infotrust**) proposes to acquire 100% of the shares in Catalyst Cyber.

Infotrust is an ASX-listed Australian technology services group. Its cybersecurity offering includes the supply of governance, risk and compliance (**GRC**) and assurance consulting, penetration testing to identify and remediate cyber vulnerabilities in networks and systems, and strategic advisory to establish cybersecurity strategies in line with applicable compliance standards, and managed information technology services. Infotrust also resells software and products for advanced security solutions in connection with its consulting engagements, as well as professional assurance and advisory services. Its cybersecurity offerings are primarily directed to commercial and enterprise customers across a range of industries.

Catalyst Cyber, trading as Catalyst Security, is a Canberra-based cybersecurity consultancy business that was established in 2024.

Catalyst Cyber is primarily focussed on the supply of cybersecurity services to Federal Government clients. Its core business comprises GRC and assurance consulting to assist government clients meet regulatory requirements and industry standards and penetration testing to advise on compliance with mandatory information management requirements. Catalyst Cyber resells to a limited degree third-party government-grade security testing software and provides professional services covering governance uplift and accreditation.

had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is a small horizontal overlap between Infotrust and
Catalyst Cyber overlap in the supply of cybersecurity
services in Australia, including a subset of these services.
b. the parties’ combined share in the supply of cybersecurity
services to customers in Australia is low and the increase in
share of supply arising from the Acquisition would be small
c. the presence of alternative suppliers of cybersecurity
services in Australia, including in the supply of the subset of
services where there is a horizontal overlap.
In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to
reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification
thresholds being met.
Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the
ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Warrego Op Co Pty Ltd and Waymark Hotel Properties No 42 Pty Ltd, which are part of the Waymark Hotels Group, proposes to acquire the land and business of the Warrego Hotel Motel in Cunnamulla, Queensland. Waymark Hotels Group owns and operates hotels (pubs) that provide food, drink (including alcohol), gaming machines, accommodation services, and takeaway liquor retailing (bottle shops). It currently operates pubs and bottle shops in regional Queensland and the Northern Territory.

Warrego Hotel Motel is a commercial hotel motel business in Cunnamulla, QLD which offers dining, accommodation, gaming machines and liquor retailing, both in-store via a drive-through bottle shop and online.

determination_table_content.details:
Warrego Op Co Pty Ltd and Waymark Hotel Properties No 42 Pty Ltd,
which are part of the Waymark Hotels Group (Waymark), applied for
a notification waiver in respect of the proposed acquisition of the
land and business of the Warrego Hotel Motel in Cunnamulla,
Queensland, as described in the transaction document provided as
part of the application (the Acquisition). As part of the Acquisition:
 Waymark Hotel Properties No 42 Pty Ltd will acquire land
located at 9 Louise Street and Lot 2 Garden Street in
Cunnamulla, Queensland, 4490 from G.A.A. Pty Limited (as
trustee for the G.A.A. Asset Trust)
 Warrego Op Co Pty Ltd will acquire the commercial hotel
business carried on at that location from G.A.A. Pty Limited (as
trustee for the G.A.A. Asset Trust).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
2 April 2026

Waymark Hotels Group owns and operates hotels (pubs) and
takeaway liquor retailing (bottle shops) in regional Queensland and
the Northern Territory. The Waymark Hotels Group is part of the
Vinta Group, a private real estate group.
The target, Warrego Hotel Motel, is a commercial hotel in
Cunnamulla, Queensland (approximately 750km west of Brisbane)
which offers dining, accommodation, gaming machines and
accommodation services and liquor retailing, both in-store via a
drive-through bottle shop and online. Warrego Hotel Motel is
operated by G.A.A. Pty Limited (Trustee for the G.A.A. Asset Trust).
Waymark and Warrego Hotel Motel both supply dining,
accommodation, gaming and liquor in Queensland.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. there is no local geographic horizontal overlap between
Waymark and Warrego Hotel Motel in the supply of on-
premises or takeaway packaged liquor, dining, gaming or
accommodation services in Cunnamulla or the surrounding
region in Western Queensland
b. there are alternative suppliers of on-premises and takeaway
packaged liquor, dining, gaming and accommodation
services in the broader Cunnamulla and Paroo Shire area.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_name: Egis Projects Asia Pacific - Fulton Hogan Egis O&M

acquirers.name: EGIS PROJECTS ASIA PACIFIC PTY LIMITED

targets.name: FULTON HOGAN EGIS O&M PTY LTD

mergers.merger_description:
Egis Projects Asia Pacific Pty Ltd (**EPAP**) proposes to acquire from Fulton Hogan Industries Pty Ltd (**Fulton Hogan Industries**) the 50% of shares in Fulton Hogan Egis O&M Pty Ltd (**FHE**) that it does not already own.

FHE was incorporated in December 2015 as a joint venture between EPAP and Fulton Hogan Industries. The acquisition involves the effective dissolution of the joint venture between EPAP and Fulton Hogan Industries via FHE and will occur pursuant to a share sale agreement.

FHE supplies operation and maintenance services (i.e., asset management, incident response, maintenance, and capital works to ensure safety, efficiency, and availability) to the WestConnex project in Sydney. WestConnex is a continuous, 33km traffic-light-free motorway network with connection to Sydney Airport and connections for future projects linking the north shore and the southern suburbs.

In future, FHE will supply facilities management services for the under-construction M6 tunnel project, also in Sydney. To date, the supply of facilities management services for the M6 tunnel project is FHE’s only non-WestConnex related business.

Post acquisition, as a wholly-owned subsidiary of EPAP, FHE will continue to provide operations, maintenance and/or facilities management services to current and future customers, and Fulton Hogan Industries will operate entirely independently of both FHE and EPAP.

EPAP is a wholly owned subsidiary of Egis S.A., a France-based engineering, consulting and operation services company. Neither EPAP nor any of its connected entities are involved in the supply of operations, maintenance, and facilities management services for roads and tunnels in Australia, other than through FHE. In Australia, other subsidiaries of Egis S.A. provide façade design and engineering services, building sustainability services, multi-disciplinary engineering consulting services (including rail signalling, rail systems, electrical, transmission/distribution, urban development, water, roads, environmental, and civil), architecture services, and enterprise asset management solutions.

Fulton Hogan Industries provides services in pavement, asphalting, civil construction, and road and asset maintenance across Australia in sectors including defence, roads, and airports.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Egis Projects Asia Pacific Pty Limited (EPAP), which is a wholly
owned subsidiary of Egis S.A. (Egis), applied for a notification waiver
in respect of EPAP’s proposed acquisition of the 50% of shares in
Fulton Hogan Egis O&M Pty Ltd (FHE) which it does not already own,
as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
22 April 2026

The target, FHE, was incorporated in December 2015 as a joint
venture between EPAP and Fulton Hogan Industries Pty Ltd (Fulton
Hogan Industries).
FHE currently supplies operation and maintenance services (i.e.,
asset management, incident response, maintenance, and capital
works to ensure safety, efficiency, and availability) to the
WestConnex project and, in future, will supply facilities management
services for the under-construction M6 tunnel project, also in Sydney.
To date, the supply of facilities management services for the M6
tunnel project is FHE’s only non-WestConnex related business.
The acquirer, EPAP, is a wholly owned subsidiary of Egis S.A., a
France-based engineering, consulting and operation services
company. In Australia, Egis S.A. (via its subsidiaries) provides façade
design and engineering services, building sustainability services,
multi-disciplinary engineering consulting services, architecture
services, and enterprise asset management solutions.
EPAP conducts business development activities relating to
operations and maintenance services supplied by FHE and other
subsidiaries.
The vendor, Fulton Hogan Industries, provides services in pavement,
asphalting, civil construction, and road and asset maintenance
across Australia in sectors including defence, roads, and airports.
Other than through the existing FHE joint venture, neither EPAP nor
any of its connected entities are involved in the supply of operations,
maintenance, and facilities management services for roads and
tunnels in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
• had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. Outside the existing FHE joint venture arrangement, there are
no horizontal overlaps between EPAP (including its
connected entities) and FHE in the supply of operations,
maintenance, and facilities management services for roads
and tunnels in Australia.
b. The Acquisition will not result in any reduction in the number
of suppliers of operations, maintenance, and facilities
management services for roads and tunnels in Australia.
c. There are alternative suppliers of operations, maintenance,
and facilities management services for roads and tunnels in
Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_name: Bendigo and Adelaide Bank – RACQ Bank

targets.name: MEMBERS BANKING GROUP LIMITED (TRADING AS RACQ BANK)

other_parties.name: THE ROYAL AUTOMOBILE CLUB OF QUEENSLAND LIMITED

mergers.merger_description:
Bendigo and Adelaide Bank Limited (**Bendigo and Adelaide Bank**) and Members Banking Group Limited trading as RACQ Bank (**RACQ Bank**) have entered into an agreement for Bendigo and Adelaide Bank to acquire RACQ Bank’s retail loan and deposit books by way of an asset sale (the **Acquisition**).

Bendigo and Adelaide Bank is an Australian, ASX-listed (ASX:BEN) authorised deposit-taking institution (**ADI**) headquartered in Bendigo, Victoria. Established in 1858, Bendigo and Adelaide Bank provides retail banking services to over 2.9m customers through 416 branches across Australia, and has over $73bn of customer deposits and over $84bn of total lending. Bendigo and Adelaide Bank’s customer-facing banking business is split into a consumer division and a business and agribusiness division.

RACQ Bank is an ADI and wholly-owned indirect subsidiary of The Royal Automobile Club of Queensland Limited (**RACQ**). RACQ Bank provides retail loan and deposit banking services, including personal, home and business loans, transactions accounts, savings accounts and term deposits to approximately 90,000 customers. At 30 June 2025, RACQ Bank’s retail business comprised A$2.7bn of retail loans and A$2.5bn of retail deposits.

Upon completion, RACQ Bank customers will be migrated to Bendigo and Adelaide Bank, and RACQ will refer its members to Bendigo and Adelaide Bank for certain banking products under a separate referral arrangement. The referral arrangement will not commence until post-completion.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Woodward pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Bendigo and Adelaide Bank Limited (Bendigo and Adelaide Bank)
applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed
acquisition of Members Banking Group Limited’s, trading as RACQ
Bank (RACQ Bank), retail loan and deposit books by way of an asset
sale, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of
the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
28 April 2026

The acquirer, Bendigo and Adelaide Bank, is an Australian, ASX-listed
(ASX:BEN) authorised deposit-taking institution (ADI) headquartered
in Bendigo, Victoria. Established in 1858, Bendigo and Adelaide Bank
provides retail banking services to over 2.9m customers through 416
branches across Australia, and has over A$73bn of customer
deposits and over A$84bn of total lending. Bendigo and Adelaide
Bank’s customer-facing banking business is split into a consumer
division and a business and agribusiness division.
The target, RACQ Bank, is an ADI and wholly-owned indirect
subsidiary of The Royal Automobile Club of Queensland Limited
(RACQ). RACQ Bank provides retail loan and deposit banking
services, including personal, home and business loans, transactions
accounts, savings accounts and term deposits to approximately
90,000 customers. At 30 June 2025, RACQ Bank’s retail business
comprised A$2.7bn of retail loans and A$2.5bn of retail deposits.
The parties both supply retail lending and retail deposit products in
Queensland and across Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. while there is a horizontal overlap between Bendigo and
Adelaide Bank and RACQ Bank in the supply of retail lending
and retail deposit products in Queensland and nationally,
their combined share in the supply of these products in each
would be low, and the increase in share of supply arising
from the Acquisition would be small
b. there are alternative suppliers of retail lending and retail
deposit products in Queensland and nationally.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Hudson Highland (APAC) Pty Ltd (**HHA**) is the holding company of Hudson Global Resources (Aust) Pty Ltd (**Hudson Global Resources (Aust)**), which provides recruitment and labour hire services in Australia.

Hudson Global Resources (Aust) is an Australian-owned recruitment and talent management agency, operating since 1985 with offices across all major Australian states and territories. They specialize in permanent, contract, and high-volume recruitment for sectors including government, tech, engineering, and finance, placing roughly 5,600 on-hire staff annually.

Oceania Capital Partners Ltd (**Oceania Capital Partners)** is a Sydney based unlisted public company that pursues private equity style investments in businesses. This includes interests in home security, aerospace, broadcasting, and health, among others.

K & K Ryan Pty Ltd as trustee for the K & K Ryan Family Trust is a private investor associated with the Hudson Global Resources (Aust)’s Chief Financial Officer, Kendall Ryan.

Oceania Capital Partners and K & K Ryan Pty Ltd propose to each subscribe for 33.15% of the ordinary shares in HHA under a share subscription agreement.

mergers.merger_description:
Pursuant to an Agreement and Plan of Merger, Asteroid Merger Sub, Inc. (**Merger Sub**), an indirect wholly owned subsidiary of Cognizant Domestic Holdings Corporation (**Cognizant DHC**) proposes to merge with and into Astreya Partners, Inc. (**Astreya**). Astreya will continue as the surviving corporation and become an indirect wholly owned subsidiary of Cognizant Technology Solutions (**Cognizant**), the parent company of Cognizant DHC (the **Acquisition**).

Cognizant operates in Australia through a number of wholly owned indirect subsidiaries (together, **Cognizant Australia**). As part of the Acquisition, Cognizant will also acquire Astreya's Australian subsidiary, Astreya Pty Ltd (**Astreya Australia**).

Cognizant and Astreya supply IT managed services. Cognizant provides IT services including, among other things, consulting, software engineering, cloud services data and AI, cybersecurity, business process outsourcing, and industry-specific solutions.

Astreya is a digital and IT infrastructure managed service provider that is focussed primarily on digital workplace and end-user computing support, as well as delivering cloud and infrastructure managed services. Astreya has limited personnel in Australia that provide IT managed infrastructure services to a small number of multinational clients under global service arrangements.

determination_table_content.details:
Cognizant Domestic Holdings Corporation, wholly owned and
controlled by Cognizant Technology Solutions Corporation
(Cognizant), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its
proposed acquisition of the share capital in Astreya Partners Inc.
(Astreya), as described in the transaction documents provided as
part of the application (the Acquisition).
By way of an Agreement and Plan of Merger, Asteroid Merger Sub,
Inc., an indirect wholly-owned subsidiary of Cognizant, will merge
with and into Astreya, with Astreya continuing as the surviving
corporation as an indirect wholly owned subsidiary of Cognizant.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

Cognizant is a global IT service provider listed on the Nasdaq Stock
Exchange and headquartered in Teaneck (New Jersey), United
States. Cognizant provides various IT services including, among
other things, consulting, software engineering, cloud services data
and AI, cybersecurity, business process outsourcing, and industry
specific solutions. Cognizant began delivering services to customers
in Australia in around 2006 and has registered offices in Sydney and
Melbourne, with satellite offices in Brisbane and Perth. Cognizant
delivers services to clients across a range of industries in Australia
including financial services and insurance, energy and resources,
and retail.
Astreya is a digital and IT infrastructure managed service provider
that is focused primarily on digital workplace services (though
audiovisual technology), as well as delivering cloud, AI and other
infrastructure managed services. Astreya is headquartered in
California in the United States and has a limited presence in
Australia.
Both Cognizant and Astreya supply IT infrastructure managed
services, such as cloud infrastructure, AI and workplace
collaboration services.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. there is a small horizontal overlap between Cognizant and
Astreya in the supply of IT infrastructure managed services
b. Astreya has a small presence in Australia
c. the presence of alternative suppliers of IT infrastructure
managed services in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
