mergers.merger_description:
EGYM SE (together with its subsidiaries, **EGYM**) and Mindbody, Inc. (together with its controlled subsidiaries, **Playlist**) intend to combine in a newly established company, Saturn NewCo, Inc. (**Saturn**), and transfer shares in Saturn to the existing shareholders of EGYM and Playlist.

Playlist is a US-based parent brand of the brands ClassPass, Mindbody and Booker. An aspect of ClassPass’s offering is a fitness and wellbeing aggregation service through which users can obtain access to various fitness, wellbeing and lifestyle experiences such as fitness classes, studios, spas, etc. ClassPass provides subscriptions directly to consumers. Mindbody offers a Software as a Service solution for the fitness, wellbeing and beauty industry, providing back-end infrastructure via a software platform to help fitness studios, wellbeing centres, spas and salons manage their business operations. Booker provides an online booking and business management system specifically for spa facilities, salons and other appointment based service businesses (such as beauty services or wellbeing centres).

EGYM is a German-based provider of smart gym equipment and software to fitness and health facilities, as well as corporate fitness and wellbeing aggregation services (primarily in relation to gyms and fitness clubs) to companies worldwide.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
EGYM SE (together with its subsidiaries, EGYM) and Mindbody, Inc.
(together with its controlled subsidiaries, Playlist) intend to combine
in a newly established company, Saturn NewCo, Inc. (Saturn), and
transfer shares in Saturn to the existing shareholders of EGYM and
Playlist, as described in the transaction documents provided as part
of the application (the Acquisition).
The application for a notification waiver in respect of the Acquisition
was jointly made by:
• Vista Equity Partners Management, LLC (Vista),
• Playlist,
• ClassPass Aggregator, LLC,
• Saturn and its subsidiary Saturn Merger Sub, Inc.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
19 February 2026

Playlist is a US-based parent brand of the brands ClassPass,
Mindbody and Booker. An aspect of ClassPass’s offering is a fitness
and wellbeing aggregation service through which users can obtain
access to various fitness, wellbeing and lifestyle experiences such
as fitness classes, studios, spas, etc. Mindbody offers Software as a
Service (SaaS) solution for the fitness, wellbeing and beauty industry,
providing back-end infrastructure via a software platform to help
fitness studios, wellbeing centres, spas and salons manage their
business operations. Booker provides an online booking and
business management system specifically for spa facilities, salons
and other appointment-based service businesses (such as beauty
services or wellbeing centres).
Playlist is controlled by funds managed and/or controlled by Vista.
Vista is a US-based private equity firm that focuses on financing
enterprise software, data and technology-enabled businesses.
EGYM is a German-based provider of smart gym equipment and
software to fitness and health facilities, as well as corporate fitness
and wellbeing aggregation services (primarily in relation to gyms and
fitness clubs) to companies worldwide. EGYM’s Gymtech offering
provides “smart” gym equipment and technology solutions for
operators of gyms, wellbeing centres, and corporate wellbeing
programs. EGYM’s Wellpass offering is a fitness and wellbeing
aggregation service enabling corporate customers of EGYM to offer
individual fitness and wellbeing benefits to their employees. The
application states that Wellpass does not generate revenue in
Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the
information provided with the notification waiver application and
certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors
in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010
(Cth) (Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is no horizontal overlap between Playlist’s and EGYM’s
offering in Australia
b. there appears to be a low risk of foreclosure or other
concerning vertical or conglomerate effects resulting from
exclusionary conduct, bundling or tying of products post-
Acquisition
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
BetterGrow Holdings Pty Ltd (**Bettergrow**) proposes to acquire 100% of the shares in Hygain Holdco Pty Ltd (**HYG**).

Bettergrow is an Australian-based company in the organic recovery sector. It recovers and re-manufactures organic residuals, waste and by-products, extracting maximum value from materials otherwise destined for landfill, transforming them into quality composts, fertilisers and bespoke soil rehabilitation products for resale. Bettergrow also produces pasture, fodder and beef. It also supplies animal bedding sourced from timber mills.

Bettergrow is part of the Borg Group, a diversified Australian-owned private group of companies and brands involved in the manufacture of building materials, waste recycling operations, forestry, packaging, and engineering and automation services.

HYG is an equine‑nutrition company that formulates and manufactures premium horse feeds and supplements. HYG (through its two brands, Hygain and Mitavite) supplies products to performance, breeding and leisure horse owners through agricultural and equine supplies retailers and wholesalers and distributors in Australia, and globally. HYG operates a manufacturing facility in Victoria and in New South Wales. HYG’s products include manufactured feeds (including pelleted and textured complete feeds and balancers), supplements and additives (including vitamins and minerals, electrolytes, digestive and hoof health products, oil/fat sources and speciality supplements). HYG also offers complimentary nutritional support services for its supplements and commercial feed products.

HYG is controlled by funds under the management of Adamantem Capital, an Australia and New Zealand private equity firm, based in Sydney.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
BetterGrow Holdings Pty Ltd (Bettergrow) applied for a notification
waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the share
capital in HYG Holdco Pty Limited (HYG), as described in the
transaction documents provided as part of the application (the
Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
27 February 2026

The acquirer, Bettergrow, is an Australian-based company in the
organic recovery sector. It recovers and re-manufactures organic
residuals, waste and by-products, extracting maximum value from
materials otherwise destined for landfill, transforming them into
quality composts, fertilisers and bespoke soil rehabilitation products
for resale. Bettergrow also produces pasture, fodder, beef and
supplies animal bedding sourced from timber mills.
Bettergrow is part of the Borg Group, a diversified Australian-owned
private group of companies and brands involved in the manufacture
of building materials, waste recycling operations, forestry, packaging,
and engineering and automation services.
The target, HYG, is an equine‑nutrition company that formulates and
manufactures premium horse feeds and supplements. HYG (through
its two brands, Hygain and Mitavite) supplies products to
performance, breeding and leisure horse owners through agricultural
and equine supplies retailers and wholesalers and distributors in
Australia, and globally. HYG operates a manufacturing facility in
Victoria and in New South Wales. HYG’s products include
manufactured feeds (including pelleted and textured complete feeds
and balancers), supplements and additives (including vitamins and
minerals, electrolytes, digestive and hoof health products, oil/fat
sources and speciality supplements). HYG also offers
complimentary nutritional support services for its supplements and
commercial feed products.
Bettergrow and HYG both supply products to agricultural and farm
supplies stores.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. there is no horizontal or vertical overlap between Bettergrow
and HYG in the supply of horse feed and supplements in
Australia
b. there appears to be a low risk of foreclosure or other
concerning conglomerate effects resulting from
exclusionary conduct, bundling or tying of products.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Zendesk, Inc. (**Zendesk**) proposes to acquire 100% of the business of Forethought Technologies, Inc. (**Forethought**) through a reverse triangular merger whereby Forethought will acquire 100% of the issued shares in a subsidiary of Zendesk which has been newly incorporated for the purpose of the merger. Through this process, Forethought will become a wholly owned subsidiary of Zendesk.

Zendesk is a US-based software company providing software-as-a-service customer relationship management (**CRM**) software designed to improve customer relationships.

Zendesk is owned by affiliates of Permira Holdings Limited (**Permira**) and affiliates of Hellman & Friedman LLC (**H&F**).

Permira is a Guernsey-registered private equity business engaged, through its subsidiaries and affiliates, in the provision of investment management services to a number of investment funds. Permira ultimately controls a number of private equity funds, which include portfolio companies active across the consumer, services, healthcare, technology, and climate sectors.

H&F is a US-based private equity investment firm with a focus on the technology sector, among others.

Permira and H&F also own Genesys, which is a contact-centre-as-a-service (**CCaaS**) provider which develops, manufactures and supplies software products and related services for customer interaction management.

Forethought is a US-based start-up company that markets AI-driven software tools which integrate with CRM software.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Zendesk, Inc (Zendesk) applied for a notification waiver in respect of
its proposed acquisition of 100% of the business of Forethought
Technologies, Inc. (Forethought) through a reverse triangular merger
whereby Forethought will acquire 100% of the issued shares in a
subsidiary of Zendesk which has been newly incorporated for the
purposes of the merger, as described in the transaction documents
provided as part of the application (the Acquisition). Through this
process, Forethought will become a wholly owned subsidiary of
Zendesk.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
6 March 2026

The acquirer, Zendesk, is a US-based software company providing
software-as-a-service customer relationship management (CRM)
software designed to improve customer relationships. Zendesk is
owned by affiliates of Permira Holdings Limited (Permira) and
affiliates of Hellman & Friedman LLC (H&F). Permira is a private
equity business engaged, through its subsidiaries and affiliates, in
the provision of investment management services to a number of
investment funds. Permira ultimately controls a number of private
equity funds, which include portfolio companies active across the
consumer, services, healthcare, technology, and climate sectors.
H&F is a US-based private equity investment firm with a focus on the
technology sector, among others. Permira and H&F also own
Genesys, which is a contact-centre-as-a-service (CCaaS) provider
which develops, manufactures and supplies software products and
related services for customer interaction management.
The target, Forethought, is a US-based start-up company that
markets AI-driven software tools which integrate with CRM software.
Zendesk, Forethought and Genesys provide artificial intelligence (AI)-
based platforms which integrate with CRM software.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
certain industry information in its possession, and had regard to the
factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act
2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. the parties’ combined share of supply of CRM software to
customers in Australia is low
b. Forethought has a small presence in Australia
c. there are alternative suppliers of CRM software and AI-based
platforms for CRM to businesses in Australia
d. there appears to be a low risk of foreclosure or other
concerning vertical or conglomerate effects resulting from
exclusionary conduct, bundling or tying of products post-
acquisition.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Autosports Group Limited (ASX: ASG) (**ASG**) proposes to acquire 100% of the Solitaire Automotive Group business from vendors Toja Investments Pty Ltd as trustee for the Bill Holst Family Trust and David Smoker (the **Acquisition**).

The Acquisition will occur through ASG acquiring 100% interests in the following Solitaire Automotive Group entities:

* Cambridge Motors Pty Ltd, which is the entity owned by David Smoker and holds shares in Russleigh Pty Ltd (**Russleigh**) and units in the Russleigh Unit Trust,
* Russleigh, which is the trustee of the Russleigh Unit Trust, which operates the Solitaire Automotive Group business, and
* the Russleigh Unit Trust.

ASG operates a total of 81 motor vehicle dealerships, 4 used vehicle dealerships, 6 motorcycle dealerships and 8 motor vehicle collision repair facilities located in Sydney, Canberra, Melbourne, Brisbane, Gold Coast and Auckland. The brands of vehicles sold across these dealerships include ALPINA, Aston Martin, Audi, Bentley, BMW, Geely, Kia, Lamborghini, Jaguar Land Rover, Maserati, Mclaren, Mercedes-Benz, MINI, Polestar, Porsche, Rolls-Royce, Subaru, Volkswagen, Volvo and Zeekr.

Solitaire Automotive Group operates 12 motor vehicle dealerships, 2 used vehicle dealerships and 1 motorcycle dealership located in Adelaide. The brands of vehicles sold across these dealerships include Aston Martin, Audi, Cupra, Ducati, Jaguar Land Rover, Maserati, Polestar, Volkswagen, Volkswagen Commercial, Volvo and Zeekr.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Autosports Group Limited (ASG) applied for a notification waiver in
respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% interests in the following
Solitaire Automotive Group (Solitaire) entities:
• Cambridge Motors Pty Ltd, which is the entity used by David
Smoker to hold the shares in Russleigh Pty Ltd (Russleigh)
and units in the Russleigh Unit Trust;
• Russleigh Pty Ltd, which is the trustee of the Russleigh Unit
Trust, which operates the Solitaire Automotive Group
business; and
• the Russleigh Unit Trust,
as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).
The Acquisition consists of ASG acquiring:
• 2,500 of 10,000 ordinary shares (i.e. 25%) in Russleigh and
64,820 of 259,295 units (i.e. 25%) in the Russleigh Unit Trust;
and
• 100% of the shares in Cambridge Motors (which in turn
owns 7,500 of 10,000 ordinary shares in Russleigh (i.e. 75%))
and 194,475 of 259,295 units in the Russleigh Unit Trust (i.e.
75%).
The effect of the Acquisition will be that ASG will acquire 100% of
the business which operates Solitaire Automotive Group's 12 motor
vehicle dealerships, 2 used vehicle dealerships and 1 motorcycle
dealership in Adelaide.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
11 March 2026

The acquirer, ASG, operates a total of 81 motor vehicle dealerships, 4
used vehicle dealerships, 6 motorcycle dealerships and 8 motor
vehicle collision repair facilities located in Sydney, Canberra,
Melbourne, Brisbane, Gold Coast and Auckland. The brands of
vehicles sold across these dealerships include ALPINA, Aston
Martin, Audi, Bentley, BMW, Geely, Kia, Lamborghini, Jaguar Land
Rover, Maserati, Mclaren, MercedesBenz, MINI, Polestar, Porsche,
Rolls-Royce, Subaru, Volkswagen, Volvo and Zeekr.
The target, Solitaire, operates 12 motor vehicle dealerships, 2 used
vehicle dealerships and 1 motorcycle dealership located in Adelaide.
The brands of vehicles sold across these dealerships include Aston
Martin, Audi, Cupra, Ducati, Jaguar Land Rover, Maserati, Polestar,
Volkswagen, Volkswagen Commercial, Volvo and Zeekr.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors
in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010
(Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is no horizontal overlap between ASG and Solitaire in
the supply of new and used motor vehicles, car servicing and
parts in South Australia
b. the presence of alternative dealerships and dealership
groups supplying new and used motor vehicles, car servicing
and parts locally and nationally
c. while ASG is one of the largest motor vehicle dealers in
Australia, the increment to ASG’s share of supply arising
from the Acquisition would be small
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Aldus Group (**Aldus**) is an Australian diversified industrial group. In Australia, Aldus primarily distributes digital printing and industrial coding equipment, manufactures and supplies industrial inks, decorative foils and thermal transfer ribbons, and provides label applicator systems and associated servicing. Aldus also operates a small precision engineering division providing computer numerical control (**CNC**) machining services and medical engineering division.

The target, Hilton Manufacturing Pty Ltd (**Hilton**), is an Australian sheet metal fabrication business. Hilton manufactures engineered metal products and assemblies for customers in the heavy vehicle and industrial sectors.

Aldus Pty Limited will acquire 100% of the issued shares in Hilton via a share sale structured on an enterprise value basis, subject to customary working capital adjustments and deferred consideration arrangements. Hilton will continue to operate as a wholly owned subsidiary within Aldus following completion.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Aldus Pty Ltd (Aldus) has applied for a notification waiver in respect
of Aldus Manufacturing Pty Ltd’s proposed acquisition of Hilton
Manufacturing Pty Ltd (Hilton), as described in the transaction
document provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
31 March 2026

The Aldus Group primarily distributes digital printing and industrial
coding equipment, manufactures and supplies industrial inks,
decorative foils and thermal transfer ribbons, and provides label
applicator systems and associated servicing. Aldus also operates a
precision engineering division providing CNC machining services and
a medical engineering division. Aldus Manufacturing Pty Ltd is a
wholly owned subsidiary of Aldus, established for this transaction.
The target, Hilton, is an Australian sheet metal fabrication business.
Hilton manufactures engineered metal products and assemblies,
such as bull bars and fuel tanks, for customers in the heavy vehicle
and industrial sectors.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular, there is no horizontal overlap
or vertical relationship between the parties.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_name: Star Hotels Group – Liquor Legends Berserker & Liquor Legends Highway A1, QLD

targets.name: The Trustee for The Garden Holdings Trust

mergers.merger_description:
The transaction involves the acquisition by Migaloo QLD Pty Ltd, a subsidiary of Star Consolidated Holdings Pty Ltd and its controlled entities (**Star Hotels Group**), of:

* the leasehold interest in the land that the Liquor Legends Berserker and Liquor Legends Highway A1 stores operate on; and
* business assets including goodwill, fixtures, fittings and all licences involved with the Liquor Legends Berserker and Liquor Legends Highway A1 stores.

The Star Hotels Group is an independently owned and operated hospitality group, primarily operating pubs along the eastern seaboard of Queensland with 4 venues in South Australia. It operates multiple licensed venues and liquor retail outlets under brands such as Star Liquor, Sense of Taste and Bob’s Bulk Booze, as well as accommodation at selected venues.

Liquor Legends Berserker and Liquor Legends Highway A1 are bottle shop businesses which are located at 169 High Street, Berserker, QLD 4701 and 86 Derby Street, Rockhampton, QLD 4700, respectively. These outlets sell a range of alcoholic beverages to retail customers. Liquor Legends Berserker and Liquor Legends Highway A1 Liquor are currently owned and operated by The Trustee for The Garden Holdings Trust.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Migaloo QLD Pty Ltd, a subsidiary of Star Consolidated Holdings Pty
Ltd and its controlled entities (Star Hotels Group), applied for a
notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of:
• the leasehold interest in the land that the Liquor Legends
Berserker and Liquor Legends Highway A1 stores operate on;
and
• business assets including goodwill, fixtures, fittings and all
licences involved with the Liquor Legends Berserker and Liquor
Legends Highway A1 stores,
as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(b) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) not to make the notification waiver
determination applied for.
2 April 2026

The Star Hotels Group is an independently owned and operated
hospitality group, primarily operating pubs along the eastern
seaboard of Queensland with 4 venues in South Australia. It operates
multiple licensed venues and liquor retail outlets under brands such
as Star Liquor, Sense of Taste and Bob’s Bulk Booze, as well as
accommodation at selected venues.
Liquor Legends Berserker and Liquor Legends Highway A1 are bottle
shop businesses which are located at 169 High Street, Berserker,
Queensland 4701 and 86 Derby Street, Rockhampton, Queensland
4700, respectively. Liquor Legends Berserker and Liquor Legends
Highway A1 Liquor are currently owned and operated by The Trustee
for The Garden Holdings Trust.
The Star Hotels Group and Liquor Legends Berserker and Liquor
Legends Highway A1 stores overlap in the retail supply of liquor to
customers in local areas in Rockhampton.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the
information provided with the notification waiver application and had
regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Act.
Based on the information provided in the application, including the
parties’ revenue in Australia, the ACCC considers that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds would be
met.
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition may raise competition issues. In
particular:
a. there is horizontal overlap between the Star Hotels Group
and the Liquor Legends Berserker and Liquor Legends
Highway A1 stores in the retail supply of packaged liquor for
off-site consumption, in the local area surrounding those
stores and the broader Rockhampton area
b. Star Hotel Group’s share of the retail supply of packaged
liquor in Rockhampton is likely to be significant
c. there is uncertainty regarding the level of competitive
constraint likely to be exerted from alternative retail
suppliers of packaged liquor for off-site consumption, in the
local areas surrounding the Liquor Legends Berserker and
Liquor Legends Highway A1 stores and in the broader
Rockhampton area.
The ACCC considers that these potential competition issues warrant
consideration through the notification process, including via
consultation with third parties.
Given the above, the ACCC has determined not to make the
notification waiver determination applied for.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Vets Central Holdings Pty Ltd (together, with its subsidiaries, **Vets Central**), proposes to acquire 100% of the shares on issue in Drovers Vet Hospital Pty Ltd (**Drovers Vet Hospital**) from Dr Wayne Shipham and Raylene Shipham as trustees for the McGuire Family Trust.

Vets Central is an Australian-owned veterinary group that owns and operates 81 first opinion veterinary practices across Australia and New Zealand. In Australia, Vets Central’s practices provide core and ancillary veterinary services, for household pets as well as livestock, and other exotic species. The services primarily include routine, planned, and emergency veterinary services (excluding after-hours services).

Vets Central was acquired by Pemba Capital Partners in September 2021 and sits within Pemba Capital Fund II.

Drovers Vet Hospital is a full-service veterinary practice providing a range of first opinion core and ancillary veterinary services for household pets (extending to exotic pets) in the Wanneroo area of Western Australia.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Vets Central Holdings Pty Ltd (together with its subsidiaries, Vets
Central) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed
acquisition of 100% of the shares on issue in Drovers Vet Hospital
Pty Ltd (Drovers Vet Hospital), as described in the transaction
documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
7 April 2026

The acquirer, Vets Central, is an Australian-owned veterinary group
that owns and operates 81 first opinion veterinary practices across
Australia and New Zealand. In Australia, Vets Central’s practices
provide core and ancillary veterinary services, for household pets as
well as livestock, and other exotic species. The services primarily
include routine, planned, and emergency veterinary services
(excluding after-hours services).
The target, Drovers Vet Hospital, is a full-service veterinary practice
providing a range of first opinion core and ancillary veterinary
services for household pets (extending to exotic pets) in the
Wanneroo area of Western Australia.
Vets Central and Drovers Vet Hospital both supply first opinion
veterinary services to household pets, livestock, and exotic species
in Western Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is no horizontal overlap between Vets Central and
Drovers Vet Hospital in the supply of veterinary services in
the Wanneroo area
b. there are alternative suppliers of veterinary services on a
local, state, and national basis.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
CleanPeak Energy Holdings Pty Ltd, through its wholly-owned subsidiary CleanPeak IFM Projects Pty Ltd as trustee for CleanPeak IFM Projects Trust (**CleanPeak IFM**) (together referred to as **CleanPeak**), proposes to acquire 100% of the shares in Sustainable Energy Infrastructure Holdings Pty Ltd and 100% of the units in the Sustainable Energy Infrastructure Holdings Trust (**SEI**) from CareSuper Pty Ltd as trustee for CareSuper and State Street Australia Ltd as custodian for Australian Retirement Trust Pty Ltd as trustee for Australian Retirement Trust (the **Acquisition**).

CleanPeak is a developer and operator of renewable energy infrastructure, including solar farms and battery energy storage systems, and a supplier of renewable energy and clean energy solutions through solar and battery energy assets to large industrial and commercial customers predominantly in New South Wales. CleanPeak also provides asset management services for compressed natural gas facilities on the premises of industrial customers in Victoria, New South Wales and Queensland. In 2025, KKR & Co. Inc. (together with its subsidiaries, **KKR**) acquired a significant interest in CleanPeak.

KKR is a global investment firm that offers alternative asset management as well as capital markets and insurance solutions. KKR sponsors investment funds that invest in private equity, credit, and real assets and has strategic partners that manage hedge funds.

SEI is a sustainable energy infrastructure investor which owns a diversified portfolio of small scale (up to 5MW) solar farms and battery energy storage systems in Victoria, New South Wales and South Australia as well as a compressed natural gas facility located in Queensland.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Woodward pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
CleanPeak Energy Holdings Pty Ltd and its wholly-owned subsidiary
CleanPeak IFM Projects Pty Ltd as trustee for CleanPeak IFM
Projects Trust (CleanPeak IFM) (together referred to as CleanPeak)
applied for a notification waiver in respect of CleanPeak IFM’s
proposed acquisition of 100% of the shares in Sustainable Energy
Infrastructure Holdings Pty Ltd and 100% of the units in the
Sustainable Energy Infrastructure Holdings Trust (SEI), as described
in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the
Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, CleanPeak, is a developer and operator of renewable
energy infrastructure, including solar farms and battery energy
storage systems, and a supplier of renewable energy and clean
energy solutions through solar and battery energy assets to large
industrial and commercial customers predominantly in New South
Wales. CleanPeak also provides asset management services for
compressed natural gas facilities on the premises of industrial
customers in Victoria, New South Wales and Queensland. In 2025,
KKR & Co. Inc. (together with its subsidiaries, KKR) acquired a
significant interest in CleanPeak.
KKR is a global investment firm that offers alternative asset
management as well as capital markets and insurance solutions.
KKR sponsors investment funds that invest in private equity, credit,
and real assets and has strategic partners that manage hedge funds.
The target, SEI, is a sustainable energy infrastructure investor which
owns a diversified portfolio of small scale (up to 5MW) solar farms
and battery energy storage systems (BESS) in Victoria, New South
Wales and South Australia as well as a compressed natural gas
facility located in Queensland.
The parties develop, own and operate renewable energy assets (in
particular, solar farms and BESS) and supply electricity generation in
the National Electricity Market (NEM) in Victoria, NSW and South
Australia. The parties also operate compressed natural gas (CNG)
facilities in Queensland.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. there is a small horizontal overlap between the parties in the
supply of electricity generation in the NEM in Victoria, NSW,
South Australia and nationally
b. the parties’ combined share in the supply of electricity
generation in the NEM in Victoria, NSW, South Australia and
nationally are estimated to be low and the increase in share
of supply arising from the Acquisition would be small
c. there are alternative suppliers of wholesale electricity
generation in the NEM in Victoria, NSW, South Australia and
nationally
d. there is no overlap between the parties’ operations in
relation to CNG
e. there appears to be a low risk of foreclosure or other
concerning vertical effects resulting from exclusionary
conduct post-acquisition.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Hillhouse, through Hermes Bidco, proposes to acquire a majority interest in KMC Solutions Hong Kong Limited.

Hillhouse is a global alternative investment manager that deploys capital across multiple strategies, including public equities, private equity, private credit, and real assets. Through its private equity arm, Hillhouse invests in long-term global opportunities across the business services, healthcare, consumer, and industrials sectors.

One of Hillhouse’s investments is in Ascentium, a Singapore-based platform focusing on trust and corporate services in Asia-Pacific region, including in Australia. It provides administration, compliance, and business solution, which includes employer of record (**EOR**) or Professional Employer Organisation (**PEO**) services.

The target, KMC Solutions is a business that primarily operates in the Philippines, supplying workspace and EOR and PEO services, including to Australia.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Woodward pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Hermes Bidco Limited, a subsidiary of Hillhouse Investment
Management, Ltd (Hillhouse), applied for a notification waiver in
respect of its proposed acquisition of a majority interest in KMC
Solutions Hong Kong Limited (KMC Solutions), as described in the
transaction documents provided as part of the application (the
Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

Hillhouse is a global alternative investment manager that deploys
capital across multiple strategies, including public equities, private
equity, private credit, and real assets. Through its private equity arm,
Hillhouse invests in long-term global opportunities across the
business services, healthcare, consumer, and industrials sectors.
One of Hillhouse’s investments is in Ascentium, a Singapore-based
platform focusing on trust and corporate services in Asia-Pacific
region, including in Australia. It provides administration, compliance,
and business solution, which includes employer of record (EOR) or
Professional Employer Organisation (PEO) services.
KMC Solutions is a business that primarily operates in the
Philippines, supplying workspace and outsourced HR services
including to Australia. KMC Solutions supplies physical workspaces
in the Philippines, with strategic locations in financial hubs or
business districts. KMC Solutions manages these workspaces
through providing 24/7 access and facilitating reliable internet
network infrastructure. KMC Solutions also offers end-to-end
outsourced HR services centred around workforces in the
Philippines. KMC Solutions recruits individuals for companies
around the world (including Australian companies) interested in
establishing an offshore workforce primarily from the Philippines, as
well as Vietnam, Mexico and Colombia. It also handles related HR
services in the Philippines such as initial talent recruitment and
negotiation of employment contracts, onboarding, training and
continuous payroll services. As part of its end-to-end outsourced
solution, it offers EOR and PEO services.
EOR or PEO services allows organisations to establish and manage
an offshore workforce. An EOR service provider will be the legal
employer of the person being hired. In contrast, a PEO service
provider will enter into a co-employment relationship with the
organisation and the person being hired.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is a small horizontal overlap between Hillhouse and
KMC Solutions in the supply of offshore recruitment and HR
services to Australian customers
b. there are alternative suppliers of offshore recruitment and
HR services to Australian customers
c. there is a low risk of foreclosure or other concerning vertical
or conglomerate effects resulting from exclusionary
conduct, bundling or tying of offshore corporate services
and offshore recruitment and HR services to Australian
customers post-acquisition.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Alcoa Gallium Holdings Pty Ltd (**AGH**, a wholly owned subsidiary of Alcoa of Australia Limited (**AoA**)), the United States Department of War (**DOW**), and the Australian Government (through Export Finance Australia (**EFA**)) propose to subscribe to shares in a recently incorporated Australian special purpose vehicle, United States-Australia Gallium Pty Ltd (**United States-Australia Gallium SPV**).

United States-Australia Gallium SPV will subsequently acquire an interest in the assets of an unincorporated joint venture (**Gallium UJV**) to be formed between United States-Australia Gallium SPV and Japan Australia Gallium Associates Pty Ltd (**JAGA**). The Gallium UJV will develop, construct and operate a gallium production plant next to AoA's Wagerup alumina refinery in Western Australia (the **Gallium Project**). As part of the acquisition, United States-Australia Gallium SPV will acquire an entitlement under the Gallium UJV to take in kind its proportionate share of the Gallium UJV’s gallium production. The acquisition comprises the proposed acquisition of shares in United States-Australia Gallium SPV and the entitlement to gallium offtake from the Gallium Project.

The Gallium UJV will start a new business in Australia to develop, manufacture and supply critical goods, specifically gallium, intended for Japanese, US and Australian industrial use, as well as other commercial purposes.

AoA is the primary vehicle for Alcoa Corporation's Australian operations. AoA is a major alumina and aluminium producer and its main operations are: mining bauxite at two locations in the Darling Range, Western Australia (Huntly and Willowdale); refining bauxite into alumina at two refineries in Western Australia; operating shipping terminals at Kwinana and Bunbury in Western Australia and Portland in Victoria; and holding a 55% interest in the Portland aluminium smelter in western Victoria, the day-to-day operations of which AoA manages.

EFA is the Australian Government's export credit agency, established as a corporation under the *Export Finance and Insurance Corporation Act 1991* (Cth). Its purpose is to support Australian export trade, overseas investment and infrastructure development that benefits Australia.  EFA's role in the Gallium Project is as an investor supporting the development of the Gallium Project.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Alcoa Gallium Holdings Pty Ltd (a wholly owned subsidiary of Alcoa
of Australia Limited (AoA)), the United States Department of War
(DOW), and the Australian Government (through Export Finance
Australia (EFA)) applied for a notification waiver in respect of their
proposed acquisition of:
• shares in a recently incorporated Australian special purpose
vehicle, United States-Australia Gallium Pty Ltd (United
States-Australia Gallium SPV), and
• an entitlement to a proportionate share of gallium produced
by a proposed gallium production plant next to AoA’s
Wagerup alumina refinery in Western Australia to be
developed, constructed and operated by an unincorporated
joint venture between United States-Australia Gallium SPV
and Japan Australia Gallium Associates Pty Ltd,
as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

AoA is a major alumina and aluminium producer, and its main
operations are mining bauxite at two locations in the Darling Range,
Western Australia, refining bauxite into alumina at two refineries in
Western Australia, operating shipping terminals at Kwinana and
Bunbury in Western Australia and Portland in Victoria, and holding a
55% interest in the Portland aluminium smelter in western Victoria.
The DOW is a United States government agency.
EFA is the Australian Government’s export credit agency, whose
purpose is to support Australian export trade, overseas investment,
and infrastructure development that benefits Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application, and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular, the Acquisition seeks to introduce a new
producer and supplier of primary gallium in Australia and does not
involve the horizontal aggregation of any existing gallium production
capacity.
In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to
reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification
thresholds being met.
Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the
ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Germany's state-owned promotional bank, Kreditanstalt für Wiederaufbau (**KfW**), acting pursuant to a Special Federal Mandate on behalf of the Federal Government of Germany, proposes to acquire a minority shareholding of up to 40% of the issued share capital of KNDS N.V. (**KNDS**).

KfW is a public law institution (*Anstalt des öffentlichen Rechts*) based in Frankfurt and governed by the *Law Concerning Kreditanstalt für Wiederaufbau* (the **KfW Act**). The Federal Republic of Germany holds 80% of the share capital of KfW, with the Federal States of Germany together holding the remaining 20%. KfW's mandate is to finance and promote sustainable development. KfW has no Australian subsidiary or physical presence in Australia.

KNDS is a Franco-German business association in the land defence sector. KNDS, together with its subsidiaries, is active in the development, manufacture and support of a product portfolio ranging from highly protected wheeled vehicles through to antiaircraft and artillery systems to main battle tanks, infantry fighting vehicles and bridge laying systems. KNDS has no Australian subsidiary or physical presence in Australia.

KNDS is currently jointly controlled by GIAT Industries S.A., France and Wegmann & Co. GmbH, which each hold 50% of its share capital. KfW proposes to acquire its interest in KNDS from Wegmann (which will retain a non-controlling minority stake in KNDS).

mergers.merger_description:
Arena HoldCo Pty Ltd, through its subsidiary, Arena BidCo Pty Ltd (Arena) proposes to acquire:

(a) 100% of the issued share capital in GameOn Holdings Pty Ltd (**GameOn**) and its subsidiaries PlayHQ Sports Pty Ltd (**PlayHQ**), PlayHQ Sports UK Limited and PlayHQ Sports Ltd from its current shareholders; and

(b) 100% of the issued share capital in RFGI Group Pty Ltd, a shareholder of GameOn,

(together, the **Acquisition**).

Following completion of the Acquisition, it is anticipated that RFGI’s shares in GameOn will be transferred to Arena, followed by the deregistration for RFGI.

Arena is an indirect subsidiary of ASG IV, LLC and part of the broader Alpine Software Group (**ASG**) investment structure focusing on acquisitions and investments in enterprise software companies. ASG is an acquirer of software as a service (**SaaS**) businesses.

GameOn is a software platform provider that simplifies the administration and management of sports, through its two SaaS platforms, PlayHQ and TeamPay.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Arena BidCo Pty Ltd (Arena), a subsidiary of Arena HoldCo Pty Ltd
and part of the Alpine Software Group (ASG), applied for a
notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of the
following, as described in the transaction documents provided as
part of the application:
a. 100% of the issued share capital in GameOn Holdings Pty
Ltd (GameOn) and its subsidiaries PlayHQ Sports Pty Ltd,
PlayHQ Sports UK Limited and PlayHQ Sports Ltd from its
current shareholders; and
b. 100% of the issued share capital in RFGI Group Pty Ltd
(RFGI), a shareholder of GameOn,
(the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
29 January 2026

Alpine Investors is a private equity firm headquartered in San
Francisco, California. ASG is a portfolio company of Alpine Investors
which buys and invests in software as a service (SaaS) businesses in
a range of industries. ASG provides software services to a range of
industries including health & human services, hospitality, real estate,
non-profits, compliance, manufacturing and legal. The acquirer,
Arena, is part of the ASG group and is ultimately wholly owned by
Alpine Fund IX which is Alpine Investors’ latest flagship fund.
The Acquisition target consists of GameOn, its subsidiaries and
RFGI. GameOn is a sports software platform provider, which offers
two SaaS platforms in Australia, known as PlayHQ and TeamPay.
PlayHQ simplifies the administration and management of sports for
participants, clubs, leagues and national/state governing bodies,
with features such as registrations, payments, fixtures, live scoring,
statistics and competition management. TeamPay is a payment
solution that allows for teams and players to pay game fees to one
streamlined and secure platform. TeamPay is available as a
standalone platform or through the PlayHQ platform. RFGI is
Basketball Victoria Inc.’s holding company for its shares in GameOn.
It does not otherwise have any commercial operations or activities.
Both ASG and GameOn are active in the provision of software
services.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the
information provided with the notification waiver application and had
regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. There is no horizontal overlap between Alpine Investors’
portfolio companies and PlayHQ in the supply of sports
management software in Australia.
b. There are a number of alternative suppliers of sports
management software in Australia.
c. While there is a potential conglomerate relationship between
Alpine Investors’ or ASG’s portfolio companies and GameOn,
the risk of the merged entity foreclosing rival suppliers of
sports management software or other software in Australia
appears to be low, given the availability of other suppliers.
Given the above considerations that material competition concerns
are unlikely to arise, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to
reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification
thresholds being met.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Russell Investment Group Pty Ltd (**Russell**) proposes to acquire 100 per cent of the issued and outstanding shares of Zurich Investment Management Limited (**Zurich**) through a share sale agreement (the **Acquisition**).

Russell provides investment and asset management services in Australia.

Zurich is an Australian incorporated member of the global Zurich Insurance Group. The ultimate holding company is Zurich Insurance Group Ltd (incorporated in Switzerland). Zurich provides managed fund solutions to Australian investors. The Acquisition only relates to Zurich, rather than the broader Zurich Insurance Group.

Both Russell and Zurich supply investment and asset management services in Australia, including issuing interests in Australian and global equities funds, property securities funds, fixed interest funds, diversified or multi-asset funds and cash funds to Australian investors.

notified.
12 March 2026

The acquirer, Russell, provides investment and asset management
services in Australia.
The target, Zurich, is an Australian incorporated member of the
global Zurich Insurance Group. The ultimate holding company is
Zurich Insurance Group Ltd (incorporated in Switzerland). Zurich
provides managed fund solutions to Australian investors. The
Acquisition only relates to Zurich, rather than the broader Zurich
Insurance Group.
Both Russell and Zurich’s supply investment and asset management
services in Australia, including issuing interests in Australian and
global equities funds, property securities funds, fixed interest funds,
diversified or multi-asset funds and cash funds to Australian
investors.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors
in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010
(Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is a small horizontal overlap between Russell and
Zurich in the supply of investment and asset management
services
b. the parties’ combined share in the supply of investment and
asset management services is low
c. there are alternative suppliers of investment and asset
management services in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Admiral AU Bidco Pty Ltd (**Admiral Bidco**) intends to acquire 100% of the Eptec Defence Solutions (**EDS**) business by acquiring 100% of the issued shares in Eptec Holdco Pty Ltd and, indirectly, its subsidiaries Eptec Marine Pty Ltd and Eptec Bidco Pty Ltd (together the **Target Group)**, from the current shareholders (the **Acquisition**).

Admiral Bidco is a newly established fund that is ultimately controlled by Arlington Capital Partners (**ACP**), a US-based private equity investment firm that acquires, manages, and grows portfolio companies in sectors such as defence & aerospace, government & technology, healthcare, and business services software.

Eptec Group carries on two businesses in Australia: EDS and Eptec Infrastructure Solutions. EDS specialises in the maintenance, preservation, and remediation of naval vessels and defence infrastructure. Prior to the Acquisition, the Eptec Group will be restructured so that the Eptec Infrastructure Solutions business will be transferred out of the Target Group and retained by the seller.

mergers.merger_description:
Princeton Digital Group (Australia Alpha) Prop Pty Ltd (**Princeton Digital Group Australia**), as trustee of Princeton Digital Group (Australia Alpha) Prop Trust, proposes to acquire land located in Campbelltown, NSW from MH Property No. 1 Pty Ltd (**MH Property**)(the **Acquisition**).

The Acquisition is for the purpose of building, owning and operating a data centre in Australia. Once developed, Princeton Digital Group Australia intends to supply data centre facilities and associated infrastructure to tenants in Australia.

The land is currently vacant and contains no existing fixtures, structures or buildings. The land will be acquired by way of a land title transfer from MH Property to Princeton Digital Group Australia .

Princeton Digital Group Australia is an indirectly wholly owned subsidiary of Princeton Digital Group Limited (**Princeton Digital Group**), a Singapore headquartered group which operates a data centre business across more than 20 data centres in seven countries – Singapore, Japan, China, Indonesia, India, Malaysia and South Korea. Princeton Digital Group does not currently own or operate any data centre assets in Australia.

MH Property is an Australian property developer which owns land in New South Wales.

The Acquisition is for the purpose of building, owning and operating

mergers.merger_description:
Mizuho Securities Co. Ltd (**Mizuho**) proposes to acquire an interest of between 61.60% and 78.30% in Avendus Capital Private Limited (**Avendus**) (the **Acquisition**).

Mizuho is a subsidiary of Mizuho Financial Group, a full service Japanese financial services company. Mizuho through its connected entities, supplies financial services within Australia, including business banking, financial advisory and auto finance and car leasing.

Avendus is an Indian investment bank and financial services firm that provides bespoke solutions in the areas of investment banking, institutional equities, wealth management, asset management and credit solutions. Founded in 1999, Avendus operates in India, the United States, Singapore and United Kingdom through its subsidiaries. It is a Securities and Exchange Board of India registered merchant banker.

Avendus has limited operations in Australia, supplying some research analyst and stock broking services via its wholly owned subsidiary, Spark Institutional Equities Private Limited.

mergers.merger_description:
CRH Americas Products, Inc., (**CRH Americas**) and Oldcastle Infrastructure, Inc. (**Oldcastle**) propose to acquire 100% of the partnership interests and voting rights in Axius Water Parent LP (**Axius Water**) (the **Acquisition**). The Acquisition will result in CRH Americas and Oldcastle having sole control of Axius Water.

CRH Americas is a Delaware-incorporated intermediate holding company. It does not directly manufacture or supply goods or services.

Oldcastle is a US-based supplier of engineered concrete products related to water, communications, energy and transportation infrastructure with a focus on sustainability. Oldcastle has no presence in Australia.

CRH Americas and Oldcastle are owned by CRH Plc (**CRH**), a global supplier of building materials and infrastructure products headquartered in Ireland and listed on the New York Stock Exchange.

CRH has various interests in Australia, including CTC Precast Pty Ltd (**Civilmart**). Civilmart supplies precast concrete infrastructure products, including stormwater and drainage components, stormwater quality improvement devices, sewer chambers and pump stations, and domestic aerated wastewater treatment systems.

Hydro International is a CRH subsidiary that supplies water filtration products globally. Hydro International supplies a very small volume of sludge screens, and stormwater separators and filters, which are used in primary treatment of wastewater, into Australia via direct supply channels.

Axius Water is a US based supplier of nutrient removal solutions for municipal and industrial wastewater and water treatment processes via a portfolio of brands (Aero-Mod, atac, EDI, Eosi, MITAwt, Napier-Reid, Nexom and triplepoint). Axius Water is currently majority-owned by investment funds, vehicles and/or accounts advised and managed by various subsidiaries of KKR & Co. Inc.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
CRH Plc (CRH), via CRH Americas Products, Inc. (CRH Americas)
and Oldcastle Infrastructure, Inc. (Oldcastle), applied for a
notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of
the partnership interests and voting rights in Axius Water Parent LP
(Axius Water), as described in the transaction documents provided
as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The ultimate acquirer, CRH, is a global supplier of building materials
and infrastructure products headquartered in Ireland and listed on
the New York Stock Exchange. CRH Americas and Oldcastle would
have sole control of Axius Water.
CRH Americas is an intermediate holding company which does not
directly manufacture or supply goods or services. Oldcastle is a US-
based supplier of engineered concrete products related to water,
communications, energy and transportation infrastructure with a
focus on sustainability. It has no presence in Australia.
CTC Precast Pty Ltd (Civilmart), which is indirectly owned by CRH, is
a supplier of precast concrete infrastructure products in Australia,
including stormwater and drainage components, stormwater quality
improvement devices, sewer chambers and pump stations, and
domestic aerated wastewater treatment systems.
Hydro International is a CRH subsidiary that supplies water filtration
products, including sludge screens and stormwater separators and
filters, into Australia
The target, Axius Water, is a US based supplier of nutrient removal
solutions and process-intensive treatment for municipal and
industrial waste-water and water treatment processes via brands
including Aero-Mod, atac, EDI, Eosi, MITAwt, Napier-Reid, Nexom and
triplepoint. Axius Water has minimal export sales into Australia and
no operations, assets, employees or subsidiaries in Australia.
CRH and its controlled portfolio companies, and Axius Water, both
supply wastewater treatment solutions in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. Axius Water has a small presence in Australia
b. while there may be a small horizontal or complementary
relationship between the products and services supplied by
CRH (including its controlled portfolio companies) and Axius
Water, there are alternative suppliers of these products and
services in Australia.
In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to
reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification
thresholds being met.
Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the
ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_name: FC Group Holdings Corporation & ors - Fluent Retail
mergers.merger_description:
Fluent Retail Pty Ltd (**Fluent Retail**) operates in the retail technology and enterprise software industry, supplying cloud-based distributed order management system software as a SaaS platform. Its solutions serve omnichannel retailers, e-commerce businesses, and grocery and fashion retailers, enabling real-time inventory visibility and order orchestration across multiple fulfillment locations.

Fluent Retail, FC Group Holdings Corporation (**FC Group Holdings**), Fluent Commerce MidCo (**FC MidCo**) and Fluent Commerce Holdings, Inc (**FC HoldCo**) propose to undertake an internal reorganisation resulting in a US redomicile that will occur in 3 stages (together, the **Acquisition**).

Firstly, all Fluent Retail shares will be transferred to FC Group Holdings in exchange for equivalent classes of FC Group Holdings shares. Following the transfer, Fluent Retail shareholders will be the sole shareholders of FC Group Holdings, holding the same number and proportion of equivalent shares as they held in Fluent Retail immediately prior (i.e. the overall ownership structure of the group will remain unchanged).

Second, immediately after the transfer to FC Group Holdings, all the shares of Fluent Retail are immediately transferred to FC Group Holdings's wholly owned subsidiary, FC MidCo, by way of contribution of capital without further issue of shares.

Third, on completion of the transfer to FC MidCo, all the shares of Fluent Retail are immediately transferred by FC MidCo to its wholly owned subsidiary, FC HoldCo, by way of contribution of capital without further issue of shares.

FC Group Holdings, FC MidCo and FC HoldCo are vehicles for the purpose of the Acquisition and do not engage in business or otherwise supply any goods or services.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Woodward pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
FC Group Holdings Corporation (FC Group Holdings), Fluent
Commerce MidCo Inc. (FC MidCo) and Fluent Commerce Holdings
Inc. (FC HoldCo) applied for a notification waiver in respect of a US
redomicile occurring in three stages:
• the transfer of all the Fluent Retail Pty Ltd (Fluent Retail) shares
to FC Group Holdings in exchange for equivalent classes of FC
Group Holdings shares, following which the Fluent Retail
shareholders will be the sole shareholders of FC Group Holdings,
holding the same number and proportion of equivalent shares as
they held in Fluent Retail immediately prior
• the transfer of all the Fluent Retail shares from FC Group
Holdings to its wholly-owned subsidiary, FC MidCo, by way of
contribution of capital without further issue of shares
• the transfer of all the Fluent Retail shares from FC MidCo to its
wholly-owned subsidiary, FC HoldCo, by way of contribution of
capital without further issue of shares,
as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

Fluent Retail operates in the retail technology and enterprise
software industry, supplying cloud-based distributed order
management system (OMS) software as a SaaS platform. Its
solutions serve omnichannel retailers, e-commerce businesses, and
grocery and fashion retailers, enabling real-time inventory visibility
and order orchestration across multiple fulfillment locations.
FC Group Holdings, FC MidCo and FC HoldCo are vehicles for the
purpose of the Acquisition and do not engage in business or
otherwise supply any goods or services.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular, there is no horizontal
consolidation, removal of a competitor, or change to Fluent Retail’s
competitive position as a result of the Acquisition.
In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to
reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification
thresholds being met.
Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the
ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
The ACCC also considered the potential application of section
51ABD of the Act. The application states that on the facts of this
matter, including the particular entities and structures involved, it
was not clear that the Acquisition fell within that provision. Having
regard to the particular circumstances, including the consequences
of non-notification in reliance on the exemption if it was found not to
apply, the ACCC considered it appropriate to make a determination
on the basis that the acquisition provisions in the Act apply to the
Acquisition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
