mergers.merger_name: Sydney Water - Windsor Sewerage Scheme Assets
mergers.merger_description:
Sydney Water Corporation (**SWC**) proposes to acquire from the Hawkesbury City Council (**HCC**) the following assets of the Windsor Sewerage Scheme in New South Wales:

* the South Windsor wastewater treatment plant; and
* the pumping stations and reticulation system (pipes and mains) required to supply wastewater services to customers in the Hawkesbury City local government area (the **Acquisition**).

SWC is a State Owned Corporation established under the *Sydney Water Act 1994* and *State Owned Corporations Act 1989*. SWC provides water and wastewater services across the greater Sydney metropolitan region, but it currently does not provide wastewater services for most of the Hawkesbury City local government area. However, Sydney Water does provide drinking water services for most of the Hawkesbury City local government area.

The HCC is a body politic of the State of NSW constituted under the *Local Government Act 1993*. HCC provides wastewater services to properties in part of the Hawkesbury City local government area through the Windsor Sewerage Scheme. The Windsor Sewerage Scheme comprises two wastewater treatment plants at McGraths Hill and South Windsor, 21 pumping stations and the associated reticulation systems.

The McGraths Hill wastewater treatment plant will continue to be owned and operated by the HCC pending delivery of a sewage pumping station at McGraths Hill at which point it is expected that the McGraths Hill wastewater treatment plant will be bypassed.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Sydney Water Corporation (SWC) applied for a notification waiver in
respect of its proposed acquisition of certain assets of Hawkesbury
City Council (HCC), as described in the transaction documents
provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The assets to be acquired are part of the Windsor Sewerage Scheme
and include the South Windsor wastewater treatment plant and the
pumping stations and reticulation systems required to supply
wastewater services to customers in the Hawkesbury City local
government area.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, SWC, is a state owned corporation established under
the Sydney Water Act 1994 (NSW) and State Owned Corporations Act
1989 (NSW). SWC provides water and wastewater services across
the greater Sydney metropolitan region, but it currently does not
provide wastewater services for most of the Hawkesbury City local
government area. SWC does provide drinking water services for
most of the Hawkesbury City local government area.
The HCC is a body politic of the State of NSW constituted under the
Local Government Act 1993 (NSW). HCC provides wastewater
services to properties in part of the Hawkesbury City local
government area through the Windsor Sewerage Scheme. The
Windsor Sewerage Scheme comprises two wastewater treatment
plants at McGraths Hill and South Windsor, 21 pumping stations and
the associated reticulation systems. The McGraths Hill wastewater
treatment plant is not subject to the Acquisition
SWC and the HCC do not compete for customers. Customers within
the Windsor Sewerage Scheme area of operations cannot currently
contract with SWC to provide wastewater services, and customers
outside the Windsor Sewerage Scheme area of operations cannot
contract with the HCC to provide wastewater services using the
Windsor Sewerage Scheme.
SWC and the HCC also acquire chemicals to be used in their
respective wastewater systems, in particular, aluminium
chlorohydrate, biosolids polymer ferric caustic soda, sodium
hypochlorite, and methanol.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. there is no horizontal overlap between SWC and HCC in the
supply of wastewater services to customers within the
Windsor Sewerage Scheme’s areas of operation in the
Hawkesbury City local government area
b. the pricing of wastewater services is regulated and set by
the Independent Regulatory and Pricing Tribunal of NSW
c. while the parties have a small overlap in the acquisition of
chemicals used in wastewater systems in Australia, any
increment arising from the Acquisition is very limited.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
MH South Brisbane Pty Ltd and SCHF Pty Ltd (together, **Star Hotels Group**) propose to acquire 100% of the business assets and freehold of Shafston Hotel, a hospitality venue in East Brisbane.

Star Hotels Group is an independently owned and operated pub and liquor retail group, primarily operating pubs and hotels on the eastern seaboard of Queensland and in South Australia. Star Hotels Group’s existing presence in the local area surrounding Shafston Hotel includes various retail liquor stores, as well as:

* Hotel West End, which offers on-premises liquor, dining, gaming, and a retail liquor store
* Hotel Kingsley, which offers on-premises liquor, dining, and accommodation, and
* The Garage Fortitude Valley, which offers on-premises liquor, dining, gaming, and a retail liquor store.

Shafston Hotel offers on-premises liquor, dining, and gaming, as well as a drive-through retail liquor store.

Following completion of the Acquisition, Shafston Hotel will continue to operate under its existing name and format and will be integrated into Star Hotel Group's portfolio.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
MH South Brisbane Pty Ltd and SCHF Pty Ltd (together, Star Hotels
Group) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed
acquisition of 100% of the business assets and freehold of Shafston
Hotel, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of
the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
2 June 2026

The acquirer, Star Hotels Group, is an independently owned and
operated pub and liquor retail group, primarily operating pubs and
hotels on the eastern seaboard of Queensland and in South
Australia. Star Hotels Group operates various liquor retailers in the
local area surrounding the target, as well as 3 hospitality venues:
• Hotel West End, which offers on-premises liquor, dining,
gaming, and a retail liquor store
• Hotel Kingsley, which offers on-premises liquor, dining, and
accommodation, and
• The Garage Fortitude Valley, which offers on-premises liquor,
dining, gaming, and a retail liquor store.
The target, Shafston Hotel, is a hospitality venue in East Brisbane
offering food, liquor, and gaming, as well as a drive-through retail
liquor store.
Following the Acquisition, Shafston Hotel will be integrated into Star
Hotels Group’s portfolio.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors
in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010
(Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. the parties’ combined shares of the supply of on-
premises liquor, dining, and gaming, as well as the retail
supply of packaged liquor, within the local area
surrounding Shafston Hotel are estimated to be low
b. there are alternative suppliers of retail liquor and on-
premises liquor, dining, and gaming in the local area
and the broader Brisbane area.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Direct Couriers (Aust) Pty Ltd (**DC**), a wholly owned subsidiary of Direct Couriers Group Pty Ltd (together, **Direct Couriers**), proposes to acquire 100% of the shares in Phoenix Transport Holdings Pty Ltd, along with its 100% wholly owned subsidiary, Phoenix Distribution (NSW) Pty Ltd (together, **Phoenix Transport**).

Direct Couriers is an Australian provider of business-to-business courier, interstate delivery and warehousing services. It services logistics, freight forwarder, commercial and retail customers across Australia and New Zealand, and operates a number of sub-brands including ‘Direct Cold’ and ‘Direct Road’. It focuses on same day point-to-point services.

Anacacia V Fund A and Anacacia V Fund B (together, the **Fund**), which are both unregistered Australian unlisted unit trusts, together own 49.7% of the shares in DC, with the remaining 50.3% held by existing and past management, as well as a passive investor.

Phoenix Transport is also an Australian provider of business-to-business courier, interstate delivery and warehousing services, operating in New South Wales and Queensland. Although Phoenix Transport has national customers, its coverage is primarily limited to Brisbane and Sydney. Phoenix Transport services a mix of logistics, retail and commercial customers, with a stronger focus on third-party warehousing and distribution, larger-scale heavy-freight dedicated hire work, and on- transport for warehouse relocations and other similar project work.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Direct Couriers (Aust) Pty Ltd, a wholly owned subsidiary of Direct
Couriers Group Pty Ltd (Direct Couriers), applied for a notification
waiver in respect of its acquisition of 100% of the shares in Phoenix
Transport Holdings Pty Ltd and its wholly owned subsidiary, Phoenix
Distribution (NSW) Pty Ltd (together, Phoenix Transport), as
described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
2 June 2026

The acquirer, Direct Couriers, is an Australian provider of business-
to-business courier, interstate delivery, and warehousing services. It
services logistics, freight forwarder, commercial, and retail
customers across Australia and New Zealand, and operates a
number of sub-brands including ‘Direct Cold’ and ‘Direct Road’. Direct
Couriers focuses on same day point-to-point services.
Phoenix Transport is also an Australian-headquartered provider of
business-to-business courier, interstate delivery, and warehousing
services. It operates in New South Wales and Queensland, with
coverage primarily limited to Brisbane and Sydney. Phoenix
Transport services a mix of logistics, retail, and commercial
customers, with a stronger focus on third-party warehousing and
distribution, large-scale heavy-freight dedicated hire work, and on-
transport for warehouse relocations and other similar project work.
Both parties supply courier and interstate delivery services in
Queensland and New South Wales.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application, and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. the parties’ combined share of supply of courier and
interstate delivery services in Queensland, New South Wales,
and nationally is estimated to be low and the increase in
share arising from the Acquisition would be small
• b. there are alternative suppliers of courier and interstate
delivery services in Queensland, New South Wales, and
nationally.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
SOILCO Pty Ltd (**SOILCO**) proposes to acquire 100% of the shares in Garden Mediums Pty Ltd (trading as **Go Grow**) from Lumime Pty Ltd and trustee of the Honey & Nut Unit Trust (the **Acquisition**).

SOILCO operates organic waste recycling facilities in New South Wales, ACT and Victoria, including a facility at Tweed Heads in northern NSW. SOILCO’s facilities process food and garden organic (**FOGO**) waste from local councils, blending it with forestry and agricultural feedstocks (and other suppliers’ inputs) to manufacture compost and soil improvement products. SOILCO supplies these products for a range of applications including, including urban landscaping and projects and agriculture and horticulture production systems (orchards, viticulture, turf farms, nurseries, and greenlife).

SOILCOs majority shareholder, Australian Waste Management Investments is part of the I Squared Capital group (**I Squared**). I Squared is a global infrastructure investment firm headquartered in the United States,

Go Grow manufactures and supplies potting mixes, fertilisers and garden products from a single site in Ballina NSW. Part of Go Grow’s business involves processing locally sourced garden waste to produce compost and mulch which is supplied directly to retail customers, as well as farms and nurseries. Go Grow does not acquire or process FOGO waste.

SOILCO Pty Ltd (SOILCO) applied for a notification waiver in respect
of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the share capital in Gardens
Mediums Pty Ltd (Go Grow), as described in the transaction
documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
1 June 2026

The acquirer, SOILCO, operates organic waste recycling facilities in
New South Wales, ACT and Victoria, including a facility at Tweed
Heads in northern NSW.
SOILCO’s facilities process food and garden organic (FOGO) waste
from local councils, blending it with forestry and agricultural
feedstocks (and other suppliers’ inputs) to manufacture compost
and soil improvement products. SOILCO supplies these products for
a range of applications including urban landscaping and projects and
agriculture and horticulture production systems (orchards,
viticulture, turf farms, nurseries, and greenlife).
SOILCOs majority shareholder, Australian Waste Management
Investments is part of the I Squared Capital group (I Squared). I
Squared is a global infrastructure investment firm headquartered in
the United States.
The target, Go Grow, manufactures and supplies potting mixes,
fertilisers and garden products from a single site in Ballina NSW.
Part of Go Grow’s business involves processing locally sourced
garden waste to produce compost and mulch which is supplied
directly to retail customers, as well as farms and nurseries. Go Grow
does not acquire or process FOGO waste.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. the horizontal overlap between SOILCO and Go Grow in the
acquisition of waste and the supply of soil and compost

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the

to be notified.

object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.

mergers.merger_description:
Treysta Wealth Management Pty Ltd (**Treysta**) proposes to acquire the businesses and related assets of Locumsgroup Asset Management Services Pty Ltd, Locumsgroup Private Accounting Services Pty Limited and Locums Nominees Pty Limited (together, **Locumsgroup**).

Treysta supplies financial planning and tax and accounting services in Sydney, Brisbane and the Gold Coast. Treysta is partly owned by a wholly-owned subsidiary of AZ Next Generation Advisory Pty Limited, which invests in small-to-medium sized financial advisory and accounting businesses. AZ Next Generation Advisory Pty Limited is a subsidiary of AZ Aust Holdings Pty Ltd, whose major shareholders are funds managed by Oaktree Capital Management LP, a global investment management firm specialising in alternative investments, and Azimut Holding S.p.a., a global asset and wealth management business listed on the Milan Stock Exchange.

Locumsgroup supplies financial planning and tax and accounting services in Sydney.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
AZ Next Generation Advisory Pty Ltd (AZ Next Generation Advisory)
applied for a notification waiver in respect of a proposed acquisition
by Treysta Wealth Management Pty Ltd (Treysta) of the businesses
and related assets of Locumsgroup Asset Management Pty Ltd,
Locumsgroup Private Accounting Services Pty Limited and Locums
Nominees Pty Limited (together, Locumsgroup), as described in the
transaction document provided as part of the application (the
Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
27 January 2026

AZ Next Generation Advisory’s subsidiary, Associated Planners
Financial Services Pty Ltd, owns a minority interest in Treysta.
Treysta, supplies financial planning and tax and accounting services
in Sydney, Brisbane and the Gold Coast. AZ Next Generation
Advisory invests in small to medium sized financial advisory and
accounting businesses and, according to its website, has made
several other acquisitions of financial services businesses in recent
years.
AZ Next Generation Advisory is a subsidiary of AZ Aust Holdings Pty
Ltd whose major shareholders are funds managed by Oaktree
Capital Management LP (Oaktree) and Azimut Holding S.p.a.
(Azimut). Oaktree is a global investment management firm and
Azimut is a global asset and wealth management business.
Locumsgroup, supplies financial planning and tax and accounting
services in Sydney.
Treysta and Locumsgroup both supply financial planning and tax
and accounting services in Sydney. Several of the AZ Next
Generation Advisory’s connected entities also carry on financial
services businesses in Sydney.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the
information provided by the applicant, including in the notification
waiver application, and had regard to the factors in section
51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act).
Based on the information provided by the applicant and publicly
available information, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is
unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In
particular:
a. While there is horizontal overlap between the services
supplied by Treysta, its connected entities and
Locumsgroup, the parties’ combined market shares in the
supply of financial planning, tax and accounting services in
Sydney and nationally are low and the Acquisition will result
in a small increment to Treysta and its connected entities’
market shares in the supply of those services.
b. The market concentration in the supply of financial planning
and tax and accounting services is low and there are several
thousand competing suppliers of such services nationally,
including many competing suppliers in Sydney, that are
expected to continue to constrain the parties.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given the above consideration that material competition
concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC considers that the
Acquisition is not required to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_name: Altor Fund Manager - Evac Holding Oy
mergers.merger_description:
Altor Fund Manager AB (**Altor**), through CAVE BidCo Oy (**Cave**), proposes to acquire 100% of the issued shares in Evac Holding Oy (**Evac**).

Altor is a Swedish-based private equity group. Altor is a well-established investment firm with a portfolio of companies across various sectors. Cave is a Finnish limited liability investment vehicle controlled by Altor that does not supply any goods or services.

The target, Evac, is a Finnish limited liability company. Evac is currently majority owned by Bridgepoint Europe V Investments S.à r.l. along with several management minority shareholders. Evac’s key brands are “Evac”, “Cathelco” and “HEM”. Evac products include integrated water and waste management solutions, primarily for the marine industry and, to some extent, for land-based applications. Evac has no Australian subsidiaries or Australian presence, but generates some revenue in Australia from sales to distributors based in Australia.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Altor Fund Manager AB (Altor), via CAVE BidCo Oy (Cave), applied for
a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of
the issued shares in Evac Holding Oy (Evac), as described in the
transaction document provided as part of the application (the
Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
30 January 2026

Altor is a Swedish-based private equity group, with a portfolio of
companies across various sectors. Cave is a Finnish limited liability
investment vehicle ultimately controlled by Altor that does not supply
any goods or services.
The target, Evac, is a Finnish limited liability company that operates
under key brands “Evac”, “Cathelco” and “HEM”. Evac supplies
integrated water and waste management systems and specialist
marine equipment, including vacuum sewage collection, wet and dry
waste handling, wastewater treatment, freshwater generation
(reverse osmosis), marine growth prevention, corrosion protection,
ultrasonic antifouling and water makers. Evac also supplies some
water and waste solutions for land-based applications.
Evac products are sold in Australia via Australian channel partners,
but Evac does not otherwise have any Australian subsidiaries or an
Australian presence.
The application indicates that none of the connected entities
controlled by Altor, including Cave, supply any products in Australia
that overlap with Evac’s products.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the
information provided with the notification waiver application and had
regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. There is no horizontal overlap in Australia between the
products and services supplied by the parties, and there are
no vertical relationships.
b. Evac has a small presence in Australia.
Given that the ACCC considers that material competition concerns
are unlikely to arise, it is not necessary to reach a concluded view on
the likelihood of the notification thresholds being met.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects
more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification
waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Sony Pictures Entertainment Inc. (**SPE**) and Sony Music Entertainment (Japan) Inc. (**SMEJ**) (together with Sony Group Corporation, Sony) propose to acquire 100% of the voting securities of DHX Entertainment (USA) Inc. (**DHX**).

The acquirer, Sony, is a global entertainment and technology business headquartered in Tokyo, Japan, and listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange and New York Stock Exchange. It operates in sectors such as game and network services, music, pictures, entertainment services, imaging solutions, and financial services.

The target, DHX, is a holding company with a controlling interest in Peanuts Worldwide. Peanuts Worldwide owns, manages, and licenses copyrights and other intellectual property rights of the Peanuts brand. This includes characters such as Snoopy and Charlie Brown. The family of Charles M Shulz, creator of Peanuts, will retain its 20% stake in the brand.

Sony and Peanuts Worldwide are both active in character intellectual property licensing for various customer use cases and the licensing of film and TV content related to those characters, globally and in Australia.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Sony Pictures Entertainment Inc. (SPE) and Sony Music
Entertainment (Japan) Inc. (SMEJ) (together with Sony Group
Corporation, Sony) applied for a notification waiver in respect of SPE
and SMEJ’s proposed acquisition of 100% of the voting securities of
DHX Entertainment (USA) Inc. (DHX), as described in the transaction
documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
19 February 2026

The acquirer, Sony, is a global entertainment and technology
business headquartered in Tokyo, Japan, and listed on the Tokyo
Stock Exchange and New York Stock Exchange. It operates in
sectors such as game and network services, music, pictures,
entertainment services, imaging solutions, and financial services.
The target, DHX, is a holding company with a controlling interest in
Peanuts Worldwide LLC (Peanuts Worldwide). Peanuts Worldwide
owns, manages, and licenses copyrights and other intellectual
property rights of the Peanuts brand. This includes characters such
as Snoopy and Charlie Brown.
Sony and Peanuts Worldwide are both active in character intellectual
property licensing for various customer use cases and the licensing
of film and TV content related to those characters, globally and in
Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. There is a small horizontal overlap between Sony and
Peanuts Worldwide in the supply of character, film and TV
licensing in Australia.
b. There are alternative suppliers of character, film and TV
licensing in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply. While
the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be
met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise,
the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on

mergers.merger_description:
TDR Capital LLP (**TDR**) and Estoril Coinvest SCSp (**Estoril**) propose to enter a Shareholders Agreement, pursuant to which TDR will hold a majority interest while Estoril will hold a minority interest in Escode.

TDR is a private equity firm based in the United Kingdom. Estoril is a co-investment vehicle managed by a private equity firm.

The target, Escode, is a UK headquartered business active in providing software escrow and related services. Escode primarily supplies software escrow services, whereby software code and data or other business critical information is deposited by a licensor in Escode's physical or virtual vaults, and released to the licensee where the release criteria are met. In Australia, Escode also supplies verification services.

Escode is a division of NCC Group plc (**NCC**), which is also based in the UK and is a supplier of cybersecurity services. NCC launched its Escode brand in Australia in 2024.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
TDR Capital LLP (TDR) and Estoril Coinvest SCSp (Estoril) propose
to acquire 100% of the two NCC Group plc (NCC) entities that
comprise the Escode business:
a. 100% of the entire issued share capital of Escode Jersey
Holdco Limited (a UK incorporated company), and
b. the entire membership interest of NCC Group Software
Resilience (Americas) LLC (a US-incorporated company),
as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).
The Acquisition will result in TDR acquiring a majority interest and
Estoril a minority interest in Escode.
TDR and Estoril applied for a notification waiver in respect of the
Acquisition.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
17 March 2026

One of the acquirers, TDR, is a private equity firm based in the United
Kingdom. The other acquirer, Estoril, is a co-investment vehicle
managed by a private equity firm.
The target, Escode, is a UK-headquartered business active in
providing software escrow and related services. Escode primarily
supplies software escrow services, whereby software code and data
or other business critical information is deposited by a licensor in
Escode's physical or virtual vaults, and released to the licensee
where the release criteria are met. In Australia, Escode also supplies
verification services.
Escode is a division of NCC, which is also based in the UK and is a
supplier of cybersecurity services. NCC launched its Escode brand in
Australia in 2024.
The acquirers and their connected entities do not provide software
escrow and verification services in Australia or globally.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. there is no horizontal or non-horizontal overlap between the
acquirers (or their connected entities) and Escode in the
supply of software escrow and verification services in
Australia
b. the presence of alternative suppliers of software escrow and
verification services in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on

mergers.merger_description:
NDC HoldCo Pty Ltd (together with its subsidiaries, **National Dental Care**), through its wholly-owned subsidiary, NDC Australia Pty Limited, proposes to acquire from Tan Dental Pty Ltd 100% of the business assets comprising the DDTA Dental clinic (**DDTA Dental**).

National Dental Care is a dental services organisation that operates a network of dental clinics that provide a range of general dentistry services. National Dental Care has dental clinics in all Australian states and territories except Tasmania.

DDTA Dental is a single dental clinic located in Launceston, Tasmania that provides a range of general dentistry services.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
NDC Australia Pty Limited, a wholly-owned subsidiary of NDC
HoldCo Pty Ltd, applied for a notification waiver in respect of NDC
Australia Pty Limited’s proposed acquisition of 100% of the business
assets comprising the DDTA Dental clinic (DDTA Dental), as
described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
10 March 2026

NDC HoldCo Pty Ltd (together with its subsidiaries, National Dental
Care) is a dental services organisation that operates a network of
dental clinics that provide a range of general dentistry services.
National Dental Care has dental clinics in all Australian states and
territories except Tasmania.
DDTA Dental is a single dental clinic located in Launceston,
Tasmania, that provides a range of general dentistry services.
National Dental Care and DDTA Dental supply general dentistry
services.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. there is no horizontal overlap between National Dental Care
and DDTA Dental in the supply of dental services in
Tasmania
b. the presence of alternative suppliers of dental services in the
area.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met based on the information in the application, given that
material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has
determined that the Acquisition is not required to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

Genesis Minerals Limited (ASX: GMD) (**Genesis Minerals**) is an ASX-listed gold producer and explorer with gold mining operations selling gold bullion produced from its operations and processing hubs at Leonora and Laverton in Western Australia.

Magnetic Resources NL (ASX:MAU) (**Magnetic Resources**) is a gold explorer and developer, with activities focused on advancing the Lady Julie and Laverton district projects in Western Australia. Magnetic does not have any active mining or gold production operations.

Genesis Minerals proposes to acquire 100% of the issued shares in Magnetic Resources by way of a scheme of arrangement.

events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Genesis Minerals Limited (Genesis Minerals) applied for a
notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of
the issued shares in Magnetic Resources NL (Magnetic Resources)
by way of a scheme of arrangement, as described in the transaction
documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
19 March 2026

The acquirer, Genesis Minerals, is an ASX-listed gold producer and
explorer with gold mining operations selling gold bullion produced
from its operations and processing hubs at Leonora and Laverton in
Western Australia.
The target, Magnetic Resources, is a gold explorer and developer,
with activities focused on advancing the Lady Julie and Laverton
district projects in Western Australia. Magnetic Resources does not
have any active mining or gold production operations.
Genesis Minerals and Magnetic Resources operate in the gold
mining and production industry in Australia. This industry involves
the exploration, development, extraction, processing and sale of
gold-bearing ore for the production of refined gold, which is sold into
international gold markets.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. the parties’ combined share in the actual and expected
production and supply of gold in Australia and globally is
expected to be low
b. the presence of alternative producers and suppliers of gold
in Australia and globally.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.

mergers.merger_description:
Vets Central Holdings Pty Ltd (together, with its subsidiaries, **Vets Central**), proposes to acquire 100% of the business assets (both tangible and intangible) of Hills Veterinary Centre.

Vets Central is an Australian-owned veterinary group that owns and operates 80 first opinion veterinary practices across Australia and New Zealand. In Australia, Vets Central’s practices provide core and ancillary veterinary services, for household pets as well as livestock, and other exotic species. The services primarily include routine, planned, and emergency veterinary services (excluding after-hours services).

The Hills Veterinary Centre is a full-service veterinary practice in the Blackwood area of South Australia. The business provides a range of first opinion core and ancillary veterinary services for household pets (extending to exotic pets) in the local area.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
VC Operations (WA) Pty Ltd, a wholly owned subsidiary of Vets
Central Holdings Pty Ltd (together with its subsidiaries, Vets
Central), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed
acquisition of 100% of the business assets of Hills Veterinary Centre,
as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(b) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) not to make the notification waiver
determination applied for.
14 April 2026

The acquirer, Vets Central, is an Australian-owned veterinary group
that owns and operates 80 first opinion veterinary practices across
Australia and New Zealand. In Australia, Vets Central’s practices
provide core and ancillary veterinary services, for household pets as
well as livestock, and other exotic species. The services primarily
include routine, planned, and emergency veterinary services
(excluding after-hours services).
The Hills Veterinary Centre is first opinion veterinary practice that
provides routine, planned and emergency (excluding after-hours)
veterinary services. Hills Veterinary Centre is located in the
Blackwood area of South Australia.
Vets Central owns one clinic in the Blackwood area, Familypet Vet
Coromandel Valley, which is located approximately 3.5km from Hills
Veterinary Centre.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
certain publicly available information and had regard to the factors in
section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010
(Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, including the
revenue of the parties in Australia and relevant cumulative
acquisitions, the ACCC considers that, if the Acquisition were put
into effect, the notifications thresholds would be met.
The ACCC is unable to reach a view that the Acquisition would not be
likely to raise competition issues in any market based on the
information available to us. It considers that the Acquisition
warrants consideration through the notification process, including
via further information gathering and consultation with third parties.
This may include a focus on:
a. the horizontal overlap between Vets Central and Hills
Veterinary Centre in the supply of veterinary services in
Blackwood, South Australia
b. the extent to which the parties are likely to face
competition from alternative suppliers of veterinary
services in the areas surrounding Blackwood, South
Australia.
Given the above, the ACCC has determined not to make the
notification waiver determination applied for.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Regent Motors Pty Ltd (Regent Motors Group) applied for a
notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of the
portfolio of franchised automotive dealerships and associated real
property located in Mandurah, Western Australia, currently operated
by WA Automotive Pty Ltd, controlled by the Lane Group, as
described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
9 April 2026

The acquirer, Regent Motors Group is an Australian-owned
automotive retail group headquartered in Perth, Western Australia.
Regent Motors Group owns and operates franchised motor vehicle
dealerships across Western Australia and the Northern Territory,
supplying new and used motor vehicles across a range of OEM
brands including Ford, Toyota, Hyundai, Suzuki, BMW, Mini, MG,
Geely, GMSV, Isuzu Ute, Subaru, and Volkswagen. Regent Motors
Group also provides vehicle servicing and repairs, genuine parts and
accessories, fleet sales and service, and facilitates finance,
insurance, and aftercare products in connection with vehicle
transactions.
The target, the Lane Group dealerships, comprises of six franchised
retail dealerships and associated storage facilities in Mandurah, WA,
supplying new and used motor vehicles for the following brands:
Ford, Mitsubishi, Hyundai, Kia, Nissan, and Suzuki. Lane Group also
provides vehicle servicing and repairs, parts and accessories, fleet
services, and finance and insurance facilitation.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is no overlap between the Regent Motors Group and
the Lane Group in the supply of new and used motor
vehicles and associated services in Mandurah and the
surrounding areas
b. while there is a small horizontal overlap between the Regent
Motors Group and the Lane Group in the broader area
surrounding Mandurah (including Perth and Bunbury), there
are independent alternatives suppliers of new and used cars
in Mandurah, Perth, Bunbury and Rockingham
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Discovery Holiday Parks Pty Limited (**Discover Holiday Parks**), a wholly-owned subsidiary of Discovery Parks Holdings Pty Limited (**Discovery Parks Holdings**), proposes to acquire the business of operating and managing "Habitat Noosa Everglades Eco Camp" (**Habitat Noosa**).

Habitat Noosa is an integrated hospitality business comprising accommodation options for backpackers, couples and families; a 200 seat bar and bistro with an on site micro brewery; and Noosa Everglades eco safari boat tours. Associated brands include Everglades Eco Safaris, Kangarooms Noosa Everglades, Eco Brewing Co and Coothara Bar.

Discovery Holiday Parks, under its trading names Discovery Parks and Discovery Resorts, owns and operates over 90 accommodation offerings throughout Australia. Discovery Holiday Parks provides short-stay holiday accommodation, worker accommodation, and long-stay accommodation, as well as tours and food and beverage services within the tourism and hospitality industry. Discovery Holiday Parks does not have a presence in Noosa, Queensland or on the Sunshine Coast – its nearest property is in Hervey Bay.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Discovery Holiday Parks Pty Ltd (Discovery Holiday Parks) applied
for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of the
assets of Habitat Noosa Everglades Eco Camp (Habitat Noosa), as
described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).
The waiver application does not include Discovery Holiday Parks
Investments Pty Ltd’s acquisition of shares in the entity that holds a
lease for the land on which Habitat Noosa operates.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
10 April 2026

The acquirer, Discovery Holiday Parks, owns and operates over 90
accommodation offerings throughout Australia. Discovery provides
short-stay holiday accommodation, worker accommodation, and
long-stay accommodation, as well as tours and food and beverage
services within the tourism and hospitality industry. Discovery
Holiday Parks is not present in Noosa or on the Sunshine Coast.
The target, Habitat Noosa, is an integrated hospitality business
comprising accommodation options for backpackers, couples and
families, a 200 seat bar and bistro with an onsite micro brewery, and
Noosa Everglades eco safari boat tours. Associated brands include
Everglades Eco Safaris, Kangarooms Noosa Everglades, Eco Brewing
Co and Coothara Bar.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. There is no overlap between Discovery Holiday Parks and
Habitat Noosa in Noosa and the Sunshine Coast and a small
overlap in Queensland in the supply of accommodation, food
and beverage, and activities and tours
b. there are alternative providers of accommodation, food and
beverage, and activities and tours in Noosa and the
Sunshine Coast.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering

effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on

mergers.merger_description:
Francisco Partners Management, LP (**Francisco Partners**) is a global investment firm specialising in technology and technology-enabled businesses, headquartered in San Francisco, United States with offices in New York and London. Francisco Partners has several portfolio companies that operate across multiple sectors, including in Australia.

CSHC Buyerco, LLC (together with its connected entities, **Capsa Healthcare**) manufactures and supplies healthcare equipment and technology-enabled point-of-care hardware, including mobile medication carts, pharmacy automation hardware, clinical workstations, and medical supply chain software. Capsa Healthcare does not have a physical presence in Australia but does generate a proportion of its global revenue from supply to Australian customers.

Pursuant to a share sale agreement and as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application, the funds advised by Francisco Partners which are currently controlled by the acquiring vehicles will acquire 100% control and ownership of Capsa Healthcare.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Catalyst Debt Merger Sub, LLC, Catalyst Holdco Merger Sub, LLC,
Catalyst Blocker Merger Sub I, LLC, and Catalyst Blocker Merger Sub
II LLC, which are each a vehicle controlled by funds advised by
Francisco Partners Management, LP (Francisco Partners), applied
for a notification waiver in respect of the proposed acquisition of
100% of the business of Capsa Healthcare (defined below), as
described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).
The Acquisition is a share sale which involves the following:
• Catalyst Debt Merger Sub, LLC is proposing to merge with
and into CSHC Buyerco, LLC, the direct parent company of
Capsa Solutions, LLC.
• Catalyst Holdco Merger Sub, LLC is proposing to merge with
and into CSHC Holdings, LLC, the indirect parent company of
Capsa Solutions, LLC.
• Catalyst Blocker Merger Sub I, LLC and Catalyst Blocker
Merger Sub II, LLC are proposing to merge with and into
CSHC Fund Blocker, LLC and CSHC Co-Investment Blocker
Corp., LLC, respectively, which hold equity interests in CSHC
Holdings, LLC.
CSHC Buyerco, LLC, CSHC Holdings, LLC, Capsa Solutions, LLC,
CSHC Fund Blocker, LLC, and CSHC Co-Investment Blocker Corp.,
LLC together form and are connected entities of Capsa Healthcare.
As a result of the Acquisition, funds advised by Francisco Partners
will have 100% control and ownership of the business of Capsa
Healthcare.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
2 April 2026

Francisco Partners, which is a global investment firm specialising in
technology and technology-enabled businesses, headquartered in
San Francisco, United States with offices in New York and London.
Francisco Partners has several portfolio companies that operate
across multiple sectors, including in Australia. The acquiring vehicles
are controlled by funds advised by Francisco Partners.
The target, Capsa Healthcare, manufactures and supplies healthcare
equipment and technology-enabled point-of-care hardware, including
mobile medication carts, pharmacy automation hardware, clinical
workstations, and medical supply chain software. Capsa Healthcare
does not have a physical presence in Australia but does generate a
proportion of its global revenue from supply to Australian customers.
Francisco Partners has two portfolio companies which operate in the
healthcare sector in Australia – Merative, which supplies data,
analytics, and software products for healthcare and government
social services, and Sermo, which supplies social networking and
platform services to healthcare professionals. However, Francisco
Partners states that neither of these portfolio companies offer
substitutable products to those which are supplied by Capsa
Healthcare in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors
in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010
(Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is no horizontal overlap between Francisco Partners
(including its portfolio companies with operations in
Australia) and Capsa Healthcare in the supply of technology-
enabled healthcare equipment in Australia, and there are no
vertical relationships in this respect
b. while there may be a small complementary relationship
between the healthcare-related products and services
provided by Capsa Healthcare, Merative, and Sermo, there
are alternative suppliers of these types of products and
services in Australia
c. Capsa Healthcare has a small presence in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Yancoal Australia Limited (**Yancoal**) proposes to acquire 100% of the shares in Kestrel Coal Group Pty Ltd (**Kestrel Coal Group**) from investment funds and/or their investors which are managed by EMR Capital Management Limited or EMR Capital Advisors Pty Ltd and Adaro Capital Limited (the **Acquisition**).

Kestrel Coal Group holds an 80% interest in the Kestrel Joint Venture, an unincorporated joint venture which owns and operates the Kestrel Coal Mine in Queensland. The remaining 20% of the Kestrel Coal Joint Venture is held by Mitsui Kestrel Coal Investment Pty Ltd.

Kestrel Coal Group was formed to hold the 80% interest in the Kestrel Coal Joint Venture and does not itself supply goods or services. The Acquisition will result in Yancoal holding an 80% interest in the Kestrel Coal Mine.

Yancoal produces thermal coal from mines located in New South Wales, Queensland, and Western Australia. It also produces a smaller volume of metallurgical coal.

The Kestrel Coal Mine is located in Queensland’s Bowen Basin and produces metallurgical coal, in addition to a smaller volume of thermal coal.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Yancoal Australia Limited (Yancoal) applied for a notification waiver
in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the shares in
Kestrel Coal Group Pty Ltd (Kestrel Coal Group) from investment
funds and/or their investors which are managed by EMR Capital
Management Limited, EMR Capital Advisors Pty Ltd, and/or Adaro
Capital Limited, as described in the transaction documents provided
as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
13 April 2026

The acquirer, Yancoal, produces thermal coal and, to a lesser extent,
metallurgical coal, from mines located in New South Wales,
Queensland, and Western Australia.
The target, Kestrel Coal Group, holds an 80% interest in the Kestrel
Joint Venture, an unincorporated joint venture which owns and
operates the Kestrel Coal Mine in Queensland. The remaining 20% of
the Kestrel Coal Joint Venture is held by Mitsui Kestrel Coal
Investment Pty Ltd.
The Kestrel Coal Mine is located in Queensland’s Bowen Basin and
produces metallurgical coal, and to a lesser extent, thermal coal. The
Acquisition would result in Yancoal holding an 80% interest in the
Kestrel Coal Mine.
Both Yancoal and the Kestrel Coal Mine supply metallurgical and
thermal coal from mines in Australia, with the majority of supply
being exported to the global seaborne coal market.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application, and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. there is a small overlap in coal production by the parties in
Australia as Yancoal primarily produces thermal coal and
the Kestrel Coal Mine primarily produces metallurgical coal,
and both parties are primarily exporters
b. the parties’ combined share of supply of thermal and
metallurgical coal in Australia is low and the increase in
share of supply arising from the Acquisition would be small
c. there are alternative suppliers of both thermal and
metallurgical coal in Australia and globally.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.

mergers.merger_description:
PH Medical Centres Pty Ltd (**PHMC**) proposes to acquire the business and assets of the Riverstone Family Medical Practice (**RFMP**) from Riverstone GP Pty Ltd (the **Acquisition**).

PHMC is part of the Partnered Health Group and an indirectly wholly owned subsidiary of Partnered Health Pty Ltd (**PHPL**).

PHMC together with other PHPL subsidiaries (the **PH Primary Care Group**) provide primary care services with more than 60 general practice clinics and medical centres across Australia, engaging more than 600 general practitioners. The PH Primary Care Group also operates skin cancer clinics, allied health providers (physiotherapy and dietetics) and TeleWell, a telehealth platform which provides 24/7 continuous access to general practitioners.

The Partnered Health Group also operates a healthcare platform which provides corporate and occupational healthcare solutions alongside its primary care network.

PHPL is part of a larger group of companies which are ultimately controlled by Quadrant Private Equity (**QPE**) through Quadrant Private Equity No. 6 (**QPE Fund 6**).

RFMP is a medical centre located at 71 Garfield Road East, Riverstone, NSW 2765, which provides primary care services through 12 general practitioners (plus four Registrars) and three allied health professionals.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
PH Medical Centres Pty Ltd (PHMC) applied for a notification waiver
in respect of its proposed acquisition of the business and assets of
the Riverstone Family Medical Practice (the RFMP clinic) from
Riverstone GP Pty Ltd, as described in the transaction documents
provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
16 April 2026

The acquirer, PHMC, is part of the Partnered Health Group and an
indirectly wholly owned subsidiary of Partnered Health Pty Ltd
(PHPL). PHPL is part of a larger group of companies which are
ultimately controlled by Quadrant Private Equity through Quadrant
Private Equity No. 6.
PHMC together with other PHPL subsidiaries (the “PH Primary Care
Group”) provide primary care services with more than 60 general
practice clinics and medical centres across Australia, engaging more
than 600 general practitioners. The PH Primary Care Group also
operates skin cancer clinics, allied health providers (physiotherapy
and dietetics) and TeleWell, a telehealth platform which provides
24/7 continuous access to general practitioners.
PHPL also provides corporate health and wellbeing services to
support workforce health, safety, and productivity, delivering end-to-
end occupational health services
The target, the RFMP clinic, is a medical centre located at 71 Garfield
Road East, Riverstone, NSW 2765, which provides primary care
services through 12 general practitioners (plus four Registrars) and
three allied health professionals.
The PH Primary Care Group and the RFMP clinic both supply general
practice medical services.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. there is no horizontal overlap between the PH Primary Care
Group and the RMP clinic in the supply of general practice
medical services in the local Riverstone area in NSW, and a
small horizontal overlap in the broader Sydney area.
b. there is no competitive overlap with Quadrant Private Equity
No. 6 Fund or other Quadrant Private Equity managed
entities.
c. there are other clinics supplying general practice medical
services near Riverstone, NSW.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Apollo Purchaser, Inc (**Apollo Purchaser**), a special purpose vehicle which is ultimately controlled by vehicles comprising funds advised by Francisco Partners Management L.P. (together with its affiliated funds, **Francisco Partners**), proposes to acquire all of the issued and outstanding shares in Blackline Safety Corp. (together with its subsidiaries, **Blackline Safety**) from its current shareholders by way of a statutory plan of arrangement under the provisions of the *Business Corporations Act* (Alberta) (the **Acquisition**). The shareholders of Blackline Safety, other than certain Rollover Shareholders (as defined and described below), will receive cash as consideration (as an upfront payment on closing and by way of a potential future contingent payment) for the Acquisition.

Francisco Partners is a global investment firm specialising in technology and technology-enabled businesses, headquartered in San Francisco, United States with offices in New York and London.

Blackline Safety is a public company registered in Canada, headquartered in Calgary and listed on the Toronto Stock Exchange (TSE: BLN). Blackline Safety specialises in designing and manufacturing hardware enabled Software-as-a-Service products for worker safety monitoring. Its products include wearable devices, personal and area gas monitors and cloud-based data analytics software. Through deploying Blackline Safety’s products, employers can monitor and communicate with lone workers and detect possible leakage of gas or other hazardous materials that may pose a threat to workers. The accompanying software streams data from devices in the field and enables employers to manage emergencies.

Blackline Safety has a worldwide presence with customers in more than 75 countries, including Australia. Blackline Safety has an Australian subsidiary, Blackline Safety Australia Pty Ltd.

Apollo Purchaser will obtain sole control of Blackline Safety by way of the Acquisition and Blackline Safety will transition to a private company. A number of Blackline Safety’s existing shareholders, namely DAK Capital Inc. (**DAK**), the Lowy Family Group, Cody Slater (the Chairman and Chief Executive Officer of Blackline Safety) and Brad Gilewich (President of DAK and a nominee director of Blackline Safety), and certain affiliates (together, the **Rollover Shareholders**) will each retain minority interests in Blackline Safety by way of a rollover arrangement.

determination_table_content.details:
Apollo Purchaser, Inc. (Apollo Purchaser) applied for a notification
waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of all of the issued and
outstanding shares in Blackline Safety Corp. (together with its
subsidiaries, Blackline Safety), as described in the transaction
documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, Apollo Purchaser, is a special purpose vehicle which is
ultimately controlled by vehicles comprising funds advised by
Francisco Partners Management L.P. (together with its affiliated
funds, Francisco Partners). Francisco Partners is a global
investment firm specialising in technology and technology-enabled
businesses, headquartered in San Francisco, United States with
offices in New York and London.
The target, Blackline Safety, is a public company registered in
Canada, headquartered in Calgary and listed on the Toronto Stock
Exchange (TSE: BLN). Blackline Safety specialises in designing and
manufacturing hardware enabled Software-as-a-Service products for
worker safety monitoring. Its products include wearable devices,
personal and area gas monitors and cloud-based data analytics
software. Blackline Safety’s products allow employers to monitor
and communicate with lone workers and detect possible leakage of
gas or other hazardous materials that may pose a threat to workers.
The accompanying software streams data from devices in the field
and enables employers to manage emergencies.
Blackline Safety has a worldwide presence with customers in more
than 75 countries, including Australia. Blackline Safety has an
Australian subsidiary, Blackline Safety Australia Pty Ltd.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is no competitive overlap between Francisco Partners
(including its portfolio companies with operations in
Australia) and Blackline Safety in the manufacturing and
supply of worker safety monitoring equipment (such as gas
detection devices and lone worker SOS devices) in Australia
b. there are alternative manufacturers and suppliers of worker
safety monitoring equipment in Australia, including global
and domestic suppliers.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Certain affiliates of American Industrial Partners propose to acquire 100% of the outstanding shares of Avanos Medical, Inc. through a share sale.

American Industrial Partners is an operationally oriented middle-market private equity firm based in New York, U.S, with a focus on acquisition and improvement of industrial businesses.

Avanos Medical is a US-headquartered medical device business focused on clinically differentiated solutions in two segments: Specialty Nutrition Systems (including enteral feeding products) and Pain Management & Recovery.
