determination_table_content.details:
A-AV Holdco I, Inc, an affiliate of AIP, LLC (American Industrial
Partners), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed
acquisition of 100% of the share capital in Avanos Medical, Inc.
(Avanos Medical), as described in the transaction documents
provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

American Industrial Partners is a middle-market private equity firm
based in New York, U.S, with a focus on acquisition and
improvement of industrial businesses. The ADDMAN group
(ADDMAN) is the only American Industrial Partners portfolio
company which supplies products with applications in the medical
technology sector in Australia.
Avanos Medical is a US-headquartered medical device business
focused on clinically differentiated solutions in two segments:
Specialty Nutrition Systems and Pain Management & Recovery.
Speciality nutrition systems includes a range of enteral feeding
products and services designed to deliver nutrition into the patient’s
gastrointestinal tract and support care throughout the entire patient
journey.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. there is no horizontal overlap between American Industrial
Partners (including its connected entities) and Avanos
Medical in the supply of specialty nutrition systems or pain
management and recovery products
b. American Industrial Partners’ portfolio company ADDMAN, a
provider of medical manufacturing solutions, has a small
presence in Australia
c. there are alternative suppliers of speciality nutrition systems
and pain management and recovery products in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the

mergers.merger_description:
UP Education Australia Pty Limited, the Australian subsidiary of New Zealand Parent company UP Education HoldCo Limited (**UP Education**), proposes to acquire Australian Academy of Beauty Dermal & Laser Pty Ltd (**AABDL**) by entering into a share sale deed (the **Acquisition**).

AABDL is an Australian tertiary education provider providing beauty, laser and dermal training courses to approximately 487 students. It has four campuses in NSW (Bella Vista, North Strathfield, Rockdale and Sydney CBD).

UP Education is a tertiary education provider in Australia and New Zealand which provides higher education, vocational education and training courses and university partnerships. UP Education offers courses in various topic areas such as health, hospitality, community services, construction and creative arts.

UP Education operates a group of higher education and vocational education and training providers across Australia and New Zealand, including 10 registered providers in Australia. This includes 4 providers that have campuses in New South Wales (including online courses), with 3 providers located in Sydney. UP Education also delivers online qualifications nationally through several providers, including to students in NSW.

UP Education does not currently supply any beauty, laser or dermal training courses in Australia.

UP Education is owned and operated by funds managed and/or advised by Pacific Equity Partners (the **PEP Funds**). UP Education is the only tertiary education business owned by the PEP Funds.

determination_table_content.details:
UP Education Australia Pty Ltd, the Australian subsidiary of New
Zealand parent company UP Education HoldCo Limited (UP
Education), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its
proposed acquisition of Australian Academy of Beauty Dermal &
Laser Pty Ltd (AABDL), as described in the transaction documents
provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
1 June 2026

The acquirer, UP Education is a tertiary education provider in
Australia and New Zealand which provides higher education,
vocational education and training courses and university
partnerships. UP Education offers courses in various topic areas
such as health, hospitality, community services, construction and
creative arts.
UP Education operates a group of higher education and vocational
education and training providers across Australia and New Zealand,
including 10 registered providers in Australia. This includes 4
providers that have campuses in New South Wales (including online
courses), with 3 providers located in Sydney. UP Education also
delivers online qualifications nationally through several providers,
including to students in NSW.
UP Education is owned and operated by funds managed and/or
advised by Pacific Equity Partners.
The target, AABDL, is an Australian tertiary education provider
providing beauty, laser and dermal training courses to approximately
487 students. It has four campuses in NSW (Bella Vista, North
Strathfield, Rockdale and Sydney CBD).

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors
in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010
(Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers
that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening
of competition. In particular:
a. there is no horizontal overlap between UP Education and
AABDL in the supply of beauty or salon management training
services
b. whilst there is a horizontal overlap between UP Education
and AABDL in the supply of tertiary education services in
NSW and nationally, the increase in share of supply arising

mergers.merger_description:
NEC Corporation (**NEC**) proposes to acquire the entire issued and outstanding share capital of CSG Systems International, Inc. (**CSG**) (the **Acquisition**).

The Acquisition will be implemented by way of a reverse triangular merger pursuant to an Agreement and Plan of Merger between NEC, CSG and Canvas Transaction Company, Inc. This will involve CSG merging with NEC’s wholly-owned Delaware subsidiary (Canvas Transaction Company, Inc.) and then surviving the merger as a wholly owned subsidiary of NEC.

NEC is a Japanese conglomerate listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange and headquartered in Tokyo, Japan. NEC is mainly active in IT services, systems integration, and telecommunications infrastructure. Netcracker Technology Corporation (**Netcracker**) is a wholly owned subsidiary of NEC, headquartered in the United States, which provides cloud-based business support systems which support customer-facing operations and operations support systems which support the technical and operational aspects of telecommunications networks.

CSG is headquartered in the United States and listed on the Nasdaq Stock Exchange. CSG is a Software-as-a-Service company that provides customer engagement and billing software to companies in various industries, including telecommunications.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Woodward pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
NEC Corporation (NEC) applied for a notification waiver in respect of
its proposed acquisition of 100% of the issued and outstanding
share capital in CSG Systems International, Inc. (CSG), by way of a
reverse triangular merger involving NEC, its wholly owned subsidiary
Canvas Transaction Company, Inc. (Canvas) and CSG, as described
in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the
Acquisition). This will involve CSG merging with Canvas and then
surviving the merger as a wholly owned subsidiary of NEC.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
24 February 2026

The acquirer, NEC, is a Japanese conglomerate listed on the Tokyo
Stock Exchange and headquartered in Tokyo, Japan. NEC is active in
IT services, systems integration, and telecommunications
infrastructure. Netcracker Technology Corporation (Netcracker) is a
wholly owned subsidiary of NEC, headquartered in the United States,
which provides cloud-based business support systems (BSS) and
operations support systems (OSS) globally.
The target, CSG, is headquartered in the United States and listed on
the Nasdaq Stock Exchange. CSG is a Software-as-a-Service
company that provides customer engagement and billing software to
companies in various industries, including telecommunications.
The application states that in Australia, NEC (through Netcracker)
supplies OSS and not BSS, whereas CSG supplies BSS in Australia
and not OSS. BSS and OSS are software applications that are used to
support telecommunications networks. BSS supports customer-
facing operations, such as billing, customer engagement and
support, customer relationship management, order fulfillment and
product management. OSS supports the technical and operational
aspects, such as network management, performance monitoring,
service provisioning and service assurance.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the
information provided with the notification waiver application and
certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors
in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010
(Cth) (Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. There does not appear to be horizontal overlap between
Netcracker’s and CSG’s software offerings in Australia.
b. While there is a potential vertical or conglomerate
relationship between NEC and CSG, there are alternative
suppliers of software for billing, customer engagement,
managing and monitoring telecommunications network
operations and/or associated applications for customers in
Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Crossmuller Pty. Limited (**Crossmuller**) proposes to acquire 100% of the issued share capital of GEMCORP Holdings Pty Ltd (**GEM**).

Crossmuller designs, engineers and delivers manufacturing, electrical and industrial automation solutions for industrial clients. Crossmuller provides project delivery services, from concept and detailed engineering through to electrical installation, software and systems integration, fitting and machining commissioning, and ongoing support across a range of manufacturing sectors.

Crossmuller owns the registered businesses Main Control Systems and Main Control Switchboards (together, **MCS**), which manufactures low-medium amperage electrical switchboards (up to 1,200 amps) for use in residential and commercial projects.

Crossmuller is part of the Borg Group, an Australian-owned private group of companies and brands involved in the manufacture of building materials, waste recycling operations, forestry, packaging, engineering and automation services.

GEM designs, engineers and manufactures custom low voltage and high amperage electronical switchboards (up to 4,000 amps), as well as motor control centres and control panels, which are built for industrial, commercial and infrastructure applications. GEM offers services encompassing design, assembly, factory testing, site installation, commissioning and maintenance.

determination_table_content.details:
Crossmuller Pty Ltd (Crossmuller) applied for a notification waiver in
respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the issued share
capital in GEMCORP Holdings Pty Ltd (GEM), as described in the
transaction documents provided as part of the application (the
Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
5 March 2026

The acquirer, Crossmuller, designs, engineers and delivers
manufacturing, electrical and industrial automation solutions for
industrial customers. Crossmuller provides project delivery services,
from concept and engineering through to electrical installation,
software and systems integration, fitting and machining
commissioning, and ongoing support across a range of
manufacturing sectors.
Crossmuller owns the registered businesses Main Control Systems
and Main Control Switchboards (together, MCS), which
manufactures electrical switchboards for use in residential and
commercial projects.
Crossmuller is part of the Borg Group, an Australian-owned private
group of companies and brands involved in the manufacture of
building materials, waste recycling operations, forestry, packaging,
engineering and automation services.
The target, GEM, designs, engineers and manufactures custom low
voltage and high amperage electronical switchboards, as well as
motor control centres and control panels, which are built for
industrial, commercial and infrastructure applications. GEM offers
services including design, assembly, factory testing, site installation,
commissioning and maintenance.
Crossmuller and GEM overlap in the supply of electrical
switchboards. The application states that MCS’ switchboards are
certified up to 1,200 amps (low-medium amperage), GEM’s
switchboards are certified up to 4,000 amps (high amperage) and
GEM does not currently offer any switchboards in the low-medium
amperage range.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the
information provided with the notification waiver application and
certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors
in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010
(Cth) (Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is a small horizontal overlap between Crossmuller and
GEM in the supply of electrical switchboards resulting in a
low combined share of supply
b. there are several alternative suppliers of electrical
switchboards in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Southern Way PS Pty Limited as trustee for PS Southern Way Unit Trust, which is a wholly owned entity of Prime Super Pty Ltd as trustee for Prime Super (together, **Prime Super**), will increase its percentage interest in Southern Way Holdings Pty Ltd and Southern Way Holdings Trust (collectively referred to as **Southern Way Holdings**) from 18.79% to 27.36% pursuant to its pre-emptive rights in relation to both entities (the **Acquisition**). The Acquisition involves a purchase of stapled units in Southern Way Holding Trust and shares in Southern Way Holdings Pty Limited.

Prime Super is a multi-industry fully independent profit-to-members superannuation fund with approximately $8.4 billion in funds under management and 140,000 members.

The target, Southern Way Holdings, is responsible for the delivery and maintenance of the Peninsula Link Project. The Peninsula Link Project is a Public Private Partnership to design, construct, maintain and operate the 27km Peninsula Link Freeway between Frankston and Mount Martha in Victoria, Australia. Southern Way Holdings is responsible for the maintenance and availability of Peninsula Link.

determination_table_content.details:
Southern Way PS Pty Limited as trustee for PS Southern Way Unit
Trust, which is a wholly owned entity of Prime Super Pty Ltd as
trustee for Prime Super (together, Prime Super), applied for a
notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of an
additional 8.57% of stapled units and shares in Southern Way
Holdings Pty Ltd and Southern Way Holdings Trust (together,
Southern Way Holdings), as described in the transaction documents
provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
As a result of the Acquisition, Prime Super will hold a 27.36% interest
in Southern Way Holdings.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
20 April 2026

The acquirer, Prime Super, is a multi-industry fully independent profit-
to-members superannuation fund with approximately $8.4 billion in
funds under management and 140,000 members. Prime Super’s
investment portfolio comprises a number of different assets
including infrastructure investments which represent approximately
16% of its total investment holdings. Those infrastructure
investments include investments in international education
infrastructure funds, energy infrastructure projects, an Australian
based parking group, and an existing 18.79% interest in Southern
Way Holdings. Prime Super does not hold interests in any
infrastructure assets or projects similar to Peninsula Link, nor does it
hold investments in any other entities which provide large scale road
infrastructure services to an Australian state government.
The target, Southern Way Holdings, is a special purpose vehicle
which is responsible for the Peninsula Link Project. The Peninsula
Link Project is a Public Private Partnership to design, construct,
maintain and operate the 27km Peninsula Link Freeway between
Frankston and Mount Martha in Victoria, Australia. The Peninsula
Link Project has a 25-year operation period after which ownership of
the road will be handed back to the State of Victoria (expected in
2038).

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
• Based on the information currently before it, including publicly
available information regarding the Peninsula Link Project, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. there is no horizontal overlap or vertical relationship
between Prime Super’s other investment holdings and
Southern Way Holdings in the supply of large-scale road
infrastructure services
b. the increment to Prime Super’s existing interest in Southern
Way Holdings is small
c. Southern Way Holding’s obligations with respect to the
Peninsula Link Project are governed, and will continue to be
governed post-acquisition, by long-term contracts with the
Victorian state government.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Buroserv Telco Holdings Pty Ltd (**BTH**) is a special purpose vehicle owned and incorporated by Salter Brothers Private Equity Pty Ltd which is in turn owned by Salter Brothers Funds Management Pty Ltd (**Salter Brothers**).

Salter Brothers is a professional fund management firm with a focus on specialist property, equities and credit investments. Salter Brothers is the fund manager of several property focused funds which own over 40 hotels and resorts around Australia, the US and Asia.

LMGPS Pty Ltd (**LMGPS**) provides telecommunication services to wholesale and retail customers in Australia, focussed on small-to-medium enterprises. LMGPS owns 100% of Buroserv Australia Pty Ltd, one of several operating entities owned by LMGPS (including Totalcom Corp Pty Ltd, Waterfront Networks Pty Ltd, Buroserv Networks Pty Ltd, V4 Communications Pty Ltd, Loaded Net Australia Pty Ltd, and Hurricane Compute Pty Ltd).

Salter Brothers, through BTH, is proposing to acquire select assets and liabilities of LMGPS Pty Ltd, including the above-named subsidiaries.

events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Buroserv Telco Holdings Pty Ltd, a special purpose vehicle owned by
Salter Brothers Funds Management Pty Ltd (Salter Brothers), applied
for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of
select assets and liabilities of LMGPS Pty Ltd (including 100% of
LMGPS Pty Ltd’s shareholding in Buroserv Australia Pty Ltd and
other subsidiaries listed below), as described in the transaction
documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
29 April 2026

Salter Brothers is a professional fund management firm with a focus
on specialist property, equities and credit investments. Salter
Brothers is the fund manager of several property focused funds
which own over 40 hotels and resorts around Australia, the US and
Asia. Salter Brothers has an interest in Skymesh. Skymesh provides
services to approximately 49,000 active fixed line services nationally.
LMGPS Pty Ltd provides telecommunication services to wholesale
and retail customers in Australia, focussed on small-to-medium
enterprises. LMGPS and its subsidiaries (Totalcom Corp Pty Ltd,
Waterfront Networks Pty Ltd, Buroserv Networks Pty Ltd, V4
Communications Pty Ltd, Loaded Net Australia Pty Ltd, Hurricane
Compute Pty Ltd and Buroserv Australia Pty Ltd) operate under the
brand name Buroserv. Buroserv provides services to approximately
37,000 NBN active fixed line services and 8,200 mobile services
nationally.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is a small horizontal overlap between Salter Brothers
and Buroserv in the supply of NBN and telecommunications
services in Australia
b. there are alternative providers of NBN and
telecommunication services in Australia.

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
DGH Design and Fabrication Services Pty Ltd (**DGH**), a wholly owned subsidiary of Cement Australia Holdings Pty Limited (**Cement Australia**), proposes to acquire 100% of the Gladstone business assets of Aylward Dowling And Wall Pty Ltd, trading as Global Manufacturing Group (**GMG**).

DGH is a fabrication and metal engineering company providing metal engineering, drafting, fabrication, fitting, machining, blast and painting services, with branches in Mackay, Emerald, and Townsville.

GMG is a fabrication and metal engineering company providing steel fabrication and coating services for all types of metal across the industrial manufacturing industry, with workshops in Maryborough and Gladstone.

The Acquisition only concerns GMG’s workshop in Gladstone. DGH does not have a branch in the Gladstone region but does supply some of Cement Australia’s Gladstone requirements from its Mackay branch.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
DGH Design and Fabrication Services Pty Ltd (DGH), a wholly owned
subsidiary of Cement Australia Holdings Pty Limited (Cement
Australia), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed
acquisition of 100% of the Gladstone business assets of Aylward
Dowling And Wall Pty Ltd, trading as Global Manufacturing Group
(GMG), as described in the transaction documents provided as part
of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, DGH, is a fabrication and metal engineering company
providing metal engineering, drafting, fabrication, fitting, machining,
blast, and painting services, with branches in Mackay, Emerald, and
Townsville. It does not have a branch in the Gladstone region but
supplies Cement Australia in Gladstone from its Mackay branch.
The target, GMG, is a fabrication and metal engineering company
providing steel fabrication and coating services for all types of metal
across the industrial manufacturing industry, with workshops in
Maryborough and Gladstone. The Acquisition relates only to GMG’s
workshop in Gladstone.
The parties both supply metal fabrication and engineering services,
including coating services, in the Gladstone region.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application, and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. there is small horizontal overlap between the parties in the
supply of metal fabrication and engineering services in the
Gladstone region, and their combined share of supply is
small
b. while there is a vertical relationship between the parties and
DGH’s parent company, Cement Australia, there appears to
be low risk of vertical foreclosure arising from the
Acquisition due to the presence of alternative suppliers and
customers of metal fabrication and engineering services,
including in Gladstone.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Sail Parent Holdco Ltd, an acquisition vehicle ultimately wholly owned by Constellation Software Inc. (**CSI**), a publicly listed company on the Toronto Stock Exchange, proposes to acquire a majority shareholding of around 90% of the issued equity interests in DerbySoft Holdings Limited (**DerbySoft**), with DerbySoft’s management retaining the remaining shareholding.

Sail Parent Holdco Ltd is part of the Vela Operating Group, a group of operating companies owned by Vela Software International Inc., which in turn is a wholly owned subsidiary of CSI. The CSI group of companies (**CSI Group**), including Vela Operating Group, acquire, manage and build industry-specific software businesses globally in a variety of public and private sector industries. CSI Group includes two operating groups which, among other things, have teams focused on providing software to the global travel and hospitality industries: the Juniper Group, which is part of the Vela Operating Group, and the Jonas Group, another operating group of CSI.

DerbySoft and its subsidiaries are a business-to-business travel technology group that provides software and connectivity platform services facilitating digital commerce across the global travel ecosystem. It acts as a travel commerce accelerator, combining connectivity, marketing technology and end-to-end content distribution into a single ecosystem.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Woodward pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Sail Parent Holdco Ltd (Parent), an acquisition vehicle ultimately
wholly owned by Constellation Software Inc. (CSI), applied for a
notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of a
majority shareholding of around 90% of the issued equity interests in
DerbySoft Holdings Limited (DerbySoft), as described in the
transaction documents provided as part of the application (the
Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, Parent, is part of the Vela Operating Group, a group of
operating companies owned by Vela Software International Inc.,
which in turn is a wholly owned subsidiary of CSI. The CSI group of
companies (CSI Group), including Vela Operating Group, acquire,
manage, and build industry-specific software businesses globally in
a variety of public and private sector industries.
CSI Group includes two operating groups which, among other things,
have teams focused on providing software to the global travel and
hospitality industries: the Juniper Group and the Jonas Group. The
services provided by these two groups include flight distribution
services, booking engine services, and digital marketing and
hospitality technology services.
The target, DerbySoft, is a B2B commerce platform operating in the
global travel industry. This industry involves platforms like DerbySoft
providing connectivity, digital marketing, and B2B marketplace
services to hotels, airlines, and other travel suppliers (e.g. tour
operators), connecting them with distributors and other channels,
such as online travel agencies, travel management companies,
metasearch platforms, and wholesalers across the globe.
The parties both supply digital software solutions and services for
the global travel and hospitality industries.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is no horizontal overlap or vertical relationship
between any of the operations of CSI Group’s portfolio
companies and DerbySoft in Australia
b. DerbySoft has a small Australian presence.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Türk Hava Yolları Anonim Ortaklığı (together with all its controlled entities, **Turkish Airlines**) is proposing to acquire a 26–27% controlling stake in Air Europa Holding, S.L. and indirectly its wholly owned subsidiaries Air Europa Líneas Aéreas, S.A.U.; Air Europa Suma Miles, S.L.U.; Aeronova, S.L.U.; León Activos Aeronáuticos, S.L.U.; and Globalia Activos Inmobiliarios, S.A.U. (together, **Air Europa**).

Turkish Airlines operates in the aviation sector, globally. It provides passenger and cargo air transportation, including international flights into and out of over 130 countries. Turkish Airlines also conducts other related activities including maintenance and repair operations, courier services, manufacturing of aircraft interiors, and ground services. These services are provided in Türkiye. In Australia, Turkish Airlines provides international passenger air transport into, and out of, Melbourne and Sydney, either directly or by way of cooperation agreements; and cargo air transportation using the belly-hold capacity of certain passenger flights.

Air Europa is a Spanish aviation group that primarily operates international and domestic scheduled air services for the carriage of passengers and cargo. Air Europa does not operate any flights to or from Australia. Its activities in Australia are largely confined to the sale of passenger tickets for routes that are wholly operated outside of Australia, with some sales for flights that include a segment operated by a third-party airline pursuant to a codesharing/interlining agreement for passenger transportation services, and that may have Australia as either the origin or the destination.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Türk Hava Yolları Anonim Ortaklığı (together with its controlled
entities, Turkish Airlines) applied for a notification waiver in respect
of its proposed acquisition of a 26–27% controlling stake in Air
Europa Holding, S.L. (together with its subsidiaries, Air Europa), as
described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, Turkish Airlines, operates in the aviation sector
globally. It provides passenger and cargo air transportation,
including international flights into and out of over 130 countries.
Turkish Airlines also conducts other related activities including
maintenance and repair operations, courier services, manufacturing
of aircraft interiors, and ground services. These services are
provided in Türkiye.
In Australia, Turkish Airlines provides international passenger air
transport services into, and out of, Melbourne and Sydney (either
directly or by way of cooperation agreements) and cargo air
transportation using the belly-hold capacity of certain passenger
flights.
The target, Air Europa, is a Spanish aviation group that primarily
operates international and domestic scheduled air services for the
carriage of passengers and cargo. Air Europa is a wholly-owned and
controlled subsidiary of Globalia Corporación Empresarial, S.A.
(Globalia). Globalia is a Spanish tourism group which does not have
any corporate presence or assets in Australia.
Air Europa does not operate any flights to or from Australia. Its
activities in Australia are largely confined to the sale of passenger
tickets for routes that are wholly operated outside of Australia (e.g.
Madrid–Paris), with some sales for international flights that include a
segment to/from Australia where that segment is operated by a
third-party airline pursuant to a codesharing/interlining agreement
for air passenger transport services.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is no direct horizontal overlap between Turkish Airlines
and Air Europa in the supply of international air passenger
transport services to and from Australia
b. Air Europa has a small, indirect presence in the supply of
international air passenger transport services to and from
Australia via codesharing/interlining agreements with a
third-party airline, however it does not operate the Australian
segment of any international flights
c. there are several alternative suppliers of international air
passenger transport services to and from Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
HCL Technologies Limited (**HCLTech**) proposes to acquire Jaspersoft, a business unit of Cloud Software Group that supplies a reporting software platform (including JasperReports®).

The acquirer, HCLTech, is a global technology company listed on the National Stock Exchange of India. HCLTech provides a broad range of services and software products with capabilities centred around AI, digital, engineering, cloud and software to a range of industries. These solutions include supporting organisations with AI infrastructure, product engineering services, digital consultancy services, and specialized industry software. Relevantly, HCLTech provides software that includes data management, integration, governance and analytics readiness.

The target, Jaspersoft, provides reporting solutions software (including JasperReports) used by organisations to develop pixel-perfect reports, dashboards and visualisations. Jaspersoft licenses its reporting software to organisations to embed within their applications and to independent software vendors for inclusion within their applications.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
HCL Technologies Limited (HCLTech) applied for a notification
waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of Jaspersoft, a
business unit of Cloud Software Group, Inc., as described in the
transaction documents provided as part of the application (the
Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
18 February 2026

The acquirer, HCLTech is a global technology company listed on the
National Stock Exchange of India. HCLTech provides a broad range
of services and software products with capabilities centred around
AI, digital, engineering, cloud and software to a range of industries.
These solutions include supporting organisations with AI
infrastructure, product engineering services, digital consultancy
services, and specialized industry software. Relevantly, HCLTech
provides software that includes data management, integration,
governance and analytics readiness.
The target, Jaspersoft, provides reporting solutions software
(including JasperReports) used by organisations to develop pixel-
perfect reports, dashboards and visualisations. Jaspersoft licenses
its reporting software to organisations to embed within their
applications and to independent software vendors for inclusion
within their applications.
While the parties both supply enterprise software in Australia,
HCLTech does not supply reporting software solutions.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. There is no horizontal overlap in the supply of reporting
software in Australia and a small horizontal overlap between
HCLTech and Jaspersoft in the supply of enterprise
software in Australia.
b. Jaspersoft has a small presence in Australia.
c. The presence of several competitors in the supply of
enterprise and reporting software.
d. There is a low risk of concerning conglomerate effects
resulting from bundling or tying of products post-
Acquisition.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Spike Media Pty Ltd, a newly-formed entity under the control of Arthur Laundy, proposes to acquire 100% of the shares in Nine Radio Pty Limited (**Nine Radio**) from Fairfax Media Limited, a wholly-owned subsidiary of Nine Entertainment Co. Holdings Limited.

Arthur Laundy currently controls the Laundy Group, which operates over 40 hospitality venues throughout New South Wales through Laundy Hotels Pty Ltd and invests in Australian assets, including in commercial property, sports, tourism, hospitality, events and accommodation.

Nine Radio is an Australian broadcaster engaged in commercial radio broadcasting through its radio stations in Sydney (2GB and 2UE), Melbourne (3AW and Magic 1278), Brisbane (4BC and 4BH), and Perth (6PR). Nine Radio also offers these radio stations through linear radio broadcast live streaming distributed via Nine Radio websites and apps, in addition to on-demand catch-up podcasts and other original podcasts.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Spike Media Pty Ltd (Spike Media), an entity under the control of
Arthur Laundy, applied for a notification waiver in respect of Spike
Media’s proposed acquisition of 100% of the share capital in Nine
Radio Pty Ltd (Nine Radio) (ACN 063 906 927), as described in the
transaction documents provided as part of the application (the
Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
20 February 2026

Arthur Laundy currently controls the Laundy Group, which operates
over 40 hospitality venues throughout New South Wales through
Laundy Hotels Pty Ltd (ACN 001 217 554) and invests in Australian
assets, including in commercial property, sports, tourism, hospitality,
events and accommodation. Spike Media is a newly-formed entity
for the purpose of this acquisition.
The target, Nine Radio, is an Australian broadcaster engaged in
commercial radio broadcasting through its radio stations in Sydney
(2GB and 2UE), Melbourne (3AW and Magic 1278), Brisbane (4BC
and 4BH), and Perth (6PR). Nine Radio also offers these radio
stations through linear radio broadcast live streaming distributed via
Nine Radio websites and apps, in addition to on-demand catch-up
podcasts and other original podcasts. Nine Radio is currently owned
by Fairfax Media Limited, a wholly-owned subsidiary of Nine
Entertainment Co. Holdings Limited.
Laundy Group and its connected entities do not currently have any
assets or investments in radio or interests in any other broadcasting,
media or communications business.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the
information provided with the notification waiver application and
publicly available information, and had regard to the factors in
section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010
(Cth) (Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is no horizontal overlap between the Laundy Group
(including its connected entities) and Nine Radio in the
supply of news and other media content to consumers or
listeners.
b. there appears to be a low risk of foreclosure or other
concerning vertical or conglomerate effects resulting from
exclusionary conduct, bundling or tying of products post-
acquisition, due to the presence of alternative suppliers of
advertising opportunities, including other radio stations and
streaming platforms.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on

targets.name: The trustee for the Burke Family Trust

mergers.merger_description:
Vets Central Holdings Pty Ltd (together, with its subsidiaries, **Vets Central**), proposes to acquire 100% of the business assets (both tangible and intangible) of West Toowoomba Veterinary Surgery from Cicuration Pty Ltd as trustee for Burke Family Trust.

Vets Central is an Australian-owned veterinary group that owns and operates 80 first opinion veterinary practices across Australia and New Zealand. In Australia, Vets Central’s practices provide core and ancillary veterinary services, for household pets as well as livestock, and other exotic species. The services primarily include routine, planned, and emergency veterinary services (excluding after-hours services).

West Toowoomba Veterinary Surgery is a single full-service veterinary practice in the Toowoomba area of Queensland. The business provides a range of first opinion core and ancillary veterinary services for household pets (extending to exotic pets) in the local area.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
VC Operations (WA) Pty Ltd, a wholly owned subsidiary of Vets
Central Holdings Pty Ltd (together, with its subsidiaries, Vets
Central), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed
acquisition of 100% of the business assets of West Toowoomba
Veterinary Surgery, as described in the transaction documents
provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
10 March 2026

The acquirer, Vets Central, is an Australian-owned veterinary group
that owns and operates 80 first opinion veterinary practices across
Australia and New Zealand. In Australia, Vets Central’s practices
provide core and ancillary veterinary services, for household pets as
well as livestock, and other exotic species. The services primarily
include routine, planned, and emergency veterinary services
(excluding after-hours services).
The target, West Toowoomba Veterinary Surgery, is a single full-
service veterinary practice in the Toowoomba area of Queensland.
The business provides a range of first opinion core and ancillary
veterinary services for household pets (extending to exotic pets) in
the local area.
Vets Central and West Toowoomba Veterinary Surgery supply first
opinion veterinary services to household pets, livestock, and exotic
species in Queensland.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that:
a. there is no geographic horizontal overlap between Vets
Central and West Toowoomba Veterinary Surgery in the
supply of veterinary services in Toowoomba.
b. the presence of alternative suppliers of veterinary services in
Toowoomba and more broadly at the state and national
level.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_name: McDonald’s Australia – McDonald’s Restaurants in Bankstown and Yagoona, NSW

targets.name: LVANT MECCA PTY LIMITED as trustee for CARMICHAEL FAMILY TRUST

mergers.merger_description:
McDonald’s Australia Limited (**McDonald’s Australia**) owns 1,076 McDonald’s restaurants around Australia. It generally operates around 15% of these restaurants (**Corporate Restaurants**) and licenses the right to operate and occupy the remainder (**Franchised Restaurants**). Both the Corporate Restaurants and Franchised Restaurants supply McDonald’s branded fast-food products through dine in, takeaway, drive through and/or digital ordering channels.

On 2 September 2025, McDonald’s Australia, Lvant Mecca Pty Limited (**Franchisee**) and Nicole Carmichael (**Guarantor**) entered into franchise agreements or licences (**Franchise Agreement**) and occupation licences, leases or subleases (**Occupation Agreement**) in respect of the Bankstown Centro F/C, Yagoona and Bankstown McDonald’s restaurants (**Target Restaurants**). These agreements granted the Franchisee the right to occupy and operate the Target Restaurants.

McDonald’s Australia, the Franchisee and the Guarantor propose to enter a Deed of Surrender, Release and Asset Purchase in respect of the Target Restaurants (**Buyback Agreement**). Pursuant to this Buyback Agreement, the Franchisee will surrender the Franchise Agreement and Occupation Agreement to McDonald’s Australia and sell the property used in the Target Restaurants to McDonald’s Australia (**Acquisition**).

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
McDonald’s Australia Limited (McDonald’s Australia), Lyant Mecca
Pty Limited (Franchisee) and Nicole Carmichael (Guarantor) propose
to enter a Deed of Surrender, Release and Asset Purchase in respect
of three McDonald’s restaurants, namely Bankstown Centro F/C,
Yagoona and Bankstown McDonald’s (Target Restaurants), as
described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).
The Acquisition will involve the Franchisee surrendering their
franchise agreement and occupation agreement to McDonald’s
Australia, and selling the property used in the Target Restaurants to
McDonald’s Australia.
McDonald’s Australia applied for a notification waiver in respect of
the Acquisition.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
31 March 2026

The acquirer, McDonald’s Australia, owns 1,076 McDonald’s
restaurants around Australia. It generally operates around 15% of
these restaurants (Corporate Restaurants) and licenses the right to
operate and occupy the remainder (Franchised Restaurants).
The Target Restaurants are three McDonald’s restaurants, which are
currently licensed to the Franchisee and are located at:
• McDonald’s Bankstown Centro: Bankstown Central Shopping
Centre
• McDonald’s Bankstown: 37 Rickard Road, Bankstown NSW
2200
• McDonald’s Yagoona: Cnr Hume Highway and Avon Street,
Yagoona NSW 2199.
Both the Corporate Restaurants and Franchised Restaurants supply
McDonald’s branded fast food products through dine in, takeaway,
drive through and/or digital ordering channels.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. the Acquisition involves the transfer from a franchisee to the
franchisor with no change in the number of McDonald’s
restaurants in the area
b. there are other providers of fast food products in the
Bankstown and Yagoona region.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_description:
Lighthouse Service Partners Holdco Pty Ltd, through its subsidiary Lighthouse Service Partners Bidco Pty Ltd (together, **Lighthouse**), proposes to acquire 100% of the issued share capital in Precise Plumbing Pty Ltd (**Precise Plumbing**), SA Pipe Relining Pty Ltd (**SAPR**) and Precise Trade Services Pty Ltd (**PTS**) (together, **Precise Services Group**) from its current shareholders.

Lighthouse’s investment structure focuses on acquisitions and investments in home services businesses across plumbing, heating ventilation and air conditioning (HVAC), electrical and related trades in Australia (together, **“integrated building services”**). Lighthouse is ultimately owned by Alpine Investors, a private equity firm headquartered in San Francisco, California.

Lighthouse owns Ken Hall Holdings Pty Ltd which operates in the Greater Adelaide region and predominantly provides residential integrated building services, including plumbing, electrical, HVAC, roofing, tiling, bathroom renovations, handyman services, and related home maintenance services.

The Precise Services Group companies provide trade services to residential and commercial customers across the Greater Adelaide region, including:

* Precise Plumbing provides integrated building services to predominantly residential customers in respect of plumbing, electrical, HVAC, bathroom renovations and handyman services; and
* SAPR provides plumbing services to residential and commercial customers and specialises in pipe relining.
* PTS is a shared service entity which is a cost centre for PPE and SAPR

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Lighthouse Service Partners Holdco Pty Ltd, through its subsidiary
Lighthouse Service Partners Bidco Pty Ltd (together, Lighthouse)
applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed
acquisition of 100% of the share capital in Precise Plumbing Pty Ltd,
SA Pipe Relining Pty. Ltd. and Precise Trade Services Pty Ltd
(together, Precise Services Group), as described in the transaction
documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
13 April 2026

The acquirer, Lighthouse, is a holding company with an investment
structure that focuses on acquisitions and investments in home
services businesses across plumbing, heating ventilation and air
conditioning (HVAC), electrical and related trades in Australia.
Lighthouse is ultimately owned by Alpine Investors, a private equity
firm headquartered in San Francisco, California.
As part of its investments, Lighthouse owns Ken Hall Holdings Pty
Ltd which operates in the Greater Adelaide region and predominantly
provides residential integrated building services, including plumbing,
electrical, HVAC, roofing, tiling, bathroom renovations, handyman
services, and related home maintenance services.
The target, the Precise Services Group, provides trade services to
residential and commercial customers across the Greater Adelaide
region, including plumbing, electrical, HVAC, bathroom renovations,
and handyman services.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application, and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there are alternative suppliers of plumbing, electrical, HVAC,
bathroom renovations, and handyman services in the
Greater Adelaide region
b. the parties’ combined share in the supply of each of
plumbing, electrical, HVAC, bathroom renovations, and
handyman services to customers in Greater Adelaide is low
and the increase in share of supply arising from the
Acquisition would be small.
In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to
reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification
thresholds being met.
Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the
ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

mergers.merger_name: Ochre Health - Newstead Medical and Kings Meadows Medical Centre, TAS

A.J FAIR & T.G GARDNER & D.P KIRK
TRUSTEE FOR FAIR FAMILY TRUST & Kirk Family Trust & Gardner Family Trust

mergers.merger_description:
Ochre Health Pty Limited (**Ochre Health**) proposes to acquire the business and all of the assets of Newstead Medical (including the co-located Urgent Care Centre) and Kings Meadows Medical Centre (**Newstead and Kings Meadows clinics**) through a business and assets acquisition agreement (the **Acquisition**).

The acquirer, Ochre Health, supports independent doctors to provide general practice medical services in regional, rural and urban communities around Australia. Ochre Health currently operates 68 medical centres and urgent care clinics located in the ACT, NSW, Queensland, Tasmania and Victoria, but currently does not operate any medical centres or urgent care clinics within 40km of Launceston, Tasmania. Ochre Health Group’s medical recruitment business, Ochre Recruitment, also works with hospitals and medical practices across Australia and NZ to source and place locum and permanent doctors across a range of specialities.

Ochre Health is majority owned and controlled by Genesis Capital (**Genesis**), an Australian-based private equity firm that invests in businesses operating in the healthcare sector.

The Newstead and Kings Meadows clinics provide general practice medical services from their respective premises in Launceston, Tasmania. A private urgent care centre is co-located within the Newstead clinic for treatment of non-life-threatening urgent medical presentations. The Newstead and Kings Meadows clinics are currently owned and operated by general practitioners Andrew Fair, Daniel Kirk, and Toby Gardner in partnership as the Newmed Group and the trustees of their family trusts in partnership as Newstead Health Nexus.

mergers.merger_description:
Publicis Media Australia Pty Ltd, an indirect wholly owned subsidiary of Publicis Groupe S.A. (**Publicis**), proposes to acquire certain assets of International Management Group of America Pty Ltd (trading as 160over90) (**160over90**) from the WME Group (the **Acquisition**).

Publicis is a multinational marketing communications holding corporation, listed on Euronext Paris. It is a global leader in communication, present in over 100 countries and employing around 114,000 professionals. Its expertise spans communication, media, data and technology.

In Australia, Publicis operates through its wholly owned subsidiaries, including Publicis Communications Australia (advertising, creative, public relations and production services), Publicis Media Australia (media planning, strategy and buying services), and Publicis Sapient Australia (digital business transformation), among others.

160over90 is a marketing agency which forms part of the WME Group. 160over90 is a global marketing and branding agency specialising in cultural, sports, and entertainment marketing, with over 670 employees across the US, UK, EMEA and APAC.

In Australia, 160over90 has a small presence, providing marketing services, including digital and social media management, event activation, partnership procurement and activation, public relations and creative services. 160over90 does not supply any media buying services.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian

mergers.merger_name: DP World Global Forwarding – Pure Global Logistics

acquirers.name: DP WORLD GLOBAL FORWARDING PTY LTD

mergers.merger_description:
DPW Global Forwarding Pty Ltd (t/as **DP World Global Forwarding**) proposes to acquire 100% of the shares in Pac Ware Pty Ltd (t/as **Pure Global Logistics**), under a Share Sale Agreement (the **Acquisition**).

DP World Global Forwarding provides contract logistics and freight forwarding services in Australia, which includes warehousing, fulfilment and e-commerce support.

DP World Global Forwarding’s services encompass freight forwarding by ocean, air, road and rail transportation across Australia, including associated warehousing and logistics support. DP World Global Forwarding is a 100% owned subsidiary of DP World Logistics Oceania (Holding) Pty Ltd, which is ultimately controlled by DP World Limited, a United Arab Emirates entity.

DP World Australia Limited (**DP World Australia**) is a connected entity of DP World Global Forwarding and operates container terminals and provides container stevedoring services at each of the Ports of Melbourne, Botany (Sydney), Brisbane and Fremantle.

Pure Global Logistics is an Australian freight forwarding business specialising in air and sea transportation and customs clearance, primarily for fashion and discount retailers in Australia.

Both DP World Global Forwarding and Pure Global Logistics supply international freight forwarding services to Australian customers (mostly to customers importing, rather than exporting, goods).

mergers.merger_description:
Turbo Fast Track Buyer Pty Ltd (**BidCo**) proposes to acquire up to 100% of the issued share capital in Grant Thornton Australia Limited (**Grant Thornton Australia**) by way of a scheme of arrangement.

BidCo is a newly incorporated Australian entity and is indirectly wholly owned by Turbo EMEA Holdings B.V. (**Turbo EMEA**), a holding entity incorporated in the Netherlands, which is also the guarantor for BidCo’s obligations under the scheme implementation deed. Both BidCo and Turbo EMEA are non-operating holding companies.

Turbo EMEA’s ultimate parent is Turbo Parent Partnership LP (**Turbo LP**), a Delaware limited partnership, which is owned by an investor group led by New Mountain Capital LLC (**NMC**), and individual Grant Thornton partners from across the Americas, Europe, the Middle East and APAC (excluding Australia).

For the avoidance of doubt, NMC has a non-controlling minority interest in Turbo LP. Turbo LP is also the ultimate parent of a number of Grant Thornton operating entities across the globe, including Grant Thornton Advisors LLC in the US (**Grant Thornton Advisors**) and other non-US Grant Thornton operating entities. These entities operate or hold interests in the Grant Thornton businesses which provide audit and assurance, tax advisory and compliance, business advisory and consulting, corporate finance and transaction advisory and other related professional services.

For completeness, the Acquirer and its connected entities currently have no presence in Australia, including any offices, employees (or other staff), assets, or operations.

Grant Thornton Australia is a leading Australian professional services firm and part of the Grant Thornton International (**GTI**) network. Grant Thornton Australia supplies audit and assurance, corporate recovery and insolvency, corporate finance, taxation advice, management consultancy, and financial planning and advisory services to clients across a range of industries in Australia, including energy and resources, financial services, health and aged care, technology, and not-for-profits. Grant Thornton Australia’s current national office network and geographic footprint cover Cairns, Brisbane, Sydney, Melbourne, Adelaide and Perth.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Turbo Fast Track Buyer Pty Ltd (BidCo), a newly incorporated
Australian entity applied for a notification waiver in respect of its
proposed acquisition of up to 100% of the issued share capital in
Grant Thornton Australia Limited (Grant Thornton Australia), as
described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
4 June 2026

The acquirer, BidCo, is a newly incorporated Australian entity
indirectly wholly owned by Turbo EMEA Holdings B.V. (Turbo EMEA),
a holding entity incorporated in the Netherlands. Both BidCo and
Turbo EMEA are non-operating holding companies.
Turbo EMEA’s ultimate parent is Turbo Parent Partnership LP (Turbo
LP), a Delaware limited partnership owned by an investor group led
by New Mountain Capital LLC (NMC) and individual Grant Thornton
partners from across the Americas, Europe, the Middle East, and
APAC (excluding Australia).
NMC has a non-controlling minority interest in Turbo LP. Turbo LP is
also the ultimate parent of a number of Grant Thornton operating
entities across the globe, including Grant Thornton Advisors LLC in
the US (Grant Thornton Advisors) and other non-US Grant Thornton
operating entities. These entities operate or hold interests in the
Grant Thornton businesses which provide audit and assurance, tax
advisory and compliance, business advisory and consulting,
corporate finance and transaction advisory and other related
professional services. BidCo and its connected entities currently
have no presence in Australia, including any offices, employees (or
other staff), assets, or operations.
The target, Grant Thornton Australia, is a leading Australian
professional services firm and part of the Grant Thornton
International network. It supplies audit and assurance, corporate
recovery and insolvency, corporate finance, taxation advice,
management consultancy, and financial planning and advisory
services to clients across a range of industries in Australia. Grant
Thornton Australia’s current geographic footprint covers Cairns,
Brisbane, Sydney, Melbourne, Adelaide and Perth.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application, and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular, there is no horizontal overlap between the
parties (or their connected entities) in the supply of professional
services (including audit and assurance, corporate recovery and
insolvency, corporate finance, taxation advice, management
consultancy, and financial planning and advisory services) in
Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.The ACCC considers that the determination is
consistent with the object of the Act and the interests of consumers
in promoting competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
